Quest
A Last Keepsake
Location
Lothering
Start
Chanter's Board
End
Chanter Devons
Previous
Bandits, Bandits, Everywhere
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Goodwife Sarha of
Lothering
has gone missing for the better part of a week. She is presumed dead, and a reward is offered for recovering any of her personal effects. (
Bandits, Bandits, Everywhere
must be completed for this job to be posted.)
Walkthrough
Sarha's body is on a grassy rise north of Lothering, near the
landmark tree
and a large pack of
wolves
. Retrieve
Sarha's Keepsake
from her body and bring it to Chanter
Devons
for a reward.
Rewards
50
150 XP
If this is the last quest on Lothering Chanter's Board to be turned in, additionally receive:
Oathkeeper
and 250 XP
Notes
This will not be a separate quest entry, but a subsection of the "Chanter's Board" entry under the "Lothering" tab in the Quest Journal.
There is a
tree
near the corpse, identified as 'Landmark tree', which is
Dog
's earliest opportunity to achieve
Mabari Dominance
.
It is presumed, though not confirmed, that Sarha is the mother of the
Little Boy
near the bridge into the second half of Lothering.

======================================================================
PAGE: A Little Dragon Hatches
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Little_Dragon_Hatches
======================================================================

Quest
A Little Dragon Hatches
Type
Companion quest
Location
The Hossberg Wetlands
,
Old Crossroads
Previous
Dragon Class
Next
Secrets of the Qunari
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
A Little Dragon Hatches
is a
companion quest
for
Taash
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. Taash confronts their own identity and makes a decision.
Acquisition
This quest unlocks immediately after completing
The Siege of Weisshaupt
. During an initial conversation with Taash in their room, Rook and Taash discuss the
dar-saam
Qunari
knots and
Shathann
's insistence Taash master them even as a young child.
If Rook flirted with Taash during
Fire on the Sands
, Taash will pin
Rook
against the mirror and ask if this is harmless flirting (
kashlok
) or genuine attraction (
taamlok
). Rook can choose to continue flirting (without full romance commitment) or permanently break off any chance of romance with Taash. If Rook chooses to continue the relationship, Taash will be pleased and interested, but if Rook chooses to end it, Taash is not disappointed or hurt, simply grateful for the clear communication.
Either way,
Harding
will arrive at the end of the conversation to report the
Grey Wardens
have found a
blighted
dragon
's lair in the
Hossberg Wetlands
, though there's no sign of the dragon itself. Taash insists on checking it out.
At the conclusion of this conversation, Rook will acquire the
A Little Dragon Hatches
quest.
Note:
This quest must be completed prior to having the library conversation of
A Fighting Chance
. (Having the kitchen table conversation will not interfere.) Otherwise, traveling to
Hossberg
will automatically begin
Fire and Ice
, thus locking out this quest. Taash will settle the question of their gender on their own (with
Neve
and
Harding
's help), and they will not be available to romance.
Walkthrough
Exploring a dragon's cave yields some new information for Taash.
Travel with
Taash
to the Old Crossroads region of the
Hossberg Wetlands
.
Near dawn, Rook catches up to Taash in the dragon's empty lair. Taash is agitated that the dragon is not there; they insist they could have fought her and are determined to find clues about whether the dragon will become a monster like
Ghilan'nain
's
Archdemon
. Rook tries to calm Taash's clear distress, but Taash pushes back on any sentimentality and begins pacing through the cavern. Eventually, they and Rook discover some forgotten
dar-saam
ropes, implicating the
Antaam
as the ones who have been blighting the dragons for the gods.
Antaam ropes left behind in a dragon's lair.
Taash is clearly devastated, lambasting them for tying her down and turning her into something "she was never supposed to be." However, they emotionally insist they will be ready to fight her as the team's dragon slayer.
When Rook questions Taash, they finally burst out with their real fears: they feel they are a bad
Qunari
, not really
Rivaini
, no good as a daughter, and not even good at being a woman. Being a dragon slayer is all they have, and they must excel at that.
However Rook chooses to support them, Taash laments the ropes (and expectations) laid on them by
their mother
and discusses some
Shadow Dragons
' use of "they" instead of "he" or "she." They like how that feels and wish to pursue it, but are afraid of Shathann's reaction and don't understand what she wants from them.
Rook can now choose one of a few options.
Encourage Taash to embrace
Rivaini
culture. This does not commit Rook or Taash to any final outcomes for Taash's companion questline, but will frustrate Shathann and lead to tension in future conversations. Dialogue with Taash and Shathann will reflect Taash's choice to pursue Rivaini culture more closely.
Encourage Taash to embrace
Qunari
culture. This does not commit Rook or Taash to any final outcomes for Taash's companion questline, but will please Shathann and ease some tensions in future conversations. Dialogue with Taash and Shathann will reflect Taash's choice to pursue Qunari culture more closely.
If romancing Taash, Rook can tell them how much they care for them and commit to an exclusive romance. Taash will affirm they'd like to use "they," but is afraid of ruining things with Rook. When Rook reassures them this will not change how they feel, they kiss and the scene concludes. This will break off all other romances and lock in the Taash relationship.
Note:
If Rook is romancing Taash, selecting any option besides this choice will end the relationship.
Rewards
Taash greatly approves
Bond increase with Taash
Results
With her confidence shaken after the tragedy at Weisshaupt, Taash learns that a blighted dragon has made a lair in the Hossberg Wetlands. Desperate to prove herself as a dragon hunter, Taash decides to explore the lair.
Exploring a dragon's cave yields some new information for Taash.
Approval
I have to figure this out. I’m a dragon slayer.
—
No changes
Yes, but not alone.
Stop and think.
Taash, you’re hurting.
I have to be a dragon slayer.
And you are. - No changes
We’re doing this here? Fine. —
Taash Approves
I’m so sorry, Taash. —
Taash Approves
(Non-Binary or Trans option) I know how this feels —
Taash Approves
What does she want me to be?
—
No changes
(Romantic) She cares for you. So do I.
Commit to an exclusive relationship with Taash.
Forget what she wants. (Ends conversation)
Forego romance and urge Taash to embrace Rivaini culture.
She’s using the Qun to help. (Ends conversation)
Forego romance and urge Taash to embrace Qunari culture.
I need to know if it changes how you feel about me.
—
No changes
It’s what matters to you.
It’s great with me, gorgeous.
Knock it off. I’m fine.
Notes
Taash reveals they can smell sexual attraction and they generally have an excellent sense of smell, thanks to their
adaari
blood.
This quest must be completed before beginning the "library conversation" objective of the main quest
A Fighting Chance
. After that point, all travel to Hossberg will automatically begin the main quest
Fire and Ice
, even if
A Little Dragon Hatches
has been acquired and is active in the journal. At the conclusion of Fire and Ice, this quest will expire and be removed from the quest log.
If this quest is skipped, Taash will elect on their own to pursue Rivaini culture rather than Qunari. (Pop-up text in following conversations may still indicate it was Rook's suggestion, however.)
If Rook commits to a romance with Taash, Taash will elect to pursue Rivaini culture. It is not possible to romance Taash and encourage Qunari cultural pursuit at this stage, though the romance does not preclude any decisions made as part of
Lair of the Dragon King
.
The initial conversation either breaks off romance with Taash or allows for the continuation of flirting, though without yet committing to exclusivity. The final conversation in Hossberg, however, requires final romance commitment (or end).

======================================================================
PAGE: A Lord's Trust/A Prince's Favor: The Second Task
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Lord's_Trust/A_Prince's_Favor:_The_Second_Task
======================================================================

Quest
A Lord's Trust/A Prince's Favor: The Second Task
Location
Carta Hideout
Previous
Entering Jarvia's Hideout
Next
Anvil of the Void
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
A Prince's Favor: The Second Task
or
A Lord's Trust: The Second Task
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
It is a sub-quest of
A Paragon of Her Kind
quest line, obtained after completing
Entering Jarvia's Hideout
.
Walkthrough
Now you are inside the
Carta Hideout
and it's time to find
Jarvia
.
There are many rooms and doors with several thugs within and most will have Chests and Barrels with loot in them.
As well as much loot, you may also begin the
Jammer's Stash
Side-Quest. In addition to this quest, there is a Jail room where you will find the character Leske, who you may free by killing the Jailor and taking his key. (Leske is only imprisoned here if you are not a Dwarf Commoner; if that is the case, the would-be Dwarven Commoner Warden's corpse is seen in the other cell)
After making it to the heart of the Carta Hideout, you will find
Jarvia
who initiates another short dialogue scene. (If playing as the Dwarf Commoner, Leske will be there as her lieutenant.) When the battle initiates, several Carta Assassins will appear to aid her.
The tripwires at the top of the stairs behind Jarvia are difficult to disarm; in order for a rogue to disable them, switch to 3rd-person top-down view and then move the mouse pointer along the tripwire to either side and into the wall until the disarm icon appears. (PC Only)
If you are intending to double-cross Bhelen and have obtained the
Shifting Allegiances
quest from Dulin, then the
Incriminating Evidence
needed to complete this quest can be found in a chest towards the back of the room where Jarvia is confronted.This will add +1 support for Harrowmont.
If you are intending to double-cross Harrowmont and have obtained the
Betrayed from Within
quest, then be sure to plant the
Forged Documents
in the chest with the quest marker before leaving. This will add +1 support for Bhelen.
Killing Jarvia will add +3 support for your chosen candidate. For detailed strategies for the fight against Jarvia, see
Jarvia (strategy)
.
After exiting the hideout, you will emerge in
Janar Armorers
.
Janar
himself will not be happy about the hole you made in his wall.
If you go to Dust Town there are 3 Jarvia Supporters south of Nadezda.  They do not attack unless you talk to them.
Take the news of Jarvia's death to either Bhelen Aeducan or Lord Pyral Harrowmont - depending on who requested the task - to complete the quest.
Result
After successfully killing
Jarvia
and reporting to either Bhelen Aeducan or Lord Pyral Harrowmont,
Anvil of the Void (quest)
will be available.
1750 XP, upon reporting back to either Prince Bhelen or Lord Harrowmont.
Remember to report back to either Dulin with the incriminating evidence or Vartag about planting documents, to complete
Shifting Allegiances
or
Betrayed from Within
, respectively, if relevant.
Bugs
If
Betrayed from Within
was obtained and the Warden planted incriminating evidence, Lord Pyral Harrowmont will give the quest
A Lord's Favor: The Third Task
but
A Lord's Trust: The Second Task
will not register as completed. Workaround requires talking to Lord Pyral Harrowmont prior to planting the incriminating evidence. After the conversation and planting the evidence,
Vartag
is the next person to talk to, who will now be in Royal Palace.
Sometimes if your stand in just the right spot towards where you find Pique's box, you will be suddenly stopped, then find yourself randomly entering Jarvia's room without going through other rooms.
xbox360
If both Bhelen and Harrowmont were at least once double-crossed, either after the first or the second task, the Warden will be presented with both options at the chest: to plant the evidence for Bhelen,
and
to get proof that Bhelen ordered attack on his brother. However, the Warden can only hear about either planting or getting the evidence, never both, and this happens despite the fact the Warden was never given the appropriate quest.

======================================================================
PAGE: A Lord's Trust: The First Task
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Lord's_Trust:_The_First_Task
======================================================================

Quest
A Lord's Trust: The First Task
Location
Orzammar
Quest Giver
Dulin Forender
End
Dulin Forender
Previous
Seek out Steward Bandelor
Next
Entering Jarvia's Hideout
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
A Lord's Trust: The First Task
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
It is a sub-quest of
A Paragon of Her Kind
quest line, obtained after completing
Seek out Steward Bandelor
.
Background
The
dwarven
kingdom of
Orzammar
finds itself in a power vacuum, with two powerful men vying for the throne,
Prince Bhelen
and
Lord
Harrowmont
. If
the Warden
wishes to
publicly
support Lord Harrowmont and earn his trust, one must perform several favors for him.
This is first quest of three which the Warden will be tasked to complete for Harrowmont.
Walkthrough
Lord Harrowmont's Steward,
Dulin Forender
, can be found in the
Orzammar Diamond Quarter
, just outside of
Harrowmont's Estate
. He will require that the Warden prove his loyalty by performing a task for Lord Harrowmont: fighting in the
Proving
in Harrowmont's name. All of Harrowmont's other champions have been blackmailed or threatened into dropping out of the competition.
Note:
Having
Zevran
in the party will result in him insulting Lord Harrowmont, and suggesting that Harrowmont is too weak a candidate to be supported. You may agree or disagree with him, though agreeing with him causes Dulin to break off conversation with the Warden.
The Proving
Before beginning the match, it is possible to find Harrowmont's former champions and convince them to take up their place again.
Baizyl Harrowmont
- A warrior being blackmailed into not fighting in the Proving, by way of some sensitive love letters. The
love letters
can be found in the south wing of the
Orzammar Proving
. You can either lockpick the door (requires a lockpicking score of 40) or steal the
key
from
Myaja
(
Leliana
should be able to do this even if her stealing skill or cunning were never increased). Note that the key only opens the door—you still need to lockpick the chest. When you return the letters you can demand a reward of 10
, or 15
if you pass a Persuasion check, which requires at most 3 points in coercion.
Gwiddon
- Unsure of supporting Harrowmont, he is located in the main room of the Proving. An intimidation or persuasion (lie) dialogue option puts him in line. A
dwarf
of
noble background
can simply order him to fight.
As late as this juncture you can still complete Vartag's tasks and side with Bhelen (
A Prince's Favor: The First Task
). Once you tell the Proving Master you are fighting for Harrowmont, the documents given to the Warden by Vartag will be removed from inventory and Vartag's quests will be marked unsuccessful (even if you completed either one) and the quest closed.
The Proving itself is a string of battles. The various opponents can be found in
The Proving
Grounds before the first match. The fights go as follows:
Seweryn
(a dwarven
warrior
).
Myaja
(a dwarven warrior) and
Lucjan
(a dwarven
rogue
).
Sister
Hanashan
(a dwarven warrior).
Wojech
(a dwarven warrior) and Velanz (a dwarven rogue/
assassin
). You may choose a partner for this battle; it can be Baizyl, Gwiddon or one of your party members (except
Shale
) .
Piotin Aeducan
(a dwarven warrior), Piotin's Right Hand (a dwarven warrior) and 2 of Piotin's Henchmen (a dwarven warrior and a dwarven rogue/
duelist
). You can have either your party or Baizyl and Gwiddon help you. Generally your party will be better than them by this point.
If you instead complete
A Prince's Favor: The First Task
, you can still fight but you can choose to do so only as an independent candidate. Afterwards you are able to dedicate your victory to Prince Bhelen or to the
Grey Wardens
(that is also the case even if you secretly work for Harrowmont via the
Shifting Allegiances
quest). The opponents you will face are also somewhat different.
At the end of the last fight, you have the option to shout a victory cry. If you completed
A Prince's Favor: The First Task
, you may choose to endorse Bhelen, but not Harrowmont, even though you did not have the option of endorsing Harrowmont during your fights. Alternatively if you fought for Harrowmont, saying "May the Assembly heed the ancestors and choose Harrowmont" will add extra political support for Harrowmont. Recruiting Baizyl and Gwiddon will also have political effect. See
here
for more information.
Return to Dulin
Dulin can now be found at
Tapster's Tavern
in the
Commons
. Talk to him to complete the quest; he will take you to see Lord Harrowmont. Harrowmont explains that he will need to remove
Jarvia
and her Carta in order to secure a stronger position. He will also ask you to find incriminating evidence in Jarvia's hideout against Bhelen, implicating him in the ordered killing of his brother
Trian
. This task will fall to the Warden. See
Entering Jarvia's Hideout
or
A Paragon of Her Kind
for more details.
Rewards
1250 XP.
Notes
If you complete this quest you have the option of secretly double-crossing Harrowmont: see
Betrayed from Within
.
Most rounds at the Proving are fought solo by the Warden, though a team may be chosen for the final battle, selecting either Harrowmont's champions or The Warden's own team. If the last one is chosen, it is advised to adjust your team before starting the last battle for it is a tough battle.
During your rest time restocking, side-quests, team changes and game saves can be performed.
Fallen enemies on the battle ground in the Proving cannot be looted.
You cannot complete this quest if you deliver the promissory notes from
A Prince's Favor: The First Task
, since you will no longer have the dialogue options to declare yourself Harrowmont's champion. However, you can still fight the proving bouts, and you have the option of subsequently double-crossing Bhelen and secretly working for Harrowmont: see
Shifting Allegiances
.
The party could be left in the arena if you make them hold position, which means you could go back, though that would probably get you stuck in there.
Bugs
Some players report that the door to Myaja's chambers cannot be interacted with, even though the interior beyond can be seen from above. This makes it impossible to obtain the documents from the chest within.
Note:
Only
after
persuading Baizyl to tell you about the documents, and offering to retrieve them, the door becomes interactable.
If Morrigan is in your party and she falls prior to you winning the final bout, only the Warden will be transported out of the arena while all of your other party members remain stuck in the arena. This can be remedied by leaving the Proving Grounds and your full party will be with you at its entrance.
xbox360
The fight may not end automatically, if this happens walk to the edge of the arena until the Proving Master is selectable and talk to him. You will appear in the lobby in front of the Proving Master. Ask about who is fighting next, then say you just want to watch, at this point you will be warped back into the arena and the winner will be announced as usual.

======================================================================
PAGE: A Lost Nug
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Lost_Nug
======================================================================

Quest
A Lost Nug
Location
Orzammar Commons
Quest Giver
Nug Wrangler Boermor
End
Nug Wrangler Boermor
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
A Lost Nug
is a side quest in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Acquisition
This quest can be obtained from
Nug Wrangler Boermor
in
Orzammar Commons
.
Walkthrough
Boermor asks the Warden to help him by finding some lost
nugs
. Several of the small, pink creatures await in
Orzammar
. The nugs can be heard squeaking when they are nearby, making them easier to find. Nug locations are listed below, going from left to right on the Orzammar Commons map:
South of the Nug Wrangler, near the
Deep Roads
entrance.
North of the Nug Wrangler, off to the left.
On the bridge in front of the
Orzammar Proving
(have to walk down it a little ways to see it).
Hiding the alleyway past
Janar Armorers
, next to
Filda
.
Next to
Brother Burkel
.
Grab the nugs and take them back to Nug Wrangler Boermor for a reward. The quest ends after retrieving one nug, but more can be collected for additional silver.
Rewards
12
and 25 XP are rewarded each time the Warden returns a nug.
Unlocks
Codex entry: Nug
upon first wrangle of a nug into a box.
Notes
Nug Wrangler Boermor accepts one nug per click. If the Warden discovers more nugs rooting about in their pack, talk to Boermor again until he takes them all. He'll say
"
Atrast Vala
, my nug-wrangling friend"
when the Warden has no more nugs left. If the Warden hasn't caught all of the nugs, Boermor will tell the Warden that there are more out there.
The
Prima Guide
states that an exceptional reward is available for collecting all 10 nugs.  When all 10 are handed in, Boermor exclaims "Is there a nug left in all of
Thedas
? Don't think so. You're astounding!" after the tenth nug. Five nugs can be found in
Orzammar Commons
; two in the Diamond Quarter, one in Hall of Heroes, one in Tapster's Tavern, and one in the Proving Grounds.  Additional nugs can be found, but cannot be captured for this quest:
The Pearl's Nugs
("surprise me" option)
Carta Hideout's Nugs
Summoning Science's Spirit Hog
It is possible to collect Leliana's
Nug
and give it to Boermor. Simply give it to her in the Orzammar Commons and she will take it and set it on the floor. You can then pick it up and give it to Boermor. This does not work if the Nug is given to her in camp as the Nug cannot be picked up at this point.
Another exploit for catching additional nugs is to make use of the tactical pause while having party members gathered around the nug. This allows you to gather the nug several times at the exact same moment, practically ripping it up into several entities.
Bugs
Due to oversight the script only initiates nugs in the area when the quest is accepted instead of all 10 nugs in Orzammar. This is turn causes multiple inconsistencies in the quest. Instead of addressing this it appears the developers allow the quest to be closed after the first nug is returned, and regard the rest as bonus nug turn-ins. All other issues in this quest it due to this.
Boermor will always say there are more nugs out there when the Warden hands in a nug, even when handing in the last available nug, unless you made use of the aforementioned pause-exploit.
ps3
Quest reward money will not cumulate: if the Warden turns in multiple nugs, only 12
will be received.

======================================================================
PAGE: A Mage of the Circle
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests, Origin story quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Mage_of_the_Circle
======================================================================

“You've heard about the war brewing to the south, I expect? Duncan is recruiting mages to join the king's army at Ostagar.”
―
First Enchanter
Irving
Quest
A Mage of the Circle
Location
Magi Origin
Start
Jowan
End
Duncan
Previous
The Harrowing (quest)
Next
Bound in Blood and Magic
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
A Mage of the Circle
is a
quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
. It is part of the
Magi Origin
questline.
Involvement
Apprentice
mages
who survive their
Harrowing
become full members of the
Circle of Magi
. After having successfully completed your
Harrowing
, you wake up in bed the next morning to find your friend
Jowan
hovering over you. He's clearly concerned that he hasn't yet been called for his own Harrowing, but he tells you that First Enchanter
Irving
wants to see you and you'd better not keep him waiting.
Walkthrough
Apprentice Quarters
Area Map: Apprentice Quarters of the Circle Tower
Your goal is to make your way to the centre of the map and up to the Senior Mage Quarters on the second floor. There's nothing else that you need to do in the Apprentice Quarters, but you may want to explore a little for Codex entries and some loot.
Immediately after Jowan leaves, you can hear two female apprentices talking. Apparently,
Cullen
has already been gossiping about your Harrowing (one or two of the apprentices around this floor may have a bit of gossip about
him
).
Talking and listening to other apprentices on this floor yields bits and pieces of background information and rumour. For example, you may find you're not the only one to notice that Jowan is acting oddly; hear that another apprentice has opted for
Tranquility
to avoid the Harrowing; or catch the rumor that an apprentice has been dabbling in
blood magic
.
In your dormitory, you can find a
Chest
and a
Vanity
.
If you turn left out of your dormitory you can creep up to a couple of
templars
guarding big double doors, and hear them gossiping about an apprentice who's going to be sent for ... something. If you talk to
Bran
he will tell you that you are not allowed out of the Tower. There's also a
Vase
in this area.
Turn back past your dormitory and enter the next one for some more gossip and another
Chest
and
Vanity
.
Ignore the Basement door for now, but make sure to get the random loot from the
Cabinet
and
Shelves
that you pass.
In the Classrooms area, you can find a Book containing
Codex entry: The Fade
. You can also observe several
enchanters
instructing apprentices.
In the central area, find the Book containing
Codex entry: Mana and the Use of Magic
, then take the exit up to the second floor.
Senior Mage Quarters
Area Map: Senior Mage Quarters of the Circle Tower
Your destination is Irving's Study at the bottom of the map - again, while there's nothing else you need to do, you may want to take a bit of time to hear the gossip (rumours of a new Blight, an army gathering in
Ostagar
and a visiting
Grey Warden
among other things) and collect some Codex entries.
Talk to
Owain
to find out a little about the stockroom and Tranquil mages.
Take the southeast exit from the central area into the Library.
Senior Enchanter Sweeney
is browsing the shelves nearby (he has nothing much to say now, but you may want to remember him for later).
At the southern end of the library are shelves containing
Codex entry: Hierarchy of the Circle
and the sections on the
Creation
and
Primal
schools. There's also a mysteriously empty bookcase. This section was cleared by Irving; the
missing books
are on his desk.
You can talk to
Eadric
a bit about the role of elves in
Fereldan
society -- or if you're a fellow elf, the lost glory of your race.
At the northern end of the library, you can eavesdrop on
Senior Enchanter Torrin
and
Niall
discussing Tower politics. Talk to the Senior Enchanter to unlock an entry about the different
fraternities
. Niall will talk to you a bit about
apostates
.
Nearby are the library's sections on
history
and the
Spirit
and
Entropy
schools.
Next door to the library are the Guest Quarters, which an elderly mage is busy preparing (for a visiting Grey Warden called
Duncan
, if you ask). The mage will tell you how he nearly became a Grey Warden himself back in the day, and how he still regrets missing the opportunity. This room contains an
Armoire
.
The next room along is the Mage Dorm. In the second cubicle is a mage who will explain that these are to be your new quarters. A
Chest
and an
Armoire
contain random loot.
Further along the corridor is
Cullen
, a junior templar, who seems a bit nervous. (If you are female, you can make him  especially nervous without even trying - lending credence to the rumors that he is infatuated with you.) He was on duty at the recent Harrowing and is glad that nobody had to be put down.
In the next room are an
Armoire
of loot and a
Bookcase
.  There is also a book on the table, next to a mage, which yields
Codex entry: The Cardinal Rules of Magic
.
The next room along is the Laboratory, overseen by
Senior Enchanter Leorah
. She will hurry you along to Irving (another mage in the room may complain that she's been rather testy lately). Also here are a
Storage Cabinet
containing some crafting materials, a locked door to the storage caverns (useful later) and books on the bookshelf which relate to
maleficarum
.
Next is the chapel, where you can speak with
Keili
(an apprentice clearly not comfortable in her own skin) and
Lily
(an initiate of the Chantry who congratulates you on completing your Harrowing). A
book
, an
altar
, a
lectern
and
two
bookcases
unlock more codex entries.
At the very end of the corridor is a templar guarding the stairs up to the next level (off limits until dinner time).
Enter Irving's office and witness the tail-end of an argument between him and Knight-Commander
Greagoir
. Duncan will notice your arrival and interrupt them, at which point Greagoir will leave. Duncan will mention that the kingdom needs the mages' help to fight the darkspawn, and that there may be another Blight, but Irving forestalls him to welcome you formally into the Circle and present you with your
robes
,
staff
and
ring
. You will also receive
Codex entry: Duncan
and updates to
Irving
and
Greagoir
's entries.
Irving will ask you to escort Duncan back to his quarters (which you can do either courteously or under protest). Before leaving Irving's office, you can sneak a peek at the
Pile of Books
on the bench by the back wall.
While you accompany Duncan, you may ask him about the darkspawn attacks (to unlock
Codex entry: Darkspawn
) and/or the argument between Greagoir and Irving (he will offer no input on the argument unless you are Persuasive).
Once you reach the guest quarters, you will receive
Codex entry: The Grey Wardens
and the quest will be completed. You can still talk to Duncan a bit further, and ask him about the Grey Wardens, darkspawn, blood magic, and the Tranquil.  (Future dialogue with Duncan - once you leave this room - will not include these particular options.)
Rewards
Mage Robes
Magic Staff
Ring of Study
The Fade
Mana and the Use of Magic
Hierarchy of the Circle
The Four Schools of Magic: Creation
The Four Schools of Magic: Primal
The Four Schools of Magic: Spirit
The Four Schools of Magic: Entropy
History of the Circle
The Fraternity of Enchanters
The Cardinal Rules of Magic
Maleficarum

The Founding of the Chantry
Andraste: Bride of the Maker
The Chant of Light: The Blight
The Maker
Duncan
Blood Magic: The Forbidden School
Darkspawn
The Grey Wardens
Result
Once the quest is complete and you're done questioning him, leave Duncan in the guest quarters to find Jowan lurking in the corridor. He wants to talk to you: this is the start of the next and final quest in the Mage Origin story,
Bound in Blood and Magic
.
Gallery
Jowan
, fellow mage apprentice and The Warden's friend
Niall
, an Enchanter of the Circle Tower
Eadric
, an elven apprentice mage
Keili
, an apprentice mage who prays daily to the Maker to help her bear what she thinks of as the curse of her magic
Lily
, an initiate of the
Chantry
Meeting
Duncan
, a
Grey Warden
, along with Knight-Commander
Greagoir
and First Enchanter
Irving

, a tranquil mage and keeper of the Circle Tower's storeroom

, the Tower's newest Senior Enchanter

======================================================================
PAGE: A Map of Sorts
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Map_of_Sorts
======================================================================

Quest
A Map of Sorts
Start
Chateau Haine Interior
End
Mountainside Path
Appearances
Dragon Age II
-
Mark of the Assassin
A Map of Sorts
is a
companion quest
in the
Mark of the Assassin
DLC
for
Dragon Age II
if
Isabela
is brought along as a companion.
Acquisition
The quest is obtained in the
Chateau Haine Interior
.  Finding the first medallion will trigger the quest.
Walkthrough
There are a total of three medallions to be found, two in Chateau Haine and one in
The Retreat
:
Chateau Haine Interior
: The first medallion is found on the floor in front of the fireplace in the cook's area (visited during the
Hard to Stomach
quest). *On PS3, the medallion is located behind the door across from the cook's area.)
Chateau Haine Interior
: The medallion is found on (not in) a chest in the cage traps in front of the Vault door.  See
notes
for further information.
The Retreat
: When the path first splits left or right, take the right path. Where the tunnel widens Hawke will battle a group of
ghasts
and the medallion can be found on the ground nearby.
Once the third medallion has been found, a treasure cache will be marked on the map.
If Hawke followed Tallis, it is found in the forest clearing where Hawke fights a
wyvern
after leaving the Retreat.
If Hawke did not follow Tallis, it is found in the last area of the pass before
The Ruins
.
Rewards
300 XP (quest completion)
Badge of the Felicisima Armada
Notes
The medallion found on a Chateau Haine chest may be
appear
to be bugged and impossible to loot.  It seems that the medallion on the chest is tied to the loot field of the chest itself, which is also lootable but requires
Hawke
to stand on the button in front of one chest while
Tallis
stands on the button in front of the other.  Once the chest is looted the medallion can be looted as well, whether or not the buttons are pressed.

A Missing Child
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Kaitlyn
needs help finding her missing brother,
Bevin
, in the undead-plagued
Redcliffe Village
. If
the Warden
speaks to Kaitlyn, who is sobbing inside the Redcliffe
Village Chantry
next to the chantry door, she will ask you to find him.
You must complete this before the nighttime battle.
Walkthrough
Telling Kaitlyn not to worry and that you will look for her brother raises
Wynne
's approval by
(
+1
) and
Leliana

(
+1
).
If, on the other hand, you tell her you'll leave her to her snivelling, Morrigan's approval increases by
(
+2
). In this case you can still receive and complete the quest by talking to Bevin.
Step outside the Chantry, turn right and The Warden will be facing towards
Kaitlyn's House
.
Kaitlyn's House
Head inside and enter the bedroom to the right. The Warden will find Bevin hiding in a dresser. Wynne will order Bevin to come out immediately. Sten will threaten to burn him out.
The Warden may:
Persuade/Intimidate Bevin to say why he was in the house.
He will tell you about his grandfather's sword that he wanted to use to defend the town, but was too young to lift.
Persuade/Intimidate Bevin to give you the key to the chest that contains the sword, or promise to pay Kaitlyn for the sword afterwards.
He will give you the key. The Warden may head upstairs and retrieve the sword from the chest. Bevin will be at the Chantry when you return.
Fail to Persuade/Intimidate Bevin.
Bevin will refuse to cooperate and run back to the Chantry. You will not be able to ask about the sword afterwards.
Not ask for the sword at all. The chest can only opened with the key and the key can only be obtained through the initial conversation with Bevin, so in this case the sword can no longer be obtained.

is required for the first
persuade
and

dialogue options.

dialogue options.

xbox360

xbox360
If the Warden attempts to return

to Kaitlyn after the battle and has it equipped, she will take it and you will net the approval and disapproval values from the various party members; however the blade will remain in your inventory and equipped to the Warden.
pc
xbox360
You cannot complete this quest after the battle in Redcliffe Village, but it will remain in your quest log.
As of Patch 1.04 the quest will update if you don't complete it before the Redcliffe village battle, saying:
"Night fell and the undead creatures started attacking Redcliffe before you could locate Bevin. You don't know if Kaitlyn ever found her brother and made it to safety, and perhaps you'll never find out"

======================================================================
PAGE: A Moment's Peace
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Moment's_Peace
======================================================================

Quest
A Moment's Peace
Type
Companion quest
Location
Treviso
Previous
A Murder of Crows
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
A Moment's Peace
is a
companion quest
for
Lucanis
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. This quest is available only if
Treviso
was saved.
Acquisition
This quest will be available on the next return to the
Lighthouse
after completing Lucanis' previous companion quest,
A Murder of Crows
. The main quests
For the Love of Treviso
and
Legacies and Legends
also must have been completed in order for this quest to become available. Once all are finished, speak to Lucanis at the Lighthouse to start this quest.
If Treviso was not saved, this quest is not available. Instead, closure to Lucanis' story arc will be given in a conversation at the Lighthouse that becomes available when Lucanis is eligible to attain
Hero of the Veilguard
status. Since
Minrathous
was saved,
The Local Hero
with
Neve
will be available instead as well.
Walkthrough
Lucanis and
Spite
celebrate their alliance. Because you helped them reach an understanding, Lucanis and Spite continue to strengthen their bond.
Lucanis has ordered Spite a cup of coffee, even though he can't drink it, and assures Rook the other patrons won't care; the
Crows
own Cafe Pietra. Spite is hesitant—he says it smells like Lucanis—and Lucanis and Rook discuss Spite's acceptance of Lucanis's decision regarding
Illario
. The two are at peace, at least for the moment.
Rook asks whether the
Talons
are accepting of Lucanis's
First Talon
status, especially since he is an
abomination
. Lucanis admits they really aren't, though they do find him more palatable than Illario or the
Venatori
.
Catarina
likewise is doing well, which (according to Lucanis) means making everyone but
Teia
miserable.
Lucanis muses about the turns his life has taken: getting out of the
Ossuary
, getting his life back, and (if romanced) spending time with
Rook
. However, if this is to be his future, he's happy to take it.
At the end of this conversation, the quest concludes.
Rewards
None.

======================================================================
PAGE: A Mother's Hope
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Mother's_Hope
======================================================================

Quest
A Mother's Hope
Location
Orzammar Commons
Ortan Thaig
Quest Giver
Filda
End
Filda
Other NPCs
Ruck
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
A Mother's Hope
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Walkthrough
Filda
Filda
can be found in the
Commons
near
Tapster's Tavern
. She is praying for her missing son,
Ruck
, who was lost in the
Deep Roads
five years ago and hasn’t been seen since.
The Warden
can promise to help her; if they ask for a reward she promises a shield.
Ruck
En route to finding
Paragon
Branka
during
A Paragon of Her Kind
, the party will pass through
Ortan Thaig
. Deep in the tunnels, Ruck will appear and scurry away leaving the party to deal with a surprise attack of some
corrupted spiders
. Afterwards the party can follow him to his cave. When the Warden speaks to the
dwarf
, they will learn that he has become tainted from eating the flesh of
darkspawn
and his mind has deteriorated. You have various options at this point:
Kill him.
Intimidate or persuade him to talk with you, which offers an opportunity to trade with him and additional options:
Tell him you will let his mother know where he is.
Tell him you will tell his mother he is dead; this is his preference because he is ashamed of what he has become. If
Wynne
is in the party
Wynne approves (
+2
).
If you persuaded him and then asked him about gems, he will attack.
If you cannot intimidate or persuade him then end the conversation. The next time you talk to him he will talk to you as if you had successfully intimidated or persuaded him.
If
Zevran
is in the party, if you choose the conversation option "I should go," he will advocate a mercy killing, saying it pains him to see Ruck like this. You will have no choice but to agree with him and attack Ruck. If you don't want to kill Ruck then either don't take Zevran along or ask Ruck to trade and exit the conversation with him by closing the trade window.
Similarly, if
Oghren
is in the party he will also suggest you put Ruck out of his misery, but he'll leave the decision up to you (no Approval change).
If both
Zevran
and
Oghren
are in the party then Zevran's suggestion takes precedence here (although Oghren will speak up as if it was him that convinced you to kill Ruck).
Whether the Warden deals with Ruck or not, they can return to
Orzammar
and speak to Filda.
Return to Filda
Killing Ruck
Lie to Filda and tell her Ruck is dead (this is an option even though it is not a lie) or that you found his remains and that he died heroically long ago. If Wynne is in the party
Wynne approves (
+1
). You get the reward.
Tell Filda you put him out of his misery. She calls you a monster and refuses to talk to you. You don't get the reward.
Tell Filda that her son is a lunatic. You get the reward.
Lie to Filda and tell her you didn’t find him.
If Sten is in the party he insists on telling Filda the truth. You have to choose one of the other conversation choices.
Otherwise Filda thanks you for looking. You get the reward.
Not killing Ruck
Tell Filda Ruck went crazy. You get the reward.
Lie to Filda and say he’s dead (this is not an option unless you told Ruck you would tell his mother that he was dead). If Wynne is in the party
Wynne approves (
+1
). You get the reward.
Tell Filda that her son is a lunatic. You get the reward.
Lie to Filda and tell her you didn’t find him.
If Sten is in the party he insists on telling Filda the truth.
If you tell Filda that Sten is talking rubbish then she chooses to believe Sten, who tells her Ruck is in the Deep Roads. She leaves to find him. You get the reward.
Otherwise tell Filda one of the other conversation choices.
Otherwise Filda thanks you for looking. You don't get the reward.
Result
The quest choices in
Dragon Age Keep
Depending on the Warden's actions, Filda will respond in two different ways:
If Filda believes Ruck is dead, she will return to her life in Orzammar, unhappy but accepting and thankful for taking the risk or resentful if she is told that Ruck was killed by the Warden's hand.
If Filda believes Ruck is alive even if she is told that Ruck doesn't wish to be seen, she will be thankful but disappointed that the Warden didn't do more for them and then state that she will go to the Deep Roads in order to find and save her son.
Rewards
100 XP
Ruck's Shield
Money
, Gold
Note:
pc
(v1.05) There is no gold given for any of the options above.
Bugs
If you choose the alternate route of lying to Filda before you enter the tunnels, you will not be able to change your mind later. However, Filda will still appear with a marker over her head, as if the quest has not been resolved.
If you talk to Ruck after completing the quest, Filda will appear with quest marker over her head, but will not speak with you.
If you tell Filda that Ruck is alive, she will say that she plans to look for him. However, she seems to remain in her same spot.
If you talk to Ruck before talking to Filda, then Filda is nowhere to be found, making the quest impossible to complete.
If Ruck spawns before talking to Filda, then Filda is nowhere to be found, making the quest impossible to complete.

======================================================================
PAGE: A Murder of Crows (The Veilguard)
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/A_Murder_of_Crows_(The_Veilguard)
======================================================================

For the side quest in Dragon Age II, see
A Murder of Crows
.
Quest
A Murder of Crows
Type
Companion quest
Location
Treviso
Previous
Bloodbath
and, if Treviso was saved,
Inner Demons
Next
A Moment's Peace
Related
For the Love of Treviso
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
A Murder of Crows
is a
companion quest
for
Lucanis
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. It's time to rescue
Caterina
and reckon with
Illario
once and for all.
Acquisition
This quest is becomes available after completing the following prerequisites:
Main quest
Unwanted Guests
The quest
Bloodbath
and its following
corpse-whispering conversation
in
Emmrich's laboratory
Companion quest
Inner Demons
(only if
Treviso
was saved)
If
Blood of Arlathan
is completed before beginning
A Murder of Crows
,
For the Love of Treviso
must be also be completed before this quest will unlock.
Walkthrough
Illario is making his move, and Lucanis wants to stop him. At all cost.
This quest has two versions, depending on whether Treviso or Minrathous was saved. If Treviso was saved, the quest features an additional preliminary segment at a newly unlocked part of the Crow's Rooftop Roads. If Minrathous was saved, this segment is skipped entirely. Regardless, the additional part of the Crow's Rooftop Roads cannot be accessed even after the quest is completed.
Get to the tower (only if Treviso was saved)
Meet Lucanis at the Cantori Diamond via the world map (even if already in Treviso) to discover Teia and Viago have already left for Illario's party. Lucanis leads the team north and unlocks a door to a new section of the Crow's Rooftops Road, suggesting they work towards a large tower. (A chest can be accessed through the broken window to the left immediately after Lucanis draws Rook's attention to the tower.) Head northeast toward this tower and Villa Dellamorte along the rooftops, fighting several groups of Venatori along the way.
Find a way inside the villa
If you arrived from the tower (in the version of the quest in which Treviso was saved), head down to water level where Rook finds a gondola to reach the outskirts of the villa. In the version of the quest in which Minrathous was saved, the quest begins at the outskirts of the villa.
Head under the bridge to a hidden escape route. To the right of the closed metal gate is a narrow passage behind some breakable wooden boards. On the other side of the passage in the cellar are Venatori to fight, as well as the
Orlesian Masterwork Linen
memento in the corner.
Head up to find another small group of Venatori in a room, along with the memento
The Northern Everywhere
. Go through the door to enter the villa proper.
Search the villa for Caterina
The villa is large and requires extensive exploration. The main entrance hall has several notable features: a table with the
House Business
note, a chest in the northwestern room, two hallway exits, and two sets of stairs.
The south stairs lead to the
Folk Art Rocking Griffon
, an ambushing Venatori, and the
Mask of the First House
memento.
The north stairs lead to some Venatori and some minor loot.
The east hall leads to the
Mask of Torrenti, Blight-Ender
memento, a southern room with a trapped chest and a fight with Venatori and demons, and a northern room with the
Mask of all Talons
memento and some stairs up. (This northern room also eventually reconnects to the northern hallway of the main entrance room.) The stairs up lead only to an ambushing Venatori and a small loot chest.
The north hall is initially blocked by a closed door, but this door can be opened from behind by taking the east hall and looping around.
Progress north into the library to find a small fight with Venatori, the
Family Tree
note, and the
Mask of the Last Talon
memento. Take the far stairway to the second level and a long balcony. Just after leaving this balcony, a door to the right will allow access to the final chest of the villa.
Continue along this path until Rook overhears a Venatori complaining about a locked door. The door which thwarts this Venatori is Caterina's. (However, a door across the hall can also be opened, revealing a bedroom which is presumably Illario's. This room contains a
Letter from Younger Days
, a
Torn Letter
in the fireplace, and the
Heartstrings
memento.)
Defeat Caterina's captors
Once the mage is defeated, Lucanis will bash down the door to Caterina's room. A cutscene will be triggered where Caterina and Lucanis reunite. Lucanis wishes to take Caterina to safety, but she refuses, deciding to go on her own to the opera house and leaving Rook and the team to confront Illario. A nearby table contains the
Crow Business
note.
Get to the Dellamorte Opera House
Go to the balcony, jump down onto the trellis, and drop into the large courtyard. Despite its size, the courtyard only contains one small chest in the northeast corner and a smaller chest up the stairs to the right of the quest marker. Go down the stairs near the trellis and through a short passage to reach the courtyard of the opera house. Head straight through to the large doors on the far side.
Once inside, another cutscene will play out where Illario, flanked by the current
Talons
(including Teia and Viago), gives a speech to the Crows about taking on the mantle of First Talon. He is interrupted by the arrival of Lucanis and Rook. Illario attempts to use his brooch's blood magic to control Spite, but Lucanis and Spite overpower the compulsion, and battle begins.
Defeat Illario
Defeat Illario alongside some Venatori enemies. Like the Venatori, Illario is vulnerable to ice and resistant to fire. He uses two sickles and fights with the rogue moveset.
Decide Illario's fate
Lucanis will confront a humiliated Illario, and Caterina will reveal herself as still alive. Rook can either tell Lucanis to show Illario mercy, or tell him that Illario's actions are unforgivable. If Lucanis is not hardened (if Treviso was saved over Minrathous), he will either forgive or imprison Illario based on Rook's suggestion. If Lucanis is hardened, he will imprison Illario regardless of Rook's advice. Either way, Caterina will choose to step down and promote Lucanis to
First Talon
.
Return to the Lighthouse
Speak with Lucanis back at the
Lighthouse
to discuss the day's events and his new status as First Talon. While he knows the Crows will call on him eventually, he assures Rook he will complete his contract and be there for the team. He's gained too much to lose, and he refuses to give any of it up.
Rewards
Completing the quest yields:
+100 Antivan Crows strength
Increased bond with companions taken on quest
If Treviso was saved, Lucanis attains
Hero of the Veilguard
status at the end of this quest, regardless of what happens to Illario. Hero of the Veilguard status comes with:
The heroic armor
Crow's Tenacity
and the
Demon of Defiance
skill, if Illario is imprisoned, or
The heroic armor
Crow's Poise
and the
Demon of Rebellion
skill, if Illario is forgiven.
If Treviso was destroyed, Lucanis will have a follow-up conversation at the Lighthouse (separate from the wrap-up conversation above) at a subsequent return. He discusses Spite's appearance being similar to his own, Treviso now being safe, and that at the end of his contract with Rook, he will seek out a way to separate from Spite for good. While he worries about Spite having grown stronger from their blending, he also worries that he'll miss Spite when he's gone. At the end of that conversation, he will achieve
Hero of the Veilguard
status.
A Time to Kill
must be completed and
To Act As One
must be available before this conversation—and his Hero status—will unlock.
Results
Illario has invited the Crow leaders to Villa Dellamorte. Lucanis, believing Caterina still alive, has decided to crash the party and set the First Talon free.
If Illario is imprisoned (available only if Treviso wasn't saved):
As Illario declared himself First Talon and prepared to hand the Crows over to the Venatori, Lucanis burst in and challenged him. The cousins battled it out before an audience of Crows, and when Lucanis stood triumphant, he ordered Illario imprisoned.
If Illario is shown mercy (available only if Treviso was saved):
As Illario declared himself First Talon and prepared to hand the Crows over to the Venatori, Lucanis burst in and challenged him. The cousins battled it out before an audience of Crows, and when Lucanis stood triumphant, he offered Illario a second chance, under watchful eyes and poisoned daggers.
Notable loot and chest locations

(warrior)
A map showing the location of the chests available during this quest.
There are four chests in this quest-specific region of Crow's Road Rooftops and the Dellamorte Estates. No chests are accessible after the quest is completed.
Only accessible if Treviso was saved. Once Lucanis directs Rook's attention to the stained-glass window in the tower, tightrope across the beam to the left, then enter the broken window on Rook's left. The chest will be on the left across one more narrow beam. Note: while the unopened version of this chest can be seen on the overworld map of
Treviso
, the opened version will not.
In addition, turning left instead of right at the far end of this hall will open a shortcut door back to the platform which housed the Blight Cyst. However, this door will not remain open on future visits to Treviso.
On the ground level of the main portion of Villa Dellamorte, in the open room directly ahead. Tucked behind some covered furniture.
On the ground level of Villa Dellamorte, through an unlocked door to the east and south of the main entry room. Initially guarded by a
Barraging Venatori Mage
, several more Venatori and
demons
will spawn once the fight engages. This chest is trapped.
This chest is visible when first reaching Villa Dellamorte, but cannot be acquired until much later (right before rescuing Caterina). After fighting through the book-lined library which also contains the
Family Tree
note and the
Mask of the Last Talon
memento, proceed through the stairs on the far side of the room, then up and around the balcony. After leaving the balcony, the next door on the right will allow access to this chest.

Trivia
'Murder' is the term of venery for crows.
Several of Lucanis's ambient banters with Spite will only take place if Treviso was saved instead of Minrathous.
If Rook has the
Crow background
, Viago chastises Rook during the final fight if they take damage from Illario.
If Lucanis has been romanced and Illario lands a hit on Rook, Illario states, "You picked the wrong Dellamorte."
During the final battle, Teia and Viago can be seen on elevated balconies fighting Venatori.

main quest
in
Dragon Age II

Hawke
will participate in a conversation between
Leandra
and

about Grandfather's will and the
Amell

Kirkwall
.

Quest
A New Path
Act
3
Location
Sundermount
Start
Merrill's Home
(
Lowtown
)
Previous
On The Loose
A Talking To
Next
Merrill, Friend or Foe?
Appearances
Dragon Age II
A New Path
is an Act 3
companion quest
for
Merrill
in
Dragon Age II
.
Acquisition
You will receive it when you visit
Merrill's Home
(or enter and leave your home) after you have completed the
On the Loose
quest.
Walkthrough
Merrill's Home
Speak with
Merrill
at
her home
at the
Kirkwall Alienage
in
Lowtown
. Merrill is disappointed that the
Eluvian
is still inactive.
If
Hawke
gave the
Arulin'Holm
to Merrill in
Mirror Image
, she explains that it was not enough to fix it.
If
Hawke
refused Merrill the
Arulin'Holm
in
Mirror Image
, she comments that she can't believe she is asking for Hawke's help again.
Regardless of previous options, she plans to consult the demon trapped at
Pride's End
, and asks for assistance and company.
Accepting the quest without question.
Merrill: friendship (
+10
).
Rejecting her plea does not terminate the quest—she says she will have to find another way, but the quest remains in the journal.
Merrill: rivalry (
+10
)
Tell her "Talk to the Keeper first."
Merrill: rivalry (
+10
)
No matter what you say to Merrill, you can continue the quest.
Sundermount
Go to
Sundermount
with
Merrill
in the party.
Note:
If you go without her you get the party selection screen on arrival, with Merrill as mandatory companion.
As you head to the peak of Sundermount, Keeper
Marethari
stands nearby the trail. You do not have to talk to her, but if you do, she greets Merrill. But Merrill is annoyed, as the clan has not left the mountains for six years. The Keeper justifies that she has tasks to accomplish. She then asks the purpose of Merrill's visit.
Note:
Though speaking with her is optional, you miss a chance in gaining more
friendship and rivalry
points if you don't.
"Talk some sense into Merrill."
Merrill: rivalry (
+10
)
Fenris: friendship (
+5
)
Sebastian: friendship (
+5
)
"Help us fix the mirror."
Merrill: friendship (
+10
)
Fenris: rivalry (
+5
)
Sebastian: rivalry (
+5
)
Marethari warns Merrill not to seek the demon's help, but Merrill continues no matter what.
Audacity
, the
pride demon
that possessed
Marethari Talas
Climb Sundermount. You are attacked by
Giant spiders
at the second clearing. Go through the
Sundermount Passage
.
Sundermount Caverns
Go through the Sundermount Caverns and exit to the Mountain Graveyard. If you have not explored this area yet, there is a
Lyrium
and an
Orichalcum
crafting resource in the caverns, if you are seeking the
Supplier
achievement.
Mountain Graveyard
Go past the Sundermount Graveyard to reach
Pride's End
at the peak.
Note:
If
A Murder of Crows
was not started or finished prior to entering Pride's End, then that particular quest could disappear (along with the exclusive characters) depending on what chat options you chose when you again exit the cave.
If you didn't clear the graveyard earlier, it has a couple of lootable graves; clicking on them will summon corpses and a skeleton archer. One of the graves contains
Robes of the Overseer
which is part of the Overseer light armor set. Another grave contains
Andruil's Braid
. Looting all the graves summons two waves of mobs: first a couple of corpses and a Shadow Warrior, followed by skeleton archers and a Shadow Assassin.
After the fight you can interact with the nearby altar, which results in a cutscene with Merrill saying a short prayer for
Mythal
and commenting "Sorry. I didn't mean to hold us. You just - It's never wise to ignore Mythal."
Continue along the path; if you have the
The Awiergan Scrolls
quest active then you will encounter three enemies that are part of the Second Aspect (see the
quest page for tactics
).
If you have not already explored this area there is a fight near the peak with dragonlings and a dragon, who drops The
Jade Serpent
. Don't forget to loot
Glitterdust
on the little curve off the path before the entrance to Pride's End.
Right before the entrance itself you have yet another encounter with corpses, a Shadow Assassin and Shades.
Pride's End
As Hawke approaches the Strange Idol, Merrill is disturbed; the artifact does not contain the demon anymore. The party finds out that Keeper Marethari has allowed
Audacity
to possess her to stop Merrill from repairing the Eluvian. She goes on to claim that the demon would have gone through the Eluvian once it was restored, with Merrill as its destined victim. The party is then forced to kill the Keeper. Dalish elders, Marethari, and the dead hunters from
Honoring The Fallen
, in spirit form spawn near the altars on the sides, attacking the party and sermonizing Merrill for her mistakes.
Hunter Radha: "Traitor!  May the Dread Wolf hunt you for the rest of your days!"
Pol
: "I was trying to rebuild my life!  Why did you have to come back and destroy it?"
Hunter Harsal: "We're cursed.  The whole clan.  And you brought this upon us."
Hunter Chandan: "You couldn't let go of the past, could you?  Now look at what you've done!"
Tamlen
: "Curiosity... is a dangerous thing, Merrill.  It will never let you rest.  Never."
Elven Mother: "What have you done?  What have you brought upon our clan, you monster?"
Keeper Marethari: "Da'len, turn back.  Everything you touch turns to ash."
Elven Elder: "You are a wound poisoning this clan!"
After fighting for a while there is a cutscene where Audacity talks to you and heals itself a bit, then the battle resumes. All characters' Health and Stamina/Mana are fully regenerated. If you haven’t fought the entire list of spirits before this happens, the remaining spirits do not appear now.
After defeating Audacity, Keeper Marethari claims that it's over.
"I'm glad that's over."
The possessed Keeper will stab Merrill, resulting in a second fight with the pride demon at one-third of its health and an injured Merrill.
Any party member that fell during the previous battle is revived.
"You said you had to die."
Merrill will stab the Keeper, releasing the demon and ending the battle.
Merrill then grieves for Marethari's death; she didn't wish for this happen.
"She was stupid."
Merrill: friendship (
+15
)
Fenris: rivalry (
+5
)
Anders: rivalry (
+5
)

+10
)

Fenris: friendship (
+5
)

+10
)
"She loved you." or "This could have been you."
Merrill: rivalry (

)

Merrill: rivalry (
+25
)
Exiting Pride's End
Upon exiting the cave, the party is confronted by the other Dalish who say Marethari came up here and hasn't returned.
"Marethari was possessed."
Battle with the Dalish.
Merrill: friendship (
+10
)

+10
)

)
"Stand down."
Battle with the Dalish.

+5

"I'll take responsibility."
The party will be allowed to leave.

+15
)

+10
)

+10
)
