Notes
If Alistair was
romanced
in
Dragon Age: Origins
, he will speak happily of his life with the
Hero of Ferelden
. He will also mention that she would have been present during the quest had she not been pursuing her own quest, that of saving herself and Alistair—and possibly all Wardens—from the Calling.
After the quest, speaking with either Alistair, Leliana or Morrigan, depending on who was romanced in
Dragon Age: Origins
and whether Alistair is present or not, will unlock the war table operation
Contact Hero of Ferelden
and provide additional information about the Warden.
The introductory videos give a somewhat different version of the events in the Fade. In the
Here Lies The Abyss
version, the Inquisitor is chased up a stairway by large spiders, looks back to the physical version of Justinia, reaches out
to her
, Justinia falls back and the Inquisitor goes forward into the veil tear. In the opening sequence, when the Inquisitor runs up the stairs, the white ghost version of Justinia is ahead and above the Inquisitor, Justinia reaches out to the Inquisitor, the anchor flashes and the screen does a white-out. The positions of the two characters are reversed between the two depictions of what is supposedly the same event and each has a different ending.
In the quest version, the Inquisitor is wearing whatever armor is currently equipped while in the Fade when fleeing from the spiders, whereas in the introductory video, the Lone Survivor will be wearing a class specific version of the Mercenary Coat.
Although the Red Lyrium dragon can be targeted, it will always have 1 hp remaining.
Gregory Dedrick
can be conscripted into joining the Wardens if he has not yet been judged in
Skyhold
.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Trespasser
.
In the Fade, Justinia states that the anchor can only be removed by killing the Inquisitor, which is directly contradicted by the ending of
Trespasser
. However, this may be due to Solas' unique abilities.
Trivia
Most of the quest names in the main plot refer to specific verses of the
Chant of Light
. "Here lies the abyss" can be seen in the Canticle of Andraste 14:11.
If Blackwall is taken to meet the Warden Ally in Crestwood, Blackwall will claim to have known
Duncan
, the Ferelden Warden-Commander in
Dragon Age: Origins,
when the Warden ally is Alistair. There's no mention of Duncan when it's Stroud or Loghain.
Trick Weekes
was the main writer for this quest.
If the Inquisitor uses tactical view, the "fearlings" that are encountered each have different names, such as "War", "Poverty", "Pestilence", etc. Amusingly, encountered in the "Find a way to keep monsters away" area of the
Fears of the Dreamers
side quest, one of the fearlings bears the name "Ironically, Spiders".
In
Dragon Age 2
,
Flemeth
says to Hawke: "The world fears the inevitable plummet into the
abyss
. Watch for that moment... and when it comes, do not hesitate to leap. It is only when you fall that you learn whether you can fly." During the quest
the Inquisitor
and Hawke are falling into the Abyssal Rift when the Inquisitor opens a Fade rift for them to leap into only to find themselves in the realm of
Nightmare
inhabited by fearlings.
In the Fade, gravestones can be found engraved with each of your companions' names and fears. They read as follows: Iron Bull: Madness; Vivienne: Irrelevance; Varric: Becoming His Parents; Cassandra: Helplessness; Blackwall: Himself; Dorian: Temptation; Sera: The Nothing; Cole: Despair; Solas: Dying Alone.
In an earlier version of the quest, the writers intended for the player to meet
the Architect
.
The Warden ally was originally going to be the
Hero of Ferelden
, but this was ultimately cut due to concerns that players would be upset if the Hero of Ferelden was given a voice.
Bugs
ps3
Upon reaching the Tevinter ritual tower, conversations may freeze and only unlock after a certain time has passed, or the player has somehow triggered the conversation to continue. Portions of conversation may be lost when this occurs.
ps3
The Warden ally may vanish in Adamant Fortress after the Warden warriors are encountered in the main bailey, but will reappear before Clarel is encountered.
pc
The second choke point may not spawn enemies even though it will be marked on the map by a quest marker.
xbox360
pc
When Clarel slits the sacrifice's throat﻿, if you quickly press the Skip button you will see the Inquisitor with their throat slit and pouring blood.
In the Fade, after reading the mage's journal and defeating the rage demon it summons, the interact point and journal remain interactive and can be re-read, though without summoning a demon.
After completing the
Broken Window
puzzle, the text says to recover the magister's amulet, but the actual loot is a
Superb Ring of Sundering
as well as a random item and gold amount.
xbox360
ps3
During the fight with the Aspect of the Nightmare the Warden ally and Hawke may, after the first assault from the Nightmare, remain stunned and only able to fight once briefly in the middle of the battle before lapsing back into a stunned state.
xboxone
During the initial cut-scene in the Western Approach, the player may not be able to select options from the dialogue wheel.  This can be remedied by toggling the radial menu using LB, which will 'activate' the dialogue wheel cursor.
xbox360
ps3
pc
Sometimes a custom Hawke will still say the default line about spiders even if they romanced someone in DAII.
pc
When a female Inquisitor tells Alistair after escaping the fade that "They can tell whatever stories they like.."   Alistair's answer refers to the Inquisitor as a male.
pc
If you target the dragon during the scene just before you see Clarel's and Erimond duel, and cast Mark of Rift just as he does a pass by, the dragon will appear to be sucked into the fade, although the sound of the wings can still be heard. The attacks from the dragon will stop.
Leliana is frequently granted +5 toward the Divine Election instead of the intended -5 for banishing the Wardens from Orlais.
References

Quest
Home Roost
Type
Companion quest
Location
High Anderfels
Arlathan Forest
Previous
The Profane and the Lost
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Home Roost
is a
companion quest
for
Davrin
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. The
griffons
are settling into their new home, and
Rook
has been invited to check on them.
Acquisition
Speak to
Davrin
at the
Lighthouse
after completing
The Profane and the Lost
. He will initially have a conversation regarding his growing bond with
Assan
. The griffon is a creature of emotion, and Davrin reflects on that purity of expression. He confirms Assan is fond of Rook and thanks Rook for their assistance with the griffons' fate.
The next time Rook speaks to Davrin at the Lighthouse, he will suggest visiting the griffons at their new home, which will trigger the quest.
Walkthrough
If griffons stayed with the Grey Wardens:
Now that the griffons are free, Davrin wants to know how his feathery charges are adjusting to their new role.
If Rook gave the griffons to the
Wardens
, they will be scattered throughout Grey Hold. However, travel to the unique location just outside the
Wetlands
via the World Map. Here,
Antoine
is training the griffons to attack
darkspawn
, but they are too intelligent to fall for his tricks. 
When Rook offers training advice, Davrin gets Rook involved in a much more direct fashion—a scarecrow training target for the griffons to attack.
If griffons went with Eldrin:
Now that the griffons are free, Davrin wants to know how his feathery charges are adjusting to their new role.
If Rook gave the griffons to the
Dalish
, they will be scattered throughout the
Veil Jumper
camp. Travel to the quest marker in Ruins Reach to meet
Eldrin
, who is training them to become the guardians of the forest and attack creatures threatening the
halla
. However, they are too intelligent to fall for his tricks.
When Rook offers training advice, Davrin gets Rook involved in a much more direct fashion—an
Antaam
training target for the griffons to attack.
Rewards
+100 Grey Warden faction strength
Bond increase with Davrin
Results
If griffons stayed with the Grey Wardens:
Davrin wants to  know how the griffons' training is going. They'll be at the Grey Wardens'  camp in the Anderfels.
Approval
Quick, to your positions! Go!
Try "please."
Try gingerwort tea.
Bribe them. —
Davrin approves
Afterwards
Davrin approves
Missive
The Griffons' New Home
This missive has a unique variant for each path.
Notes
This quest does not appear in the journal once completed.

======================================================================
PAGE: Honor Bound
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Honor_Bound
======================================================================

Quest
Honor Bound
Location
Denerim Market District
Start
Denerim Market District
End
Alley Behind Gnawed Noble Tavern
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Honor Bound
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Ser Landry
recognizes
the Warden
from
Ostagar
and denounces them as a
Grey Warden
traitor.
Acquisition
Walking by Ser Landry in the
Denerim Market District
automatically triggers this quest.
Walkthrough
With a high enough
Coercion
skill, you can lie to him and claim that you aren't a Grey Warden; he apologizes and walks off.
You can persuade him that if he's wrong he'll kill an innocent; he says he feels you're guilty but he has no proof, so you'll meet again when he finds some. He walks off.
If you fail harder persuade / intimidate checks regarding
Loghain
's role in Ostagar (he's personal friends with Loghain), he insists on challenging you to a duel and demands you meet him in the back alley behind the
Gnawed Noble Tavern
. He then heads to the alley.
If you decline the duel - he walks away insisting "this isn't over". Later, while traveling in Denerim you will have a random encounter where he and his seconds ambush you, forcing you to kill him.
If you select one of the 'persuade' options and are successful, the quest will not appear in your journal.
Note:
If you don't want to trigger the encounter but still want to enter the tavern you can avoid it by having as much distance to Ser Landry as possible. By staying on the far left side and making a wide circle around him you will be able to enter the tavern. Using stealth would also be a possibility.
This way you can decide when to confront Landry, like in cases where
the Warden
does not have a sufficient Coercion skill.
Result
If you follow him to the back alley you can either attack him with your whole party (in which case his 3 knights will also attack) or accept his one-on-one duel (you gain
(
+1
) from Oghren). During the fight, they will use the following skills:
Ser Landry:
Rally
(possibly with
Motivate
)
War Cry
(with the
Superiority
)
Perfect Striking
Shield Bash
Assault
Landry's Seconds:
Precise Striking
Assault
Shield Pummel
Shield Cover
During the one-on-one duel, if you run back to the beginning of the alley Ser Landry will say that he doesn't want to potentially harm the people in the marketplace and withdraw. He will later ambush you in a
random encounter
.
Rewards
Engraved Mace
from Ser Landry
Either a substantial amount of money, or some lesser poultices, runes and crafting reagents.
Ser Landry may also drop a piece of the
Heavy Plate armor set
.
Bugs
100 XP was supposed to be awarded if you persuaded Landry that the wardens wouldn't side with the darkspawn or that he'd be killing an innocent. 250 XP was supposed to be awarded if you dueled one-on-one.

PAGE: Hope on the Wing
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests

Quest
Hope on the Wing
Type
Companion quest
Location
Hossberg Wetlands
Previous
The Palate of a Griffon
Next
The Profane and the Lost
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Hope on the Wing
is a
companion quest
for
Davrin
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Acquisition
Speak to Davrin in the
Lighthouse
after completing
The Palate of a Griffon
.
Note:
This quest is missable and must be completed before starting
Blood of Arlathan
. Otherwise, completing
The Palate of a Griffon
will lead straight into
The Profane and the Lost
.
Walkthrough
Davrin checks in with Evka on the state of the Wardens in Hossberg and learns her thoughts on the griffons.
Check in with Evka
Meet Davrin in the
Hossberg Wetlands
and talk to
Evka
, who stands with
Mila
at Grey Hold. Evka has been struggling to find information in Isseya, but Mila is excited to talk about the griffons, who went extinct in the Exalted Age.
Mila asks if she can ride Assan, but Evka suggests giving it a few years. Davrin reports his training with Assan has been improving, and he hopes to use similar techniques on Assan's brothers and sisters when they're found. Mila is overjoyed to hear there are more griffons; Evka hopes to have the griffons at their sides once more as a symbol of hope and purity.
Rook reminds Evka that the griffons weren't always treated well with the Wardens, and Evka promises to do better, treating them like warriors and rebuilding the griffons together. Mila, delighted, offers to feed them while her father forges them armor. Evka tells Assan he will always have a home with them, and Davrin promises to stay in touch.
Results
After everything Hossberg has weathered, Davrin would like to check up on Evka to see how she and the other Grey Wardens are doing.
Approval
Check in with Evka
That sounded more like he's hungry.
-No changes
We'll rebuild together.
(Diplomatic) Glad to hear it.
- Davrin Approves
(Humorous) Bring the snacks.
(Stoic) You better.
- Davrin Approves

Acquisition

Lighthouse
after completing both
Words of Fire
and
Through the Shadows
, including that quest's follow-up conversation with Bellara at the Lighthouse. This quest will not unlock unless Bellara is a full
Hero of the Veilguard
.
If Rook has just completed the conversation regarding the fate of the
Archive
, they may need to leave the Lighthouse and return for the quest icon to appear over Taash's head.
Walkthrough
Bellara joins Taash at the Hilt for an evening of casual drinks and storytelling.
Spend time with Taash and Bellara
Meet Taash and Bellara at the Hilt Bar & Grill & Bar in the
Hall of Valor
. There, Isabela regales them with a story of Taash prying a ruby out of an enormous statue of a snake. When Taash managed to get the ruby free, the snake statue came to life, and Taash was forced to fight it one-handed while still clutching the ruby.
However, Isabela accidentally describes Taash as "she" while describing the fight. When Isabela realizes this, she declares she is "pulling a Bharv" and does ten push-ups on the floor. At Bellara's surprise, Isabela explains Bharv was a
Lord of Fortune
who took to doing ten push-ups rather than apologizing, as sometimes excessive apologies made the hurt party feel more guilty for being hurt. In addition, the push-ups demonstrate genuine remorse and have the added benefit of eventually bulking muscle.
Isabela also explains there have been other non-binary Lords of Fortune such as Hollix (who looked better in a dress than Isabela), which surprises Taash. They are grateful for the support, however, and Isabela says Kirkwall taught her about family.
She resumes the story, describing Taash telling Isabela to get up the snake's ass to meet them in the middle. However, the story is interrupted when Taash realizes Bellara has eaten the last breaded cheese wand (Taash's favorite). However Rook replies, Bellara is determined to make it right: by pulling a Bharv of her own.
Rewards
None.
Results
Taash has invited Bellara to the Hilt, the bar the Lords of Fortune use as their base of operations, for an evening of drinks and storytelling.
Approval
Approval from both at the end.
Trivia
The story
Isabela
tells about Taash prying a gem out of a statue may be a reference to the cover of the
Player's Handbook
for the TTRPG
Advanced Dungeons & Dragons
, though the cover depicts a demon rather than a big snake.

======================================================================
PAGE: House of the Dead
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/House_of_the_Dead
======================================================================

Quest
House of the Dead
Type
Companion quest
Location
Lighthouse
Blackthorne Manor,
Nevarra
Previous
Arrangements
Next
The Flame Eternal
or
The Sacrifice of Souls
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
House of the Dead
is a
companion quest
for
Emmrich
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. Rook accompanies Emmrich to investigate Blackthorne Manor after learning that this is the seditious death mage's lair.
Acquisition
This quest becomes available after completing either of the
main quests
which unlock after completing
After the Storm
:
The Cauldron
Bloodbath
(if Treviso was destroyed)
The Cobbled Swan Case
(if Minrathous was destroyed)
Speak to Emmrich in his room at the
Lighthouse
, where a victim's corpse from
Where the Dead Must Go
has been recently delivered. He tells Rook the
Hand of Glory
is how the
Venatori
broke into the Grand Necropolis; it masks the living's presence from weak undead. However, only a death mage could have provided this relic, and they need to track them down and stop them from assisting the Venatori.
Speaking to the corpse
reveals only a single name:
Blackthorne
. Emmrich recognizes this as the name of an abandoned manor outside of Nevarra City, and he asks Rook to help him investigate.
Walkthrough
Emmrich tracks down a rogue necromancer in an abandoned Nevarran mansion, where he discovers a terrible new danger to the Fade.
Interrogating one of the Venatori's victims has revealed a new lead: the abandoned Blackthorne Manor located just outside
Nevarra City
. Travel there via the World Map or the
Crossroads
to begin the quest.
Find the rogue necromancer
Rook, Emmrich, and their third companion arrive at the dilapidated Blackthorne Manor, where Emmrich can feel the spirits are disturbed. They've also brought along the Hand of Glory, hoping it will lead them to the rogue necromancer.
The Ground's Keeper, a unique isolation demon.
Walk up to the mansion, but consider taking on an optional, potentially high-level boss fight with unique enemy the Ground's Keeper (if this fight is not completed, it will be available again on another visit to the mansion in a subsequent quest). To fight it, head left through the graveyard to the mausoleum and climb down. Be prepared to fight off waves of summoned shades and deal with multiple bouts of Isolation, possibly by exploiting its weakness to fire damage. Dispatch it, then head forward onto the ledge, climb the scaffolding, and loop back around into the crypt, collecting chests and loot bags along the way. Emmrich will note that the corpses which should be present are missing, implying the necromancer has already taken them.
To proceed with the quest, enter the manor through the main doors. Blackthorne Manor is highly complex, with multiple twists and turns and dead ends. Track your directions carefully to avoid becoming lost.
Before following the quest markers, take an immediate right by breaking some boards to loot the room behind. There's an extra chest hidden behind breakable barrels on the scaffolding furthest into the room. There are also small lootables in the ground-floor west wing (which is also a dead end) and the landing between floors. All passages are currently blocked, however, save the east wing on the upper floor.
Follow the quest markers upstairs and east to the library, which contains multiple display cases that can be destroyed and looted. A door there is locked behind six magic seals, a necromantic evocation drawing on the suffering of nearby spirits; in front of the door lies a skeleton key to the rest of the house.
Find the points of suffering
There are two trapped spirits which must be freed to open the locked door.
To find the first spirit, take the left door leading deeper into this wing of the mansion. Continue up the stairs until Rook passes through a corridor lined with statues, but head left at the end instead of the indicated right. Open a chest (on the right) in this room, then take another left to a short platforming section with a trapped chest at the top. Then return to the statue-lined corridor and take the right turn instead.
The markers lead to the tormented spirit of a victim who has been sacrificed by the unknown necromancer. Two shades will appear, resistant to Necrotic and vulnerable to Ice. After defeating one, another shade and a Voltaic Shade with a barrier will appear. After defeating them, Emmrich will free the tortured soul, which seems to hurt him in some way -- but he is much more concerned with the suffering of the spirit itself.
Return to the library to find that half of the seals are now successfully broken. Take the skeleton key to the second level of the library, then back to the foyer. Cross the room and unlock the door to the wing opposite the library. Follow the quest marker to the Ruined Balcony, where the markers continue ahead and to the left. However, note that a path to the right leads to the North West Wing, an area not used during this quest - it will be a partially explored during the
next visit
to the mansion, but is already available to those who wish.
Find the second tormented spirit and free it like before. It will spawn four Frenzied Shades, which are all vulnerable to Ice and resistant to Necrotic. Emmrich will release the spirit, again doing it at his own expense. A folded ladder can be dropped from the balcony after the fight, and the east door leads back to the foyer.
Explore the North West Wing (optional)
This rightward path from the Ruined Balcony leads to the North West Wing, a large set of rooms filled with crates. An alcove on the east side overlooks an empty stage hall; undead can be seen loitering on the floor below. An open hallway on the west leads to additional storage. In this room, stairs up on the left lead to a chest accessed by jumping back across the storage boxes; a stairway down leads to the fight with the undead on the lower level. A chest is visible behind a grated door, but it is not accessible during this quest.
Continuing this optional side exploration will lead to the Servants' Wing, which contains hints as to what drove away the Blackthornes. Enough exploration of this maze of rooms will lead to the discovery of a hidden bookcase door with a broken floor beyond. Inside this Hidden Room is a corpse, a
chest
, and the
Seneschal's Notebook
. The note states Lord Blackthorne brought home an antique bronze whistle with the Old Tevene inscription "Quis est iste qui uenit," and that one of Blackthorne children, a girl named Felicity, blew the whistle, possibly summoning something dreadful to haunt the manor.
The southeastern door will eventually lead back to the ground level of the main foyer.
Investigate the strange door
Return to the library to find all seals now dispatched.
Enter the door, and the group finds themselves transported directly into the Fade. Take the rightmost path up a hill to find a corrupted wisp that Emmrich can soothe. Take it and jump down to let the wisp open the door there. On the left-hand side of the next archway lies a Tattered Note, a possible reference to Dark Souls, indicating that there's treasure below - drop down to the left to find out.
Drop down through the archway and defeat the rage demon below. It will spawn with multiple undead, which are vulnerable to Necrotic and resistant to Ice.
The next area is a corner of the Fade influenced by the library. Take the stairs down on the right-hand side to reach a trail of floating book piles. Jump between these to reach a chest, then jump back to the path by first climbing up the ladder next to the chest. Keep to the trail a while to face the rogue necromancer: Emmrich's old acquaintance and former Mourn Watcher
Johanna Hezenkoss
. The
Gloaming Lantern
she holds will tear open the Veil if blood spills on it, so listen to her villain speech and watch her escape, leaving a Despair Demon, two Voltaic Shades, and several undead.
Confront Hezenkoss
After the fight, Emmrich attempts to free another spirit but fails, as its essence is now trapped in Johanna's lantern. Instead, focus on escaping the Fade, as trying to cross the far ridge is impossible due to a large gap. Near the chest Rook just passed is a candle needed to proceed; another candle is located on the elevated stage where Johanna gave her speech. Use the candles to help Emmrich forge a path ahead that leads back into Blackthorne Mansion.
As Rook and team return to the library, the resonating Hand of Glory points to the foyer. Head there while Hezenkoss rants at Rook's escape, then enter the main hall to reunite with Hezenkoss herself. She reveals that the hand is in fact her own, that she intends to rule
Nevarra City
, and that she has transformed into some kind of undead herself. Her left hand eagerly reunites with her, after which she'll summon a boss demon called Rampant Ego and disappear.
Rampant Ego is a Pride Demon (vulnerable to Necrotic and resistant to Electric) with both barrier and armor, and it is accompanied by three Shades. Occasionally the Shades will turn into Lesser Pride Demons. Kill the boss and take the chest, then speak with Emmrich to return to the Lighthouse where he fills Rook in on more details of Johanna's past.
He reveals Johanna was once a Watcher -- and a friend of his -- who was exiled for forbidden magic and blood sacrifice. She was a brilliant student, but felt constrained by Watcher oaths, and he now fears she's turned herself partly undead in her ambition. In addition, the stolen life contained in her lantern provides tremendous power, and he is determined to stop her plans with Rook's help.
Rewards
Completing the quest yields:
3,000 XP
100 Mourn Watch Strength
Additionally, defeating the Ground's Keeper in the crypt awards an additional 50 Mourn Watch Strength. If the Ground's Keeper is not defeated during this quest, the rewards for completing the fight can still be claimed on the next visit to Blackthorn Manor.
Codex update
Increased bond with companions taken on quest:
Emmrich: +500 (mandatory)
optional: +50 for defeating the Ground's Keeper in the mausoleum (now or on the next visit)
the rest: +50
Results
Emmrich has tracked down a dangerous rogue necromancer who has been practicing forbidden magic. Determined to stop them, the professor must travel to the abandoned Blackthorne Manor, where he and the team will confront the terrifying necromancy lurking within.
The rogue necromancer inside Blackthorne Manor has been preying on spirits. With the Fade dangerously thin, Emmrich and the team must get to the bottom of this cruel mage's plans by uncovering the mansion's mysteries.
The necromancer Emmrich has been hunting is none other than his one-time colleague turned bitter enemy: Johanna Hezenkoss. She has torn open a rift into the Fade in order to drain power from spirits, and Emmrich must confront her to close the rift before demons spill into the countryside.
Emmrich discovered an old colleague inside Blackthorne Manor: the exiled Mourn Watcher Johanna Hezenkoss. She has created a dangerous Fade rift that lured in spirits so she could drain their power with an enchanted lantern. While Emmrich and the team closed the rift, Hezenkoss escaped, boasting that she would use the now-energized lantern to conquer Nevarra City. Emmrich lamented the dark, power-hungry path his one-time friend had taken, but renewed his pledge to stop her.

Trivia
"Quis est iste qui uenit," per Emmrich, is an old, dangerous invocation that summoned something too dangerous to be described. It is Latin for "Who is this who comes?"

Quest
How to Train a Griffon
Type
Companion quest
Location
Arlathan Forest
Start
Lighthouse
End
Arlathan Forest
Previous
A Warden's Best Friend
Next
Cries from the Past
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
How to Train a Griffon
is a
companion quest
for
Davrin
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. Davrin has asked
Rook
to accompany him to
Arlathan
to look for
Assan's
favourite food.
Note:
This quest is only available during Act 1 of the main story.
Acquisition
This quest is acquired by talking to Davrin in the Lighthouse while he has a New Quest icon for the first time after completing either
On Blighted Wings
or
On Deadly Wings
.
Walkthrough
Davrin  wants to take Assan on an outing to stretch his wings.
Search for truffles
Follow Assan along the path, talking with Davrin, until you come to a closed door. Directly to the right after opening it is an
Evanuris altar
and a chest can be reached down the stairs by jumping across a waterfall. Continue and Assan will happen upon a nug that runs off. Davrin suggests helping by searching for truffles around the bushes but after looking in three places with no luck it seems that Assan has found one after all. Perhaps following the nugs could lead them to mushrooms? They decide to give it a try.
Continue towards the quest marker for a while. At the point where the road ends and some cliff climbing starts, turn left to unlock a shortcut via a lever for future use. Up the cliff Assan finds a herd of nugs and investigating the area finishes the quest with a conversation.
Rewards
500 XP
Davrin's bond increases
Results
Davrin is  planning to take a restless Assan to Arlathan Forest, where he hopes that the  young griffon can stretch his wings - and that griffon and Warden can get  better acquainted.
Davrin is looking  for ways to build trust with Assan. He hopes that finding the griffon's  favourite treat - gingerwort truffles - will help the two bond.
Davrin has  finally found a food Assan enjoys eating: gingerwort truffles. In hunting for  the elusive treat, Davrin gained new understanding of Assan's intelligence,  and both Warden and griffon bonded a little more during their forest  excursion.
If not completed before the end of Act 1:
Davrin has cut short spending time with Assan to focus on finding the Gloom Howler.
Approval
Finding the nug herd
(Happy squawk)
You can trust Assan.
Don't be too tough.
Behave, Assan. -
Davrin Approves
...not that I don't enjoy punching it squarely in the face.
(Diplomatic) That's my motto. -
Davrin Approves
(Humorous) Repeatedly.
(Stoic) Don't get reckless. -
Davrin Approves
(Romantic) Confidence is sexy.

======================================================================
PAGE: Howe Treachery
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests, Origin story quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Howe_Treachery
======================================================================

Quest
Howe Treachery
Location
Castle Cousland
End
Enter the pantry
Previous
Father's Task
Next
Joining the Grey Wardens
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Howe Treachery
is a
quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
. The
Couslands
are forced to deal with betrayal by Arl Howe.
You went to sleep after the pleasant family reunion, only to find that Arl
Howe
's men had
infiltrated the castle
, and had begun killing everyone in it. Your mother doesn't know what happened; she has joined you as you search the halls of your home for the rest of your family.
Walkthrough
Oriana and Oren Cousland lying dead
You awaken in the middle of the night to
Dog
barking. Equip your gear immediately and kill the two attackers that invade your room. Exit your room and take out the two Howe soldiers at your father's bedroom door. Once they are dead, your mother joins your party after a conversation. Make sure to loot the chests in your father's room to the south. Open Fergus' door to find
Oriana
's and
Oren
's bodies.
Open the door to the north to confront four enemies, including a Howe archer. After that skirmish, proceed north. During the next conversation with your mother, choose "What should we do?" followed by "Is there nothing else we can do?" to receive the side quest
The Cousland Treasury
.
Head east and try to persuade the servant to stand and fight with you. You will fight several groups of enemies, possibly without a break between. Make sure to check side rooms and rubble for loot and get the items from the treasury. Be aware of an attack after looting the Family Chest.
Enter the main hall prepared to join in a battle where Ser Gilmore and two guards are fighting off 10 enemies, including a Howe Mage. After the fight, Ser Gilmore tells you that your father was heading towards the kitchen pantry.
Castle Cousland under attack
Note:
If you speak to Ser Gilmore before he activates the dialogue with you, you will not receive the key needed to open the door.
Prepare yourself and exit the main hall from the northern door. A guard is fighting off two enemies outside. Recover and then head west. The next group of enemies includes a Howe Knight, a more powerful "ranked" enemy. Use caution. The Howe Knight randomly drops the
Executioner's Helm
, a great helm that adds a full 25 points of Stamina, a great boost for a low level character. It may be worth saving and reloading the game until it drops. If you choose to do so, keep in mind that the PC or a party member must kill the Howe Knight; if a surviving guard delivers the final blow, the corpse will be empty. Be patient, as it may take many tries for a successful drop. He can also drop the
Heavy Maul
.
You are now free to enter the kitchen and open the pantry door, which will initiate a conversation that ends this quest. Be sure you have finished exploring the castle and completed the treasury side quest before opening the door.
Result
XP (~1280 at level 2)
Your mother and father stay behind at the castle to fight and die while you join
Duncan
as a
Grey Warden
recruit.
The achievement
Last of Your Line
will be awarded.
Notes
The persuaded servant will accompany you through all the fights up to the kitchen, if he's still alive then.
Also the first guard you manage to save from Howe's soldiers will "join" your little group and fight at your side until you find your father.
Both of these two "companions" might die in the fights, however.
Enemies
Howe Soldiers - wear medium armour. Critters are restricted to
Precise Striking
, while Normal-ranked Soldiers will also use either the
Shield Pummel
or
Dirty Fighting
/
Below the Belt
.
Howe Archers -
Aim
and
Pinning Shot
. Normal-ranked archers will also use either
Shattering Shot
or
Dirty Fighting
.
Howe Mage - casts
Lightning
,
Shock
and
Mind Blast
.
Howe Knight - a heavily armoured warrior wielding a two-handed maul. Activates
Indomitable
and uses
Pommel Strike
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Human Noble Origin
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Human_Noble_Origin
======================================================================

“For generations, your family, the
Couslands
, has stewarded the lands of
Highever
, earning the loyalty of your people with justice and temperance. When your
country
was
occupied
by the
Orlesian Empire
, your
father
and grandfather served the embattled
kings
of your land. Today, your
elder brother
takes up House Cousland’s banner in service to the Crown—not against the men of Orlais, but against the bestial
darkspawn
rising in the south.”
Quest
Human Noble Origin
Location
Castle Cousland
,
Highever
Start
New campaign with
Human
Noble character (
Warrior
or
Rogue
)
End
Ostagar
Next
Joining the Grey Wardens
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The
Human Noble Origin
is one of six
origins
available in
Dragon Age: Origins
for the
Playable character
. This character begins as the youngest child of the
Teyrn
of
Highever
, one of the most powerful nobles in
Ferelden
. Your father has tasked you to steward Highever while he and your elder brother leads your family's soldiers to join King
Cailan Theirin
's army at
Ostagar
.
The default name is "Aedan
Cousland
" if male, "Elissa Cousland" if female. The first name can be changed to whatever the player chooses. However, the surname is fixed.
Rogue
and
Warrior
humans start with this origin. For human
mage
characters, see
Magi Origin
.
Introduction
“Some lands are ruled by men and women who believe that they have been elevated to their rank by the
Maker
Himself, but in
Ferelden
, rulers must earn their place. The nobility is not suffered gladly, as the
Orlesian
Empire discovered to its dismay when it attempted to occupy the land.
The Couslands have stewarded the lands of
Highever
for many generations, dating even to before Ferelden's
first king
was crowned. They have persevered so long because of their reputation for justice and temperance, as well as their willingness to lead men into battle. With the rise of the
darkspawn
horde in the south, it thus falls on the
Teyrn of Highever
to send out the call once again: duty demands that an army be sent to
King Cailan
's aid at once.
As the Couslands will quickly discover, however, the evil horde to the south is not the only darkness in Ferelden; treachery stalks the halls of
Castle Cousland
as well.
As a young scion of the
Cousland family
, the duty of carrying its banner will fall to you. Will you live up to your family's proud heritage? Or will you forge your own path, and damn the consequences?”
Plot
Teyrn
Bryce Cousland
and his oldest son
Fergus
are preparing to heed King
Cailan
's call to battle
darkspawn
at
Ostagar
. The Human Noble is charged with stewarding
Highever
with a token force in their absence. Arl
Rendon Howe
of
Amaranthine
waits for his forces to join the teyrn's at Highever. At
Castle Cousland
, the tardiness of Howe's men is being discussed in the main hall as Fergus' younger sibling (the
Playable character
) enters; they are greeted by Arl Howe and Teyrn Cousland, and introduced to
Duncan
, a senior
Grey Warden
. Duncan explains his mission to look for Grey Warden recruits and suggests that the Human Noble would be a promising recruit—already knowing this idea will be rejected by Teyrn Cousland and that Bryce would politically pressure him to not pursue it any longer. Even so, Duncan also has his eye on Ser
Gilmore
as a potential candidate.
After being tasked with instructing Fergus to depart immediately with the bulk of the Cousland forces, the Human Noble is intercepted by Ser Gilmore, a knight in Highever's service; Teyrna
Eleanor Cousland
, the Human Noble's mother, wishes them to collect their
dog
from the kitchens. As it turns out, Dog chased a bunch of rats and cornered them in the larder. After the rats revealed themselves, the Human Noble and Ser Gilmore helped Dog kill the pests.
If the Human Noble seeks out their mother Eleanor, they will find her entertaining guests—Lady
Landra
, accompanied by her son,
Dairren
, and her lady-in-waiting,
Iona
. Flirting with either Dairren or Iona could result in the Human Noble inviting either admirer to their bedroom for sex later that night.
The Human Noble finds Fergus telling his goodbyes to his wife,
Oriana
, and his son,
Oren
. Bryce and Eleanor join the rest of the family to complete their farewells. After finishing the tasks asked of them, the Human Noble heads for bed.
In the middle of the night, Arl Howe's "late" men unexpectedly turn on the Couslands, and
seize
the castle. The Human Noble wakes up when they hear the sounds of battle and Dog growling at their door. A servant opens the bedroom door to warn the Human Noble that the castle is under attack (or the Human Noble's lover is killed as they were investigating the commotion outside) before being slain by enemy soldiers. The Human Noble and Dog fights their way out of their bedroom and searches for the rest of their family. The Human Noble finds Eleanor, who explains that the teyrn is missing. A quick search of the other rooms will reveal Fergus' son and wife murdered. Teyrna Eleanor, saddened and outraged,
suggests
retrieving a
family heirloom
or two
. She also suggests using the servants' exit at the larder to escape. In the main hall, Ser Gilmore, directing the few remaining defenders and holding off Howe's soldiers, tells the Human Noble and Eleanor that Teyrn Bryce was wounded but was last seen headed for the larder—ostensibly he was hoping his family would meet him there.
In the larder they find Bryce, gravely wounded and unable to move. Bryce states he was ambushed by Howe's men but Duncan rescued him and brought him there. Duncan fights his way to the larder and confirms Bryce's fear: Howe's men have surrounded the castle and with Bryce injured, he cannot make the escape. Duncan asks to recruit the Human Noble in exchange for helping them escape; since the
Blight
demands he leaves with a Grey Warden candidate. Bryce acquiesces, wanting his youngest child to tell Fergus what has happened. Duncan offers to save Eleanor too, but she refuses to leave her husband's side, and they are both killed as Duncan and the Human Noble escape.

The PC travels with Duncan to
Ostagar
, where they are greeted by
King Cailan
. The king mentions that
Fergus
is in the
Korcari Wilds
on patrol, but there is no opportunity for the PC to seek him out. (The fate of Fergus is not revealed until the
Epilogue
.) When the PC first arrives at Ostagar the achievement
Last of Your Line
will be awarded.

Trivia
Teyrna Cousland recognises Howe's men by their shields, but very few of those encountered bear shields, let alone
this
one.
After killing the Giant Rats in the Pantry, Ser Gilmore mentions that it "sounds like the start to every bad adventure tale my grandfather used to tell me". This is a reference to "giant rats" being the first enemy fought in many fantasy RPGs.
The majority of the Human Noble Origin was written by
BioWare
lead writer
David Gaider
.
According to
Dairren
, "everyone" says that the Human Noble is more likely to succeed the teyrn of Highever over their brother Fergus.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
This origin is the only background which allows the player to marry
Alistair
or
Anora
.
Bugs
During
Mischief in the Larder
, the pantry door leading to the kitchen area may not open or unlock after defeating the rats preventing the opportunity to name Dog. To avoid this, refrain from spamming the Escape key to skip ahead, and let Ser Gilmore initiate the next conversation instead of interacting with him.
The northern door of the main hall may become stuck and prevent you from completing the quest. To avoid this, save just after
The Cousland Treasury
is initiated; make opening that northern door your first priority (after surviving the fights along the way, and
before
retrieving gear from the treasury) and wait for Ser Gilmore to tell you about the teyrn (rather than attempting to initiate conversation yourself).

======================================================================
PAGE: Hummingbird's Lament
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Hummingbird's_Lament
======================================================================

Quest
Hummingbird's Lament
Type
Companion quest
Location
Arlathan Forest
Previous
The Demon's Bargain
Next
Through the Shadows
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Hummingbird's Lament
is a
companion quest
for
Bellara
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. She intends to confront Cyrian, and she could use Rook's support.
Acquisition
After completing the quests
Unwanted Guests
and
The Demon's Bargain
, speak to Bellara in her room at the
Lighthouse
.
Walkthrough
Bellara  intends to convince her brother to leave Anaris's service. She could use some  support.
Rook interrupts Bellara interrogating the
Nadas Dirthalen
about
Anaris
. Anaris once made an army of
demons
to fight the
Evanuris
, and Bellara wonders why
Cyrian
is wiling to work with such a person.
It turns out Cyrian wants to speak with Bellara, and she worries whether it's a trap. While he once kept her grounded and focused, she doesn't know if he is still the same person, especially if he is willingly working with Anaris. She intends to meet him and asks Rook to come along.
Rook and Bellara meet Cyrian in
Arlathan Forest
. He has come alone, and he regrets what happened to the other Veil Jumpers; he believes his failures are what caused the ritual to fail and them to become demons. He is determined to do better, both for Anaris and his people, though sacrifices will continue to be necessary.
When Bellara and Rook challenge him, he defends Anaris and states he fought against the Evanuris to make their people great. He is convinced Anaris can raise the people again with his help. Bellara suggests the mask is controlling him, but Cyrian insists he has chosen this of his own will and that Anaris truly longs for the people's return to power. He also admits that Anaris is afraid of something, though he doesn't know what, and is determined to help him face it.
Bellara attempts to remind Cyrian of what he taught her: to trust her heart and that her heart will not lie to her. She and Cyrian share a moment, clasping hands, but Cyrian withdraws to Bellara's dismay and says he must do what he must. Bellara and Rook depart, sure they will have to stop Cyrian and Anaris soon.
Rewards
Jumper's Compass
— Rare keepsake for Bellara
250 XP
Results
Bellara's  brother, Cyrian, has reached out, looking to speak to her. Though he is  currently allied with Anaris, the Forgotten One, she believes she can  convince him to abandon that quest - and has agreed to meet.
Approval
Cutscene at the Lighthouse
Oh, Rook.
What’re you asking? -
Bellara Approves
Is this a bad time?
Hey.
And that’s if it isn’t all a big trap.
What does your gut say? -
Bellara Approves
He’s still your brother.
So don’t go.
But Anaris is evil. Full stop. And if my brother’s working with him…
-
No changes
Maybe he has a reason.
People change.
We need to stop them.
Cutscene in
Arlathan Forest
I will do better. I must do better. For Anaris. For our people.
-
No changes
You need better allies.
Cold comfort to them.
You did Anaris’ bidding.
He showed me. The world as it once was - as it could be again.
-
No changes
Yeah, he’s trustworthy.
At what cost?
You’re deluded.
No, but… no doubt he keeps it from me for good reason.
-

Where does it end?
You’re a fool.
After the cutscene
Bellara's bond increases
Notes
Cyrian's eyes never glow red throughout this scene, since he's not close/connected to Anaris.

======================================================================
PAGE: In Entropy's Grasp
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/In_Entropy's_Grasp
======================================================================

Quest
In Entropy's Grasp
Type
Main quest
Location
Arlathan Forest
Start
Elven Ruins
End
Arlathan Forest
Previous
Shadows of Ancient Arlathan
Next
Shadows Crossing
and
Echoes of the Past
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
In Entropy's Grasp
is the third
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
and
Bellara Lutare
's recruitment mission. Upon returning to the ritual site through the eluvian, the Veilguard is met by the
Veil Jumpers
, who tasks them to find one of their experts currently in the field.
Acquisition
This quest is received automatically after completing
Shadows of Ancient Arlathan
and reaching the
Veil Jumpers
' camp.
Walkthrough
The Lighthouse eluvian is currently locked on Arlathan Forest. The Veil Jumpers, an elven group, may be able to help it reach other locations.
Find
Bellara Lutare
After speaking with the Veil Jumpers, the team finds themselves in a small clearing. Head down the hill to encounter a magical
sentinel
that activates once the team draws near. After dispatching it, a cutscene ensues wherein Bellara protects the team from another charging sentinel.
Rook and their companion make a brief introduction, informing Bellara that they've been sent to find her by
Strife
and
Irelin
due to the release of
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
. While taken aback, Bellara says the rise of the
Elven gods
would explain the surge of raw magic in the area and the split in the sky that she saw. She meant to return after observing these events, but she became trapped inside a Veil bubble - which now also holds Rook and their companion as well, unless they can manage to bring it down.
Bellara speculates there must be something powerful at the center of the bubble that is responsible for generating it which, if removed safely, would allow them to escape. Bellara then joins the party.
Help Bellara deactivate the artifact
Follow the Forest Path until it opens up, revealing a large temple entrance with a bridge spanning the river. Keeping to the river will lead to the base of the bridge; on the far side is a ladder (near the whirlpool anomaly) from which Rook can access a small wooden platform with two skeletons and
A Barely Legible Note
. Also accessible from the river in this area is a path up the cliffs to the bridge. Alternatively, crossing the river using the fallen tree will lead up to the start of the bridge to continue into the temple.
Bellara deactivating a resonance amplifier.
Upon stepping onto the bridge, a group of demons will attack. Dispatch them and enter the Elven Temple where Rook can find a health pot and a trapped chest. Continue through the closed double doors at the back of the chamber, then follow the Forest Path for a short distance until reaching the Ruins Approach.
Pick up the power crystal next to the nearby waterfall and slot it into the socket to spawn a walkable bridge across the river. Once across, Bellara can be used to deactivate the malfunctioning resonance amplifier nearby so the team can continue up the Mountain Pass. Doing so will cause a group of demons to attack the party as the magical energy dissipates.
Further up the Mountain Pass will be another health pot and a magical sentinel guarding the entry to the Cliff Wolf Temple. Rook can take a path to the left before the stairs to find a small ledge with another health pot and
A Weather-Stained Note
.
Once inside the temple, pick up the power crystal from the socket next to the door on the left and place it next to the door on the right. Doing so will cause the doors on the left to close, so collect any valuables you want from that room beforehand. Through the newly opened door lies a chest in the hallway leading to a chamber containing four magical constructs, which begin to activate once you try to move past them.
When they are defeated, exit the chamber and continue up the mountain path where you'll encounter a group of darkspawn feeding on
halla
. Defeat them and then deactivate the resonance amplifier in this area, which will summon more darkspawn you need to kill. Afterwards continue across the small bridge. In the next area, use the available ballista to break through a weak section of wall to reveal a short bridge and then deal with the resulting darkspawn.
Take the bridge to the Abandoned Camp and use Bellara to deactivate the last resonance amplifier - this time resulting in a fight with the magical sentinels surrounding it. Break the health pot just below the platform with the amplifier if needed. Continue up through the Broken Stairway, grabbing another health pot along the way. Rook can take a small detour to the left to read
A Cracked Scroll
.
When Rook reaches the top, the group will encounter a Frenzied Sentinel guarding the entrance to the inner sanctum. Stepping into the area will start the encounter and spawn several demons and darkspawn. Loot the chest after surviving the fight and grab the power crystal by the door to the inner sanctum. Rook will need to use the power crystal to unlock the other door opposite with the two blue lights on either side.
After unlocking the door, head up the stairs, clear the blight boil from the ballista, and break through the opposite wall to reveal a hidden room with the other needed power crystal. Return it and the first power crystal back to their sockets to unlock the door and enter the Floating Archive Temple. Just inside the door to the left is a chest; the pathway right will lead to the last room with a health pot just before it.
Enter the room to begin a short cutscene and collect the artifact responsible for generating the Veil bubble, the
Nadas Dirthalen
. Before Bellara is able to collect the artifact, though, an
ogre
will burst through the wall. There are several health pots available in the room to aid Rook during the fight. The ogre, like all darkspawn, is vulnerable to fire and resistant to necrosis. It has several attack movesets which will be reused across various ogre-type enemies in this game, so it is worthwhile to begin observing them here.
Once the ogre is defeated, Bellara successfully extracts the artifact, resulting in the Veil bubble collapsing. An Archive Spirit introducing itself as the Nadas Dirthalen appears briefly before disappearing due to a damaged crystal. The team collects the artifact before heading back to the Veil Jumper camp to update them on what they've seen.
Wrap up any unfinished business
A chance to explore the room is given before speaking with Bellara to head out, during which Rook can loot the chest near the broken wall.
The team arrives at the Veil Jumper camp to find it in disarray, as many are injured or missing. Some notable missing team members are those sent to evacuate a nearby village,
D'Meta's Crossing
. Strife is pleased to see Bellara and Rook's team arrive back safely and reports that they've encountered darkspawn popping up all over Arlathan, which matches the group's experience with darkspawn in the temple. They urge Rook and their companions to travel to D'Meta's Crossing to save any surviving Veil Jumpers and villagers they encounter.
Rewards
+100 Veil Jumpers Strength
Codex Update
Results
Bellara, a Dalish elf with the Veil Jumpers, has found the team in a Veil bubble in Arlathan Forest. To escape the bubble, Bellara will need the team's help to disable an ancient elven artifact… and survive the demons and sentinels that stand in the way.
With the team's help, Bellara disabled the artifact and freed them from the Veil bubble. She also uncovered an ancient repository of elven knowledge, the Nadas Dirthalen. While Bellara intends to unlock its secrets, she has joined the team as they search for the escaped elven gods.
Bellara joins the Veilguard and becomes available as a
companion
.
Rook, Bellara, Neve and Harding head towards
D'Meta's Crossing
.
Notable loot

(light armor)

(rogue) /

(mage) /

(warrior)

(rogue) /

(mage) /

(warrior)

(rogue) /

(mage) /

(warrior)

(Belara)

Nadas Dirthalen
(Immediately before the
Ogre
fight)

(Diplomatic) What's tricky about it?
(Sarcastic) Of course it is. -

Irelin
and

Quest
In Hushed Whispers
Location
Redcliffe Castle
,
Hinterlands
Start
Fiona
in
Val Royeaux
End
Redcliffe Castle
Previous
The Threat Remains
Next
In Your Heart Shall Burn
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
In Hushed Whispers
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Grand Enchanter
Fiona
, leader of the mage rebellion, has offered an alliance with the
rebel mages
at
Redcliffe
.
Acquisition
The quest starts upon attempting to leave
Val Royeaux
during the main quest
The Threat Remains
.
Grand Enchanter Fiona
stops
the Inquisitor
and invites them to meet in
Redcliffe
. Unlike
Champions of the Just
, the
Inquisition
must meet the mages in Redcliffe before the operation becomes available on the
war table
.
Preparation
Make sure that all of the
Haven
-only
war table
missions (except those that follow this quest) are done.
Make sure that the Inquisitor's inventory is as empty as possible as there is a long stretch where there are no merchants.
It may be useful to keep in inventory armor, staff and accessories for
Dorian
to replace his default equipment which is usually below what the Inquisitor can find or make.
Walkthrough
Note:
Choosing the mages will make it impossible to
work with the templars
.
Redcliffe Castle and the mages who could close the Breach are under the control of
Magister Gereon Alexius
, a
Venatori
cultist with an unhealthy interest in the Inquisitor and the power to reorder time itself. He has "kindly" invited the Inquisitor to the castle - alone - to negotiate. It's an obvious trap, but Leliana and her agents can infiltrate the castle and remove the Venatori threat while Alexius's attention is diverted. A dangerous game, but it's our best chance.
Recommended Levels: 4 to 7
Return to
Haven
and discuss Fiona's offer with the advisors
A scene will begin upon returning to the
Haven Chantry
. Everyone bickers about which group to approach for help sealing the Breach. There is a chance for approval from
Cassandra
.
Cullen: You think the mage rebellion is more united?  It could be ten times worse.
We should meet the mages. -
Cassandra Slightly Disapproves
We should find the templars. -
Cassandra Slightly Approves
You must choose soon. -
Cassandra Approves
Travel to Redcliffe and make contact with the mages
As the party approaches Redcliffe, a fade rift opens just outside the main gate. This rift will be different from other rifts encountered thus far. In addition to spawning demons, there will be temporal distortions at random spots on the ground, represented by green and yellow circles. Green circles will slow members/enemies down, while yellow circles will speed them up.
This includes attack speed and the time it takes to disrupt the rift.
Enter Redcliffe
A party member and the Inquisitor will comment on these distortions after the rift is closed, and the guard will return and open the gate. After passing through the arch, a scene begins in which an Inquisition scout will inform the Inquisitor that no one was expecting them. A mage named Lysas will then arrive and say that a
Magister Alexius
is in charge, but they can meet with Fiona in the meantime.
Go to The
Gull and Lantern
to meet Fiona
The Negotiations
On the way to the tavern, the party may encounter
Connor Guerrin
near the docks if he survived the events of
The Arl of Redcliffe
.
Tell me about what you did. -
Solas Slightly Approves
Connor: That's me. The boy with Redcliffe's blood on his hands.
It wasn't your fault. -
Blackwall Slightly Approves
|
Solas Slightly Approves
|
Cassandra Slightly Disapproves
|
Sera Slightly Disapproves
|
Vivienne Disapproves
I'm surprised you're here. -
Sera Slightly Approves
|
Iron Bull Slightly Approves
|
Varric Slightly Approves
They should have killed you. -
Vivienne Approves
|
Cassandra Slightly Approves
|
Sera Slightly Approves
|
Blackwall Slightly Disapproves
|
Solas Slightly Disapproves
Enter the tavern to begin a scene with Fiona (bring
Vivienne
for some amusing dialogue). She will claim that she hasn't been to Val Royeaux since before The Conclave. She also informs the Inquisitor that the rebel mages have pledged their service to the
Tevinter Imperium
:
Fiona: As one indentured to a magister, I no longer have the authority to negotiate with you.
You've made a huge mistake. -
Vivienne Approves
That was fast.
You're ignoring the Breach?
Then tell me who does.
Magister Gereon Alexius will show up at this point, and the Inquisitor begins to negotiate with him to acquire the assistance of the mages. They are interrupted when Alexius' son, Felix, stumbles into the arms of the Inquisitor. But this is merely subterfuge to pass a note to the Inquisitor. Concerned for his son, Alexius leaves with Felix and Fiona, telling the Inquisitor that they will have to continue at a later date. After they leave, the Inquisitor reads the note: "Come to the Chantry. You are in danger."
Meet Felix in the Chantry
Before leaving, a few codex entries can be found in the tavern, as well as
Clemence the Tranquil
, who can be recruited as an Agent for the Inquisition. There is also a bitter mage named Linnea nearby, if the Inquisitor is a human mage there will be some unique dialogue.
Linnea: We don't need your kind here. Your Inquisition's just templars by another name.
I want to help the mages. -
Cassandra Slightly Disapproves
The Inquisition's different. -
Sera Slightly Approves
You need me. -
Sera Slightly Approves
|
Blackwall Slightly Approves
Upon exiting the tavern, veer to the right and fall down onto the grass. The mage who greeted the party when they first entered Redcliffe, Lysas, will be standing near a column and the Inquisitor can choose to speak to him:
Lysas: Were you really looking to
ally
with us?
Yes. Mages should be free. -
Cassandra Slightly Disapproves
|
Vivienne Slightly Disapproves
|
Sera Disapproves
You sound doubtful.
No. You should be contained. -
Cassandra Slightly Approves
|
Sera Slightly Approves
|
Vivienne Approves
Help the strange mage close the Fade rift
Upon entering the Chantry, the party will find a mage fighting off demons, and he asks for assistance closing the rift. The temporal distortions will be present, so the party should use them to their advantage.
Upon closing the rift a scene begins, and the mage introduces himself as
Dorian Pavus
. Dorian will explain his relationship with Alexius as well as how Alexius managed to arrive before the Inquisition.
Dorian: Magister Alexius was once my mentor, so my assistance should be valuable—as I'm sure you can imagine.
Why help us?
Quit grandstanding. -
Dorian Slightly Disapproves
(hidden)
Did you send the note?
Dorian: As if by magic yes? Which is exactly right. To reach Redcliffe before the Inquisition, Alexius distorted time itself.
That sounds bad.
To get here before me? -
Dorian Slightly Approves
(hidden)
That's ridiculous.
Dorian: The magic Alexius is using is wildly unstable, and it's unraveling the world.
This is a lot to swallow.
Do you have evidence? -
Vivienne Slightly Approves
I don't trust you.
Felix will arrive and explain that his father is involved with a group of Tevinter supremacists, the
Venatori
.
Felix: And I can tell you one thing: whatever he's done for them, he's done it to get to you.
How do we stop him?
I'm flattered. -
Varric Slightly Approves
,
Dorian Slightly Approves
(hidden)
He doesn't scare me.
Approach the mages
Back in the war room, choose the operation and a scene will play. Alexius has invited the Inquisitor to Redcliffe Castle to continue their negotiations, and it's obvious to everyone that the invitation is a trap.
Leliana
recalls the secret passage that leads from the windmill (which is now partially ruined) in Redcliffe Village to the castle (this was used by the
Hero of Ferelden
during
The Arl of Redcliffe
). So the Inquisition decides to set a trap of its own, requiring the Inquisitor to distract Alexius, while Leliana's agents infiltrate the castle via the secret passage.
The operation will now be available at the war table. Spend 15 Power to begin. Dorian will be a locked party member, so only two other party members can be chosen.
Note:
This is the point of no return with regards to which group you side with. Once you commence this operation the templars are no longer an option. Also, there will not be a chance to equip Dorian before leaving for Redcliffe, so it is useful to have a staff, armor and accessories in inventory to equip him with better than the starting Igneous Staff and Enchanter Coat.
Redcliffe Castle
A lengthy scene begins with the Inquisitor's party arriving at the castle. If you did not add any companion to the active party except Dorian then
Varric
and Cassandra will be automatically added. Alexius reveals that his motive in all this is to cure his son, as the
Elder One
promised it could be done. Only Solas, Iron Bull and Sera have approval changes for the conversation.
Alexius: Fiona, you would not have turned your followers over to my care if you did not trust me with their lives.
Perhaps we should include her. -
Solas Slightly Approves
Because you simply ooze trust. -
Iron Bull Slightly Approves
Let's get to business.
Alexius: The Inquisition needs mages to close the Breach, and I have them. So, what shall you offer in exchange?
We have connections. -
Sera Slightly Disapproves
Nothing. -
Sera Slightly Approves
I know you want me dead. -
Sera Slightly Approves
Let's talk about time magic.
Tell me about the Venatori.
Alexius: Felix, what have you done?
He's concerned about you. -
Solas Slightly Approves
Your trap has already failed. -
Iron Bull Slightly Approves
Why are you really here?
Alexius: You're nothing but a mistake.
What was supposed to happen? -
Solas Slightly Approves
What is the mark? -
Solas Slightly Approves
Who killed the Divine?
Alexius: The Elder One has power you would not believe. He will raise the Imperium from its own ashes.
Who is the Elder One? -
Solas Slightly Approves
You're a fanatic. -
Iron Bull Slightly Approves
What kind of power?
In the end, Alexius uses an amulet to create what appears to be a rift. Dorian attempts to counter the magic, chaos ensues and the scene blacks out.
The Inquisitor and Dorian wind up in a jail cell, which surprises a couple of guards, who will then attack.
Note:
This is the first time the player has control of Dorian, so as soon as the battle starts, open the character record and put points into his abilities and adjust his tactics and behaviors.
A scene begins immediately upon the guards' defeat. Dorian surmises that the rift not only moved them to a different location within the castle, but a different time. Investigate through dialogue to learn more.
Find out what happened
Upon regaining control, loot the Prison Key that one of the guards will have dropped. Move ahead and up some stairs to a dead end. The stairs to the left lead to a dead end with some loot. Also, Dorian will make a comment on the state of the castle. Return to the intersection and take the other set of stairs. Eventually a larger room will be reached and Lysas will be in the first cell on the right. Approach him for a brief interaction.
Moving on, the Inquisitor and Dorian will end up in a large cavern of sorts. A couple of zealots are blocking the way north and south, and there is a drawbridge to the west that is raised, for the moment. The remaining members of the party can be found in prison cells to the north. Which cell they will be in, depends on the party member.
Head north, down the stairs...
The first set of cells is through the door on the right.
Varric will be in the first cell on the right, humming to himself.
Iron Bull will be in the second cell on the left, singing the "Bottles of beer on the wall" song.
The second set of cells is down the next flight of stairs and through the door on the right.
Cassandra will be in the first cell on the right, reciting the Chant of Light.
Vivienne will be in the first cell on the left, and asks 'Who's there?'.
Solas will be in the second cell on the right, and asks 'Is someone there?'.
The final set of cells is down yet another flight of stairs.
Blackwall will be in the first cell, and says 'I hear you'.
Sera will be in the second cell, trying to remember how a song goes.
The first party member rescued will inform the Inquisitor that the Elder One is the real threat, not Alexius. They will also explain that the Empress of Orlais was assassinated and the Elder One has a demon army at his disposal.
The second party member rescued will say that they overheard the guards saying that Alexius never leaves the throne room.
Exit the cells and head down the southern path. At the end, the party will find Fiona in a cell and a scene begins. She reveals that they are now in
Harvestmere
9:42
Dragon
, a full year into the future. She also mentions that Leliana is somewhere in the castle.
Find Leliana
Head back upstairs, and the sound of the drawbridge lowering will be heard. A group of Venatori will be guarding the newly opened path. There is a
supply cache
just past them, so don't be afraid to use potions if necessary. In fact, there are several supply caches throughout the castle, so don't feel the need to hoard supplies. Head up the stairs to reach the Guard Barracks where you can find the aforementioned supply cache and
A Prayer to the New God
in addition to various items of loot. There is a locked door to the right. Of particular interest is
A Guard's Journal
in the left room, which gives some indication of Alexius' paranoia and what life is currently like in Ferelden.
When ready, proceed up the next set of stairs to the Torture Chambers. At the top of the next set of stairs, the party will hear a torturer plying his trade on a victim. The victim's death is scripted cannot be saved. The torturer,
Hanley D'Urvain
, is an Elite Spellbinder, so expect a tougher battle. He always drops a
Bianca Arms II
and the unique dagger
Bleeder of Souls
, a fantastic upgrade for any dual-wielder, after being defeated.
Continue down the corridor and as you approach the door on the left another torturer will be heard working on Leliana. Open the door to begin a scene.
Find a way back to Alexius' throne room
Pick up the Prison Key by the dead torturer. Proceed and you'll emerge in the drawbridge room, except much higher up. Enter the next door and the mark will begin to glow, indicating a fade rift is up ahead. After closing the rift, turn the gear to raise the gate and proceed up the stairs to the Docks.
Move ahead quickly to witness
Linnea
, now an Elder One loyalist, sacrifice both
Talwyn
and herself by summoning two lesser shades. There is another supply cache nearby. Move up more stairs to emerge in the Courtyard.
After taking in the scenery, proceed up one of the sets of stairs and another fade rift appears. Remember that a mage can use Dispel to prevent any demon from spawning, which certainly helps on higher difficulties. If you have a rogue in your party and don't want to fight at all, you can use
stealth
, command your followers to disengage and just run through the courtyard. The enemies won't follow you into the castle.
Another rift will be encountered just past the first.
The party will finally enter another part of the castle, the
Upper Royal Wing
. Upon entering a corridor, enter the first door on the right.
Connor
may be encountered here in front of the fireplace, but will commit suicide to avoid becoming an abomination. A
Spellbinder's Journal
can be found on a desk here. The journal gives further insight into Alexius' paranoia and behavior.
Alexius' Journal
can be found on a desk directly across the corridor, and it hints at what exactly is wrong with Felix. The party will come to a larger room which contains another supply cache. The pyramid shaped loot box contains the Circle of Magi banner. Proceed down the stairs to the Lower Royal Wing.
The radar will indicate many enemies in the next room, but it's not as bad as it seems. There is a spellbinder and two marksmen inside, as well as a few demons. The demons will attack the marksmen, but won't attack the spellbinder. The marksmen will likely take out 1-2 demons, but will ultimately succumb to them. On the radar, the demons will take up positions in the room once the marksmen are dead. This is the party's cue to move in. A second wave of demons spawns from the fade rift.
Once all the fighting is done, locate the loot that the spellbinder dropped, as it will contain a red lyrium shard. Approach the shard door to the throne room and the party is denied access, for the time being. Dorian comments and now four more of these will need to be found.
Locate red lyrium shards
The shards are all being held by spellbinders (one of which has already been wiped out), and the quest markers indicate where they are on the map. There is no proper path to take here, but the following is efficient enough.
Spellbinder 2
The second spellbinder is on the current level, in a room off the NW corner. As the party approaches the room, one of them will remark that the Venatori are too busy praying to be guarding the room. Indeed, all the enemies are facing away from the entrance, allowing the party to get the jump on them. The spellbinder is up a small landing, almost in the middle of the room. He is flanked by two marksmen, and two gladiators guard the entrance. Immediately upon entering the room, there is a ladder that leads to some scaffolding on the right. A stealthed rogue archer can get off a sneak attack on the spellbinder from here.
Spellbinder 3
Head back to the main hall and proceed directly across to the other side but don't enter the door. Instead turn right and enter the next door on the left. Another supply cache is here. Head upstairs to the Upper Royal Wing and enter the open archway into a corridor. The spellbinder is in the first room on the right, which is a dilapidated library. Upon entering the room turn right and then left and he will be at the end of the walkway. Half a dozen zealots litter the lower area, and there are no marksmen. They're positioned quite a bit away, so the spellbinder could be sneak attacked into oblivion before his help arrives. If not, try to position party members at the top of one set of stairs and use them as a choke point.
Spellbinder 4
Proceed down the long corridor to the next room. Upon entering, there will be some stone blocking the Venatori from immediately noticing the party, and a party member will mention this. Immediately to the right is a ladder leading to more scaffolding. The spellbinder is behind a desk adjacent to the eastern wall. He is accompanied by two marksmen and three zealots.
Spellbinder 5
Both of the other doors in the room will lead to the final spellbinder. The SW door leads to a room with a supply cache, and to a locked door which leads into another corridor. In this corridor you'll face three more doors, and the one on the right is locked. There's no trickery here, as the spellbinder is by himself in the middle room. However, bashing one of the walls to get to him will alert him immediately. So, if a rogue sneak attack is in order, then it is best to just enter through the door directly to his room. A note,
Studies on the Blight
, can be found on the desk in the spellbinder's room. It describes what Leliana has been subjected to over the past year.
Confront Alexius in his throne room
Proceed down the corridor and there will be a door on either side. A Masterwork Wave Blade schematic can be found on a table in the room on the left. Proceed down the stairs to find yet another supply cache. Enter the main hall and interact with the large door to begin a scene. Regardless of dialogue choices, the party will be forced into battle with Alexius.
Defeat Alexius
Alexius is a mage, level 6-8 with 16000 HP minimum (Nightmare, level 7: 25134 HP). He is immune to all disabling effects (asleep, frozen, paralyzed and stunned) and is Perceptive. However, he is NOT immune to physical effects so he can be panicked, chilled, shocked and knocked down. Keeping him knocked down seems to be the easiest way to victory, so skills like
Explosive Shot
,
Payback Strike
,
Mighty Blow
, and
Twin Fangs
to his back will be very useful. His glyphs inflict a large amount of spirit damage and slowdown status. His
Mind Blast
inflicts spirit damage and has a knockdown effect. He will also occasionally cast
Barrier
on himself.
After he loses about 1/3 of his health, he'll create a
fade rift
, and move behind an impenetrable shield by the throne. He will do this again when he loses about 2/3 of his health. The temporal distortions will be present at random spots for both rifts, and the rifts only spawn one wave of
demons
, unleashing
Wraiths
,
Terror demons
and
Shades
. The shield he hides behind will dissipate immediately after a rift is closed. Be sure to grab any rift droppings for loot before the final segment or they will be lost when the cutscene occurs.
The
Staff of Stasis
and a
Cooldown Amulet
are acquired immediately after the battle, as the scene initiates.
The Inquisitor will need to decide how to receive the rebel mages' aid.
Note:
The following approval changes take place regardless of whether or not any of the companions listed below are in the active party. In addition, the points awarded for the
Divine election
are also listed.
Fiona: It seems we have little choice but to accept whatever you offer.
You're our allies.
(Offer the rebel mages a full alliance.)
Solas Greatly Approves
Blackwall Approves
Varric Approves/Disapproves
(only if he's in the active party. Approves if Hawke sided with mages in
Dragon Age II
, Disapproves if Hawke sided with templars)

Cassandra Greatly Disapproves
Sera Greatly Disapproves
Cole Greatly Approves
(occurs regardless if recruited or not; will not be stated in-game)

(occurs regardless if recruited or not; will not be stated in-game)
•
Points awarded to candidates for the next
Divine
: Leliana +10, Cassandra -5, Vivienne -10
You're our prisoners.
(Conscript the rebel mages into the service of the Inquisition.)

Iron Bull Greatly Approves
Cassandra Approves

(only if he's in the active party. Disapproves if Hawke sided with mages in
Dragon Age II

Solas Greatly Disapproves

(occurs regardless if recruited or not; will not be stated in-game)

(occurs regardless if recruited or not; will not be stated in-game)
•
Points awarded to candidates for the next
Divine
: Leliana -10, Cassandra +1, Vivienne +10
Note:
There are no approval changes indicated for Vivienne for either decision, even if she was in the active party. Her approval change occurs the next time the Inquisitor speaks to her in Haven. For additional information see
Vivienne/Approval
.
Vivienne Greatly Disapproves
if the mages are made allies
Vivienne Greatly Approves
if the mages are conscripted
Back in Haven
If the
Underworld Knowledge
Perk is active, an additional dialog option will be available in the conversation regarding acquiring 'additional' lyrium. The Inquisitor can then choose to recruit Dorian permanently.
If the mages were made allies, conversing with Blackwall will yield
Blackwall Approves
. Additionally, praising that he was heroic, if he accompanied the Inquisitor to Redcliffe Castle, yields another
Blackwall Approves
.
The quest ends as preparations to seal the Breach are now underway.
