======================================================================
PAGE: Jammer's Stash
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Jammer's_Stash
======================================================================

Quest
Jammer's Stash
Location
Carta Hideout
Start
Common Box
End
Jammer's Stash
Previous
Entering Jarvia's Hideout
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Jammer's Stash
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Walkthrough
Location of boxes
As the party progresses through
Carta Hideout
as part of
Entering Jarvia's Hideout
,
the Warden
will eventually find Jammer's Journal. It is in the first side passage to the right, and has details about opening a stash box. Reading the journal starts the Jammer's Stash quest (and, unlocks
Codex entry: Jammer's Stash
). The journal mentions that the key to opening the stash box can only be obtained by taking the cheapest item from three other chests.
The Warden will then come across named 'common' boxes when progressing through Jarvia's tunnels. When a box is opened, ensure only the cheapest item from each box is taken, because for every wrong item taken, an
injury
will be received. This must be healed with either an
Injury Kit
, a trip to the
party camp
or the
spell
Cleansing Aura
. These injuries do not impact the Indestructible or I'm Kind Of A Big Deal achievements.
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Correct items
Kanky's Common Box: take the
Silver Costume Ring
Jammer's Common Box: take the
Iron Letter Opener
Pique's Common Box: take the
Garnet Trinket
Jammer's Box
If you do take a wrong item, it will disappear. So you don't need to worry about lost profits.
Once the cheapest item from each box has been taken, a
key
will be received (note that there is no notice given - it just appears in your inventory when the last cheap item is taken). This is the key for Jammer's Stash, a chest found in tamed animal pens which are located in one of the further tunnels near where you fight
Jarvia
.
Taking ALL or the wrong items may result in not being able to obtain the key to open the chest, therefore not completing the quest and receiving the reward.  (On
ps3
xbox360
you can still open the chest after you take all the items.)
Jammer's Stash Box
Jammer's Stash Box is located at the four-way intersection located on the northernmost portion of the 'Carta Hideout" map. Go into the tunnel located east of the crossroads to find Jammer's Stash Box, which contains the rewards.
Rewards
Longrunner's Cap
7
55
45
See also
Jammer's Stash
Bugs
There is a bug in the
pc
version (1.05) that prevents wrong items from disappearing. You are able to solve the quest and get the reward, but the injuries will stay. The only solution seems to proceed with the story, such as the injuries were gone after the fight with the broodmother.
There is a bug in the
pc
version (1.05), it is possible to get two Jammer's Stash Key in the Plot Items section of your inventory.
In the
ps3
version, the quest may fail to be completed even after looting the stash.
In both console versions
ps3
xbox360
, it is possible to get the Jammer's stash with the key even if you take all the items in the other chests.

======================================================================
PAGE: Joining the Grey Wardens
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Joining_the_Grey_Wardens
======================================================================

Quest
Joining the Grey Wardens
Location
Ostagar
Quest Giver
Duncan
End
Duncan
Previous
Origin
Next
Tainted Blood
The Grey Wardens' Cache
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Joining the Grey Wardens
is a
main quest
and the first quest of the post-origins story and will be given by Duncan on arrival in
Ostagar
. After arriving in Ostagar, Duncan informs you that, in order to become a
Grey Warden
, it is necessary to go through a
Joining
ritual. As a first step, he wants you to find
Alistair
, another Grey Warden, somewhere in Ostagar and return to Duncan at his fire near the gate leading to the
Korcari Wilds
. You have the opportunity to explore the king's camp during this time.
Walkthrough
Explore Ostagar
There are a number of things to do in Ostagar.
The Hungry Deserter
:
This quest can be done either before or after joining up with Alistair, but if you do it after then you have an opportunity to affect Alistair's approval (see side quest article for details).
The Mabari Hound
:
If you are not a human noble, talk to the
Kennel Master
to start this side quest.
Codex:
There are a number of codex entries to collect, including:
Speak to the guard just across the bridge from where you arrived to obtain
Codex entry: Ostagar
Speak to
Wynne
to obtain
Codex entry: The Fade
(If not of Magi Origin)
Speak to the Tranquil mage to obtain
Codex entry: The Tranquil
. He'll also talk to you about crafting and augmenting weapons.
Speak to the
Ash Warrior Leader
to obtain
Codex entry: The Legend of Luthias Dwarfson
(you must ask him how it is to be an Ash Warrior)
Speak to
Teyrn Loghain
to obtain
Codex entry: King Maric Theirin
(you need to persuade his guard for an audience with him)
Examine a scroll on a bench near the priest by the infirmary to obtain
Codex entry: The Chant of Light: The Blight
You can also meet the other Grey Warden recruits here:
Daveth
and
Jory
. You will receive XP if you talk to BOTH of them. Daveth has been spying and knows a bit about the mission ahead. Jory isn't thrilled about having to leave his wife back home.
There are a number of locked chests throughout the camp that can be picked if you are a rogue (or if you have
Daveth
unlock them before completing the Tainted Blood quest). Note that they are typically higher difficulty than those in your Origin story, so you may need
deft hands
to pick them.
After first seeing him talking to the Ash Warrior Leader, you can track down Pick the Elven Messenger and, if you are persuasive enough, convince him to give you
Ser Garlen's Sword
. Pick moves around, but will first head to
Loghain
's tent (You can also intimidate him into giving you the sword if you don't care about him thinking that you are cruel).
Trade with the
Quartermaster
to clear your inventory and purchase your first
Backpack
(it is possible to acquire two low price backpacks, one in daylight, and one after the
Tainted Blood
quest, as Quartermaster's inventory resets after the quest). Although the Quartermaster's reference to finding an elf sounds like the set up for a sidequest, no mission is actually given.
Find Alistair
Locate Alistair in the northeast area, arguing with a mage.
After he has joined your party, you can ask Alistair about various topics, such as the Grey Wardens, the Blight, the coming battle and Duncan. While discussing Duncan, Alistair will ask your opinion of him. You gain +2 approval if you reply, "He seems a kind man, if firm." and +1 approval if you reply, "I owe him, too. He saved me."
In this area there is a white chest. It cannot be looted before you've spoken initially with Alistair. After the conversation, look for the chest to the north of where you were standing. It will be locked and in need of a rogue. (This must also be done in the day, before completing
Tainted Blood
, as it will not be there at night.)
Return to Duncan
After Alistair has joined you and you have done enough exploring in Ostagar, head south to the fire to meet with Duncan.
If you have unsuccessfully
stolen
from someone in Ostagar, when you approach Duncan, a Soldier is talking to him claiming you have been stealing. Duncan vouches for you, and the Soldier leaves.
If you haven't yet met Daveth or Jory, Alistair will now introduce them.
Result
When you return to Duncan, you receive two new quests,
Tainted Blood
and
The Grey Wardens' Cache
- you will now be heading to the
Korcari Wilds
.
Rewards
100 XP for each Warden found (300 XP total possible).
250 XP when all Wardens are found.

======================================================================
PAGE: Jowan's Intention
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Jowan's_Intention
======================================================================

Quest
Jowan's Intention
Location
Deep Woods
Start
Arl
Eamon
healed
Previous
Arl of Redcliffe
,
Urn of Sacred Ashes
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Jowan's Intention
is a
Chanter's Board
quest that can conclude
Jowan
's story. In order to get this quest, it is necessary to free Jowan from the dungeon in
Redcliffe Castle - Basement
. Jowan will want to stay and help, so he will need to be told to "Run. I never want to see you again." If Jowan does stay to help, then
Bann Teagan
will either:
Imprison him and refuse to free him until Arl
Eamon
is cured.
Order his execution.
Order his execution and let you kill him.
If he is imprisoned for Arl Eamon to decide his fate, he will then either execute Jowan or send him to the
Circle Tower
, where his fate will be decided by the Circle.
Once
The Urn of Sacred Ashes
is complete and Arl Eamon is cured, "Jowan's Intention" will automatically appear in the Chanter's Board section of the Quest Journal. Brother Bism requires that Jowan is pursued and that any threat he represents is eliminated.
Note:
This quest is bugged – see below for details.
Walkthrough
A new world map location marked "Jowan" (locally called Deep Woods) appears just north of the
Brecilian Forest
on the
World map
.
Travel to this location to find Jowan protecting some refugees from
Blight Wolves
and
Bereskarn
.
Once the fight is over, Jowan will explain he is trying to help out. You can let him go or attack him.
Return to any Chanter's Board representative to complete the quest.
Result
Jowan's story is concluded.
Rewards
3
175 XP
Boss-grade "Ferelden" loot in the Very Hard locked chest.  This chest is one of the places in the game where the rare
Commander's Armor
set and the
Standard Bearer's Helm
can potentially be found.
See also
Magi Origin
Bugs
The location for this quest does not appear on the world map when the quest is received. The quest can therefore only be completed on the PC with one of these methods:
Install an unofficial
fix
for this quest. This fix is also included in the
DAO Fixpack
which you should install anyways.
Debug console:
Set up a shortcut to run
Dragon Age: Origins
with the debug console enabled as described in the
Console
article on this wiki.
Run DA:O using this shortcut and load a game in which this quest is active and which will not be broken by a trip to the world map (i.e. not in the middle of
Broken Circle
,
Anvil of the Void
or other quest that does not allow ducking out and in).
Use the key to run the console (the back tick - ` - by default). Note that you will not see any changes, and will not be able to see what you are typing next (a
mod
is available to enable the text) - you will know the console is running because the keys you use will not trigger their normal actions such as opening the character sheet.
type "runscript zz_ran_debug" (without the quotes) and <ENTER>.
You will be prompted to select a random encounter type: select "1. Forest".
You will be prompted to pick an encounter: select "4. Jowan".
You will be teleported to the Deep Woods, the destination for this quest.
The actual bug is very simple to fix on PC 1.04, once and for all. The cause is that the quest is mislabelled as a "Plot" quest rather than a "Chanter's Board" quest.  Because of this, at the end of the
Arl of Redcliffe
, the trigger to activate the quest on Denerim Chanter's Board instead puts it up directly in the quest log.  This causes it to skip the "quest accepted" script, which is supposed to activate Jowan's location on the world map, and thus breaks the quest.
To fix it yourself, use the
Toolset
to change Plots -> Light Content/lite_chant_rand_jowan.plo's Entry Type property from Plot to Chanters, and then recompile Scripts -> Light Content/Plot Scripts/lite_chant_rand_jowan and Scripts -> Arl Eamon/Plot Scripts/arl000pt_contact_eamon.  Drop all of those changed files into the override folder, and this quest should trigger normally.
Another method is using
this savegame editor
, find the journal entry marked "lite_chant_rand_jowan", cut the string then save and attempt to reacquire the quest at the Chanter's Board. Doing this should reset the quest and make the Jowan/Deep Woods area available.
pc
If Jowan is killed by the blighted beasts, then the quest does not appear to be updated and cannot be completed.
There appears to be an inaccessible
Deathroot
plant in the area for this encounter.

======================================================================
PAGE: Justice (quest)
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Justice_(quest)
======================================================================

For the character in
Awakening
, see
Justice
.
Quest
Justice
Act
3
Location
The Sewers (
Darktown
)
Drakestone Mines
(
Bone Pit
)
Start
Anders's Clinic
End
Anders's Clinic
/
Hawke Estate
Previous
Check on Anders
Next
Questioning Beliefs
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Justice
is an Act 3
companion quest
for
Anders
in
Dragon Age II
.
Acquisition
This quest becomes available after completing the
Check on Anders
and leaving
Darktown
for any other location. It is not available if Hawke told Anders to go away after
Dissent
in Act 2.
Walkthrough
Anders's Clinic
Travel to
Anders's Clinic
in
Darktown
and speak with
Anders
. He says that what he did with Justice is unnatural and should never have happened.
If
Sebastian
is in the party for this conversation he will make a funny comment.
Darktown (Sewers Entrance)
The first of the two ingredients,
Sela Petrae
, is found in
the sewers
of Darktown. Head there with or without Anders (he will appear at the entrance and Hawke will have the option to choose remaining party members).
The Sewers
If Hawke is romancing either
Fenris
or
Merrill
, Anders will stop Hawke and comment on it briefly.  He's critical of either of them:
He claims Merrill "seems sweet" but she'll never pick Hawke over her
demon
.  Merrill retorts that he has no right to question them and asks if Justice is any different.
He claims Fenris is "less a man to me than a wild dog." Hawke responds that Anders just doesn't know him, to which Anders retorts he's as likely to know as much as he ever will.  Fenris simply barks, "That's right, mage."  Anders criticizes him, once again, for letting one bad experience "color his whole world," and wonders if Hawke might want someone more open-minded.  Fenris states to Hawke, "A mage and a hypocrite.  What company you keep."
Regardless, Hawke will have to collect five deposits of Sela Petrae and along the way they'll encounter Lyrium Smugglers (who are Mercenaries by another name), along with some Carta dwarves, and a group of apostates and a Raider Reaver.  The "Pit Boss" in the northern section is a Carta Assassin with a fancier name.  Hawke will also be able to find a part of
The Enigma of Kirkwall
(for the
Archaeologist
achievement) near the foot of some stairs in the southern section.
Note:
There are 4 standard traps (20 Cunning to reveal/disarm – 100 XP each) and one complex chest (30 Cunning to unlock – 150 XP) in the sewers.
The Bone Pit (Drakestone Mines Entrance)
To continue the quest, head to the
Drakestone Mines
in the
Bone Pit
outside
Kirkwall
with or without Anders (again, he will appear at the entrance and there will be the option to choose remaining party members).
Drakestone Mines
Fight your way through
poisonous spiders
,
corrupted spiders
, and a monstrous spider to collect five deposits of the second ingredient,
Drakestone
. Don't forget to pick up the
Deep Mushroom
in the mine.
Note:
There are 7 Complex-ranked traps and one chest (30 Cunning to reveal/disarm/unlock, 150 XP each) in the mine and that many of the spiders only spawn if a trap is triggered or removed.
Anders's Clinic (with ingredients)
Head back to the clinic and speak with him once more. He will reveal that the potion was a ruse to get Hawke's help and ask for one final favor: to distract the
Grand Cleric
in the
Kirkwall Chantry
for an undisclosed reason. No matter what you say, Anders will not reveal his plan.
The Chantry
Go to the Chantry with or without Anders and speak with Grand Cleric
Elthina
about mages. If Sebastian is with you, he will leave as soon as you enter the Chantry, saying he will have no part in this. He can be re-added using the party summoning horn, however, but he has no friendship or rivalry changes at any point in this quest.  There will not be an option to choose every dialogue option before Anders appears by Hawke's side claiming to have been looking for them everywhere.
Anders's Clinic/Hawke Estate
If Hawke is not Anders' 100% friend, talk to him in the Anders's Clinic or in the Hawke Estate (if he moved in) to finish the quest. He will say that the spirit inside him is exultant. He will refuse to explain what happened in the Chantry, but will say that the war will happen: the clock is ticking down and it will be midnight soon. If Hawke is Anders' 100% friend, this conversation will not trigger, the quest will end automatically and another conversation during the follow-up quest
Questioning Beliefs
will trigger instead.
Notes
If Merrill is Hawke's romantic partner and
A New Path
has been completed, Anders' comment does not change to reflect the fact that "her demon" is dead.
After Hawke refuses to gather ingredients or to distract Elthina, Hawke gains a special option in the dialogue with
Elthina
regardless of Anders' friendship/rivalry score. Being warned that the apostates are plotting against the Chantry, Elthina replies that mages have threatened her before and she will not succumb to fear. If Anders is in the group, Elthina mentions him.
If Hawke helps Anders with both of his requests then they can try to warn the Templars and the Chantry about Anders' plans:
Hawke can talk to
Cullen
at the Gallows and warn him.  He says that he will pass along the warning (if Anders is in the party), or that he has already warned Meredith and Elthina about Anders and a force will be sent to apprehend him soon.
Anders: rivalry (
+15
)
Sebastian: friendship (
+15
) (only if Anders is not in the party)
You can only do this if you snuck Anders into the Chantry.
if you warn Cullen then you cannot warn Elthina (as described below).
Hawke can talk to
Elthina
in the Chantry and warn her.  She says that she cannot turn on the Templars.
Anders: rivalry (
+15
)
Sebastian: friendship (
+15
)
You can do this whether you snuck Anders into the Chantry or not.
Nothing in the plot changes if you warn either of them.
When Anders meets up with Hawke talking to Elthina, she says that she hopes he found a
balm
there, a pun on his being there to place a
bomb
and dramatic foreshadowing of the events of the last act.

main quest

Doing this quest is not required for Anders to end up going the final route in the storyline. You can leave this quest just sitting in your log and The Last Straw will still pop as normal.

Bugs
When you need to talk to Anders in his clinic, the icon for Darktown will have the quest marker over it, while the actual quest takes place in Anders's Clinic.
The icon for Hightown will have the quest marker over it, while the actual quest takes place in the Chantry.
The quest can glitch and show up as still active in journal.  If the quest still points to the Chantry, this can be fixed by talking to Anders in either his clinic or in
Hawke Estate
as appropriate.
If you refuse to help Anders at the start of this quest then you can still go to Elthina and warn her, even though (as far as you know) Anders was trying to separate himself from Justice, and this quest had nothing to do with him sneaking into the Chantry.

======================================================================
PAGE: Justice Must Be Served
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Justice_Must_Be_Served
======================================================================

Quest
Justice Must Be Served/A Gift of Silence
Location
Redcliffe Village
,
Denerim Market District
Quest Giver
The Mages' Collective
End
The Mages' Collective
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Justice Must Be Served/A Gift of Silence
is a quest received from
The Mages' Collective
Quest Board
.
Background
The Collective needs a courier to effectively bribe
Knight-Commander Harrith
with ten
Lyrium Potions
, thus solidifying the relationship between the
Templars
and the Collective.
If you chose to abandon
Redcliffe
during
A Village Under Siege
then this quest will be unavailable. However, if you activate this quest before arriving in Redcliffe, then it is still possible to do this quest and abandon Redcliffe if you so choose.
Walkthrough
There are two options here:
Give the potions to
Knight-Commander Harrith
(located by the
Windmill
in
Redcliffe Village
).
Inform
Knight-Commander Tavish
, located in the
Denerim Market District
, about the illegal dealings.
Note:
As is the case with
health poultices
in
Restocking the Guild
, the quest will accept
any
10 potions of
lyrium
, starting with the least potent ones in the player's
inventory
, then the next least, until the number of 10 is reached. While the wording may suggest otherwise, it does not specifically require
Lyrium Potions
, so the most economical solution would be to have at least 10
Lesser
Lyrium Potions
in your inventory before handing in the quest.
Result
If you talked to Harrith you must return to the Mages' Collective for your reward.
If you talked to Tavish you get your reward after your conversation with him.
You get:
1
175 XP
Betraying the Collective by choosing Tavish on this quest prevents the
Defending the Collective
quest from being accessible.
Notes
Due to a bug it is possible to side with Knight-Commander Tavish after completing the quest with Knight-Commander Harrith. In this case he will take 10 more lyrium potions and a "note" from you and reward you with an additional 1
and an appropriate amount of XP (although the quest log will remain unaltered). This was not the original intent for the quest you were suppose to choose one or the other.
There is a bug in which you automatically side with Tavish after you speak with him, even if you don't chose the option to actually pick his side

Neve has a  personal errand in Dock Town… but she wouldn't mind company.

Approval

His light was out when I got here. I should've been back sooner.

Quest
Lady in Waiting
Location
Denerim Market District
Start
Slim Couldry
End
Lady's Maid
Next
A Stolen Blade
Related
Crime Wave
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Lady in Waiting
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
. It is the first mark of the
Crime Wave
quest line.
Acquisition
A Lady's Maid is in the central bazaar and she's carrying a
purse with gems
which
the Warden
is tasked to steal. She appears only if the Warden pays 50
to
Slim Couldry
.
Walkthrough
The Warden needs to find the maid and with a
stealing
action to relieve her of her valuable possession.
A successful stealing action requires the Master Stealing skill or the equivalent in
cunning
and/or
Stealth
.
Notes
She is standing next to
Cesar
and the Warden cannot initiate a conversation with her, thus making the steal action the only available option.
Failing to steal her purse will generate a comment from her, however this won't fail the quest.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lair of the Dragon King
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Lair_of_the_Dragon_King
======================================================================

Quest
Lair of the Dragon King
Type
Companion quest
Location
Rivain Coast
Start
The
Lighthouse
Previous
Slaying the Fangscorcher
Next
Words of Fire
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Lair of the Dragon King
is a
companion quest
for
Taash
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. The Dragon King has laid an impossible trap for
Taash
, resulting in a difficult decision.
Acquisition
This quest becomes available after meeting the following three requirements:
Completing the main quest
Blood of Arlathan
Completing Taash's previous companion quest,
Slaying the Fangscorcher
Completing the Fangscorcher quest's follow-up conversation with Taash in the dining room at the
Lighthouse
, which results in
Shathann
visiting for a meal with Taash and
Rook
Note:
This conversation becomes available immediately after completing
Slaying the Fangscorcher
. However, depending on the order of quests completed, it may be blocked by the dining room conversation with Bellara and Neve regarding her worry for Cyrian (which becomes available after completing
Blood of Arlathan
and
The Demon's Bargain
) or by
Lucanis
asking for help with the Crows. In this case, complete the outstanding conversations, leave the Lighthouse, and return to make the conversation with Taash and Shathann available.
During this conversation, Shathann comments on Taash's initially prepared dish (either rejecting Taash's Armada Special, requesting something simpler with vegetables; or approving of their
isskapp
, though she states it could use more ginger). While Taash is making this requested alteration, Shathann and Rook discuss Taash's place in the team.
If Taash embraced Rivaini culture:
Shathann disapproves of Rook's influence, claiming Taash now respects all cultures except the
Qun
. Rook suggests Shathann is being too hard on Taash, especially since Shathann was the one who brought them to
Rivain
.
If Taash embraced Qunari culture:
Shathann expresses surprise in Taash's new interest in the
Qun
. She demands to know if the
Qunari
have asked Rook to push Taash back towards the Qun. When Rook denies this, Shathann explains Taash's status as an
adaari
makes Taash a desirable warrior. When Rook points out that Shathann has been the one teaching Taash about Qunari culture and language, Shathann muses that this education might have been an error.
Regardless, Shathann reveals she was never taught to be a mother (she was trained as a scholar instead) and says she is aware that she has many failings in that role. However, she cannot bear to see Taash in danger without acting.
When Taash returns, they express their identity as non-binary to Shathann. Shathann is taken aback, initially suggesting Taash might be
aqun-athlok
instead (one who is born as one gender but lives like another, a concept outlined in the
Qun
), but Taash rejects this and asks Shathann to simply understand their feelings. Unfortunately, the conversation devolves into an argument over the phrase "shokra toh ebra," with Taash demanding to know why they must constantly struggle with who they are. Clearly overwhelmed, Shathann excuses herself and leaves.
Afterwards, Rook can express support for Taash in several ways, but Taash is surprisingly accepting of the outcome. They encourage Rook not to let good food go to waste, and they finish the meal together.
Once all prerequisite requirements have been met, leave and return to the Lighthouse, then speak to Taash in their room to begin this quest.
Walkthrough
When the Dragon King's attacks hit close to home, Taash must face him in a final, fiery showdown.
Meet Taash in Rivain
The interior of Shathann's house.
Shathann has sent word to Taash regarding the Qunari tablet, requesting they meet at her home; use the World Map to reach it. However, when Taash and Rook arrive at the house, it is only to find an empty building and Shathann's blood on the floor. Taash sends Rook for
Isabela
and
Cutter
, as they can smell him in the home, but when Isabela arrives without him, they realize Cutter must have been there to kidnap Shathann.
Worse, Isabela has found letters in Cutter's room between him and
The Dragon King
regarding Shathann and the Qunari tablet. The
Antaam
—and Isabela, from private conversation with Shathann—now know it contains secrets of the fire-breathing
adaari
, and the
Dragon King
likely hopes to learn how to acquire the power for himself.
Taash discovering Shathann's blood.
Also found in Cutter's room was a map, revealing the Dragon King has holed up in a dormant volcano to the north: Sharksmouth Mountain. Getting inside will be a challenge, but Isabela assures Rook and Taash that the
Lords of Fortune
will fight alongside them.
From this point on, Taash is locked to the party.
Rescue Shathann
The team arrives at the
Rivain Coast
. Rook suggests heading straight for the Dragon King in the mountain, but Taash points out their mother could be hidden anywhere; instead, they'll find somewhere Cutter and Shathann have been and track by scent. A newly beached boat on the south edge of the Winding Shoreline could be Cutter's, and Rook asks Taash to track the scent. It does indeed include their mother, so the team now has Cutter's trail.
Head east to discover a squad of Antaam who assure Taash their "priest" is safely with the Dragon King. Once they are defeated, Taash finds a splash of their mother's blood on the east stairs beside a note from the Antaam to Cutter, promising him the powers of an adaari. Speak to Taash to confirm they are still on the trail, then melt the metal barricade just beyond with their fire breath.
Defeat the Antaam at the top of the ladder, then head east through the Clifftops. Speak to Taash again at the waterfall to confirm they are still on the trail and melt the barricade blocking the way. The trail continues east through the ruined Warden fortress, where another Antaam garrison blocks the path. Defeat them, speak to Taash (who says the team is getting closer), and melt the barricade at the bridge.
Note:
Crossing this bridge opens up the northern half of the
Rivain Coast
exploration map. All areas, all chests, and all collectibles within will remain accessible after the completion of this quest. Some chests will in fact be unreachable until
Lair of the Dragon King
concludes, so do not fear potentially overlooking valuables or quest items.
Progress across the bridge into the Ravine Descent. After dropping down a ladder, Rook can take the stones stairs on the left further down, or briefly detour via wooden platforms on the right (and a short zipline) for some valuables and a small chest. At the bottom of the stairs wait another batch of Antaam and the next scent clue for Taash.
Sharksmouth Mountain.
Melt the barricade to reach the Tide Flats, where Sharksmouth Mountain looms in the distance. This area will remain available after this quest, so continue northeast to find
Isabela
,
Mateo
, and
Seer Rowan
rummaging through several dead Antaam. They report the main path up to Sharksmouth has been destroyed, so Rook and the Veilguard will need to climb. They will make noise elsewhere to draw the Antaam's attention away from the rescue as well.
A ladder to the west leads to a series of wooden platforms. Scale these and dispatch the Antaam waiting on the first landing. To the left, climb more stone ledges and drop a ladder for future access. Double back as needed, staying on the stone path, until entering a building. A ladder leads down immediately to the right. Take it and loot the valuables, opening the doors for future access on the way, but continue southwest inside the building.
A set of stone stairs in the next room leads upwards. Follow them, jumping across the gaps where needed, and take the ladder up on the other side. On this upper level, a narrow, rickety wooden platform can be taken eastward; follow it and emerge from the building to find another encampment of Antaam. Drop the folded ladder to the right, then jump across to the wooden beams and platforms just to the right of the ladder.
Platform, dangle, and swing upwards, detouring briefly through an opening in the stone wall for a chest. Continue along the balance beams, wooden platforms, and reinforced stone paths until Rook reaches solid ground on the other side. A large group of Antaam wait here; kill them, then climb up the ladder to finally enter the caves of Sharksmouth Mountain.
Reach Shathann and the Dragon King
Sharksmouth's lava caves.
The rest of the abandoned Warden fortress lies in ruin within the magma flow of Sharksmouth. Take either the left or right path; they will converge again shortly, though there is a small cave on the left path with minor loot. Taash shouts for the Dragon King as the team arrives, and the Dragon King calls back an exhortation to the Antaam to defend against the intruders.
Continue downward via the ruined tower all the way to the ground, where the Antaam will begin attacking with weapons and
gaatlok
cannons. It's possible to walk on the edges of the lava for short periods, but remaining too long leads to death. Defeat the Antaam, then take the stone stairway up on the left side. Follow the path around to the right; drop off the right side to another elevated platform about halfway along.
This path curves rightward, then enters the Warden fortress proper. Take the ladder up at the end for
Cutter
to make his entrance. He claims he'll become a fire-breather but will, in fact, become a corpse very shortly.
Cutter during Lair of the Dragon King.
Cutter is categorized as a Physical-Imbued Glass Cannon. He wields two axes, is vulnerable to lightning, and is resistant to fire. He also throws smoke bombs throughout the fight, obscuring Rook's vision, and will taunt both Rook and Taash until he is defeated.
However, Taash is not calmed by Cutter's death; their only objective is the Dragon King and Shathann. Unfortunately, the large gate to the left is too strong for their fire, and the team must find a different way around. Immediately to the right, melt the red drakestone crystals and jump across to the wooden platform ahead and to the left. Climb up the ladder at the end.
Use the gaatlok cannon here to destroy the massive gate. Jump back across the wooden planks and enter the now-open gate while the Dragon King expounds on how he and the Antaam will claim a power forgotten by the Qunari. At the end of this hall, clamber up a stone ledge to reach a large Warden storeroom overrun with Antaam. Kill them, drop the nearby ladder, and pull the wall lever to open the gate for return trips.
On the second floor, the surrounding items (and the
Grey Warden Book
note on a crate) reveal the Antaam discovered how to blight the dragons for the gods from this place, which greatly offends Taash. Continue along the second-floor balcony until reaching a broken place in the wall, which allows access to a more ruined part of the fort. Atop this final set of stone ledges is a vantage point from which Taash and Rook can finally see Shathann—and the Dragon King.
Unfortunately, this vantage point is a trap, and a cage drops down on Rook and Taash from above, pinning them in place. The Dragon King greets Taash, introducing himself as Ataashok, and beside him kneels a bound Shathann. He reveals himself as an adaari as well, and he blames Shathann for stealing Taash away as a child, though he's glad Taash's fire has not been quenched in the meantime.
He desires Taash's blood to further strengthen his Antaam into an army of adaari, though they have already been enhanced by Ghilan'nain. Rook will have an opportunity to challenge him, but he laughs off any threat, and Taash's fire cannot melt the bars. The Dragon King mocks the concept of "shokra toh ebra" and insists the tablet contains secrets which will make the Antaam dominant on the continent.
Shathann, however, interjects, and corrects the Dragon King: "shokra toh ebra" is not that one must struggle
with
what they are; it is instead that through struggle, one
finds
what they are. She states the Dragon King has never done such a thing, but that Evataash has. She asks Rook to keep "them" safe, and when Rook agrees, she defiantly throws herself against the lever, raising the cage and freeing Rook and Taash.
The furious Dragon King strikes Shathann with the tablet, instantly killing her. Taash lets out a scream of rage, and they and Rook quickly backtrack to find a way to reach the Dragon King and avenge Shathann.
Avenge Shathann
Head to the bottom level of the Grey Warden storeroom. The wooden beam has now fallen, allowing access to a new part of the fortress. Continue along the beams, hopping up to grab the
Rider Figurine
memento in the second gap, and follow the wooden path as it curves around to the right. The Dragon King taunts the Veilguard as they approach, and the roar of a dragon can be heard as the team approaches.
High dragon Kaltenzahn.
A chest is accessible to the right of the broken wall beside the gate. Through the gap itself are several Antaam who claim Taash's adaari fire. Kill them all, loot the chest straight ahead, and follow the hall up a short staircase to find several potion pots for one last healing opportunity. At the end of this hall waits Taash's revenge—and the high dragon Kaltenzahn.
Kaltenzahn is a high dragon who breathes ice. She is vulnerable to fire and resistant to cold. She has the standard array of dragon attacks: diving at Rook, feet and claw attacks, and cold blasts of breathed ice. In addition, at approximately 70% and possibly again at 30% health, she will use her wings to create enormous wind gusts which obscure the air and will knock the Veilguard off their feet.
As the dragon falls in defeat, Taash shoves Ataashok to the ground. In despair, he insists he is the Dragon King, but Taash snaps back that dragons would have queens, then burns him to death with their dragonfire.
In the aftermath, the volcano of Sharkmouth begins to rumble. Isabela arrives to beg the team to leave, but Taash is unwilling to leave Shathann's body and begs Rook to helm them carry her out. Unfortunately, the cavern is too unsteady. They hold her as long as possible before Rook manages to persuade them to leave, and as Rook and Taash make their escape, Shathann's body is surrendered to the erupting volcano.
Help Taash decide their path
Back at the Lighthouse, Taash mourns their mother and grieves that they were unable to save her. They struggle with Shathann's last words, "shokra toh ebra," and worry that they will forever misunderstand the Qunari culture Shathann so loved.
They also show Rook a small piece of Shathann's horn, which they cut off in the last moments with her body. The Rivaini custom would be to wear it as jewelry, and the Qunari custom would be to attach it to one of their own horns. They ask Rook what they should do.
Rook can now encourage Taash to pursue either their Qunari heritage or their Rivaini culture. Either route will lead to Hero of the Veilguard status for Taash and an honoring of Shathann's sacrifice.
Regardless of the choice, Taash thanks Rook, then breathes fire across the Qunari tablet, revealing a message hidden in the fiery letters.
Rewards
Completing the quest yields:
3500 XP
Golden Casque
(helm)
100 Lords of Fortune strength
Dragon Bone
(valuable)
Bond increase with party companions
Taash attains
Hero of the Veilguard
status along with
Rivain's Legacy
(heroic armor) and the skill
Rivaini Flair
, if Taash embraces Rivaini culture
The Qun's Honor
(heroic armor) and the skill
Qunari Focus
, if Taash embraces Qunari culture
Results
Taash's mother Shathann has asked to speak with Taash to offer new information about the ancient Qunari tablet, and possibly ease recent tensions between them. As Shathann tries to make peace, however, the Dragon King prepares to make war.
The Dragon King, eager to claim Taash and the mysterious Qunari tablet, has kidnapped Taash's mother. Desperate to get Shathann back, Taash has launched an all-out assault on the brutal warlord in his volcano lair.
The dragon King, revealed to be a fire-breather like Taash, has captured the heroes in a trap at his volcano lair. Taash's mother Shathann sacrificed herself to free her child from his clutches. Now Taash will let nothing stand between them and vengeance as they burn through the Dragon King's forces.
If Taash was encouraged to embrace being Rivaini:
When the Dragon King kidnapped Shathann, Taash raced to his volcano lair to rescue her, only to fall into his trap. Shathann sacrificed herself to free Taash, who then defeated the Dragon King and his dragon minion in a final, deadly fight. While the Antaam remain a threat in Rivain, the Dragon King's fire has burned out. Taash will honor their mother with Rivaini customs.
If Taash was encouraged to embrace being Qunari:
When the Dragon King kidnapped Shathann, Taash raced to his volcano lair to rescue her, only to fall into his trap. Shathann sacrificed herself to free Taash, who then defeated the Dragon King and his dragon minion in a final, deadly fight. While the Antaam remain a threat in Rivain, the Dragon King's fire has burned out. Taash will honor their mother through Qunari traditions.
Notable loot
This quest opens up the entire north section of the Rivain Coast. All areas unlocked by this quest, along with their loot, can be returned to after completing this quest. Note that some of the chests in the new areas can be accessed only after fully completing Lair of the Dragon King.
The following is dropped by a quest-specific enemy at the Lava Caves:
Rialto Sunsetter
(rogue) /
Proving Steel
(warrior) /
Enchanted Athame
(mage)
Approval
Taash approves of defeating groups of Antaam along the way.
Speaking with Isabela
He was here. I caught his scent. He took her.
(Diplomatic) We'll get your mother back.
- Taash Approves
(Stoic) He's a dead man walking.
- Taash Approves
(Assertive) Cutter? Why him?
Confronting the Dragon King
And with your lifeblood, Ghilan'nain can create an army of adaari for me. Our fire will burn this land pure!
(Diplomatic) Let us out and live.
- Taash Approves
(Humorous) Your plan is
what
?
(Stoic) Prove how strong you are.
- Taash Approves
Back at the Lighthouse
We went there to save her.
(Diplomatic) She died at peace.
(Stoic) She had other plans.
- Taash Approves
(Emotional) I'm sorry.
- Taash Approves
Note texts
Grey Warden Book
Antaam Note (Winding Shoreline)
Notes
Bond increase with Taash, experience, and loot may be farmed during this quest. When dropping down into the bottom of the volcano, a pack of six Antaam Warbrands are waiting, two each of Anadaar, Karataar, and Valotaar. Kill all six, save, and load back in to the drop down point with them in front of Rook; the bond increases will stack and can be repeated as desired. Sometimes the second Anadaar will get caught on the stairs behind the ruined building in front of where you load in, but Rook can climb the stairs to kill it.
Trivia
The assets used in the dinner scene with Taash and their mother are reused assets, not reflecting the actual food being mentioned. The
Armada special
is a sandwich with meat and cheese that can be made "Nevarran Style"
, but a plate of ham and cheese is served that is seen in
The Hall of Valor
and other locations. While isskap is a Qunari dish that uses melon and is served with powdered ginger
and is represented by a platter of skewered, roasted squid with a side of ginger and vegetables that is shown in the
Cobbled Swan
, and in Blackthorn Manor.
A plate of ham and cheese that is served as a place holder for the "Armada Special".
A plate of skewered, roasted squid with a side of ginger and vegetables served as a place holder for isskap.

passed along the letter written by Dorian's

. Dorian may wish to read it, but it may be best just to take him to the

Acquisition
The Inquisitor

Walkthrough
Upon speaking to

, she informs
the Inquisitor
that she has received a
letter
from
Magister
Halward Pavus
, Dorian's father. He asks that she brings Dorian to a secret meeting, and that a "retainer" will be watching for them. (The letter is shown in the Gallery.)
The Inquisitor has the option of telling Dorian about the letter. If done so, a bit of extra dialog and a brief scene will play, without approval changes. However, at a later stage of the quest, a chance to gain approval is not given, if this option was chosen.
Whether he is aware of the meeting or not, entering the
Gull and Lantern
in
Redcliffe Village
,
Hinterlands
with Dorian will present a deserted tavern, and no "retainer" in sight. Instead Halward himself appears at the base of the stairs, and a discussion between father and son starts.
On several points the Inquisitor has a comment, even offering to leave, or might ask questions, until there's finally a
dialog wheel
with four choices that have an effect on the outcome of the quest and may change Dorian's
approval
. The first two (Don't leave it... / You didn't let him speak) is required for great approval and a debate of father and son, the second two (Let's get you out of here... / Are we done?) will leave them estranged and may result in no great approval at the end, depending on future dialog choices.

Bugs
There was a known issue that kept the quest from triggering if Dorian's approval rating became too high before discussing the fate of Felix. An essential dialog would be skipped, resulting in Mother Giselle never appearing in Skyhold's main hall with the letter. This bug was fixed in Patch 5.
If the quest is initiated but cannot activate the cutscene at Redcliffe Village's Gull and Lantern, enter the tavern with Dorian as the controlled character.
Another workaround is to save the game inside the Gull and Lantern, quit, and reload.

======================================================================
PAGE: Last Will and Testament
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Last_Will_and_Testament
======================================================================

Quest
Last Will and Testament
Location
Korcari Wilds
Start
Corpse of Missionary Rigby
End
Redcliffe Village
Next
Chasind Trail Signs
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Last Will and Testament
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
The Warden
will discover a note on the body of Missionary Rigby in the depths of the
Korcari Wilds
. His last request is that a lockbox hidden elsewhere be taken to
Jetta
in
Redcliffe Village
.
Walkthrough
As you're looking at the dead men hanged from the fallen tree, go left and after engaging some
darkspawn
you should see a fight between a pack of
wolves
and a few darkspawn.
You should now see a
corpse
of Missionary Rigby. As you pick up his
will
, unlocking
Codex entry: Rigby's Last Will and Testament
, you will be given the quest.
Head as far west as you can, then proceed northward and you should see some ruins. At the base, there is a chest containing
Codex entry: Signs of the Chasind
and a
Hidden Cache
(under the campfire) for the
Sealed Lockbox
. Note that the area will be guarded by darkspawn when first discovered.
Now you have two options:
Take the lockbox to give to Jetta, who is located in
Redcliffe chantry
.
Inform Jetta about the death of her husband and son, and keep the contents of the lockbox, which includes: 2 health poultices, an
emerald
, a
malachite
, a note, and the
Beloved Amulet
, a plot-item amulet that has no particular use.
The first part of this quest has to be completed before leaving the Korcari Wilds because after that the Korcari Wilds are no longer available.
Result
If you chose to take the lockbox, take it to Jetta in the chantry in Redcliffe. Upon giving the lockbox to her, she assumes the fate of her husband, and kindly thanks you.
Giving Jetta the lockbox grants you no money or XP, so keeping the contents is the most profitable choice. You can still inform her about her husband's and son's fate at the chantry, but you can't give her the Beloved Amulet.
Bugs
A portion of the intended rewards for this quest fail to be awarded. In particular, you get no experience for delivering the lockbox.

======================================================================
PAGE: Legacies and Legends

Quest
Legacies and Legends
Type
Main quest
Location
Dock Town
Previous
The Words of the Wolf
Next
To Act As One
Related
For the Love of Treviso
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Legacies and Legends
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. The
Inquisitor
wants to speak to
Rook
, and Rook agrees to meet them at the
Cobbled Swan
.
Acquisition
Acquired automatically after completing
The Words of the Wolf
.
Walkthrough
A request to return to the Cobbled Swan leads to learning more about Solas's relationship with the Inquisition.
Meet with the Inquisitor
Travel to the Cobbled Swan in
Minrathrous
.
Morrigan
has bought out the building, leaving the Inquisitor and Rook to discuss
Solas
privately.
The Inquisitor asks about Solas's actions during
Blood of Arlathan
's rescue of the
Dalish
, and wants to know Rook's estimation of his motives. When Rook asks, the Inquisitor discusses their history with him during the events of
Dragon Age: Inquisition
, and offers insight into how they felt when discovering who he was and his reasons behind his leaving.
The Inquisitor wonders if Solas wants someone to change his mind, considering the frequent clues he left behind in his trail. Rook asks about the Inquisitor's relationship with Solas, and when the Inquisitor expresses doubt, Rook can either encourage the Inquisitor to be willing to reunite with Solas, or recommend against renewing the friendship (or relationship, if romanced).
Both options on the left will discourage the Inquisitor from reuniting with Solas; both options on the right will encourage them to do so. Regardless of Rook's choice, the Inquisitor will warn Rook not to discount Solas's plans to escape. The Inquisitor concludes the conversation by either asking about the embarrassment of Elgar'nan in front of his cultists, or, if Rook has locked in a romance, about Rook's current relationship partner.
Results
The Inquisitor has likely already heard about the team rescuing the Dalish from Elgar'nan and wants to meet again to learn more about the fight against the gods.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Rook's choice in this quest regarding the Inquisitor's willingness to reunite with Solas will directly impact the events of
The Dread Wolf Rises
. If Rook encourages reunion, the Inquisitor will arrive atop the
Archon
's palace to help persuade Solas to stop. If Rook suggests the Inquisitor let Solas go, they will not appear, and Solas must be convinced (or fought) by Morrigan and Rook instead.
Codex entries
Notes from the Inquisition
(variant will depend on the Inquisitor's romance, if any)
Missives
Message from the Front: The Coming Storm

======================================================================
PAGE: Leliana's Past
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Leliana's_Past
======================================================================

Quest
Leliana's Past
Location
Party Camp
Start
Leliana
End
Marjolaine
Rewards
Marjolaine's Recurve
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Leliana's Past
is
Leliana
's
companion quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
. It is based on the incidents that happen during the
Leliana's Song
DLC
.
Walkthrough
Talk to Leliana after
Lothering
. You should be able to ask why she joined the
Chantry
. If you choose the correct options you should get a Codex update. This can only be done if her
approval
rating is above 25. Once her approval is around 30+, make sure to ask her about "minstrels and spies in Orlais". She will tell you about bards.
Note:
Her dialogue options regarding "minstrels and spies in Orlais" may not appear if you're in a romance with Leliana and her approval has been raised rapidly to a 71 or higher ("Adore") via gifts. This can still happen even in the player is not in a romance with her. Applying the version 1.02 (or newer) bug patch to the game may fix this problem, however it has been reported by many users that the bug still exists in current patch versions. You can also simply lower her approval rating to a 70 or below ("Warm" or "Care") and she will tell you about the spies. Her approval can be lowered via dialogue, actions, or through use of the developer
console
, but one of the easiest ways to lower her approval is by use of the
Feastday Gifts and Pranks
DLC (specifically, the ugly boots, but the rotten onion or charcoal work as well). Also you may choose to "end" the relationship with her to lower her approval and still harden her.
The next time you return to
Party Camp
, Leliana will tell you about
Marjolaine
.
The encounter with the assassins
This conversation should prompt a
random encounter
that starts her quest after you leave camp and travel on the
World map
with her in your party
. You will be confronted by assassins hired by Marjolaine. Only the leader must be killed to end the combat.
Go to
Denerim
and enter the
residence
pointed to by the assassin's contact (second door on the right, as you enter the
Denerim Market District
from the North-West corner; the door is marked as
Assassin's Contact for Leliana
when selected). Kill two
Qunari
guards before entering the foyer.
Confront Marjolaine.
If you back up Leliana's demand that Marjolaine stays out of her life, she will walk away, but vows that it's not over between them.
However, if you suggest to Leliana that she cannot allow Marjolaine's actions to go unpunished, you will enter into a fight with Marjolaine. Two guards and two travelling mages will join in the fight. They have the following profiles:
Guards:
Same guards as the two you fought when entering the house. Wear massive armor and fight with
Mauls
: Use the following:
War Cry
(with
Superiority
)
Rally
(possibly with
Motivate
)
Travelers:
Saarebas
, their staves seem to do fire damage. Can cast the following:
Spell Might
Stonefist
Heal
Heroic Defense
Heroic Aura
Dispel Magic
Rock Armor
Earthquake
Marjolaine:
Unarmed and unarmored, yet does 30+ damage per hit. Uses the following:
Evasion
Song of Courage
Dirty Fighting
Captivating Song
After dealing with Marjolaine, there's a chest in the bedroom that contains a longbow named
Marjolaine's Recurve
which is restricted to Leliana, as well as an assorted selection of other valuable Orlesian gear, such as pieces from the
Chevalier armor set
and grandmaster versions of various
runes
. Make sure to take all the loot out of the chest, as the building will become inaccessible once you have left.
Note:
If you wish to find a particular item in the chest, you should reload before first entering Denerim, as this is the time that these items will spawn and determine what you will find afterwards.(You can save just before the door of her house as it is a separate game cell and contents in chests and on Marjolaine and her guards reset. tested and confirmed - Xbox 360)
No matter how the meeting ends, there is no approval change from Leliana and party members (Alistair, Dog, Morrigan, Shale, Sten, Wynne and Zevran confirmed).
Hardening Leliana
Leliana starts out the game as a repentant ex-bard, duplicitous and deadly combination of assassin, entertainer, and courtier. Nevertheless, she greatly enjoyed the intrigue of her previous life as a bard, more so than her new life as a chantry lay-sister. This leaves her feeling internally conflicted. At the end of this quest, the words of her former mentor
Marjolaine
spark an internal crisis. The player can help Leliana sort through her feelings once back in camp.
Effects
Depending on the conversational options chosen, one of two results will occur:
Leliana's faith in the Maker and her new, more virtuous path will be reaffirmed and strengthened. The result is that she remains unhardened.
Leliana will decide that her new self was just an attempt to run away from her previous life after her mentor's betrayal, and that the real Leliana is a worldly bard who loves all of the danger, intrigue, and at times even the killing that goes along with that lifestyle. This results in her being hardened.
Whether Leliana's personality is hardened can
affect the game in a number of ways
.
Hardening dialogue walkthrough
The hardening dialogue begins when talking to her in camp after the quest is completed. She will respond with: "Oh, hello... is there something you wanted to talk about?"
There are many ways to get to the list of conversational choices that can result in her hardening. The key is the point in the conversation when she eventually confesses: "What we're doing... what we've done--hunted men down, killed them--part of me loves it. It invigorates me and this scares me. I... I feel myself slipping."
A choice of four responses follows, two of which will harden her. The ones that will harden her are:
"You're not slipping. This is who you are."
"Don't punish yourself for doing something you enjoy."
As long as either of these two lines are selected, Leliana will be hardened. Selecting either of the other two choices will result in her remaining unhardened. The rest of the conversation has no effect on her hardening.
Note that hardening must be done before any proposal to Queen Anora, otherwise it may be impossible to harden Leliana afterwards, even if her approval is at 100.
Journal
If Marjolaine dies
Marjolaine mistakenly believed that Leliana's reason for emerging from the cloister was to exact revenge on her. She made the first move, sending men to kill Leliana. Marjolaine could not be allowed to hound Leliana, so you killed her.
If you let Marjolaine go
Marjolaine mistakenly believed that Leliana's reason for emerging from the cloister was to exact revenge on her and made the first move, sending men to kill Leliana. Leliana told Marjolaine to leave and to never come back, but Marjolaine does not seem to be finished.
Result
If you kill Marjolaine you can join Leliana during the epilogue, telling her that you want to go with her, adventuring together or travel to Orlais.
If you let Marjolaine live, Leliana will go to Orlais herself to deal with her. Leliana will not let you come along.
Notable items
Forest Stream
Qunari Sword
- obtained from the Assassin Leader
Antivan Longbow
- obtained from the Assassin Leader
Marjolaine's Home
Marjolaine's Recurve
in a chest
Randomly found:
Heavy Maul
Chevalier's Armor
Chevalier's Gloves
Chevalier's Boots
Standard Bearer's Helm
Heavy Infantry Helmet
Executioner's Helm
Soldier's Helm
Rewards
250 XP for resolving the quest
Additional XP awarded for killing the guards, travelers, and Marjolaine
Notes
The rooms with travelers have
Pressure Plates
in the doorways, and triggering them will activate a
Fireball
-like explosion.
An easy way to win this fight is to have all your party members run back to the first room of the house as soon as the fight starts and close the door. Only one or two guards will be drawn out, unsupported. Dispatch him or them (taking care to stay within the room). If you can, use Mana Clash (with the door shut) to dispatch the two mages. Then enter the room and fight Marjolaine (cone of cold and the shield stun talents work great) - or you set all your party members to ranged mode and shoot at Marjolaine from the doorway. When her health hits 75%, she will run to you and activate Captivating Song, alone. Having at least one 2-hander with
Indomitable
will help tremendously with this battle. If not, simply move as many party members as possible out of the song's AoE and shoot at her until she dies, as she will never deactivate the song. After that, you might even be able to save your game before going back and finishing off the rest of the enemies.
When the Warden returns to camp to talk to Leliana, she speaks of Marjolaine in the present tense ("What if she thinks I...") even if Marjolaine was killed.

Literary Theft

-

Literary Theft
is a
companion quest
in the
Mark of the Assassin
DLC
for
Dragon Age II
if
Varric Tethras
is brought along as a companion.
Background
Hawke
finds a chapter of a book called
Hard in Hightown II
. It's an unauthorized sequel to one of Varric's stories. The quest mission is to find and destroy all copies of this work.
Acquisition
This quest appears in
Chateau Haine
after you enter the building. The First book will be on a bench beside the first guard you encounter.
Walkthrough
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age II
.
There are a total of three books you need to destroy.
1.
Chateau Haine Interior
- Beside the first guard you encouter in the actual Chateau.
2.
Chateau Haine Interior
- Right after the
Hard to Stomach
quest. On a table on the right, before the stairs.
3.
The Dungeons
- After your escape from your cell in the dungeon, head west. Once you defeat the guards at the end of the hallway the book will be on the table.
If you decide to fight through
Chateau Haine
then the first book will be right after the
Hard to Stomach
quest. The second book will be in the room where you find
Codex entry: A Library of Distinction
on a table.
Once the third book has been destroyed a treasure cache will be marked on the map. Its location is found in
The Retreat
after your fight with
Cahir
.
Rewards
The True Author's Token
Notes
Hawke will comment "This spelling is even worse than
Anders
's manifesto" even if Anders has not been recruited or even met.
A copy of the book can be seen on the table with the Thedas map in the teaser trailer "Fires Above" for
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.

Dragon Age: Origins
Loghain's Push
is a quest received from the
Chanter's Board
in
Denerim
. Through the magic of coincidence, some of
Loghain
's troop movements have been made public. Using this information, go "investigate" the status of Loghain's troops.
Walkthrough
After the quest has been accepted, a new location named
Civil War
will be added to the
World map
. Upon arriving, you will discover that a group of Loghain's troops are attempting to seize Bann Telmen's land. With the help of the Bann's men, you can fight and defeat them.
Return to any Chantry representative for the reward.
Rewards
2
175 XP
Notes
This quest is no longer available after
The Landsmeet
.
It is not required to save the Bannorn Knight or any of the Soldiers in order to complete the quest. Even if every soldier dies the reward is the same.  The journal simply states "You reported the loss of Loghain's troops to the Chantry Board" and makes no special mention of having saved all of them.
This quest's location can no longer be entered after updating the quest and leaving.

Traveler
achievement.

Flame
/
Frost
/

' rune from the 'Bannorn Knight' who stays in the area after the battle.

======================================================================
PAGE: Long Way Home
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Long_Way_Home
======================================================================

Quest
Long Way Home
Act
1
Start
Sundermount
End
Kirkwall Alienage
(
Lowtown
)
Next
Blackpowder Promise
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Long Way Home
is an Act 1
main quest
in
Dragon Age II
.
During this quest
Hawke
must travel to the top of
Sundermount
with
Merrill
in order to perform a ritual involving the amulet received from
Flemeth
during the Prologue.
Acquisition
This quest may be performed after gaining entry into
Kirkwall
, having been received from
Flemeth
during the Prologue.
Walkthrough
Travel to the Dalish camp at
Sundermount
, (don't forget to pick up the
Elfroot
to the south, before entering the camp), and speak with
Keeper
Marethari
. Hawke will automatically get
Codex entry: The Dalish Elves
. Marethari will send
Hawke
to meet her First,
Merrill
, who will accompany Hawke up the mountain to perform a ritual involving the amulet received from
Flemeth
. She acts as a guest party member for the duration of this quest. Although she cannot be directly controlled during this quest, she will contribute to battles as she sees fit.
Hawke won't get far before a small group of undead attacks and Hawke discovers that Merrill is a
mage
. After a brief conversation, continue up the mountain until reaching a cave entrance. Fight your way through spiders in the cave, emerging on a hillside.
Note:
Hawke will also encounter a
shadow warrior
accompanied by
shades
and
corpses
if Hawke goes upstairs before leaving the cave
Flemeth
emerging from the amulet
The way forward is blocked by a magical barrier.  When prompted, Merrill will remove it via
blood magic
.  On the other side of the barrier lies a spooky graveyard. The party will be attacked by an
arcane horror
accompanied by several undead and a
shadow warrior
. After dispatching the creatures, go to the altar where Merrill will perform the ritual.  Flemeth emerges from the amulet and explains (in her own weird way) that Hawke may well have saved her life, in return for saving Hawke's.
If
Fenris
or
Anders
are in Hawke's party, there is an option to hear some extra dialogue from one of them via the dialogue wheel, with no approval changes. If both Fenris and Anders are in your party, Fenris takes priority (when choosing Call on Companion/ "What do you think?").
Bethany
or
Carver
will have dialogue at the very end of the conversation. Flemeth then transforms into a dragon and flies away.
Return to the cave entrance and Hawke's party is automatically transported back to the Dalish camp. From there, Hawke's party is transported to the
Elven Alienage
in
Lowtown
where Merrill asks if she can expect a visit from Hawke in her new home.
Strategy
The hike itself is not too difficult, especially with Merrill acting as an invincible fifth party member.  However, on Nightmare difficulty all of the enemies (save for shades, which are optional encounters only) are simply immune to her Nature-element staff's basic attacks, making her little more than a useful distraction when she's not using her default spells of
Spirit Bolt
,
Stonefist
, and
Hex of Torment
.  Beyond this, all enemies encountered on Sundermount are highly vulnerable to electricity, with fire, frost, and spirit as useful elements in their own right.  The only enemies of significant threat are the Shadow Warriors.  They fight like "commander"-style enemies among the Elite-ranked templars and mercenaries of Kirkwall, only they are immune to nature damage, thus they can easily tank a full-party assault while casting a health-regenerating aura on nearby corpses, making it even more difficult to take them down as they hack away at your party.  There are three in total on Sundermount; two are optional, thus they can be left until the party is more powerful, but the mandatory one comes on the heels of a battle with an Arcane Horror.  These enemies, while Elite-ranked, have little to no protection and can fall quickly with a full party assault, especially since they're vulnerable to Merrill's nature element.  However, they can cast spirit-element area-of-effect spells and wipe out the entire party in less than a few seconds.  Full DPS all-out assaults will often eliminate the Arcane Horror before it can do too much damage of its own.  This approach may encourage the Arcane Horror to unleash its AoE attack at its feet against all melee companions, however, so either allow it to make a single cast and move each companion around the AoE, or take no more than 3 seconds to kill it, remaining ready to flee if it cannot be done in time (scatter each companion rather than moving them as a group).  Dual-wielding talents can deal the greatest damage for this purpose while leaving nearby allies unscathed, whereas a two-hander's techniques may end up harming the party more than the Arcane Horror, so they should stick to basic attacks and
Cleave
if available, if not simply focus on the corpses and draw threat, leaving the demon to the mages and rogues.
With the Arcane Horror out of the picture, the corpses aren't much more threat than in any other encounter on the hike.  The biggest concern are the Skeleton Archers, three Normal-ranked and one Critter-ranked, as they can attack from range while the corpses must shamble after party members, leaving them vulnerable to kiting and focused fire.  Send companions to engage them where they would be most effective (AoE spells on the two furthest from the shrine, dual-wielder to Backstab the Critter-rank, et cetera), then focus the full party on each corpse one at a time.  The Shadow Warrior will appear after 6-7 kills, ideally with no one nearby to register as the greatest threat, thus targeting Merrill.  Focus on eliminating the remaining corpses before committing the full might of your party to defeating him.  His grenades can deal a significant chunk of damage while inflicting knockback and reducing movement speed, therefore attack him from all sides so he cannot catch multiple characters in a single bomb (which he re-uses every 5-10 seconds).  If you retained enough healing potions in the hike up and still have more than two party members alive you can defeat him with attrition.
Friendship and rivalry
Interactions of companions before meeting the Dalish keeper at Sundermount**
Investigating "What did you call me?":
Aveline
"An elven slur for human."
Fenris
"It's the elven word for human." Will take priority over Carver's comment if both are present.
Carver
"Elven gibberish."
If more than one of these companions are present, Fenris' comment will take priority, followed by Aveline's.
Investigating "What are the Dalish":
Hawke
"I'm pretty sure I've seen elves around who weren't Dalish."
Varric
"Keep that in mind Hawke, Elves you see elsewhere? Figments of your imagination."
Fenris
"So you say. Frequently." Will take priority over Varric's comment if both are present.

removes the barrier:

Note:
pc
If Fenris is present during the conversation with Flemeth at the altar, Hawke has the option to let him speak, which provides some supplemental information about Flemeth.

Kirkwall
towards the end of the quest, the response to her question about a possible visit from Hawke will affect friendship/rivalry as follows:
choosing "I will."

+5

Either way,
Hawke
will still be able to visit Merrill at her new home in the
Kirkwall Alienage
(
Lowtown
) later. There are no friendship/rivalry changes for Merrill as she has not officially joined Hawke's party yet.
Result
Merrill
joins Hawke's party.
Regardless of whether or not Hawke told Merrill that she could expect a visit, the
companion quest
Welcome Home
becomes available.
The next time Hawke leaves Kirkwall, you will find yourself at the
Dead Man's Pass
and receive the main quest
Blackpowder Promise
.
You can talk to Arianni, Feynriel's mother, in the Elven Alienage in Lowtown and start the
Wayward Son
quest.
Rewards
After the conversation with Flemeth:
800 XP
8
Notes
If seeking the
Supplier
achievement, be sure to grab the
Silverite
in the caverns (next to the door in the firepit room just after the entrance), the
Elfroot
near the rock slide below the cavern entrance, and the
Deep Mushroom
in the Elven Graveyard.  If you are trying to recruit everyone at low levels it is best to avoid these items for now, as they are rich in experience but can be claimed at any time.
Inside the mountain cave, after killing the spiders and in a little room to the right are the class-specific Act 1
item set
gloves.
It may be worthwhile to continue past the Altar, to the top of
Sundermount
, while
Merrill
is following Hawke around as a "bonus" party member.  The
Arm of Adruil
awaits at the summit, but Hawke must defeat a
Shadow Warrior
and nine shades, as well as touch the magical barrier nearby to trigger the encounter, which incurs a knockback in the middle of a group of freshly-spawned shades.  On the way up the party must win a difficult fight against corpses, a
Revenant
, and a few shades spawned after the corpses are slain.  Even though Merrill's basic attacks deal no damage to the enemies save for the shades on Nightmare difficulty, having her as an invulnerable decoy should make the climb much easier.  It is still possible with only four party members, as are any encounters on the highest difficulty, but it may necessitate returning only after acquiring more powerful weapons, talents, and passives.  There is also a line of four Simple-ranked traps on the way from the altar to the top.  Before examining the Altar, it is possible to return to Kirkwall to add a rogue party member and still retain
Merrill
in a bonus capacity.
Note:
Be careful to head up the path before getting too close to altar as, once the encounter with the Arcane Horror is triggered, Merrill will not follow you up the mountain.  She stays in the altar area even after talking to
Flemeth
. (Fixed in 1.04)
The
Arcane Horror
guarding the Altar drops ≈6
and a
Ring of the Magister
when he dies.  As a guaranteed accessory, it is a worthwhile addition to the party's inventory.
Completing the Dalish ritual earns 8
.  Together with the loot from the Arcane Horror, this makes the quest one of the best early sources of income for Act 1.
Fenris
has a lot to say during this quest.  He trades barbs with the Dalish when they refer to him as a "city elf," chastises Merrill for using blood magic, and even has optional commentary on Flemeth (which in turn leads Flemeth to "see" and reveal a bit of Fenris' history and state of mind).
Isabela
wonders if Flemeth is a "friend of [Hawke's]," to which Flemeth notes she "says the word like it were cheap coin to be traded away."  Isabela retorts "You don't know me," and Flemeth responds "I know your type."
Anders
has commentary on Flemeth, but their exchange is superseded by Fenris' comments.
If Hawke uses the sarcastic dialogue after Flemeth's advice to them about the future ("Cheap advice from a dragon"),
Bethany
or
Carver
respond that the party is going to regret bringing her to the Free Marches.  Flemeth wistfully comments that she knows regret too well, and warns them not "to hold it so close that it poisons your soul."
The barrier at the top of the mountain causes knockback as the enemies spawn, however the knockback can be successfully prevented with the right items or abilities.  It should only be a minor deterrent at best, however.
Trivia
The verbal component of the ritual is the first stanza of
"In Uthenera"

Walkthrough
After defeating

during
Rescue the Queen
,
the Warden
can access the northern part of
his dungeon
.
Irminric
, a
Templar
in a state of
lyrium
withdrawal, can be found in one of the cells. The Warden learns that Irminric was one of the templars sent after
Jowan
to hunt him down, as per the
Magi Origin
story. He tells the Warden that he cornered Jowan near
Redcliffe
, but Teyrn
Loghain

dungeon
. He begs the Warden to give his

to his sister,

, in the

in Denerim.

Giving Alfstanna the ring will win her family's support in the coming
Landsmeet
. You also receive experience, gold, and the magical amulet
Spirit Ward
. Taunting Alfstanna will cost you her vote at the Landsmeet.
2
250 XP
Spirit Ward
Notes
If the Warden speaks to Irminric and learns his name, the templars on duty outside the chantry in the
Denerim Market District
can be asked about him. The templars believe Irminric to have died fighting
maleficarum
over a month earlier, and will assume the Warden is irrational with grief if they insist that Irminric is still alive.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lost in the Castle
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
