======================================================================

Quest
Red Jenny's Stashes
Location
Winter Palace
Start
Sera
Related
Wicked Eyes and Wicked Hearts
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Red Jenny's Stashes
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Sera
's contacts have tipped her off about the locations of various stashes, hidden around the palace by nobles for other nobles. They're ripe for the picking.
Acquisition
If you have
Sera
in your party during
Wicked Eyes and Wicked Hearts
, you can ask her in the ballroom if her contacts have any information. She will tell you that there are several stashes of items hidden around the
Winter Palace
.
Walkthrough
Sera wants you to find 3 hidden stashes around the Winter Palace.  It is required to empty the stashes for completion of the side quest. For a slight approval increase, make sure to talk to Sera after looting all three stashes.
Guest Wing Garden
In the room with cobwebs within the Guest Garden (near where you eavesdrop on the nobles).
Servant's Quarters' Garden
In the garden area of the Servant's Quarters, in the South-Western most corner. From the fountain go south, take the west path, follow it to the end and then go south to find the stash next to some
Embrium
.
Lower Garden
Go through Lower Garden Door, through the building and down to the garden. The stash is behind the vine fence opposite from the three Council members.
This is a Tier 3 chest, and may contain Tier 3 schematics.
Rewards
Sera Approves
for every stash opened.
Random loot.
Sera Slightly Approves
when asking about the stashes after finding them all.
Gallery
Lower Garden Red Jenny Stash is behind the lattice behind the bench

======================================================================
PAGE: Repentance
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Repentance
======================================================================

Quest
Repentance
Act
2
Location
Harimann Estate
(
Hightown
)
Quest Giver
Sebastian
(
Kirkwall Chantry
)
End
Sebastian
(
Kirkwall Chantry
)
Rewards
11
Previous
Duty
Next
Faith
Appearances
Dragon Age II
-
The Exiled Prince
Repentance
is an Act 2
companion quest
for
Sebastian
in
Dragon Age II
. It requires
The Exiled Prince
downloadable content
.
Acquisition
You must have access to the
The Exiled Prince
downloadable content.
Complete
Duty
Note that this quest is not available until Act 2, even if
Duty
is completed during Act 1.
Walkthrough
The Chantry
Speak to
Sebastian
in the
Chantry
and agree to help. (There will be a funny exchange with
Varric
after Hawke's first dialogue choice if he's in the party).
Hightown
Travel to Hightown and enter the
Harimann Estate
. Sebastian will be locked into the party from this point forward. There are three chests with Complex-ranked locks in this quest (30 Cunning to open, 150 XP each).
There are enemies who employ spirit damage, thus a
Rune of Spirit Warding
in companion armor will reduce most of it.  The design is available from
Mage Goods
in the Gallows as soon as Act 2 begins.  All enemies have some form of elemental
immunity
on Nightmare difficulty, but the nature immunity of Undead and Revenants is counterbalanced by a weakness to spirit damage and vice versa in the case of Shades, Desire Demons, and Arcane Horrors.  All demons and undead are weak to electricity, save for Rage Demons, who suffer normal damage.  They are immune to fire and weak to frost.  Additionally, Revenants are immune to frost, but it works well against every other enemy encountered here, so an Elemental mage is highly recommended in spite of these resistances.
Harimann Estate
The journey through the mansion will pass without incident aside from a few cutscenes. The first is in the wine cellar, and the second in the parlor.  Collect the
Mail Undertunic
from the chest in the parlor. In the hallway to the east, the quest continues to the south, but take a detour through the door upstairs to a third, amusing cutscene and a Complex-ranked chest. If
Isabela
or Varric is in the party, they will have some amusing comments.  Merrill also has a comment.
The waypoint marker leads to another cellar to the south.  After a cutscene with the Harimanns, and a battle with shades and a Desire Demon will follow.  Defeat the Desire Demon first to keep her from buffing the shades, then focus on stunning them before taking them down one or two at a time.  The
Starkhaven Longbow (gift)
can be picked up off a nearby dead Flint Company mercenary. Picking it up activates the companion quest
The Starkhaven Longbow
, which can be completed upon returning to the Chantry.
Enter the Underground Passage to continue the quest.
Underground Passage
The layout of these ruins is similar to the "mining caves" from
Blackpowder Promise
,
Wayward Son
, and similar areas, only with the more ominous textures of the caves in
Forbidden Knowledge
.  Thus, the same chokepoints will corral the enemies.  In the tighter chokepoints they will not be able to get ahead of each other to even attack, making them easier to defeat.
The first battle features a rage demon and numerous shades.  For some ease, head downstairs to draw a few of them plus the rage demon, then back upstairs to take them out as they come.  Stun the rage demon and defeat it before it can "backstab" too much.  The remaining shades will spawn down below, so engage them when you are ready.
The next fight is with a
Revenant
and a selection of corpses.  Pull the Revenant into the previous chamber without aggro'ing the corpses for an easier encounter, then take them out, prioritizing the archers when they appear.
Down some steps to the north, two more rage demons attack at once.  Retreat back upstairs if necessary and try to keep them from fighting together.  Beyond them is a Revenant and an
Arcane Horror
, but they do not have to be fought together.  Lure the Revenant to the previous chamber first and defeat it, then stun and rush the Arcane Horror before it can unleash a single AoE.  Alternatively, rush the revenant with everyone and ignore the corpses, defeat it, then quickly turn attention to the Arcane Horror before it can fire a single spell.  After the battle, go up the stairs to the west to find the
Codex entry: The Demon's Gift
and a Complex-ranked chest.  Before entering the final room, there is roughly 4
(exact amount varies with level) in some rubble in the corner.
At the end of the ruins is
Allure
, a Desire Demon, and Lady
Harimann
, who is an Elite-ranked blood mage.
Allure says that Sebastian and Hawke both possess the desire for power.
"Die, fiend!":
Sebastian: friendship (
+10
)
Anders: friendship (
+5
)
Aveline: friendship (
+5
)
Otherwise:
"I do like power":
Fenris: rivalry (
+5
)
Anders: rivalry (
+10
)
No matter what you say, Allure says that Sebastian swore to put aside worldly goods and possessions, but they couldn’t stop him from wanting them.
“That’s a good point”:
Sebastian: friendship (
+15
)
Anders: friendship (
+5
)
Aveline: rivalry (
+5
)
Varric: friendship (
+5
)
"Don't listen to her ..."
Sebastian: rivalry (
+15
)
Anders: friendship (
+5
)
Aveline: rivalry (
+5
)
Merrill: friendship (
+5
)
Varric: friendship (
+5
)
"Die, fiend!"
Sebastian: friendship (
+10
)
Anders: friendship (
+10
)
Aveline: friendship (
+5
)
Regardless of dialogue choices, they must be defeated, along with four shades.  One method is to lure them back to the previous room and single-file them to be killed one at a time, or a little further so Lady Harimann and/or Allure give up their pursuit.  Alternatively, Lady Harimann is easy enough to kill with a quick stun and four-on-one assault, and Allure doesn't have the durability of other "commander" style enemies, so retreat may not even be necessary.  Simply kill Lady Harimann and the rest of the fight should fall into place.
Return to the estate via the nearby exit.
Return to Harimann Estate
Flora Harimann will speak to Sebastian, apologizing profusely and swears to make amends for everything her family did, including helping Sebastian retake Starkhaven.
Diplomatic:
Sebastian: friendship (
+5
)
Sarcastic/Aggressive:
Sebastian: rivalry (
+5
)
Return to Chantry
Return to the
Chantry
and speak to Sebastian to complete the quest.
"That's a good question"/"What do you want to do?"/"Listen to your heart"
Sebastian: friendship (
+10
)
"You must take back your land." (Eliminates Flirt option.)
Sebastian: rivalry (
+10
)
Flirt option gives
Sebastian: friendship (
+10
)
Result
Sebastian joins as a permanent companion.
If the item was picked up, the companion quest
The Starkhaven Longbow
will activate.
Rewards
Mail Undertunic
- Sebastian's armor upgrade (+2% Critical Chance)
Fade's Fury
- loot Desire Demon
Allure's Crook
- loot Allure's corpse
11
from Sebastian
1000 XP(quest completion)
Notes
Due to game auto-assigning attribute points until a character is officially recruited, Sebastian's Cunning will likely not be high enough to open the locked chests within the house or ruins. The building remains open throughout Act 2 however, and it is possible to return later to empty them.  There's no further dialogue with the surviving Harimanns, so if you were able to loot everything there's no need to return later, be it day or night.
If you have
The Black Emporium (DLC)
installed, you may use a
Maker's Sigh
potion before doing the quest.
The
Band of Stolen Shadows
may be sufficient to raise Sebastian's Cunning to an effective score of 30.
If Varric's Cunning is already high enough, just return to the estate with him instead of Sebastian.
You can also accept the quest to officially recruit Sebastian but delay entering the Harimann Estate until your level is about 15-16.  This will provide Sebastian enough attribute points to reach the Cunning threshold of 30 (There are no Master-ranked chests in the estate).
Certain outcomes of
Night Terrors
will be reflected in the dialogue with Allure.  If you ask it about a deal ("I do like power..."), and if Isabela betrayed you, it will say, "Just make sure whatever it is doesn't disappear this time.  Bloody demons with their promises and their stupid imaginary boats."  If you select "I'd never betray my friends," it will respond, "How loyal were your friends to you?  Everyone has a price.  Everyone wants something."  Oddly enough, even if you have yet to enter the Fade, Allure will respond in the same way.  This implies that this quest was originally written with the assumption that Night Terrors would have already been completed (or the demon could be referring to
Bartrand
, but calling him a friend would be generous, to say the least).
Bugs
pc
(1.04) Before and after the conversation with Allure and Lady Harimann, the screen goes black and the "Loading..." message pops up. To proceed with the quest you may simply press Esc as soon as you see the message, however this causes you to miss some dialogue.
pc
(1.04) A full workaround has been discovered however that allows you to not miss anything (see also
Faith
in Act 3 for how this bug is "game-breaking" to that quest).  You must enter your Dragon Age II program folder and temporarily delete the patch folder under GameFolder\Addins\da2_prc_drk\module as that's where the interference lies.  Then, after completing Repentance, restore the patch folder. The source of this information is at
Freezing issue in The Exiled Prince DLC
Gallery
Lady Johane Harimann
Lady Johane Harimann before Allure
Lord Hariman, the "Prude"
Flora Harimann, drunk in the cellar

======================================================================
PAGE: Rescue the Queen
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Rescue_the_Queen
======================================================================

Quest
Rescue the Queen
Location
Denerim
Quest Giver
Arl Eamon Guerrin
,
Arl Eamon's Estate
,
Denerim
End
Arl Eamon Guerrin
,
Arl Eamon's Estate
,
Denerim
Next
Unrest in the Alienage
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Rescue the Queen
is the mainline quest to free
Anora
,
King Cailan's
wife, from
Arl Howe
at the
Arl of Denerim's Estate
. After completing
Arl of Redcliffe
and gathering the other allies,
the Warden
travels to
Denerim
with
Arl Eamon Guerrin
. After arriving, Arl Eamon arranges for Anora's handmaid,
Erlina
, to meet with the Warden. Erlina tells the party that the queen is being held prisoner by Arl Howe  at the Arl of Denerim's Estate.
Walkthrough
See also:
Arl of Denerim's Estate
General Outline
Arl of Denerim's Estate
Travel to the Arl of Denerim's Estate
Meet with Erlina, who helps you sneak in
Explore the main level and find your way to the dungeon
Dungeon
Confront Arl Howe
Converse with other prisoners
Main level reprise
Fight interior guards
Rescue Anora
Escape or surrender
Exterior
Map of Arl of Denerim's Estate – Exterior
Travel to Arl of Denerim's Estate from the City Map and meet with Erlina. She will explain that, due to peasant unrest, only the back entrance is open. If
Shale
is in the party, Erlina will insist you leave it behind, since a golem could not possibly pass for one of Howe's soldiers. Sneak past the crowds at the main entrance and follow the path to the back of the estate. If the party is spotted, fight the four or five guards who appear as they will not sound the alarm. One way to avoid being spotted is to leave the entire party before entering the balcony area and then sending a stealthed character forward to pick up all of the loose items. Once the stealth character has reached the door with the two guards, Erlina and the rest of the party will warp forward, skipping the balcony area completely.
When the party meets up with Erlina again, she will warn the Warden that the door guards will recognize your party as impostors, so let her distract them. Take a few steps away from the door to trigger the cutscene and then advance towards the door to enter the main level. Should the Warden choose to fight the guards outside, the disguises will no longer work and the party will have to fight their way through the estate. If the Warden chooses the disguise route, the party's equipment will be re-equipped the first time combat occurs.
Note:
pc
The disguises still work until the party enters the dungeon, even if the exterior guards were killed.
Note:
If a party member gets a little too close, the guards may activate (red circles) but not attack. If this happens, Erlina will not go to distract them. As long as they are not attacked, the party can run far enough away and combat will end. Return and walk toward the back wall, away from Erlina and further from the guards, and the scene of her distracting them should trigger at that point. If the party does fight them, then Erlina only says "So much for subtlety..." in a resigned tone.
Interior
Map of Arl of Denerim's Estate – Interior
Once inside, search the western part of the main level, thieving and looting as you might anywhere else. Eventually, you will end up at the Queen's room, which has been magically sealed, forcing you to find the mage responsible and thus—most likely—the Arl himself.
You can continue to loot the remaining rooms with impunity except for one belonging to
Vaughan Kendells
. You can blow your cover now or save that room for later. Either way, trouble is on its way. If you do enter, you will interrupt a maid and a guard enjoying an intimate moment. He recognizes that you are not one of Howe's guards and sounds the alarm. Although he is easy to kill, the remaining guards will turn hostile.
In the last hallway before reaching the dungeon you will find a locked door (very hard difficulty). Behind it lies piles of treasure, including
Howe's Shield
,
Medium Gold Bar
, and a lot of money. In the last room before the dungeon, loot a
Chest of Documents
for the Grey Warden
papers
, required for gaining access to the
Grey Warden Vault
later in the game.
Dungeon
See also:
Arl of Denerim's Estate - Dungeon
Map of Arl of Denerim's Estate – Dungeon
As you arrive in the dungeon, one of the prisoners takes advantage of the distraction of your entrance to overpower his guard.
Riordan
is a
Grey Warden
from
Orlais
who has a brief conversation with you before heading out. Give him the Grey Warden documents you took from the chest in the previous room.
Your disguises will no longer work, so get ready to start fighting. Eventually, you will find your way to Arl Howe, who offers very little conversation before attacking; if playing the
Human Noble Origin
, there will be slightly more dialogue available than for the other origins.
Morrigan, Zevran (
+7
) if a Human Noble Warden successfully refutes Howe's taunts with a successful Intimidate/persuade dialogue. His
corpse
has
Biteback Axe
and his
key
, allowing you to free the Queen as well as taking a shortcut back to the main level (via the stairs just outside the room with him or through the metal bar gate to the right).
See also:
Rendon Howe (strategy)
Before you leave (or on your way to Howe), you also have an opportunity to chat with (and perhaps free) some of the other prisoners. Freeing
Oswyn
and
Irminric
and speaking to Bann
Sighard
and Bann
Alfstanna
at the
Gnawed Noble Tavern
will grant you their support in
The Landsmeet
. Freeing
Rexel
and sparing him will grant a reward when the
Missing in Action
quest is completed at the
Chanter's Board
. Freeing
Soris
will net
Zevran approves (
+4
) to
Zevran approves (
+7
) and he can later be found in
Cyrion's Home
. Releasing Soris will also make
Shianni
somewhat less hostile to the non-City Elf Warden when she's spoken to during the
Unrest in the Alienage
quest. Furthermore, Soris will be furious and insulting towards a City Elf Warden if they accepted Vaughan's offer during the Origin story.
Take special note of
Vaughan
, who can be convinced to give you a
key
to a
lockbox
that contains 40
in exchange for his release. Obviously he won't be there if you killed him during the
City Elf Origin
; however, if you didn't, then the ensuing dialogue will be quite different and the Warden also has the option of demanding him to join the Chantry in order to repent for his sins in exchange for his freedom. His lockbox is located in the room with the guard that sounds the alarm when the warden interrupts the guard and the maid. Vaughan can also be spared in exchange for his vote at
the Landsmeet
. With high
persuasion
, it is possible to convince him to give you the key before unlocking the cell door, and then you can free him, leave, or kill him. If you release him from the cell then
Zevran disapproves (
-5
) and
Morrigan approves (
+2
). Note that he sometimes only snores when trying to start the conversation with him, making it impossible to start the conversation. It has been reported that this bug can be avoided by selecting the locked cell door to initiate dialogue, rather than Vaughan himself.
Reprise: Interior
There are two exits back to the main level--the southern stairs just outside the room with Howe and the northern one back up to his bedroom. Up the southern stairs, there is a locked door, requiring
Arl Howe's Key
to unlock. This opens into the corridor extending south from the main entry room (the Queen's room is on the opposite side). All the guards in adjoining rooms are alerted (as indicated by red hostile dots on mini-map). So, you may want to turn around and use the other path. The northern stairs can be reached easier now by unlocking the metal bar gate just right of the room with Howe. It is a longer but safer walk on the main level.
If the Warden only engaged hostile Howe guards in the dungeon, the return path to the Queen's room is free of any guards (no entry room guard and no patrolling one between the library and the chantry). The mini-map still shows red hostile dots in southern rooms, but you can avoid them and not actively look for combat. If you have
Vaughan's Key
, you can enter his room and open his
lockbox
, as long as you don't interrupt the guard and the maid, by clicking on them.
If you decide to fight, as soon as you open one door in the southern corridor, all others will open simultaneously and the guards and mabari will come out swinging.
Area of Effect
spells,
traps
, and
bombs
are very helpful in controlling the masses and choke points. Regardless of the path, return to the Queen's room, where you will find her disguised for travel back to her estate.
Note:
If the player wishes to explore the various options that can follow on from this point (choosing to be
Captured!
, or fighting
Ser Cauthrien
), then after finding Anora is the best time to make a named save the player can come back to.
Note that these guards are likely the ones that you would have otherwise fought and killed on your way to completing this quest if you had chosen not to wear the disguises.
However, if you dutifully wore the disguises, did not fight any of the estate's guards except those in the dungeon level, and bypassed the estate's guards on your way back, the party will receive 1250 extra XP upon speaking with Anora, as a reward for completing the rescue without unnecessary bloodshed. This exceeds the amount you'd get from murdering all 39 counted guards with 28 XP each, thus giving you a total of 1092 XP.
Note:
pc
The game script actually checks for the deaths of entire guard squads (the guards in the hallway, break room, the guards and dogs in the mabari kennel, the guards in the armory, and the guards in the large dining room, and the single guard with the servant and Vaughan's chest), rather than individual guards.
After shedding the disguises upon coming up from the dungeon, a player could carefully pull and kill all but 6 guards (leaving 1 guard in each squad alive) and still get the extra XP for "peacefully" rescuing the queen, though the small amount of additional XP gained this way is usually not worth the hassle. Alternatively, all of these guards can be killed with no repercussions after talking with Anora, although only one can be fought if you surrender to Cauthrien.
Note:
If you have a rogue that can stealth in your party, then it is possible not to fight at all when you use the southern stairs. Take control of your rogue leaving the rest of the party on top of the stairs. Stealth and open the door which will begin combat, however, you will not be detected. Now move through the right door into the main entry room and down to the Queen's room under stealth. The rest of your party will be there once you begin the cutscene and combat will be avoided.
After eliminating Howe's guards, the exit is blocked by Ser Cauthrien, who offers you the choice to surrender or die.
Note:
If you tell Ser Cauthrien that Anora is with you, Anora will claim that you kidnapped her.
Choosing to surrender
You can decide to surrender to Cauthrien, which causes the Warden (and
Alistair
, if present) to be locked up in the jail at
Fort Drakon
and activates the quest
Captured!
(see the main article for details).
Not all companions think surrender is a pragmatic choice:
Sten
will react with
(
-15
) approval and
Oghren
with
(
-5
) approval. However you will gain
(
+4
) from
Leliana
or Zevran if they are in your party. Cauthrien releases your party members and takes only you (and Alistair if present) to Fort Drakon, admitting that she did not expect things to end peacefully.
Choosing to fight
See also:
Ser Cauthrien (strategy)
Cauthrien's team presents one of the toughest challenges in the game. Winning is rewarded with
The Summer Sword
and the freedom to walk out. If the party loses, the Warden (and potentially Alistair) will be captured. Either way, Anora will escape during the chaos and retreat to Arl Eamon's estate.
Best XP and Loot Scenario
If you surrender to Ser Cauthrien, you miss the XP and loot from her and her guards. If you fight and win, you miss doing the entire "
Captured!
" part of the quest. The option that gives the most loot and XP is to fight and kill Cauthrien and her party except for one guard. Loot the bodies, then proceed to suicide using AOE spells (if playing a difficulty that allows friendly fire) or unequipping all weapons and armor for your characters, leaving them vulnerable enough so that you may be captured by the single remaining guard. This way you get the XP and loot for both fighting and surrendering. If you used the disguises to sneak through the estate, you may wish to kill and loot those guards as well before allowing yourself to be captured.
Furthermore, you will meet Ser Cauthrien again later and get yet another chance to kill her and a different set of guards, earning a second Summer Sword. This is presumably because Ser Cauthrien will be revived after the first battle, just like you would if you fell in battle but one of your companions wins the day.
"Just the Summer Sword" Scenario
pc
If you want Cauthrien's Summer Sword and its codex entry, but do not want to surrender, do not want to be struck down by Cauthrien's soldiers, and do want to keep Ser Cauthrien alive for story purposes, there is another option if you brought a rogue with combat stealth. You need to leave at least one guard alive — the game doesn't count Cauthrien as dead for story purposes until all the soldiers in her party are dead.
When the fight begins, pause, withdraw into the queen's room, and close the door once Cauthrien follows you inside.  If done properly, some guards (most of the archers) won't follow you, and they will remain standing in the big chamber.  Strike down Cauthrien and take her Summer Sword while in the queen's room.  Enter stealth mode with the rogue and withdraw the rest of the party back toward the Arl's bedroom, far away from the main chamber, and have them hold position.  Now, walk the stealthed rogue past the archers and to the estate's exit.  If done correctly, the archers lose aggro after both the rogue and the party are far enough away from them.  You'll exit combat mode, and can then exit the Arl's estate.  Report back to Eamon to continue the quest line.
You eventually meet Ser Cauthrien again in front of the Landsmeet Chamber, where you can persuade her to stand down (leaving her alive for story or roleplay purposes), or kill her for good and get another Summer Sword.
You can accomplish a similar feat without a rogue by moving your party past the main entrance after killing Cauthrien and down the south hall. Once the troops guarding the main entrance have lost aggro, you can then exit the Arl's estate through the servants' entrance.
Result
Queen Anora is rescued (no matter how you played it) and can be found at
Arl of Redcliffe's Estate
.
Elven Alienage
is unlocked;
Unrest in the Alienage
is activated.
Rewards
1250 XP if no alarm sounded on the main floor of the estate and no guards and mabari were killed on that level.
Biteback Axe
, source:
Arl Howe
Optionals:
Grey Warden Documents
, source:
Chest of Documents
in Howe's bedroom
Howe's Shield
, source:
Chest
in Treasure Vault
Medium Gold Bar
, source:
Treasure Pile
.
The Summer Sword
, source:
Ser Cauthrien
40
(or 80
on
ps3
(not on the downloadable version)) in
Vaughan's Lockbox
(need his
key
)
Notes
In most areas of the estate, if you find the guards too tough to handle, retreat to another room. Only some of the guards will follow.
If you choose to kill the guards outside the servants' entrance, once inside, Erlina will still offer to fit you up with disguises. However, you will still have to fight the interior guards.
pc
The disguises still work until you enter the dungeon, even if the exterior guards were killed.
If you alert the interior guards you will not have to enter dialogue with the dungeon guards; they will be positioned further into the dungeon (making the first fight in the dungeon easier).
If you return to the dining hall after rescuing Anora (subsequently fighting and killing Ser Cauthrien and her guards) you will find a group of off duty guards that you can kill for extra XP.
Trivia
If you entered the sequence in
Morrigan
's Romance where she gives you her
ring
she mentions she can track you down with it if you're captured by Cauthrien.
If you have
Wynne
in your party, during the run for the servant's entrance if you step into the side courtyard at the back containing a large fountain, she comments that the fountain was built by Arl Eamon's father, "and after that
all
the nobles had to have one."
Alistair
: "Join the Grey Wardens! See the sights from the floors of the best prisons in the land! It's not much of a recruitment slogan, is it?" A possible reference to an old US Navy recruitment slogan, "Join the Navy! See the world."
The Grey Warden's dialogue option to disguised Queen Anora, "Aren't you a little short to be a guard?" is an allusion to
Star Wars
. Humorously, Anora's response to a dwarven Warden will be, "Is that supposed to be ironic?"
The following line may be a reference to Prince Humperdinck and Westley's encounter in the Fire Swamp, from the film and novel
The Princess Bride.
Ser Cauthrien: "Surrender and you may be shown mercy."
Warden: "Death first!"
Bugs
Attempted dialogue with Vaughan can lead to hearing him snore in his cell, and the inability to initiate dialogue. It's been reported that this is can be avoided by targeting and selecting the locked cell door to initiate conversation with him, rather than Vaughan himself.
If the Warden is not of the City Elf origin, it is possible that Soris will simply repeat "Oh, Maker..." and will not respond to the Warden unlocking his cell door. It has been reported that this bug can be fixed by addressing Vaughan and then returning to Soris afterwards, where dialogue should now be available.
Morrigan can sometimes lose her upgraded armor when the Guard disguise is automatically removed; instead of her
Robes of Possession
, she ends up with
Morrigan's Robes
. To work around this, equip Morrigan with any other armor before beginning the quest or choose not to disguise yourself.
Alistair can sometimes lose his upgraded armor when the Guard disguise is automatically removed; instead of his
Blood Dragon Armor Set
, he ends up without any armor except for his helm.  To work around this, equip Alistair without any armor before beginning the quest or choose not to disguise yourself.
Another possible bug is to end up with the "disguised" equipment permanently, including the battle with Howe and beyond. This bug can even extend itself to Fort Drakon, rendering your actual play-run useless (disguised equipment cannot be removed).
(present in 1.04 patch, known as the 'Armor Disguise Bug' in Bioware's official forums. Recommended workarounds are the "runscript zz_economizer" console command, and blowing your cover by entering the room with the guard and the maid sharing an intimate moment.)
If the Warden has
Ranger
abilities and has summoned an animal, when the Warden first speaks to Anora at the magically sealed door, the summoned animal can sometimes be placed in the locked room with Anora. Anora can be interacted with, but she will only mention that the Warden must find a way to unseal the door. Unfortunately, since the summoned animal is now trapped in the room with Anora, it can now no longer follow the party and must be banished. The room itself contains nothing of value.
If you kill Howe and loot the key of his body before the cutscene of his death, the Quest Marker will point to his body, although you already got the key.

======================================================================
PAGE: Restocking the Guild
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests

Quest
Restocking the Guild
Location
Varies
Quest Giver
The Blackstone Irregulars
End
The Blackstone Irregulars
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Restocking the Guild
is a quest received from
The Blackstone Irregulars
Quest Board
in Denerim. The Irregulars have suffered as of late, and they require some supplies.
Walkthrough
Bring 20
Health Poultices
to any Blackstone representative.
Note:
Lesser Health Poultices
will serve just as well, making this quest much less costly to complete.
Warning! The next time you talk to the Blackstone Liaison, if you have at least 20 total health poultices, he will take them from you and give you the reward without giving you a chance to argue, etc. So, be careful not to talk to him until you have enough that you can spare 20.
Rewards
3
98
0
175 XP
Notes
As is the case with lyrium potions in
A Gift of Silence/Justice Must Be Served
, the quest will accept any 20 health poultices, starting with the least potent in the player's inventory.  While the wording of the quest may suggest otherwise, it does not specifically require
Health Poultices
, thus it is best to have on hand (or brew via
Herbalism
) at least 20
Lesser Health Poultices
prior to handing in the quest, as this will save more valuable recovery items and/or the materials required to craft them (plus the cost in gold to purchase them, if necessary).

Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Revelations
is a
companion quest
for
Blackwall
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Blackwall
has left the
Inquisition
. It appears to have something to do with the impending execution of a man called Mornay, one of the soldiers responsible for the Callier massacre.
Acquisition
This quest is initiated after talking to Blackwall in
Skyhold
following the events of
Here Lies the Abyss
and
Wicked Eyes and Wicked Hearts
as soon as you have a high approval with him. Blackwall invites
the Inquisitor
for a drink at the
Herald's Rest
tavern where he shares an anecdote from his youth. The next time the Inquisitor approaches the stables, they will find a
letter
attached to the griffon Blackwall carved (using the Search function).
Note:
If the Inquisitor was in a relationship with Blackwall the aforementioned note will be replaced with the following
letter
instead.
An Inquisition messenger then approaches and informs the Inquisitor that Blackwall has disappeared and hands over a
note
missing from the reports, which Blackwall surreptitiously removed from
Leliana
's files.
Note:
If pursuing a romance, the player must have completed Blackwall's
romance quest
Explanations
prior in order for this quest to become available.
Walkthrough
Travel to
Val Royeaux
next. The execution is taking place on the west side of the
Summer Bazaar
. Just as Mornay is about to be hanged, Blackwall interrupts the execution, and informs the crowd that he was responsible for the murdering of Callier family. His real name is in fact Thom Rainier, a former captain in
Celene I's
army and Mornay's former commanding officer.
Note:
Bringing
Cole
and
Varric
will lead to further interesting commentary.
After this the Inquisitor can access the
Val Royeaux Prison
in order to speak to Rainier and learn more details of his past. Rainier is in the farthest cell on the left. Afterwards
Cullen
arrives to discuss the situation. Cullen hands the Inquisitor a report of Rainier, saying that, if needed, the Inquisition could use its resources to get Rainier into their custody. The Inquisitor can then choose to leave Rainier in the prison or get him out to judge him at
Skyhold
instead.
If the Inquisitor wants to judge Rainier in Skyhold, two potential
war table
operations become available:
Thom Rainier's Fate
and
Rainier's Release: An Alternative
.  Completing either operation progresses this quest to the judgment, see
notes
for the differences in results between the two choices.
See
Sit in Judgment of Captain Thom Rainier
for specifics.
Rewards
1,934 XP
Influence 400
Power 3
Results
If the Inquisitor chooses to free Blackwall to atone for his past, or to send him to the Grey Wardens after the defeat of Corypheus, Blackwall
Approves
.
If a female Inquisitor has romanced Blackwall up to this point, he will approach her throne and ask what is to become of them. The Inquisitor has four choices, two of which will continue the relationship, and the other two which will end it.
Blackwall
Greatly Disapproves
of the Inquisitor keeping him in servitude to "continue the lie", which will also be reflected in later
dialogue
choices. A female Inquisitor who previously romanced Blackwall won't have the option to continue the romance if this option is selected.
Decisions made in this quest have consequences for the
Trespasser epilogue
.

Val Royeaux Prison

he

Trivia
Even if pardoned to live on as Thom Rainier, all of the in-game interfaces (party picker, inventory and so on) will continue to refer to him as Blackwall.
Bugs
After completing
one
of the
two
war table missions to make Rainier available for judgment, performing the action to 'Sit' on the Inquisitor's throne and begin the judgment may instead lead to a very long load screen, followed by the game suddenly returning to the audience chamber and several relevant actors (namely Rainier, Josephine, and a dozen Inquisition soldiers) spawning close to the throne.  The Inquisitor is unable to interact with the throne, Josephine, or Rainier.  Reloading a save from prior to selecting the throne will usually solve this problem and allow the judgment to be carried out as intended.

Scraping the Barrel
is a quest received from
The Blackstone Irregulars
Quest board
in Lothering or
Redcliffe
. After recent casualties, the Irregulars need to recruit fresh blood. Seek out three prospective recruits and inform them of their
conscription
.

Patter Gritch
is found in the
Lothering's Chantry
, just outside the door to the
Revered Mother
's office.

is found in a
house
in

(across the wooden bridge next to the stone bridge from the path to the windmill).

======================================================================
PAGE: Sea of Blood
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Sea_of_Blood
======================================================================

Quest
Sea of Blood
Type
Main quest
Location
The Ossuary
,
Treviso
Previous
The Enemy of My Enemy
Into the Crossroads
Next
Friends in Hidden Places
or
The Smuggled Relic Case
, and
Coffee with the Crows
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Sea of Blood
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
and
Lucanis Dellamorte
's recruitment mission. Neve has suggested recruiting the Antivan Crows' most famous mage assassin, The Demon of Vyrantium. Rook travels to Treviso only to discover he's supposedly been dead for a year.
Acquisition
This quest is received automatically after completing
The Enemy of My Enemy
.
Walkthrough
If the team needs an assassin, the famed Demon of Vyrantium is their best bet - if he's free to take the contract.
Talk to the Crows to find the mage-killer
The team must find the
eluvian
that leads to
Treviso
by completing
Into the Crossroads
before continuing on this quest.
Recruit the Demon of Vyrantium
Teia
, the contact from the
Crows
, leads the team to the Cantori Diamond through the streets of Treviso. The
Antaam
have occupied the city since their stalemate with the war on
Tevinter
and can be seen guarding strategic checkpoints and interrogating "suspicious" citizens. Take a zipline to arrive at the Cantori Diamond, where the group will join
Caterina
,
Viago
, and
Illario
to discuss details on your contract. Note that it is not required to follow Teia immediately. Rook may investigate what side roads are available and buy things in the market. Regardless of how far ahead Teia gets, the cutscene will play out normally on entering the Cantori Diamond.
Caterina states that her grandson, Lucanis, who is known as the Demon of Vyrantium and a feared mage-killer, is the one you seek. Viago informs you that Lucanis has actually been dead for a year, but Caterina shares that she actually knows her grandson is alive, though currently held by the Venatori in a place known as
The Ossuary
; this is resulting from a betrayal that gave away his location during his mission. Caterina promises that you may have Lucanis's help if you manage to free him.
Find Lucanis
Follow Illario to the nearby docks where Caterina has arranged a boat to take you out to Lucanis's location. He will guide you while voicing his displeasure at Caterina from keeping his cousin's survival from him all this time. The team will take the boat out to find Lucanis while Illario remains in Treviso. The Crow that came with you gains you access to the Ossuary, a secret underwater prison kept safe by numerous powerful enchantments. Continue through the prison and you'll find many bodies belonging to the Venatori scattered about.
The Ossuary
Once you've arrived at the High Security area, break the barrier crystals to undo the large barrier preventing access to Lucanis's cell. You'll open the cell to find a large room containing a crystal and three Venatori guards, though before the are able to attack you Lucanis bursts out of the crystal and dispatches them. You'll find out that while Lucanis is alive, he has been possessed by a demon despite not being a mage. Lucanis promises that he can still fulfill the contract promised by Caterina as long as you destroy a vial of his blood they keep within the prison to control him and let him fulfill his original contract on
Calivan
, the prison warden located elsewhere within the Ossuary.
Find and destroy the vial of Lucanis's blood
Once out of Lucanis's cell, defeat the Venatori mage ambush and collect the key dropped from one of their bodies. Use the key to unlock the door in the High Security area and you'll find your first Memento on a table to the left. After you exit the first room, you'll encounter an undead being killed by a fleeing group of Venatori. In this area, you'll see an empty power crystal dock leading to another room that contains a chest behind a barrier. Fight a group of Venator in the third room of the Outer Cells area and use the free power crystal located in an open cell to access the chest room in the previous area. Take the power crystal again and use it to build a walkable bridge to continue your loop back to the broken bridge.
Once at the bridge, use Lucanis's ability, Spite's Reach, on the Veil Instability to pull a makeshift bridge through the Fade so you can hop across. The next room has you fighting The Fabricator and two other Venatori mages who are dealing with a handful of Frenzied Dead. Break the barrier obstructing your path and hop down to the next area where you'll find a chest. A power crystal to your right can be used to gain access to an optional area on the left where you'll be ambushed by several Demonic Experiments; at the end of this area lies a chest for you to loot.
Continue towards the quest marker by jumping down a few ledges to find a room with a handful of dead Venatori. Up on a ledge to your right is another Memento. Jump down to next area to fight a group of Venatori, Frenzied Dead, and Demonic Experiments. An optional large open room to the right will contain more Demonic Experiments and a chest to loot. Destroy the barrier shard and enter the Warden's Tower Approach where you'll use Spite's Reach to make another makeshift bridge. After which, it'll be a short trip to the Blood Vault. Once in the Blood Vault, another memento can be found on a table to the right. Take down the barrier surrounding the room containing Lucanis's blood vial by destroying the barrier crystals located throughout the area, then destroy the vial itself.
Fulfill the contract
Head to the nearby elevator to deal with Calivan. Throughout the fight, he will spawn body doubles and even a pride demon once he reaches 50% health that the party will need to take down. Note: Calivan is vulnerable to cold and resistant to fire. Calivan's Pride is vulnerable to Necrotic and resistant to lighting.  After Calivan is taken care of, a short cutscene will take place where it is revealed that Lucanis can see Spite as if it had a body of its own.
Wrap up any unfinished business
Talk to Lucanis when you're ready to head out, but first make sure to grab the two available chests in the area - one placed in the middle of the room and one behind a barrier at the back of the room.
Lucanis and Spite arguing
Back in Treviso, the team arrives to find that Zara has taken Caterina as a message to the Crows. Despite the upset, Lucanis promises to help Rook and honor the last contract Caterina made. The team then heads back to the Lighthouse where they discuss how to deal with Lucanis' demon, Spite.
Rewards
+100 Antivan Crows Strength
Codex Update
Bond with Lucanis and companion taken on this mission increased
Results
The team needs an expert in fighting mages. Neve has arranged a meeting with the Antivan Crows to see if they have one for hire.
At the bottom of the Nocen Sea, an ancient prison holds the Venatori's most terrifying foe: Lucanis Dellamorte, the infamous "Demon of Vyrantium." The team will get their god-slayer when his chains are finally broken.
With the team's help, the Demon of Vyrantium escaped his chains and completed his contract for Calivan, warden of the Ossuary. But his reunion with the Crows was bloodied by a counterattack from Zara Renata, who struck down Caterina before his return.
Lucanis is recruited into the team.
Unlocks the quest
A Slow Poison

======================================================================
PAGE: Secrets of the Qunari
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Secrets_of_the_Qunari
======================================================================

Quest
Secrets of the Qunari
Type
Companion quest
Location
Shathann's Home,
Rivain Coast
Previous
A Little Dragon Hatches
Next
Slaying the Fangscorcher
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Secrets of the Qunari
is a
companion quest
for
Taash
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. Taash invites Rook to accompany them home for dinner with their mother
Shathann
.
Acquisition
Speak to Taash in their room after completing
A Little Dragon Hatches
; or, if that quest was skipped,
Fire and Ice
.
Walkthrough
A dinner in Rivain with Taash and their mother Shathann yields some new information.
Join Taash for dinner
Travel to "Shathann's Home" in the
Rivain Coast
via the world map.
The interior of Shathann's home.
Rook
and Taash arrive at Shathann's home to discover Taash did not tell Shathann they were bringing a guest. Taash comments on the absence of
isskapp
, a melon dish they always prepared on Tuesdays, and Shathann asks Taash to begin the meal as she is not finished with her day's studies. Though annoyed, Taash complies.
When Taash leaves, Shathann tells Rook that the mysterious inscribed tablet she holds (as seen during
The Dragon Slayer
) has old
Qunari
runes engraved on it, but some of the text has been hidden from sight, rendering the tablet currently unreadable. It is old enough to provide information on
The First Expedition
, which she studies: a voyage of
Qunari
people who traveled to
Thedas
over a thousand years ago, well prior to Thedas's currently-documented Qunari arrival of about three hundred years ago during the
Steel Age
. In other words, she studies Qunari who predate the
Qun
itself.
Shathann's desk.
Taash returns, having prepared something besides isskapp. If Taash embraced Rivaini culture during
A Little Dragon Hatches
, they have prepared an unnamed Rivaini dish, which irritates Shathann. If Taash embraced Qunari culture, they say the isskapp would have taken too long and they wanted to hear about the tablet, so they have prepared something else, which surprises Shathann.
The meal is eaten offscreen, and on the way out, Taash and Shathann have a brief exchange. If Taash embraced Rivaini culture, Taash explains new bookshelves should be arriving soon, but Shathann, clearly still irked from dinner, makes a terse statement regarding Taash's lack of appreciation of her studies. If Taash embraced Qunari culture, they and Shathann practice the phrase "
Herah meraad zeshsaam
," and Shathann smiles at Taash's effort before wishing them a warm goodbye.
Regardless, Shathann's use of "she" makes it clear Taash has not yet come out as non-binary to Shathann. When Rook asks, Taash says they were going to explain but lost their nerve, then reassures Rook that they will tell her eventually.
On the conclusion of this conversation, the quest will complete.
Approval
I was going to. Then we got here and she was... like that.
(Diplomatic) There's no hurry.
- Taash Approves
(Humorous) Tell me in advance next time.
(Stoic) You don't have to tell her.
(Emotional) You're worth standing up for.
- Taash Approves
Rewards
None.
Codex entries
Script for Shathann
Notes
If Rook says they brought wine, Shathann explains she does not drink alcohol.
Results
A relaxed dinner with Taash and Shathann offers a chance to share information and learn about Qunari culture.

Quest
Seeing Red
Location

Storm Coast

Rewards

Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Seeing Red
is a
companion quest
for
Varric
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Varric
saw enough
red lyrium
in
Kirkwall
for one lifetime. Now it's everywhere, and the worst of it "sings" like the
shard
that drove his
brother
mad. Varric would take no small pleasure in destroying those. Locate and destroy
Primeval
Red Lyrium nodes.
Acquisition
This quest becomes available by talking to Varric in
Haven
or
Skyhold
about his reaction to
red lyrium
.
Walkthrough
Twelve nodes of Primeval Red Lyrium are needed for the quest, however there are a total of 18 nodes scattered across the various locations. This gives the player some freedom to complete the quest regardless of the player's progress in game.
How to destroy the nodes
The nodes can be destroyed by using the regular interact button, no specific class ability is required. They can also be easily destroyed by using the
Explosive Shot
or
Shield Bash
abilities.
pc
Destroying the nodes requires a Bash ability used by a
warrior
, an explosive Shot from a
rogue
or a Mind Blast / Veilstrike / Immolate (twice) from a
mage
.
Node locations
Locations of all 18 veins and the notable levels of higher ranking enemies encountered:
3 in the
Hinterlands
inside
Ash Warrior's Refuge
(the cave located on the far southeast corner of the world map)
inside
Fort Connor
at
Lady Shayna's Valley
(level 15 enemies)
3 in the
Western Approach
inside
Sand Rock Mine
(level 11 enemies)
inside the
Forgotten Mine
(level 11 enemies)
inside
Shimmer Stone Mine
(level 15 enemies)
4 on the
Storm Coast
found scattered about
Daerwin's Mouth
; accessible during the side quest
Red Water
(level 18 enemies)
3 in the
Emerald Graves
inside the
Veridium Mine
(level 15 enemies)
just outside
Villa Maurel
on the left hand side (level 15 enemies)
at
Southfinger Watch
in the northernmost part of the Graves, near the Red Templar Camp containing the
Chasind Sack Mead
(level 18 enemies)
5 in
Emprise du Lion
near the Drakon's Rise Inquisition Camp
near
Valeska's Watch
found midway between the Sahrnia and the Highgrove Inquisition Camps
in the Sahrnia Quarry
found midway between the Highgrove and Drakon's Rise Inquisition Camps, just west of the vein found near the Drakon's Rise Inquisition Camp
Rewards
967 XP
Influence 200
Power 2
Approval
Destroying the nodes with Varric in the party -
Varric approves
Destroying the nodes without Varric in the party -
Varric slightly approves
Notes
Even though only 12 nodes are required to complete the quest, Varric will still give approval for every subsequent node destroyed, so it may be worth seeking out the rest.
Bugs
pc
The usual right-click or F to interact may not register properly for some players.
Despite the broken shield icon representing a two-handed
warrior
's
Shield Bash
ability, the destroy action can be done by any warrior regardless if the ability is available.
pc
xboxone
Annoyingly, the quest completion and sound effect will trigger each time a playable character is near a destroyed vein after the quest has already been completed.

Dragon Age: Origins
Seek out Steward Bandelor
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
It is the first sub-quest of
A Paragon of Her Kind
quest line, obtained after entering
Orzammar
through the
Frostback Mountain Pass
.
Walkthrough
The Assembly lies in the
Diamond Quarter
. You must go there to find
Steward Bandelor
. Entering the
Assembly Chamber
yields a short cut-scene with
Assembly
members in a heated debate. Steward Bandelor then calls a recess.
Result
This quest is marked as completed once the cut-scene is over.
You get the follow-up sub-quest to speak to
Vartag Gavorn
and
Dulin Forender
if you haven't obtained those already.

Weapon
Ser Garlen's Sword
Type
Longsword
Material
Grey Iron (Tier 2)
Damage
7.70
Requires
13 strength
Strength Modifier
1.00
Critical Chance
2.20%
Armor Penetration
2.30
Stats
+2 attack
+10 physical resistance
Locations
Pick
Value
27
50
Description
Somewhere on the field there is a bewildered knight fighting with his dinner fork. Pity him.
Item ID
gen_im_wep_mel_lsw_mes
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Ser Garlen's Sword
is a
longsword
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Acquisition
Pick, the elven messenger
To obtain this sword,
the Warden
must lie to
Pick
, the
elven messenger
running around the camp at
Ostagar
, by saying that they have a message for him. If the Warden has sufficiently high
Cunning
, or 1 point in
Coercion
, they can persuade Pick to hand the sword over. Alternatively, Pick can be intimidated, which requires no skill check.
If an initial coercion check fails, it is still possible to get the sword from Pick by following up with the intimidation dialogue choice and the insistent choices after it. Pick will be resentful and distressed, but will nonetheless surrender the sword, asking for an excuse he can give afterwards regarding it. The choice is inconsequential, and the player can kill him at this point.
Pick is first encountered when the player approaches the
Ash Warrior
Leader near the entrance to the
Korcari Wilds
.  This triggers background dialogue between the Leader and the messenger.
If you happen to lose track of the messenger after the initial encounter, he can be found at the south-eastern end of the west side of Ostagar, overlooking the valley, with a praying
Chantry
sister nearby. After a short delivery route, he will stay there permanently.  He can only be found during the day.
Note:
Alistair
will lose
(
-2
) if he is present when you coerce Pick.
Notes
Ser Garlen's Sword is especially useful with the
Studded Helmet
and the
Superior Dwarven Guard Armor
. Playing as the
Dwarven Noble
will grant access to all three items.  Together, they grant a total of
+23 Physical Resistance
. This is enough early-game physical resistance to be nearly impervious to
Shield Bash
and other abilities that rely on breaching
physical resistance
.
This is an unlisted
side quest
yielding 100 XP upon successfully obtaining the sword.
It would seem that the sword cannot be upgraded to higher tiers at Mikhael's Smithy

Quest
Shades of the Blight
Location
Dock Town
Previous
Where the Dead Must Go
or
The Dragon Slayer
Next
Something Wrong
or
A Change on the Wind
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Shades of the Blight
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Morrigan
requests a private meeting with
Rook
at the
Cobbled Swan
.
Acquisition
This quest is acquired automatically after both
Where the Dead Must Go
and
The Dragon Slayer
have been completed.
Walkthrough
The team pursues leads that reveal disturbing information about the spread of the blight, but also gains some unexpected aid.
Meet Morrigan at the Cobbled Swan
Travel to the Cobbled Swan in Dock Town and speak with
Harding
to begin the conversation.
Morrigan
has brought the
Inquisitor
to speak with
Rook
. The Inquisitor shares that their political power has dwindled since the events of Inquisition and that they have been traveling with Morrigan through the mirrors in the Crossroads.
They also reveal that
Ghilan'nain
and
Elgar'nan
's strange new
darkspawn
have been wreaking havoc across the South. In particular,
Val Royeaux
,
Halamshiral
, and
Ferelden
are in dire straits.
Denerim
has been lost, and
Orzammar
is helping hold the line at
Redcliffe
. The
Free Marches
are also in bad shape, with Acting Viscount
Aveline
shepherding the refugees from an evacuated
Kirkwall
to
Starkhaven
under Prince
Sebastian
's protection. Without the Inquisitor's help, these regions might have fallen entirely. The Inquisitor has also rescued Harding's mother from the encroaching danger.
The Inquisitor is unable to provide much assistance, but does gift Rook a wolf statuette that appeared shortly after
Solas
was sealed away during his failed ritual. The statuette seems tied to Solas, and Morrigan suggests bringing the statuette to the
Lighthouse
to see if it reacts to anything there where it could potentially provide more information on him.
Rewards
Codex update
Results
Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain's forces are at work in many places. Fortunately, the team already has contacts who have begun pushing back. Lady Morrigan and Grey Wardens Antoine and Evka want to discuss their investigations into the gods and the blight.
Rook gains a wolf statuette
If no wolf statuettes have been collected so far:
Unlocks the quest

If Something Wrong has already been completed:
Unlocks the quest

======================================================================
PAGE: Shadows Crossing
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Shadows_Crossing
======================================================================

Quest
Shadows Crossing
Location
D'Meta's Crossing
Arlathan Forest
Previous
In Entropy's Grasp
Next
The Singing Blade
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Shadows Crossing
is the fourth
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.  With reports of blight breaking out all across
Arlathan Forest
, two
Veil Jumpers
were sent to check on the town of
D'Meta's Crossing
—but unlike other scouts, these two have not returned. Rook, Harding, Neve, and Bellara travel there to investigate the situation.
Acquisition
This quest is received automatically after completing
In Entropy's Grasp
.
Walkthrough
The team must investigate when the Veil Jumpers report that the town of D'Meta's Crossing has gone silent.
Reach D'Meta's Crossing
After reaching the Veil Jumpers camp, Bellara suggests that the team investigate the nearby town of
D'Meta's Crossing
, where two Veil Jumpers have already gone to investigate.
Head outside the camp towards the docks, where you will find your injured companion waiting for you. Speak with them to travel to D'Meta's Crossing.
Investigate D'Meta's Crossing
Bellara finding a Veil Jumper
The team finds the town eerily quiet and empty. Further investigation reveals that the town is overrun with a strange new form of Blight, and that the town's few survivors have been mind-controlled with blood magic. They tell the team that the mayor ordered them to lure the Veil Jumpers into the town square.
Upon reaching the town square, Rook finds one of the Veil Jumpers, Mihlva, dead and the other, Jahel, enveloped in blight tentacles. In his final moments, he tells them that
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
performed a blood ritual to release the Blight.
The mayor and his gold
Continue through the town until you find Mayor
Julius
, who has been trapped by some blight tentacles. Before you can talk to him properly, a dragon appears, but is called away by
Ghilan'nain
before attacking. Talking to the mayor reveals that he sold the town out to the elven gods in exchange for gold. In his defense, he claims that the gods were influencing his mind. Rook can decide the mayor's fate: release him, leave him to die, or banish him to the
Grey Wardens
(only available to a Warden Rook).
Return to the Veil Jumpers Camp
After deciding the mayor's fate, return to the Veil Jumpers' camp for a debrief with
Strife
and
Irelin
. Partway through the conversation,
Morrigan
flies into the camp as a raven and introduces herself to Rook. She warns them that D'Meta's Crossing was only the beginning, and that they will have to fight the gods to save the world from the blight. She also tells them to recover Solas's dagger if possible, as they will need powerful magic to defeat the gods, and informs them that Solas's eluvian in the Lighthouse should be able to travel to any eluvian.
Rewards
+900 XP
+200 Veil Jumpers Strength
Codex Update
Increased bond with Bellara, Harding and Neve
Results
If Mayor Julius is saved:
After investigating the town of D'Meta's Crossing, the team discovered the gods had laid waste to the town with blight. Worse, they tempted the mayor into betraying his people for gold. The team decided to spare the mayor's life.
Julius will later be spotted during the quest
Blood of Arlathan
.
If Mayor Julius is banished to the Grey Wardens:
After investigating the town of D'Meta's Crossing, the team discovered the gods had laid waste to the town with blight. Worse, they tempted the mayor into betraying his people for gold. The team decided to banish the mayor to the Grey Wardens.
Julius will later be encountered in the
Hossberg Wetlands
during
Lives Spared
.
If Mayor Julius is left behind:
After investigating the town of D'Meta's Crossing, the team discovered the gods had laid waste to the town with blight. Worse, they tempted the mayor into betraying his people for gold. The team decided to leave the mayor to his fate.
This leads to the side quest
A Growing Corruption
.
Rook, Neve and Harding head back towards Solas's ritual site in search of his
dagger
.
Bellara heads back to the Lighthouse to try and fix the eluvian.

Our community
needs more
information on this page! Can you help out?

.
Quest
Shadows of Ancient Arlathan
Location
The
Lighthouse
Arlathan Forest
Start
The
Lighthouse
End
Arlathan Forest
Previous
The End of the Beginning
Next
In Entropy's Grasp
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Shadows of Ancient Arlathan
is the second
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. The team regroups after Solas' failed ritual and discuss what to do going forward.
Acquisition
This quest is received automatically after reaching the
Lighthouse
.
Walkthrough
After stopping Solas's ritual, the team must investigate his claim that ancient, blighted gods have been released into the world.
Check in with
Neve
or
Harding
Leave the library through the main door to find yourself in an area of the
Fade
known as the
Lighthouse
. After a brief talk with Neve or Harding (the quest will point to whoever wasn't injured in the ritual), you have a chance to explore the Lighthouse before gathering the team at the library table. This is a good opportunity to solve the Lighthouse statue puzzle that opens up the music room.
Gather the team at the library table
Before gathering the team, Rook has the option of talking to Neve and Harding to talk about the current situation and their future plans. At the library table, Rook tells the team that Solas talked to them in a dream, informing them that disrupting his ritual released
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
, two of the tyrannical, blighted
Evanuris
. Somewhat doubtful of Solas's claims, the team decides to return to the site of ritual to investigate the situation.
Head downstairs to find a massive
eluvian
that takes you directly back to
Arlathan Forest
.
Investigate Solas's ritual site
As they reach Arlathan Forest, the team is interrupted by the arrival of a group of
Veil Jumpers
,
Strife
,
Irelin
and Tarith, under attack by a magical sentinel. Tarith is injured when Irelin fails to deactivate the sentinel, though Strife eventually destroys the artifact powering the sentinel. Harding recognizes Strife and Irelin from a
previous adventure
, and informs them of Solas's claim that Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain have been released. Strife and Irelin believe that it would explain the magical disturbances in Arlathan Forest and tell Rook to go find
Bellara Lutare
, their expert on ancient magical artifacts. Whoever was injured in the ritual stays behind with the Veil Jumpers while Rook and the other companion head in search of Bellara.
Results
The team stopped Solas from tearing down the Veil, trapping him in the Fade. However, Solas insists that sabotaging his ritual unleashed malevolent elven gods named Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain. Though wary, the team can't afford to ignore his warning; they must hunt for signs that dangerous gods stalk the land.
Rook, and the companion who was not injured by following Rook, head off to find Bellara while the other remains behind with the Veil Jumpers
Approval
Speaking with Neve
Neve was not injured:
Neve Approves
after conversation, regardless of dialogue choices
Neve was injured:
Neve Approves
after conversation, regardless of dialogue choices
Speaking with Harding
Harding was not injured:
Oh, hey, Rook. How's it going?
(Diplomatic) Are you okay?
- Harding Approves
(Humorous) You have plants.
Harding was injured:
There's so much to do. We have to keep going.
(Diplomatic) You sure you're okay?
- Harding Disapproves
(Humorous) You're unstoppable, huh?
(Stoic) No rest for the weary.
Before leaving the lighthouse
And how many more people will get hurt - get killed - while we spend time investigating?
Neve's right.
- Harding Disapproves, Neve Approves
Harding's right.

======================================================================
PAGE: Shadows of Minrathous
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Shadows_of_Minrathous
======================================================================

Quest
Shadows of Minrathous
Location
Dock Town
Previous
Friends in Hidden Places
Next
In Peace, Vigilance (quest)
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Shadows of Minrathous
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Acquisition
This quest is received automatically after completing
Friends in Hidden Places
.
Walkthrough
Rook seeks out the Shadow Dragons in Minrathous while hoping for news on the elven gods.
Find the Shadow Dragons in Dock Town
Head to
Dock Town
using the Crossroads or fast travel.
Get information
Talk to the Viper,
Ashur
, in the Shadow Dragons hideout where he'll inform the team that darkspawn have been causing trouble in the Anvallenim, a secret passage that has been used to help free elven slaves. He'll request the team's assistance in clearing it out.
Investigate the Anvallenim
Exit the Shadow Dragon's hideout and find the tunnel entrance outside by heading South and taking a right down an alley. Interact with the snake-themed wall sculpture to access the hidden entrance. A short distance in, the path is blocked by inactive blight; destroy the boil in the room nearby to activate it and then cut through to continue on your path. In the next room, drop down and deal with a group of Venatori and some darkspawn coming from the nearby blight pools. Grab the contents of the chest in a room at the far end of the chamber, then destroy the blight blocking your path out.
Return to the Shadow Dragons
Follow the path back up through the tunnels, only stopping to grab a memento hidden behind a breakable wall of wood, until reaching the door back to the hideout. Speak to Ashur to report the Venatori attack and coordination with the darkspawn. Tarquin will offer a piece of intel about Venatori activity in a warehouse that may be a lead.
Investigate Venatori activity at the warehouse
Head North through the Central Market and take the elevator down to the Smuggler's Dock. Eliminate the group of Venatori in the area and check the door to the warehouse to find it locked. Head around to the East side of the building, destroy the barrier crystal, and fight the Venatori guard in the alley before heading up the ladder to the roof.
Use the rooftop entrance to drop into the warehouse after taking care of the two Venatori mages guarding it. In the next room, head down the stairs and break some boxes leading to a storage area and then break the wooden barrier leading into a stairwell. Drop down into the stairwell that leads down to a room where a ritual is being performed to control darkspawn. Once the Venatori have been taken care of, grab the contents of the chest in this room and then search the warehouse for evidence. There are three notes scattered around the ritual room and a floor drain to be investigated. Once that is completed, the warehouse door will unlock and the Viper will be waiting outside. Speak to him to let him know what was uncovered.
Return to the Shadow Dragons
Once back at the hideout, speak to Ashur again. Now that it has been confirmed the Venatori are working with
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
, the Shadow Dragons are planning their next steps.
Maevaris
joins the conversation and establishes herself as one of the leaders of the Shadow Dragons and a former magister. She'll inform you that the First Warden is waiting at The Cobbled Swan to speak regarding the blight. Before you set out, Maevaris asks you to come back when you can as they'll still need your help.
Rewards
3,750 XP
Battle Robes
(medium armor, only if Rook has a Shadow Dragons background)
Knight's Sword
(Common Appearance, Warrior Rook only)
+400 Shadow Dragons strength
Increased bond with Neve and other companion
Results
Rook has sought out the Shadow Dragons in hopes of gaining information about the elven gods, but the rebels have problems of their own. Their Dock Town refuge is under attack from darkspawn, and they need Rook's assistance.
The Venatori serve the risen gods, who have given the cultists magic that grants them control over darkspawn. The cult's influence and power spreads, which means trouble for Minrathous and the world beyond.
Unlocks the quest
In Peace, Vigilance (quest)

Quest
Shepherding Wolves

1

(night)

Sister Petrice
Previous
Blackpowder Promise
Appearances
Dragon Age II
Shepherding Wolves
is an Act 1
main quest
in
Dragon Age II
.
In this quest
Sister Petrice
asks
Hawke
to lead a chained and collared
Qunari
out of
Kirkwall
to freedom.  The Qunari, whom Petrice has dubbed
Ketojan
, can only make gurgling sounds.  Ketojan is actually a
Saarebas
, a Qunari
mage
.
Acquisition
Blackpowder Promise
must be completed for this rumor to become an active quest. The rumor has it that someone from the
Chantry
is offering good money for a mission. Go to
Lowtown
at night to find out more. Just south of The
Hanged Man
Hawke will see Sister Petrice setting herself up for a first-class mugging. Rescue her and she offers Hawke the quest, inviting the party to meet her at her safehouse to learn more.
Walkthrough
Lowtown
Sister Petrice's safehouse can be found in the Old City Slums region of Lowtown (Night), almost directly across the alley from
Gamlen's House
. Go inside and speak to her. She asks Hawke to lead Ketojan to freedom.
If Hawke accepts the mission:
Anders: friendship (
+10
)
Bethany: friendship (
+5
)
Merrill: friendship (
+10
)
Varric: rivalry (
+5
)
Note:
pc
xbox360
Hawke may not get
Bethany: friendship (
+5
)
If Hawke refuses:
Anders: rivalry (
+15
)
Bethany: rivalry (
+5
)
Varric: friendship (
+10
)
As this is a main quest, even if Hawke initially refuses, it must be completed.
In order to escape the city unseen, Sister Petrice instructs Hawke to lead Ketojan out of the city via an underground passage.
Undercity Warrens
Within the warrens the party will face a series of spiders, and ultimately a band of thugs. While there are no locked chests in the warrens, there is a single
simple
ranked trap (any rogue can detect and disarm) followed by five
standard
ranked traps (requiring a rogue with 20 cunning to detect and disarm). The simple trap is located in the middle of the first room where spiders are first encountered, taking the form of a line of three pressure plates. The five standard traps consist of a single pressure plate trap at the top of the first flight of stairs, a line of pressure plates midway through the tunnel past the first flight of stairs, a single pressure plate trap at the end of the aforementioned tunnel, another single pressure plate trap at the bottom of the second flight of stairs, and one final single pressure plate trap in the choke point entryway to the U-shaped section of the map just past the second flight of stairs.
Just before battling the band of thugs, there is a cutscene where the thug leader confronts Hawke, hurling insults at him and Ketojan. The insults angers Ketojan, and when the thug leader tries to stab Hawke, Ketojan is provoked into action and unleashes his magic upon the thug leader. This causes the rest of the thug band to attack the party.  After the battle, there are a couple of chests with assorted loot available before leaving the area. Depending on Hawke's class a chest will contain:
Boots of the Spiral Eye
(mage)
Boots of the Fallen
(warrior)
Last Descent Boots
(rogue)
Vimmark Mountain Pass
Upon exiting the underground tunnels to the Wounded Coast, Hawke encounters an encampment of other Qunari, who call Ketojan "Saarebas," meaning "dangerous thing". Apparently, the members of Ketojan's karataam were killed, and their bodies were left in a trail leading to exactly where Hawke's group would emerge. The
Arvaarad
proclaims that since Ketojan has spent some time without his handler, he may have been corrupted by
demons
and thus cannot be allowed to live.
If there is a Mage in the party and you investigate to ask why the Qunari collar their mages, and you tell them that there is a mage in the party, then Arvaarad uses a binding spell on Ketojan and the Qunari attack immediately.
Merrill: friendship (
+5
)
If you let the Qunari take Ketojan then the Qunari will unbind Ketojan, and with his final words he acknowledges Hawke as worthy of following, before submitting to a ceremonial death. Arvaarad will then reveal that since Hawke is worth following, they are also worthy of the ceremonial death now required by the
Qun
because Hawke spent time with an unsupervised mage. The Qunari attack.
Anders: rivalry (
+10
)
If you say that Ketojan must be set free then Arvaarad uses a binding spell on Ketojan and the Qunari attack.
Anders: friendship (
+10
)
Aveline: friendship (
+5
)
Bethany: friendship (
+10
)
Carver: friendship (
+5
)
Merrill: friendship (
+5
)
If Ketojan is alive after the battle you free him using the staff that Arvaarad held. He tells you that he must return to the Qun. No matter what you say, he gives Hawke the
Talisman of Saarebas
and immolates himself with fire magic.
Grab the
Silverite
deposit before leaving, then return to Lowtown to confront Sister Petrice, who has clearly sent Hawke into a trap.
Strategy
For Primal and fire-centric Elemental mages, this fight can be a challenge, as Qunari resist both fire and electricity damage, having outright immunity on Nightmare difficulty.  This also means that
Combustion Grenades
are out of the question as well.  A ranged party can maintain a buffer, whereas a melee one, even with tanks, will get overwhelmed.  Lacking in cold spells, Spirit is the next best option, which has the added benefit of penetrating 50% of all damage and magic resistances.  Since the Qunari are Normal-ranked and have similar maximum health, and begin in a tight-knit cluster, Walking Bomb can take off half of the group's health, regardless of which one goes boom.  After your opening move, focus on the Ashaads, the Qunari faction's equivalent of archers (who act as spear-throwers) while keeping the Arvaarad's healing aura suppressed with stun effects, like Horror and Petrify, then work on the Karashok, the melee units.  The Qunari hit hard enough with their basic attacks to knock most non-warriors around, so be wary of this.
Retreating to the lowland depression near the easternmost cliff, around the bend from the starting path, is also an option, as it will help funnel the enemies into an ant trail of sorts as they pursue, making for easier elimination, and also provide temporary cover from the inevitable second wave.  If the Arvaarad does not pursue as aggressively as the Karashok, this can also pull them away from his healing aura.  Lastly, as two of the Ashaad reinforcements spawn directly behind your starting position, retreating before they spawn will prevent you from getting flanked.  In short, retreating to a more defensible position is always a good idea.  However, retreating while the initial group of Ashaad are alive is ill-advised, as they will be able to auto-hit from range, and the Arvaarad should still be stunned lest he join the pursuers and heal them all the way to your new position.
When enough of the initial group is eliminated, three more Ashaad and three more Karashok will appear.  Let them come to you and hit them with the full force of your party one at a time.  If worst comes to worst, as long as the Arvaarad, Ashaad and Karasaad are defeated, a ranged character can kite the Karashok around the coastal trails without them getting healed mid-chase.
On the other hand, anyone with sufficient investment in the cold side of Elemental will find this battle incredibly easy.  They can run up and blast the entirety of the tightly-clustered Qunari with Cone of Cold, and having
Winter's Blast
,
Deep Freeze
, and
Elemental Mastery
, along with a sufficiently powerful staff to power the spells will also help.  The freezing and Brittle effects will also be welcome.  The
Acolyte's Staff
from Lowtown is a good option, but if you've got 28 Magic or more, nothing beats the
Staff of the Primal Order
in this fight.  Having some of these upgrades is preferable, having all of them can empower the mage to eliminate them all in one use of each spell.  The new wave will spawn in, and your party will still be at maximum strength, able to take down the two Ashaad who spawn behind you.  When Winter's Grasp/Blast recharges, it can take care of any enemy that's getting too annoying.  Finally, what one Elemental mage can do, two will do better, and using Walking Bomb in conjunction with a barrage of cold spells can, in some cases, put out enough damage to kill the Arvaarad in the initial wave.  Ultimately, this fight can either be one of the easiest or hardest in the Act depending on how you've built your mages.
Return to Lowtown
Return to Sister Petrice's safehouse in Lowtown at night.  Hawke finds Petrice and her Templar assistant packing up, intending to leave without a trace.  When confronted she reveals that had Hawke been killed, she would have used the events to turn the
Chantry
against the Qunari.
"I don't care, just pay me":
Aveline: rivalry (
+5
)
Carver: friendship (
+5
)
Varric: friendship (
+5
)
"I should kill you":
Varric: rivalry (
+5
)
Note:
Fenris will not gain friendship or rivalry throughout this quest regardless of responses.
Rewards
400 XP
Money
(7
)
Talisman of Saarebas
- if freed from the Qunari party, Saarebas will give this to Hawke before he kills himself
Notes
It is possible to tell the
Arishok
about the events, which helps in obtaining the
A Worthy Rival
achievement.
If seeking the
Supplier
achievement, be sure to grab the
Silverite

Trivia
When returning to Petrice, Varnell will bark "Mind your tongue, [noun]" at whichever companion accosts her. If that companion is Bethany, Carver, Aveline, or Anders, he will call them "
Fereldan
." Isabela is called "woman," Merrill and Fenris are simply referred to as "elf" and Varric as "dwarf." All of these descriptors use a tone that is intended to be disrespectful.
After completing the quest Hawke can talk to
Elthina
, who says the following: "Several of our brothers and sisters take the Qunari threat too personally. The Maker would not have sent them if not to teach us something." This line triggers only once in Act 1 and only after this quest.
When encountering the thugs in the Undercity, one of them will suggest letting the party pass after Ketojan growls at them. The encounter and character voice are very similar to the encounter the
The Warden
had with bandits outside of
Lothering
.
Bugs
ps3
Hawke may be able to inform the Arishok of the quest's events twice, despite being told by him not to repeat them.
