======================================================================

Quest
The Grey Wardens' Cache
Location
Ostagar
,
Korcari Wilds
,
Deep in the Wilds
Quest Giver
Duncan
End
Duncan
Previous
Joining the Grey Wardens
Next
After the Joining
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Grey Wardens' Cache
is a
main quest
which is given by
Duncan
to the
Grey Warden
recruits and
Alistair
along with
Tainted Blood
. Only the latter is materially necessary for the upcoming
Joining
ritual, but the recruits also have an opportunity to retrieve several
Ancient Treaties
.
Note:
Tainted Blood can be completed more quickly than The Grey Wardens' Cache, but the ritual will not commence until both are finished
Walkthrough
Leave
Ostagar
and venture into the
Korcari Wilds
(where several sidequests are available).
Fight through the
darkspawn
to the far right/top of the map. Behind the
Hurlock alpha
's starting position is the ruin containing the remains of the cache.
Examine the cache which triggers a cutscene introducing
Morrigan
. She will inform you that the treaties are no longer here, then (perhaps after the party takes another look around the area) guide you to her mother's hut. Her
mother
states that she took the treaties because the Wardens' seal had worn off of the cache. Despite any mistrust from the party, she is quite willing to hand over the scrolls.
Morrigan will now guide you back to camp, and night has fallen; now that you have the treaties in hand, return to Duncan.
See
Exploits
for information about an infinite experience bug with this quest in some versions of the game.
Result
Codex entry: Morrigan
is added during your conversation at the ruin (if you go directly to the cutscene with her mother, you will not be notified of this until Morrigan has returned you to camp at dusk).
If you have also finished Tainted Blood, you are about to officially join the Grey Wardens: the dialogue with Duncan will proceed to the Joining cutscene.
Rewards
2000 XP - for recovering the Grey Warden
treaties
, completing this quest, and going through the
Joining
ritual.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Harrowing (quest)
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests, Origin story quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Harrowing_(quest)
======================================================================

For information on the rite, see
The Harrowing
.
Quest
The Harrowing
Location
Magi Origin
Start
New game with
Mage
class
End
Defeat demon
Next
A Mage of the Circle
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Harrowing
is the first sub-quest of the
Magi Origin
story
In the High Tower of the Mages
. A cinematic starts immediately after the character generation, at the end of which you touch the
lyrium
and go into the
Fade
.
Walkthrough
Enter The Fade
The Fade: you will start in the area at the top right of the map.
Note:
Creatures in the Fade do not drop loot, and you cannot take items from the Fade back to the material world. This includes Valor's Staff.
You will start at the top right (northeast) of the map. To your left, you can find a
Vase of Healing
containing
Shimmering Orbs of Light
. These can be used to heal yourself whilst in the Fade but will vanish from your inventory once you complete your Harrowing, so don't be afraid to use them. (
ps3
and
xbox360
: instead of Orbs there are
Health Poultices
in the Vase; if unused these
will
stay in your inventory after you leave the Fade.) Next to the Vase is a Disturbing Statue which will unlock
Codex entry: Beyond the Veil: Spirits and Demons
. Progress through the Fade to find a few
Wisp wraiths
that attack with lightning. Fortunately they come at you one at a time, and shouldn't require much more than an
Arcane Bolt
to kill.
Mouse
Along the way, you will meet what appears to be a talking rat, who will introduce himself as
Mouse
(he may explain that he's been in the Fade too long to remember his real name). Mouse tells you he was once an apprentice like you, but that he was murdered by the Templars for failing to complete his Harrowing. He will explain a bit about the Fade and your test, and warn you about other spirits in the area. Further down the path is an open area to the right. Mouse will explain that this is where the demon you are to face will manifest. The Lyrium Vein seen here (which unlocks
Codex entry: Lyrium
) can be used to recover Mana during combat (and will replenish itself later if used now).
Spirit of Valor
To prepare further for the battle, proceed westwards. The path will open up again. Up a slope to your right are another
vase
of
orbs
and a Twisted Structure (which unlocks
Codex entry: The Black City
). To the left is
Valor
, who may reveal during conversation that he can create weapons with his will alone. If you ask him for a weapon, Valor will challenge you to a duel. To obtain
Valor's Staff
, you can take him up on this challenge and win the duel. (After winning you can attack and kill him for XP.) Alternatively, you may accuse him of plotting to kill you, then demand he prove his good intentions by helping you. Equip your new staff and progress onwards, and fight a few spirit
wolves
.
Note:
After dealing with the Spirit of Valor, if you travel back to where the Harrowing began, you can fight several more wisps, which are more powerful than those you originally faced and offer more XP.
Sloth
At the far west of the area, you will fight 3 Spirit Wolves and [further on] meet a
Sloth demon
in the shape of a
bereskarn
. There are three ways he will agree to teach Mouse to take on his form. You can defeat him in combat or answer his riddles; if one riddle is answered incorrectly, you'll have to fight him. The answers to his riddles are, in order, "a map", "my tongue" and "a dream". Alternatively, if you have 30
cunning
(which is not possible without cheating), you can tell him you would pester him endlessly until he gives in. After you defeat Sloth one way or another, he will teach Mouse to turn into a
bear
(though not a bereskarn) and then resume his "rest". Mouse will now join the party as a companion. If you've not done so already, you may have more of a discussion about the Fade and Mouse's memories.
The Spirit of Rage
Note:
Before entering the circle and facing the demon, if you travel back to the beginning of the Fade, you can fight several more wisps that are more powerful than those you originally faced and offer 82 XP, instead of 27 XP. You can do this again even if you did it after meeting Valor. Both of these extra side-fights are worth it for the very easy XP.
Now your preparations are complete; double back toward the middle of the map. More spirit wolves will attack you - but now you have Mouse in bear form on your side. The
Spirit of Rage
now appears in the central area you passed earlier. After some dialogue, battle will ensue. Four Wisp Wraiths will join the fight. You can ignore these and focus on the Spirit of Rage, because once it is defeated the battle will cease. However, it is advisable to take them out for easy XP; usually, Mouse will distract the rage demon while you are attacking the wisps.
If you have it,
Winter's Grasp
is very effective against the fiery Spirit of Rage; conversely
Flame Blast
won't help you here. If your mana gets low, remember to use the Lyrium Vein, if you haven't already done so.  If not, then Mouse in his bear form should be able to occupy the Spirit of Rage while you cast spells or use your staff from afar. Don't forget the healing Shimmering Orbs of Light, which will shortly become useless for anything else, and which Mouse can also use.
Completing the test
Once the Spirit of Rage has been defeated, Mouse will want to discuss a proposal ... but it swiftly becomes clear that Mouse isn't the timid little individual he pretended to be. Once you make clear you see through Mouse's ruse, he will shift into his true form (though this is not clearly shown on screen due to camera angle, its implied size and a fair bit of Mouse's dialogue suggest he is really a
Pride Demon
). Resisting Mouse, it turns out, was the real test, which you have now passed.
If Mouse dies during the fight against the Spirit of Rage, the dialogue with Mouse still happens but you do not fight the Pride Demon after the cut scene.
Note:
There appears to be no way to fail this test (even if it would have meant death via templars), as all the dialogue options end with the demon showing itself.
Codex entries
First Enchanter Irving
Knight-Commander Greagoir
The Harrowing
Beyond the Veil: Spirits and Demons
Wisp
Wolf
Sloth Demon
Rage Demon
Lyrium
The Black City
Note:
Depending on what actions are taken, various
Controls codex entries
.
Result
You wake up in bed the next morning, to find
Jowan
hovering over you. You can now become
A Mage of the Circle
.
Gallery
"Your magic is a gift, but it's also a curse." (Knight-Commander
Greagoir
)
Cullen
and another Templar witness The Warden's Harrowing
Mages
can use
lyrium
to enter the
Fade
, the realm of dreams and spirits
Using lyrium to enter the Fade for the Harrowing
Meeting what appears to be a talking mouse
Mouse
claims to be another mage apprentice, killed by the Templars for taking too long to complete his Harrowing
A Spirit of Valor can aid The Warden by giving them
Valor's Staff
Meeting a spirit called
Sloth
in the Fade
The main opponent of The Warden during their Harrowing appears to be a
Rage demon

======================================================================
PAGE: The Heart of the Titan
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Heart_of_the_Titan
======================================================================

Quest
The Heart of the Titan
Type
Companion quest
Location
Isana Negat
Previous
The Bond Rekindled
Next
The Eternal Hymn
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The Heart of the Titan
is a
companion quest
for
Harding
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. Whatever sinister force attacked Harding in the
Deep Roads
has taken
Stalgard
and some of the other
Kal-Sharok
dwarves
captive, and Harding won't abandon her new friends to their fate.
Acquisition
Speak to Harding at the
Lighthouse
after completing her previous companion quest,
The Bond Rekindled
.
If Rook is romancing Harding, a conversation in
Emmrich
's room regarding Harding's emotional response to Rook must also be completed. In this conversation, Harding worries that the way her blood races when around Rook will cause the lyrium to become even more dangerous. Rook reassures her they'll figure it out together, even if that means they can't touch yet.
Note:
This conversation only becomes available once Rook has spoken to Emmrich after
Fire and Ice
and acquired
Arrangements
, left the Lighthouse, and returned.
Depending on the order of quests completed, the acquisition conversation may also be blocked by other Harding conversations, such as Harding being upset at
Solas
's impact on the
Titans
, or Harding and
Taash
's discussion of their respective powers in Taash's room. If so, complete these conversations, leave the Lighthouse, and return until Harding is in her room with a non-bubble exclamation point.
Walkthrough
Harding must once again return to the Deep Roads to rescue newfound friends and confront the sinister force haunting her.
Find the missing dwarves
Harding finds traces of the missing dwarves.
Travel to
Isana Negat
via the
Crossroads
or fast travel. Explore the initial area, including the smaller chamber on the left-hand side (containing
Miner's Talisman
and a memento), before following Harding down. Speak to her at the base of the stairs; she's discovered traces of the missing dwarves, so the
Veilguard
are in the right place.
Enter the next building, where small alcoves on either side hold a number of minor loot items and a memento. The door here leads to an old mine (with small loot items scattered about) and the Deep Roads' entrance down a rubble-choked ramp, which Harding clears with her stone magic.
Descend into the Deep Roads, sliding down a ramp and dropping down several ledges into tunnels where lyrium deposits creep along the walls.
Undead
wait in ambush in the underground grotto; once they are dead, Harding discovers another trace of recent dwarven passage (a pickaxe and pack) on the far side.
Ask Harding to move the Resonant Slab out of the team's path. In the next chamber is a strange dwarf-sized slab of stone wrapped in
red lyrium
; disturbingly, Harding senses that someone is trapped inside. Once the woman inside is freed—surprisingly still alive—she is initially confused, somehow recognizing Harding despite them having never met.
Once she connects herself, she explains she and her squad were out on patrol with
Stalgard
. When he went to investigate the sound of someone weeping in the dark, he never returned, and soon after, the rest of the squad began hearing the same weeping and whispers. As this dwarf rested, a shadow tinged with red took her into suffocating darkness—the last thing she remembers. She does not know how she survived within the rock, though Rook suggests it's something to do with dwarves being
Children of the Stone
. Harding reassures the woman as they leave; whatever lies in wait is no longer after the dwarves.
Explore the lyrium cavern
The lyrium cavern of Isana Negat.
Collect the small chest (
Old Reliable
), then continue down another series of ledges and through more Resonant Slabs. The team has now reached the Lyrium Cavern, a large area full of mining equipment and many crisscrossing cart tracks.
Across the bridge are a series of suspicious dragging scuff marks. Continue through the cavern, finding a
Worn Journal
on the left, and shoot down a locked ladder on the far side with a ranged attack. Climb up and loot the immediate platform, including the alcoves on both sides; the left one has a small hidden chest (
Jumper's Binding
) behind some wooden crates.
The easily missed left path to the smaller chest.
Clamber up the stone ledge with the potion pot, but instead of jumping across the broken bridge to the right (where the map marker leads), drop down the ledge on the far side. Tightrope across the wooden beam and sidle through a narrow doorway to reach a lyrium chest on the left (
Paragon's Silent Gaze
) and a memento to the right. Even further along the right path, up several stone ledges, is a small chest with
Plate of the Paragon Unyielding
.
Note:
The lyrium chest does not appear on the map, though all other chests in this area do.
Retrace Rook's steps to the central cavern, take the ladder again to the upper floor, and this time, jump across the broken bridge. Harding can feel another trapped dwarf nearby, so slide down the ledge and defeat the demons, including a Greater Voltaic Shade and a Sadistic Envy Demon, which lie in wait at the bottom. One of these drops the rune
Repulse
. Free the trapped dwarf, who thanks the team for the rescue and staggers away.
Open the Resonant Slab stones and proceed. The team is back in the Lyrium Cavern, though farther south than before. Ignore the chest on the left ledge for now; cross the bridge into a new cavern, the Old Shafts, and kill the undead and demons, including the Toxic Despair Demon, waiting there. Clamber up the stone ledges, including a Resonant Slab which must be elevated, and loop left along the wooden platforms overlooking the chamber. A
Hand-Drawn Map
indicates a missing dwarf named Kora may be nearby, indicating the team is on the right track.
Sidle through the narrow opening here, then destroy the planks and crates immediately on Rook's left to reveal a second hidden opening. Sidle through here as well, take the ladder down, and loot the alcove to the left before jumping across the gap with another Resonant Slab. Follow this winding passage all the way back to the Lyrium Cavern and the previously ignored chest, which holds the
Frontline Plated Mail
. While Rook can now activate a zipline back to the far side with a wall lever, it's much faster to simply drop down left of the chest and climb back up the stone ledges in the cavern.
After sidling through the first opening, continue straight, taking the ramp down as Harding senses another trapped dwarf. However, as Harding frees this man, the earth around the team rumbles ominously.
Open the Resonant Slabs to the right, emerging even further south than before in the Lyrium Cavern. Loot the small chest on the left, take out the demons, and cross the stone bridge. On the left, at the end of a wooden path, are another large chest (
Golden Casque
), some minor loot, and a
Scribbled Warning
from Stalgard.
Stalgard trapped in red lyrium.
More Resonant Slabs block the path, but as Harding moves the first layer out of the way, the second layer recedes on its own, alarming the Veilguard. Take the destroyed wooden stairs down into The Cavity to find several demons and undead, including a Bursting Rage Demon. The team is worried about fighting in the midst of red lyrium, known to be dangerous, but they must take out the demons first. On death, the Rage Demon drops the rune
Ascend
.
A freed Stalgard chastises Harding for putting herself in danger. While he is grateful for the rescue, the kidnappings were a trap designed to lure Harding here, down to whatever hunts her.
“You still came. You fools. You magnificent fools.”
―Stalgard
While his memory is clouded, he remembers hearing a cry in the shadows. He then awoke in darkness, unable to even breathe, while a voice murmured despair in his ears. He describes it as a shade or rot within the Stone and agrees it's after Harding, though he is afraid it will consume her.
Harding insists on pressing forward into the
Titan
's heart. At Rook's surprise, Harding reveals the truth she's only just realized herself—the team has been inside a dead Titan all along.
Confront the Titan's shade
The heart of a long-dead Titan.
Harding know this is a trap, but she must confront this mysterious shade anyway or risk being haunted by it forever. She also can tell that while
red lyrium
is usually
blighted
, the red lyrium surrounding them is
not
; it is only angry.
In the next room, it becomes undeniable that the Veilguard is, in fact, standing inside a long-dead Titan: its heart, spine, rib cage, and veins make up the entirety of the chamber. The sounds of the heart beating can be heard as the team moves forward, and Harding can feel that is where she must go.
However, as they reach the central clearing, the nearby stone erupts, and out of it bursts The Titan's Shade. It looks exactly like Harding, though it's made of red lyrium, and it demands Harding look at her and "remember" as it tears the stone platform on which the team stands up into the air. At the last second, Harding uses her stone magic to divide the platform further, sending Rook and the other companion safely back to earth.
The enraged Titan's Shade.
The Shade continues to rage, lamenting that "they broke us into a million pieces, and this is all that's left." When it attacks Harding with red lyrium shards, she throws up a stone wall just in time, overwhelmed by the anger and pain she can feel pouring from the Shade. Even worse, Harding realizes at least some of this is her own anger and pain reflected; the Shade declares Harding cast it out.
Harding tells the Shade she couldn't bear the strength of her own emotions, and she apologizes to the Shade as she approaches. As she cups the Shade's cheek, it grieves that the world was built on the wreckage of the Titans, and all they have left is vengeance. It seizes her wrist, and Harding desperately shouts at the group to run.
Rook now must fight off a number of undead while trying to reach Harding. She can be seen warring with the Shade on an towering platform in the back, surrounded by red and blue light. Rook begs Harding to stop before they're all killed, but Harding—now speaking both as herself and a Titan—seethes that the elves stole the Titans' dreams to still the earth.
Wrath of the Stone.
A unique
golem
enemy, Wrath of the Stone, bursts from the ground. It fights with similar moves to ogres and other golems; it is vulnerable to necrosis and resistant to lightning. It can also cause lines and cones of red lyrium to erupt from the ground with swings of its massive hammer. Like all golems, it can dive into the earth and emerge in several places, attacking Rook from below.
Once it is dead, Rook manages to climb up to where Harding wars with herself atop the stone pillar. The Shade is gone, absorbed into Harding; her eyes flicker blue and red as she remembers what the elves did to the Titans and demands the world remember as well.
Rook can now encourage Harding to either honor the Titans' anger or ease it with her compassion. Should Rook choose to honor their anger, Rook validates Harding's pain and anger as justified; the elves ripped out the Titans' souls and doomed them to madness. However, Harding must not let the anger control her; she must make it hers instead.
Should Rook choose to ease the anger with compassion, they remind Harding she is more than this rage; she believes the world is beautiful and that people are good. They ask Harding to remember who she is behind the anger.
Harding, almost completely overcome by the power of her emotions, struggles to regain control of herself, but Rook and the other teammate will place their hands on Harding's shoulders, grounding her with the contact and helping her recover herself. Regardless of the choice made, Harding realizes that the dwarves have persisted despite the Titans' deaths and the elves' actions—they will thrive in spite of them.
If Rook is romancing Harding, they emerge alone atop the pillar and will embrace Harding despite the danger of lyrium poisoning. As they sink to the ground, reassuring Harding with their last conscious breaths that she is not alone, Harding will place her hand on Rook's chest, calling on her stone magic to siphon away the lyrium and save them. As Rook returns to consciousness, they weakly laugh that they knew Harding couldn't resist their moves, and Harding smiles as her eyes return to their normal brown.
Collect the big chest (
Plate of the Paragon Unyielding
, and the only loot in the chamber), then speak to Harding to return to the
Lighthouse
.
Debrief at the Lighthouse
Back at the Lighthouse, Harding discusses these events with Rook. She reports Stalgard and the others have returned to Kal-Sharok to spread the news.
If Harding honored the Titans' rage:
She explains she was always afraid of her anger, feeling it was better to act nice even when she didn't want to be. Pushing away the Titans' anger caused it to fester and become destructive, but now it is part of her, along with everything else she has gained from the Titans. While she thinks she might always be angry, she is now no longer afraid of it. She is glad she wasn't alone and thanks Rook for being there for her.
If Harding embraced her compassion:
Rook asks how Harding is. She says she used to worry she would lose herself to the Titans, but realizes she wouldn't be here without them. She now understands she can't lose herself to something that has always been part of her. She wants to stop the elven gods, but after that, she's ready to take life one step at a time and see where it takes her. She thanks Rook for their endless support and for being there when she needed them most.
At the conclusion of this conversation, the quest completes, and Harding will unlock
Hero of the Veilguard
status.
Rewards
Completing the quest yields:
Bond increase with Harding and the companion taken on the quest
Hero of the Veilguard
status for Harding, along with:
The heroic armor
Inquisition Spotter
and the skill
Heart of the Titan
, if the Titan Shade's anger was contained
The heroic armor
Titan's Vengeance
and the skill
The Stone's Vengeance
, if the Titan Shade's anger was honored
Results
Harding has sensed through the Stone that the sinister force haunting her has turned its anger on innocent dwarves. Unable to ignore their plight, Harding must return to the Deep Roads to attempt a rescue and confront the mysterious shadows hounding her steps.
The team has returned to the Deep Roads to help Harding attempt a rescue of dwarves who have fallen victim to a sinister force that has been shadowing Harding.
Harding returned to the Deep Roads to rescue innocent dwarves who had fallen prey to a shade from the Stone. Confronting the shade, Harding learned that it was born of her own misplaced rage at the fate of her Titan ancestors. Acceptance of the truth and her own shadow self has given Harding complete ownership of her Titan gift. What this means for her remains to be seen.

======================================================================
PAGE: The High Dragon's Champion
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_High_Dragon's_Champion
======================================================================

Quest
The High Dragon's Champion
Location
The Gauntlet
Start
Kolgrim
End
Urn of Andraste
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The High Dragon's Champion
is a quest that is part of the
Urn of Sacred Ashes
quest line. It can either be accepted or refused.
Walkthrough
Meeting the leader of the cult,
Father Kolgrim
, may lead to Kolgrim offering a task to defile the ashes with a
vial of
Andraste's
blood
during
A Test of Faith
or you refusing the task. Refusal results in the final cult battle.
Accepting Kolgrim's task and defiling the ashes is the only way to unlock the
Reaver
specialization and obtain the
Sacrilegious
achievement. Afterwards, you can get a second opportunity to for the final cult battle or stay sided with the cult.
Whether the Warden refuses to listen to Kolgrim's proposition, or accepts the task, or even if the Warden accepts the task verbally but does not follow through with it, the companions in the party (and some even if they are not in the active party) will react accordingly:
If the Warden sides with Kolgrim:
Morrigan approves (
+2
) or
Morrigan approves (
+3
)
Shale approves (
+2
) or
Shale approves (
+4
), or
Shale approves (
+11
) if you let it talk you into it. Note that this approval takes place immediately when you accept the blood from Kolgrim.
To get
Shale approves (
+11
), refuse Kolgrim's offer, refuse it again when he says, "I will not make this offer again," then when Shale says, "Is there a reason it refuses?", choose "You really think I should go along with it?" followed by "Maybe you're right."
Zevran approves (
+2
)
Sten disapproves (
-3
)
Alistair disapproves (
-5
)
May be avoided through Persuasion
Oghren disapproves (
-5
)
Leliana
is, of course, opposed to the defiling, but will not react until the deed is actually done. Her reaction depends on various parameters, although it is not immediate when you accept Kolgrim's task. An unhardened Leliana will attack you immediately if she witnesses the defiling. Even a
hardened
Leliana will still object to the defiling of the Urn, but if the following conditions are met, she may be present at the defiling and, instead of reaching her
crisis
, simply
Leliana disapproves (
-10
):
Leliana must be hardened;
The Warden must have a high intimidate skill (Coercion + Strength bonus = 75 or higher, where each point in Coercion is equal to 25 intimidation points. Thus 3 points in coercion should do the trick regardless of what your character's strength score is);
The Warden must have passed through the flames after leaving all items at the altar. If the Warden fails this test and kills The Guardian before defiling the urn, the following dialogue option to intimidate Leliana won't appear;
The player must select the
"Before you do anything stupid, remember who I am."
dialogue option when she calls the Warden a "fiend".
If she is not present at the defiling, Leliana will confront the Warden at the Camp. Unless the Warden passes a high persuasion check (75+) to convince her the Ashes are safe she will leave.
Wynne
will turn hostile and attack if the ashes are defiled and she is present in the party, although she will not react until the deed is actually done.
If she is not present, she will initiate a conversation the next time the Warden enters the Party Camp and leave.
Note:
Wynne's crisis point can be avoided if she is not acquired until after defiling the ashes, but the Party Camp must be visited between defiling the ashes and recruiting her in order to let Leliana's argument happen; this will clear the plot flag that would have triggered Wynne's crisis as well. On PS3 Wynne will leave regardless of when she is recruited. However, her cutscene can be pushed down the queue by some cutscenes such as Alistair's cutscene which occurs after delivering the ashes allowing the Warden to return to camp under certain conditions.
Leliana's cutscene can conflict with Wynne's if Leliana was not present. If Leliana is convinced the Ashes are safe by passing a persuasion check, Wynne's confrontation will not happen. On PS3, the cutscene still occurs the next time the Warden returns to camp.
Zevran approves (
+2
) if the Warden listens to Kolgrim's offer and accepts it (even if the Warden won't follow through with the defiling);
Zevran
can be persuaded that killing Kolgrim is a better option and his approval rating won't change.
Note
the dialogue option to persuade Zevran calls Kolgrim 'shifty' and will trigger the cultists to attack.
If the Warden fights Kolgrim:
The fight can be tricky at first, but try to kill the Cultist mages and have someone keep the melee attackers on them (someone with a lot of health)
Oghren approves (
+2
) if "shut up, Oghren" and then "I'll take my chances with the arrows" is selected after Kolgrim warns of a "rain of arrows" awaiting the Warden if they so dared attack "Andraste"
Shale disapproves (
-10
) but if you declare to Shale directly, "It is my decision", it will answer that there is nothing to say against this logic and there is no approval lost;
Morrigan
disapproves;
Morrigan
's and
Shale
's negative reaction can be avoided by picking a fight with Kolgrim early on in the dialogue, without asking about his offer; alternatively, if the dialogue leads them to express their opinion, a successful Persuasion check may reduce their loss of approval:
Shale disapproves (
-5
)
Morrigan approves (
0
) if started within the first two dialogues. If
Sten
is in the party, and you let Kolgrim explain his plan, Sten will comment negatively. If in the ensuing dialogue option you change your mind,
Morrigan
won't disapprove.
Wynne
,
Leliana
and
Alistair
voice their opposition to defiling the urn, but fighting Kolgrim does not result in a change of approval.
If you listen to all that Kolgrim has to say and changes your mind in the end, Sten disapprove (-3) and the fight begins. If you start the fight before Kolgrim explains his plan completely, there is no approval change to Sten.
Note:
This list is incomplete - the reaction of other companions to either option has not been reported yet. Please add the approval changes you experience.
If/when you fight Kolgrim, he will drop his axe,
Faith's Edge
, and depending on the time the fight took place,
Kolgrim's Horn
(
pc
only).
Note:
Kolgrim can be slightly easier to fight if tricked into thinking you will help because on the mountaintop he has fewer allies.
If you want to fight Kolgrim outside without losing any approval on the above-mentioned dialogue choices simply choose the dialogue option that you want to see the risen Andraste yourself, which will take you outside where you meet Kolgrim again. He will have some help there too which includes another drake and will earn you a 7th drake scale.
Rewards
750 XP
Reaver
specialization
Faith's Edge
(if you slay Kolgrim)
Kolgrim's Horn
(if you slay Kolgrim before the mountain top)
Notes
To unlock the
Reaver
specialization and obtain the
Sacrilegious
without keeping the results in your game, be sure you have saved before you initiate the conversation; you can unlock the achievement and specialization and then revert to the save. Your rewards will be preserved even if you then choose another course of action.
Bugs
Originally if you sided with the cult and you previously killed the shopkeeper, you were supposed to get a new shopkeeper and new store containing some currently unobtainable items.
pc
This can be restored via
Qwinn's Fixpack
.
See also
The Urn of Sacred Ashes
A Test of Faith

======================================================================
PAGE: The Hungry Deserter
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Hungry_Deserter
======================================================================

Quest
The Hungry Deserter
Location
Ostagar
Quest Giver
Prisoner
End
Prisoner
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Near the infirmary in
Ostagar
, there is a set of suspended cages. Inside one of them is a prisoner, with a guard standing nearby. Talking to the prisoner before heading into the
Korcari Wilds
triggers the
side quest
The Hungry Deserter
.
Walkthrough
Note:
This quest can be started before or after completing
Tainted Blood
and
The Grey Wardens' Cache
in the Korcari Wilds
The prisoner will beg for
food
and
water
and will explain that the nearby guard has some. If
Alistair
has previously been found and is in the party when speaking with the prisoner, then this quest can influence his
approval
. Alternatively, the quest can be completed before speaking with Alistair to avoid any impact on approval.
The Warden can then:
Kill the prisoner without asking for any more details, generating
Alistair disapproves (
-5
).
Help the prisoner without asking for any more details, generating
Alistair approves (
+2
).
Ask why the prisoner is locked up.
Ask why the party should help.
The prisoner will offer the
Key to Mages' Chest
in return for help and explain that he stole it from a
mage
in the camp by getting him drunk, and was later caught wandering around the camp at night and locked up as it was assumed he was trying to desert. He swallowed the key, and it has since "come back into his possession."
To obtain the food and water from the guard, The Warden can:
Persuade the guard to forgo his lunch (requires 1 rank of
Coercion
).
Steal the food and water from the guard (requires
Stealing
).
Buy the food and water for 10
.
To obtain the
Key to Mages' Chest
, The Warden can
Obtain food and water from the guard, generating
Alistair approves (
+1
).
Kill the prisoner, generating
Alistair disapproves (
-5
) and netting 100XP.
Steal the key from the prisoner.
The key opens the chest by the Magi Encampment, next to the
tranquil mage
. It cannot be used until returning to Ostagar after completing the
Tainted Blood
and
The Grey Wardens' Cache
quests in the Korcari Wilds. At that point, the tranquil mage will have moved: prior to this, attempting to open the chest will only prompt him to object.
If the key is not used before entering the
Tower of Ishal
, the quest will be marked as complete. However, the key can still be used during the
Return to Ostagar
DLC
, when the chest will contain the
Corrupted Magister's Staff
in addition to the loot available during the main quest. If the key is used as part of this quest, then it will
not
be possible to open the Mages' Chest or obtain the Corrupted Magister's Staff during
Return to Ostagar
.
Note:
If the Key to the Mages' Chest is not obtained as part of this quest, either because The Warden never spoke to the prisoner, or because The Warden fed the prisoner before finding out about the key, then the key can be looted from the prisoner's corpse during
Return to Ostagar
.
Rewards
Key to Mages' Chest
Loot from Mages' Chest
:
Acolyte's Staff
Apprentice Cowl
Enchanter's Arming Cap
Flask
Health Poultice
Lesser Injury Kit
Lesser Spirit Balm
(2)
Money
15
If Mages' Chest is looted during
Return to Ostagar
, the above plus
:
Corrupted Magister's Staff
Bugs
Be aware that if the key is used before the Battle of Ostagar, then there is no way to obtain the
Corrupted Magister's Staff
during
Return to Ostagar
.
pc
If you want to solve the quest and also be able to come back later,
this mod
can help.
pc
Alternatively,
Dain's Fixpack
solves this bug by preventing the key from ever being removed, allowing the chest to be revisited without console commands.
If you obtain the key but do not use it before the
Battle of Ostagar
, then the quest will be marked as complete in the journal, but the text will not reflect the actual steps taken.
ps3
This may fail to occur, resulting in the quest being stuck in "Current Quests" even should you return to get the chest during
Return to Ostagar
.
If you are too far away from the Prisoner (e.g. standing by the guard), sometimes the prisoner cannot be interacted with, even though the cursor shows him as clickable. Be sure to get right next to the cage so that you can interact with him.
If you persuade the guard to give you the food and water, give them to the prisoner, and then steal from the guard, you will get another food and water but can do nothing with them.
The only way you get XP from this quest is if you A) give him food without getting the key, or B) kill the Prisoner.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Ideal Romance
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Ideal_Romance
======================================================================

Quest
The Ideal Romance
Location
Skyhold
Hinterlands
Val Royeaux
Start
Cassandra
End
Cassandra
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
The Ideal Romance
is a romance-specific
companion quest
for
Cassandra
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
When it comes to romance,
Cassandra
prefers the ideals. Someone who sweeps her off her feet with
candles
, poetry, and flowers. Track these things down for her.
Acquisition
Once in
Skyhold
, if you have gained enough approval, flirted at every possible opportunity, and completed her previous companion quests, Cassandra informs you that your advances haven’t gone unnoticed. She however remains conflicted as she wants an ideal storybook romance and feels that this isn't possible with the Inquisitor. If you still insist on courting her this way, the quest will unlock.
Note:
You do not need to complete
all
of her companion quests to acquire "The Ideal Romance". A high approval and the completion of "
Guilty Pleasures
" can be enough to trigger the quest. It is, however, suggested that you complete the other quests as they give high approval ratings.
Walkthrough
You will need to find three ideal
gifts
consisting of flowers, candles and a poetry book:
Talk to the
elven
gardener in the southern most courtyard of the
Summer Bazaar
in
Val Royeaux
about flowers.
Purchase
The Most Romantic Candle in Thedas
from
Pierre-Marie's
shop in the
Summer Bazaar
located in
Val Royeaux
.
Talk to the
Dwarven Bookseller
in
Redcliffe Village
about poetry books.
Track down the merchant's missing caravan in the western part of the
Hinterlands
and retrieve a poetry book.
Return to Cassandra and ask to speak privately.
Results
The relationship between Cassandra and the Inquisitor becomes official.
Bugs
There is a bug in the quest where the Redcliffe bookseller may not talk to you about the poetry book. While you can still get the book by going to the Forest Camp in the Hinterlands and finding the broken caravan, the journal will not recognize the quest as completed. In some cases, restoring a save and talking to the bookseller last (the candles, and flowers first) will fix the bug entirely.
The initial conversation happens to take place in a possibly restricted area of
Skyhold
, that only
gets accessible after
completing a
main plot quest
. If that hasn't been done yet, the Inquisitor will be unable to reenter Skyhold's Main Hall by normal means – through a door. One solution is to use the map and travel; another would be to simply jump over the mural and on the roof, then down into the garden since falling damage is disabled in Skyhold.
Gallery
The Redcliffe merchant's caravan of books

======================================================================
PAGE: The Imperial Enchanter
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Imperial_Enchanter
======================================================================

Quest
The Imperial Enchanter
Start
Val Royeaux
End
Ghislain Estate
Rewards
177 XP
Influence 80
Power 1
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
The Imperial Enchanter
is a
companion quest
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
, in which the Inquisitor is given an option to recruit
Vivienne
.
Vivienne de Fer, the
First Enchanter
of
Montsimmard
has extended an invitation to her salon at the
chateau
of Duke
Bastien de Ghislain
.
Acquisition
After the
encounter
with Lord Seeker
Lucius
and Revered Mother
Hevara
in
Val Royeaux
, a mage approaches (or can be approached) and delivers
the Inquisitor
an invitation from the First Enchanter.
Walkthrough
Travel to Duke Bastien's
estate
via the world map.
The Inquisitor will join a party with
Orlesian
nobility, and be quickly caught in dialogue with a pair of guests. The man will express that Gaspard will lose the
Orlesian Civil War
. The woman will be more interested in the Inquisitor, asking if the stories about them are true.
Once an answer is given, Marquis
Alphonse
will walk down the stairs and join the conversation, accusing the
Inquisition
of being nothing but a revolutionary movement filled with political outcasts pretending to answer a higher calling, but in fact only interested in grabbing power and will challenge the Inquisitor to step outside for a duel.
As he reaches back for his sword, Vivienne freezes the marquis and walks down the stairs before the Inquisitor can even answer. She will ask the Inquisitor how she should handle the offending marquis, to which the Inquisitor can ask her to kill him, let him go, or that (s)he does not care.
Later, in a more private conversation, Vivienne offers to join the Inquisition. One romance dialogue is available in the wheel, and the Inquisitor can inquire about her motives in joining the Inquisition. Finally, you can decide whether or not to recruit her.
Codex entry: Vivienne: Madame de Fer
will unlock upon completion of the quest.
Rewards
177 XP
Influence 80
Power 1
Results
Vivienne joins the Inquisition (if accepted)
Unlocks the
war table
operation
Truth or Dare: The Imperial Court
Codex entries
Vivienne: Madame de Fer
Note texts
Invitation: First Enchanter Vivienne
Bugs
In the final dialogue wheel with Vivienne, the question
"What are loyal mages?"
does not disappear properly unless the question is asked as the last
(Investigate)
option.
After completing the quest, it doesn't appear in the completed quest section of the journal.

The Inner Circle quests
are types of quests in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
. They are similar to
companion quests
found in previous games except that the quests are for both
companions
and
advisors
.
Note:
* means that the quest is only available if the person is being romanced.

Quest
The Key to Your Heart
Location
Hawke Estate
Quest Giver
Anders
End
Anders
Appearances
Dragon Age II
The Key to Your Heart
is a
companion quest
for
Anders
at the beginning of Act 3 in
Dragon Age II
. It is part of
Check on Anders
, and is not marked in the journal.
Acquisition
This quest is only available if
Hawke
is in a
romance
with Anders. Go to the
Hawke Estate
to start his companion quest, Check on Anders.
Walkthrough
A star option in the
dialogue wheel
("I have a gift for you") appears twice upon talking to Anders. Hawke will give him a key to the
cellar
of the Hawke Estate. This is the only time the key can be given.
Rewards
250 XP (quest completion)

======================================================================
