======================================================================
PAGE: The Nobles' Feast
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins quests, Origin story quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Nobles'_Feast
======================================================================

Quest
The Nobles' Feast
Location
Orzammar
Previous
None
Next
A Noble Expedition
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Nobles' Feast
is a main quest of the
Dwarf Noble Origin
.
King
Endrin Aeducan
has organized a
feast
and a celebratory Proving in order to celebrate his second child's appointment as
Orzammar
's newest commander. Before the feast is held, the Dwarf Noble has the opportunity to explore the city.
Walkthrough
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Upon conversing with
Gorim Saelac
, the Warden receives
Codex entry: Orzammar Politics
and
Codex entry: Dwarven Faith
. Don't hasten to leave the bedroom just yet; of the interactive items aligned against the wall, from right to left: Vanity, which yields 5
; a crystal ("containing an image of the city of Orzammar"), which yields
Codex entry: The City of Orzammar
; a chest, which gives a Dagger (iron), Mace, Maul, Shortbow, one lesser health poultice, and a small grease trap. Finally, at the left side of your bed is a bust of King Endrin, your father, which naturally yields
Codex entry: King Endrin Aeducan
.
Now that you are finished with your room, exit it. On the Warden's immediate right is a book shelf, the top shelf of which yields
Codex entry: Orzammar History: Chapter One
. Continue down the hall; the Warden will be greeted by two guards. Notice that Prince
Trian
's room is locked. Turn right to see that a mistress named
Rica Brosca
, a
noble hunter
and sister of the
Dwarf Commoner
, hurriedly disappears into
Bhelen
's room. You may leave her alone or you may enter Bhelen's room and confront her. In Bhelen's room, from right to left, you will find a letter
Codex entry: A Letter from Rica
, a pile of books
Codex entry: House Aeducan, Shield of Orzammar
, a locked chest (11
), and Armorie which yields a
Dwarven Merchant's Belt
.
───────
Visit the Merchants
After leaving the Royal Palace, you come across two dwarves arguing with each other. Upon investigation of the incident, you find that
Bruntin Vollney
is unhappy with Scholar
Gertek
who published works detailing how House Vollney came to be. If you hear out the scholar's side of the story, he tells the Dwarf Noble that everything he has written has been recorded in the
Shaperate
, and is thus fact, despite Bruntin's hatred of it as Bruntin believes Vollney was more than a man. The Dwarf Noble then has the option of siding with House Vollney and covering up the hated truth, or siding with the scholar.
Side with Scholar Gertek by ordering Vollney to leave, they can ask for a reward of 3
25
or the
book
he wrote for the
Paragon
Aeducan
. Then Gorim will ask if the Dwarf Noble wants to have him killed and if the Dwarf Noble wants to, it can be made publically or secretly. Gertek can also be asked and he will be also in favour of killing Bruntin.
Side with Scholar Gertek by ordering him to say more positive things about Paragon Vollney which will also satisfy Bruntin.
Side with Bruntin Vollney by ordering him not to kill scholar but rather drive him off.
Side with Bruntin Vollney by allowing him to kill Gertek.
Gorim will comment that the
House Vollney
is now in big trouble if the Dwarf Noble sided with Vollney as Gertek enjoyed the patronage of several major noble
houses
such as House Meino. Further afterwards in the plot,
Dwarven Scholar
, will comment and possibly give more information to the Dwarf Noble of what happened afterwards.
Then, the Dwarf noble can travel the
Diamond Quarter
and converse with the
Silk Merchant
and the
Armor Merchant
. Eventually the Dwarf Noble will meet their siblings, Trian and Bhelen. Trian is the rightful heir to the throne, yet his abrasive manner has made him appear like he would be a horrible king. Whispers tell of the favored child of King Endrin Aeducan, namely the Dwarf Noble, taking the crown upon the death of the king. Trian will order you to go to the feast held in your honor, but you can tell him off or comply to his demands.
The quest choices in
Dragon Age Keep
In the Diamond Quarter you can also encounter two women,
Mardy
and
Teli
. Upon conversing with them you will find out that they are
noble hunters
. Then you have the choice to let them be or have Gorim thrown them out of the Diamond Quarter. A male Dwarf Noble also has the chance to sleep with Mardy or both of them the upcoming night. Choosing this option will lead to the
Of Noble Birth
quest later on.
Lastly, a point of interest for the Dwarf Noble is to converse with the
Weapon Merchant
. He had tasked a messenger to bring a finely crafted dagger to the young prince, but Bhelen had tricked the messenger into trying to get Trian to hand it to the Dwarf Noble. The
Noble's Dagger
is a very impressive dagger, however it might send the wrong message due to it being a kingly gift, indicating that the Dwarf Noble may also want to assume the throne of Orzammar after King Endrin's death. The weapon merchant also implies such thing, and the Dwarf Noble can order Gorim to kill him for these words, take the dagger regardless of what it could mean, or refuse to take it.
───────
Attend the Provings
The Proving Grounds
At the end of the Diamond Quarter is a royal escort that King Endrin had issued for the Dwarf Noble should they deign to travel to the
Proving arena
. With so few merchants being allowed to sell their wares in the Diamond Quarter, King Endrin feared that the Dwarf Noble would be harassed by countless merchants.
When the Dwarf Noble travels to the Proving arena, they can then talk to the Proving Master and tell him that they want to fight in the Proving to honor the warriors and test their skill. There are four fights total, and none of them are too difficult.
The first fight is against Aller
Bemot
, youngest son of Lord Bemot.
The second fight is against
Adal Helmi
, daughter of
Lady Helmi
.
The third fight is against Ser
Blackstone
, a
Deep Roads
veteran.
The fourth and final fight is against
Frandlin Ivo
of
House Ivo
.
Gorim states that House Ivo is relatively small, but Frandlin might be the one to raise up their status amongst the dwarven people. After defeating Frandlin, you are awarded the
Proving Helm
, commissioned by King Endrin himself for the champion of the
Provings
. You can choose to either hand it to Frandlin or keep it for yourself or just refuse to get it. With the Provings over with, you can now choose to head back to the Royal Palace, where your commission as commander awaits, and nobles bicker over who gets what and what deals should be had, provided that you haven't been to the Feast already.
Note:
When facing
Adal Helmi
, a male Dwarf Noble can claim to be offended that he is asked to fight against a woman and can drop the provings. The Proving Master will apologize as he didn't mean to offend the Dwarf Noble and mention that
Lady Helmi
demanded it.
Aller Bemot
In the Proving Grounds, instead of fighting, you can tell the Proving Master that you wish to watch the provings. If you choose this option, you'll see from a balcony Frandlin Ivo and
Ser
Blackstone dueling at the arena while a
Proving Trainer
will also join your company. If you speak with him, Gorim will identify him as the trainer who is responsible for training the higher houses of the
warrior caste
and the lower houses of the noble caste who cannot afford to have their own house trainer, in dual weapon combat. If you ask Gorim if he was trained by him, he will uneasily mention that his father considered it would be useless for him to learn duel-style fighting as Gorim was expected to become the Dwarf Noble's
second
. The Proving Trainer will also admit that the trainer of
House Aeducan
is much better than him and will inform the Dwarf Noble about an incident that happened a few weeks ago in the Provings. If the Dwarf Noble asks him to continue, he will tell the story of the
Dwarf Commoner
and also mention that this incident caused a large uproar in the warrior caste. Once the discussion is finished the Dwarf Noble can talk to the royal escort to return to the Diamond Quarter. If they return to the Proving grounds before this quest is completed, the Proving Master will ask whether the Dwarf Noble wishes to congratulate the winner of the Provings or not. If you accept, Frandlin Ivo will come and bow. Then the Dwarf Noble can congratulate or humiliate him; however the latter option will earn the Proving Master's disapproval.
───────
The Feast
The throne room is the Dwarf Noble's next stop. Here, many nobles await their turn to ask King Endrin about any deals they might be concocting. If Bruntin was publicly killed by the Dwarf Noble, a dwarven noblewoman will mention how well you handled the situation. Usually, they only care about something when it involves a potential fortune. Not only are the nobles among the crowd gathered in the feast, but
Duncan
of the
Grey Wardens
is here, at the behest of King Endrin. Talking to him yields several codex entries.
Another person of interest is Lord
Ronus Dace
, a member of
House Dace
. He asks for a moment of your time to tell you of a mission of his. He wants to help out the
surface
caste, or in the minds of most dwarves, the "sky-touched". He feels that they should be allowed to return to caste and clan upon returning to Orzammar. When probing him further he says that truthfully he couldn't care less for the plight of the surfacers, but that it's for his wife that he is going through all of this. You can choose to help him, and in return he will provide either information or a
Note of Credit
that he says is worth 100
. However, because of the way merchants work, the paper which has a listed value of 100 sovereigns can only be sold for 25 sovereigns.
Yet all is not as it seems to be in the matters of dwarven politics.
Lady Helmi
of
House Helmi
calls you over to say that you would be making a foolish decision to side with Lord Dace. Recently he invested a great deal of money in an expedition that went horribly awry, and his want for a better life for the surfacers is just a way to regain his losses. Many houses would have to pay off the debts of the money he invested, Aeducan among them. So it falls to the Dwarf Noble to decide whether they should help Lord Dace in his scheme for a paltry amount of money that might in the end make House Aeducan repay House Dace, or if they want to publicly humiliate him among the other nobles. Other reasons the Dwarf Noble would have to support Lord Dace's scheme is because they genuinely believe that surface dwarves deserve to have their castes restored. If the Dwarf Noble abruptly ends the conversation with Lady Helmi before she informs them about Dace's exact scheme, and then they confront Lord Dace, the latter will claim that Lady Dace is angry with him after he uncovered her scheme to use orphan children as forced labor for her house. This answer will satisfy the Dwarf Noble as they don't know his exact schemes.
Note:
Additionally, after receiving the details from Lady Helmi, the Dwarf Noble can be offended by the scheming of Lord Dace against them and their
House
and challenge him in an Honor Proving. House Dace will be represented by Lord Dace's son,
Mandar Dace
. It's a fight to the death which will be watched by King Endrin Aeducan along with several other major lords and when the Dwarf Noble wins the duel, Lord Dace will apologize and leave to mourn for his son.
Note:
Three unnamed Nobles who are present in the feast will also have some comments if you side with Lord Dace or choose to humiliate him when he asks the Dwarf Noble about the surface dwarves.
In any case, the Dwarf Noble in the end attends the feast held in their naming as the new Commander of Orzammar in which Lord
Harrowmont
of
House Harrowmont
, Lord Meino of
House Meino
, Lord Bemot of
House Bemot
and Duncan of the Grey Wardens are also present. During the ritual, if the Dwarf Noble agreed to Lord Dace's plan they will ask the opinion of the commander about the surface dwarves. If the Dwarf Noble sides with Lord Dace, the only answer which would satisfy him is the third, that surface dwarves should have their rights restored. Alternatively, if you betray him, and after the ceremony the Dwarf Noble talk to him, Ronus Dace proposes to scheme together next time or if the Dwarf Noble insults him he vows to not forget that insult.
After the ceremony, King Endrin Aeducan will also ask the Dwarf Noble to find Trian and tell him that their father wishes to speak with him. If you fought in the Provings or watched them and conversed with the
Proving Trainer
, Trian will be located along with his brother in his room, in the
Royal Palace
. Otherwise he will be located in the
Proving Grounds
. After you speak with him and inform him about your father's request, he will leave. Bhelen will then inform you that Trian is plotting against you because your popularity within Orzammar and the
Assembly
is very high. Then, you can either choose to agree with what Bhelen said and kill Trian or refuse to take any action.
You can also ask Gorim for his opinion and he will mention that everyone knows that Trian would make a terrible king but he has enough support in the Assembly to keep it in a lockdown, so killing him now will make the house stronger and avoid needless bloodshed later.
Note:
This is your last chance to sell the items in your inventory. After you tell Gorim that it is time to have a rest, the next quest will start while having no chance to sell your items to the merchants before they are being taken from you.
Result
The celebration day ends and
A Noble Expedition
quest starts.
Bugs
If the Dwarf Noble challenges the honor of Lord Ronus Dace and fights
Mandar Dace
, the quest will update in the same way by letting Ronus believe that the Dwarf Noble would speak for his cause but instead ridiculing him in front of the other nobles during the ceremony.
If the Dwarf Noble will decide to fight Mandar Dace before going to Provings and speaking with Proving master, then when Dwarf Noble actually will go there and choose to fight, he will stuck on the arena after the first fight with Aller Bemot forever.
Furthermore, when the Dwarf Noble challenges the honor of Ronus Dace, but then retreats and does not fight Mandar, Ronus will still show up in the ritual and still ask the Dwarf Noble about the surface dwarves, as if he didn't know that the Dwarf Noble is knowledgeable about his scheme.
pc
If you fight in the Provings, the names of the defeated fighters may be shown on the floor of the arena even if they are not physically there. This is only possible by pressing TAB (default key).
If you watch the Provings but leave the Orzammar Proving (via the Royal Escort) before the Proving Trainer has not been conversed and you return, to the upper level, and you click on the Trainer, you will be transferred on the lower level and initiate a conversation with him. Afterwards you won't have a way to get out, however.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Odd Ensign
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Odd_Ensign
======================================================================

Quest
The Odd Ensign
Start
Chateau Haine Interior
End
The Retreat
Appearances
Dragon Age II
-
Mark of the Assassin
The Odd Ensign
is a
companion quest
in the
Mark of the Assassin
DLC
for
Dragon Age II
if
Carver Hawke
is brought along as a companion.
A
Chevalier's
pennant has been found. Be on the lookout for others.
Acquisition
This quest is obtained inside of
Chateau Haine
. There is a pennant hanging on a wall in the Chateau. Taking the pennant will trigger some dialog from
Hawke
and begin the quest.
Walkthrough
There are a total of three pennants you need to find.
Chateau Haine Interior
: West on the map in the Chateau. At the top of the stairs, just past the cook's alcove where you pick up the quest
Hard to Stomach
. This one is red and black.
Chateau Haine Interior
: The Great Hall Gallery. You know you're in the right place if you overhear a conversation between 2 people in regards to the cheese tasting of Despair. This one is blue and triggers more dialog from Hawke.
The Dungeons
: The Middle of the map in the northern cell (off of the large southern room where you fight the
harlequins
). This one is tan and triggers dialog from Carver.
Once the third pennant has been found a treasure cache will be marked on the map. Its location is found in
The Retreat
.
Rewards
Amulet of the Enthusiast

======================================================================
PAGE: The Palate of a Griffon
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests

======================================================================

Quest
The Palate of a Griffon
Type
Companion quest
Location
Arlathan Forest
Previous
A Friend in Need
Next
Hope on the Wing
or, if missed,
The Profane and the Lost
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The Palate of a Griffon
is a
companion quest
for
Davrin
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Acquisition
Speak to Davrin at the
Lighthouse
after completing
A Friend in Need
and
Unwanted Guests
.
Walkthrough
Davrin's  idea of a relaxing picnic in Arlathan Forest goes slightly awry thanks to a  culinary mishap.
Help Davrin relax
Speak to Davrin at the quest location in the Hidden Grove of
Arlathan Forest
. He's set up a picnic for
Rook
at the suggestion of
Antoine
and
Evka
, who've recommended a break considering everything they've been through lately. Emmrich has helped Davrin brew a
gingerwort truffle
tea, and he wants to try it out.
Rook reports it's gingery with garlic and hints of mushrooms, along with something else, to which Assan helpfully replies, "Worms." Rook agrees at the earthy taste, then is shocked at the realization that Assan is now speaking. Davrin, however, can't hear it, and suggests the magical effects of the tea may be stronger on Rook than others.
Regardless, Rook thanks Davrin for the outing and the company, and the quest concludes.
Results
After all the  team has been through, Davrin wants to spend some time out in nature for its  own sake. He is looking forward to picnicking in the glades of Arlathan.
Approval
After completion
Davrin Approves

======================================================================
PAGE: The Private Collection
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Private_Collection
======================================================================

Quest
The Private Collection
Location
Denerim Market District
South Wing of Bann Franderel's Estate
Quest Giver
Slim Couldry
End
Slim Couldry
Previous
A Fistful of Silver
Next
The Tears of Andraste
Related
Crime Wave
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Private Collection
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
. It is the third burglary of the
Crime Wave
quest line.
Acquisition
One of the richest men in the
kingdom
, Bann Franderel of
West Hill
, is organizing a grand ball in his
estate
in
Denerim
in which he will display some of his treasures such as paintings of old masters, the arnaments of
King Calenhad
and much more. By paying 10
to
Slim Couldry
a new marker named
Bann of West Hill's Estate
will become available for you to travel to on the map of
Denerim
.
If Slim Couldry offers to
the Warden
the pick-pocketing quest line as well, then this quest can be obtained only after the side-quest
The Traitor's Crown
is completed. Otherwise it can be initiated after
The Landsmeet
has begun.
Walkthrough
The south wing of Bann Franderel's Estate
During this visit only part of the estate can be explored. The Warden arrives from a trapdoor, and accesses the south wing. Along the way a room with a lever is encountered. Moving the lever does nothing during this visit.
Once the map marker (a white X) is reached, the quest log is updated. The whole thing was a trap! The area is now filled with guards and a number of traps, not to mention a couple of
apostates
.
Even though the quest update states that "You'll have to fight or sneak your way out now", the only option is to fight, unless at least one party member was stationed at the entrance to the area (to activate the City Map);
and
combat mode is not triggered (which happens when enemies come into direct line-of-sight). Otherwise, if only a
rogue
was used to explore the area, sneak back to the entrance. Then kill the guards closest to the area transition icon in order for the game to drop out of combat mode. The whole party may need to move into the "lever room" for this to happen.
Either way, once back at the entrance, interact with the city map door to leave. Then return to Slim and he'll apologize most profusely and return the 10
back to you.
Bugs
There is a bug present here whereby Slim won't talk about the ambush, nor return your money. This usually happens if you pulled the lever, in the first room on your left, and didn't return it to its original position. Return to the trap and pull the lever again. Go back to Slim and he should apologize and return your money to you.
If pulling the lever and returning to Slim does not fix the bug, try returning to the trap and taking your entire party to the end of the trap and then back to the beginning.
If you have tried pulling the lever, killing all the guards, and walking through the estate to the trap room, try walking into the Denerim Alienage to "unlock" the area. Check your quest log to see if it has updated, and then return to Slim for his apology.
The same bug can appear if choosing the mentioned method of not engaging in combat but leaving the area with one party member left at the entrance. Fighting the house guards instead would fix this.
If you go directly back to the
Party Camp
via the world map then back to Denerim, Slim Couldry will be stuck in a dialogue where continuing the questline is not possible. It can be fixed by going back to Bann Franderel's Estate and then back to Denerim.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Profane and the Lost
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Profane_and_the_Lost
======================================================================

Quest
The Profane and the Lost
Type
Companion quest
Location
Hossberg Wetlands
Previous
Hope on the Wing
, or, if missed,
The Palate of a Griffon
Next
Home Roost
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The Profane and the Lost
is a
companion quest
for
Davrin
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Isseya
has finally been located, and it's time to rescue the
griffons
at last.
Acquisition
Speak to Davrin at the
Lighthouse
after completing both
The Palate of a Griffon
and
Blood of Arlathan
.
Evka
and
Antoine
have sent word about Isseya's location, and he's ready to retrieve the griffons.
Walkthrough
Davrin  must rescue the kidnapped griffons from Isseya, before she blights them and  dooms them to extinction.
Investigate the lead on Isseya
Speak with Davrin in the
Hossberg Wetlands
. Antoine and the Wardens have found Lucas Kryson, a
Warden
mysteriously returned months after leaving for his
Calling
. He has re-emerged from the
Deep Roads
to report the dead are refusing to stay dead, and an elf is planning a crusade of vengeance against all Grey Wardens. He claims she has the griffons with her as well, but before he can say more, he collapses. Antoine provides the location of Kryson's emergence from the Deep Roads so that Davrin can finally hunt down
Isseya
.
Descend into the Deep Roads
Na-Than, the Blighted Warden
Follow the linear path down to the elevator, collecting some minor
loot
and a
note
, as Davrin gives Assan a pep talk and states his determination to rescue the griffons. At the bottom, a blighted Grey Warden confronts Rook and the team, saying he has found something greater than the Calling: the song of the
Old Gods
. If Rook is a Warden, he asks them to join him; otherwise, he rejects their entry, even though Davrin is a Warden himself.
Either way, kill Na-Than, the Blighted Warden, in order to progress. A
note
and a
memento
can be found on a nearby table. The passage opens into an enormous cavern with an underground lake, where Davrin realizes the Gloom Howler has been building a corrupted, profane version of
Weisshaupt
.
There are several small loot containers and ores scattered around this area. Collect them and proceed east, where Corrupted Wardens lie in wait, followed by
darkspawn
on the wooden bridge. The Wardens are vulnerable to fire and resistance to necrosis. Shortly after crossing the bridge, hook left and climb up a wooden scaffold for a small chest with ore and valuables and a
note
before continuing.
Rook and the team emerge into a large courtyard full of darkspawn and Corrupted Wardens waiting to destroy the Veilguard. Kill all of them, making optional use of the elevated ballistae, A chest near the entrance can be acquired by climbing the half-ruined wall on the left and crossing over a short wooden bridge; it contains
Pursuant's Implements
.
Continue up the crumbling staircase southeast, while Davrin expresses concern over lingering memories. Destroy the blight boil high on the wall and proceed through the arch. Collect the
Cutthroat's Sword
(rogue)/
Urthemiel's Ash
(mage)/
Captain's Heater
(warrior) from the chest before entering the doors.
Speak to the First Warden
The Blighted First Warden
On the other side is a reconstruction of the war room of Weisshaupt, containing none other than the
First Warden
himself, who is now completely blighted. Depending on how Rook handled him during
The Siege of Weisshaupt
, he will be cooperative or incoherent. If he is lucid (if Rook talked him down rather than punching him), he will alert Rook to a preserved feather on a nearby desk which has captivated Isseya's attention. Regardless, the reunion ends with Davrin putting him out of his misery for good.
Collect the feather (if applicable), along with the
Blade of Warden Janos
and
Folk Art Rocking Griffon
mementos, a
note
, and a
journal page
, as well as several small loot items and a small chest. Rook can also investigate the war table and a scroll on some southern scaffolding for comments on Weisshaupt's blueprints and battle plans.
Escape the dungeons
As Rook leaves the room, the Blight bridge collapses, dropping Rook down into the Darkspawn Dungeons. These are full of darkspawn, as the name suggests; destroy the ghouls to progress. A
memento
is in the back left cell.
From now on, the Blight archways will seal off behind Rook, so be sure to finish looting before walking forward. Each blight boil will continue spawning ghouls until destroyed; at the third boil, make sure to detour to the right to collect the chest before continuing through the arch, which will seal behind Rook. The chest contains the
Reforged Bulwark
for Davrin.
Large Blight boils wait at the top of the next two flights of stairs, which will spawn a variety of darkspawn. Open the archway at the end to reach Garahel's Point, another broken courtyard on the large underground lake.
Use Assan to destroy a cyst high on the wall to the left and drop a ladder. A large chest containing
Weisshaupt's Wail
(rogue)/
Chilled Grasp
(mage)/
Winged Glaive
(warrior) is just to the left atop the ladder. Follow the passage to reach the final courtyard, the outskirts of Isseya's lair.
Break into Isseya's lair
Several large darkspawn wait here, including javelineers, hurlocks, and ghouls. After killing them, explore the surrounding stairways for a
journal page
and several small loot containers. One in particular, on the northernmost outcropping of a high wooden pathway, contains the
Warden's Key
amulet.
Destroy the central blight boil at the blocked gate to loot the
Hardbitten Belt
and stop the ghouls from spawning. Isseya senses Assan and calls out to him, demanding he rejoin his family. Finally, destroy the boil blocking the gate to reach Isseya at last.
Stop the  ritual
Finally face-to-face with
Isseya
, the team must stop her from blighting the remaining griffons, who are trapped high above the blight. Break each of the blight bulbs to make her vulnerable, then again when they reappear. As all darkspawn, the level 38 Isseya is vulnerable to fire and resistant to necrosis.
At various stages, she will retreat behind multiple walls of blight; defeat any spawned darkspawn and break through it to chase after her. She will continuously summon both Wardens and darkspawn to aid her, so take them out quickly.
Isseya preparing to kill Assan
When she is finally brought low, Assan dives in for the killing blow. However, Isseya manages to gain the upper hand in the grapple; she pins Assan beneath her and tries to stab him with the blighted dagger from the
Cauldron
.
Davrin interrupts at the last moment, trying to reason with Isseya. He knows who she is and that she blighted the griffons and doomed them. She claims she hid the eggs to save them; she will save them again now by blighting them into immortality, thereby preventing the Wardens ever harming them again.
If Rook has the griffon feather, they can intervene with a special dialogue option to remind Isseya of her lost griffon, Revas. In remembering her friend, Isseya realizes the blight drove her mad and resulted in her having to put the griffon down. Davrin confronts her with her plan to do the same to these griffons, and in her grief, Isseya collapses and dies.
Otherwise, all dialogue options lead to Isseya choosing to stab Assan. A last-minute call from Davrin allows Assan to escape, and Davrin stabs Isseya through the stomach, fatally wounding her and ending the threat.
Decide the fate of the griffons
With Isseya finally dead and the
griffons
freed, they must be found a permanent home.
Evka
and
Eldrin
meet
Rook
and
Davrin
in
Arlathan Forest
, along with all the griffons, where Davrin thanks Rook and asks for their help deciding the griffons' purpose, as he is too close to the issue to choose on his own.
Evka promises the
Wardens
will treat them with the respect they deserve as companions and warriors; Eldrin suggests Arlathan Forest can benefit from their strong protective side, which will nurture the
halla
and other forest creatures. Davrin will honor whichever path Rook chooses, and the representative thanks Rook for their trust.
Davrin then offers to Assan the choice to stay with his family.
Assan
flits over to his griffon friends for a brief chirped discussion, but just as Davrin turns away sadly, Assan leaps on him, choosing to stay by Davrin's side for good. Rook hugs Assan, and they and Davrin leave Arlathan Forest together.
Rewards
4,000 XP
Bond increases for present companions
Choice-dependent
allied strength
gain:
100 Grey Wardens strength, if the griffons are returned to the Wardens
100 Veil Jumpers strength, if the griffons become sentinels of nature
Champion's Brilliant Regalia
(heavy armor)
Hero of the Veilguard
status for Davrin, along with:
The heroic armor
Grey Benefactor
and the
Defender
skill, if the griffons are returned to the Wardens
The heroic armor
Wildwood Benefactor
and the
Battlemaster
skill, if the griffons become sentinels of nature
Results
Davrin has  important news: a Warden has arrived in Hossberg, and he claims to have  information on Isseya. The man is waiting to speak to the team in Lavendel.
Isseya has  imprisoned the kidnapped griffons down in the Deep Roads and plans to unleash  some sort of "crusade" against the Grey Wardens. Davrin must find  her lair and rescue the griffons before Isseya dooms them to extinction by  blighting them.
Isseya has hidden  the kidnapped griffons down in the Deep Roads, inside a twisted mockery of  Grey Warden architecture raised by blighted hands. Davrin and the team must  contront her and save the griffons before Isseya blights them and turns them  into mindless monsters.
Isseya, in her  grief, had planned on blighting the young griffons to "save" them.  Davrin and the team not only defeated her but stopped Isseya's army of  corrupted Grey Wardens from sweeping over Hossberg and the rest of the  Anderfels. After freeing the griffons, Davrin had been resigned to Assan  departing with the others... and was overjoyed when Assan refused to leave  his side.

Notes
The blade of Janos
memento near the First Warden is the same sword thrown across the war table by Rook during the
siege of real Weisshaupt
, which came from the dead Warden Janos found earlier in that quest.
The rocking griffon memento is carved by
Thom Rainier
and likely also came from the genuine Weisshaupt.
The Profane City is in the
Anderfels
, but is not a part of the
Hossberg Wetlands
as it has its own marker on the world map. Despite that marker being the icon of a mirror, there is no
eluvian
at the start of the area.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Qunari Prisoner
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Qunari_Prisoner
======================================================================

Quest
The Qunari Prisoner
Location
Lothering
Start
Sten
End
Sten
Other NPCs
Revered Mother
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Just outside the north end of
Lothering
proper, a
Qunari
warrior
named
Sten
has been imprisoned for killing a family.
The Warden
can convince him to seek atonement by joining the battle against the
Blight
, but must find a way to release him from the cage.
Walkthrough
Note:
As soon you complete
any
of the
main questlines
, you will be unable to return to Lothering - so if you want Sten in the party, be sure to rescue him before said completion.
Speak with Sten in his cage. He is dismissive of most conversational options, but he may clarify that he would rather die in battle than in a cage. Regardless of how the conversation goes, most companions vocally disapprove of his situation and support releasing him.
You can open the cage in several ways:
If the Warden has
Mechanical Expertise
or at least 20
Cunning
(or
Leliana
is in the party,) you can pick the cage's lock. (PC v1.05: a Cunning score of 11 is sufficient)
You can
persuade
the
Revered Mother
to release Sten into the Warden's custody. This is easier if you make a donation to the Chantry, gaining Leliana
(
+2
). However, if Leliana is in the party, she alone convinces the Revered Mother to hand over the
key
.
You can intimidate the Revered Mother into handing over the key. Whether or not this succeeds, it amuses
Morrigan
(
+4
), but offends
Alistair
(
-13
), and severely disappoints Leliana (
(
-15
)).
pc
On PC, Leliana does not lose any approval.
mac
On a MAC, Leliana grants +2 approval.
In older versions of the game, you may be able to
steal
the key from the Revered Mother. A
patch
has fixed this.
Return to Sten's cage and release him.
Result
Sten becomes available as a
companion
.
Reward
250 XP for opening the cage
Note:
Even if the Warden decides not to recruit Sten, it is prudent to open the cage to gain experience points.
Exploits
This bug can only be exploited by a Warden with
less than 35 Strength
and
no Coercion skill
. Otherwise, the intimidation succeeds, the quest progresses, and the exploit ceases to be available. It's possible to gain maximum approval from Morrigan before freeing Sten. Every time you attempt to intimidate the Revered Mother, Morrigan expresses her appreciation for the entertainment. Simply choose the intimidation option,
fail
, end the dialogue, then start another. This yields
(
+4
) each time. Repeat until maximum approval is reached.
Leave Alistair out of the party for this; he will not only disapprove, but also convince the Revered Mother to give you the key regardless, making the exploit unavailable.
(
potentially, depending on game version
) However you manage to get the key and open Sten's cage, there is a way to continually exploit the quest for the XP bonus.  To start the exploit, choose the first few dialogue options, informing Sten you're a Grey Warden, and freeing him from the cage, he will say "And so it is done. I will follow you into battle," and the last five of the dialogue options will become available.  When you choose the last of the 5 options, "I don't want your help; you are free to go," Sten will make some remark that although the cage is open it doesn't mean he will leave it. You will get 250 XP for completing the quest, but the quest will not actually be finished until you have him try to join your party, thus allowing you to do this as many times as you want.
Bugs
If The Warden gets the key by intimidation, Sten will be surprised that the Revered Mother agreed to part with the key. He suspects The Warden has stolen the key off her, and sometimes you can't deny it, even if you didn't steal it.

Quest
The Returned Cultist Case
Type
Companion quest
Location
Dock Town
Previous
Keep a Light On
Next
The Local Hero
(conditional)
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The Returned Cultist Case
is a
companion quest
for
Neve
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Acquisition
The quest is unlocked by speaking to Neve at the
Lighthouse
after
Blood of Arlathan
.
Walkthrough
Aelia has  taken Dock Town's people. She plans to increase her blood magic. But Neve  knows where Aelia's hiding. Time to meet face-to-face.
Rewards
3000 XP
400
100 Shadow Dragon strength
Serpent's Tail
(scepter for Neve), given if Minrathous was not saved. If Minrathous was saved, this item is given in Neve's next quest.
If Minrathous was saved, Neve attains
Hero of the Veilguard
status at end of this quest (if Minrathous was not saved, additional requirements apply, as described in the next section below). Hero of the Veilguard status comes with:
The heroic armor
Threader's Plates
and the
Winter's Wrath
skill, if Neve becomes Dock Town's protector, or
The heroic armor
Investigator's Robes
and the
Cold Justice
skill, if Neve becomes Dock Town's inspiration.
Results
Thanks to a tip  from Rana, Neve knows Aelia is hiding in Sanctum Lusacan - an old temple,  lost and forgotten in the Minrathous catacombs - and there's an entrance in  Dock Town. The time has come to face Aelia. But there's a few people Neve  needs to talk to first.
Aelia will soon  complete her blood magic ritual. If there's any hope for Dock Town's missing  people, Neve needs to find them now. Rana and Elek have agreed to help. As  everyone descends into Sanctrum Lusacan, a meeting with Aelia approaches, and  Neve must decide how this ends.
Neve finally  confronted Aelia face-to-face, stopping her blood magic ritual and freeing  Dock Town residents from the cultists's hold. Aelia will be interrogated by  the cleanest justicar in the city. Neve is a symbol of hope for Dock Town.  People know they can turn to her in times of trouble. And if Neve's ever in  trouble, she knows the team will have her back.
If Minrathous was saved, the next quest,
The Local Hero
, is unlocked if other perquisites are met.
If Minrathous was not saved, Hero of the Veilguard status can be attained at a later conversation at the Lighthouse that becomes available after completing all Dock Town region quests (including
The Tempered Soul, Everlasting
) and advancing the main story to certain point. This conversation involves a visit from Elek Tavor and will provide Neve with the Hero of Veilguard status, along with
200
and 300 Shadow Dragons strength.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Sacrifice of Souls
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Sacrifice_of_Souls
======================================================================

Quest
The Sacrifice of Souls
Type
Companion quest
Location
Blackthorne Manor,
Nevarra
Previous
Arrangements
Next
Heir to the Dead
or
Will and Testament
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The Sacrifice of Souls
is a
companion quest
for
Emmrich
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Acquisition
This quest is available after completing Emmrich's previous companion quest
Arrangements
and the main quest
Blood of Arlathan
. When these conditions are met, there will be a conversation at the
Lighthouse
with Emmrich,
Myrna
, and
Vorgoth
that provides this quest.
The
Mourn Watch
have investigated the
Gloaming Lantern
. Hezenkoss can use it to leech life from hundreds at a time, making it exceptionally dangerous, but Myrna and Vorgoth have discovered she intends to hold a soiree at Blackthorne Manor. While Emmrich still fears death, he and Rook will infiltrate the party and stop Hezenkoss's plans.
Walkthrough
To stop Hezenkoss from unleashing the power of the Gloaming Lantern, Emmrich must test his necromancy—and an old friendship—to their limits.
Find Hezenkoss's lantern
Travel to Blackthorne Manor via the World Map. Arriving at the mansion with the party in full swing, Manfred is sent to blend in as one of the undead servants. When the team tries to follow, the front door is discovered to be locked, so take the ladder left of the entrance instead.
If the optional boss, The Ground's Keeper, was not defeated on the
last visit to the mansion
, this quest is the last opportunity to do so. See that quest for details. If the mausoleum (left of the front door) was completed already, it will only contain some Etheric Remnants this time.
Take the ladder to the balcony. A door to the right (to the foyer) may be opened here, but then go left, following the quest marker to a balcony overlooking the main hall. Manfred has successfully infiltrated the guests, but there's no sign of the lantern. Instead, the team overhears a guest discussing a spectral tome in the library which can reveal the location of an ancestral shrine here in the manor. With no other leads, head to the library to look for this tome -- Emmrich thinks he can
corpse whisper
to the shrine's dead for the lantern's location.
Retrace Rook's steps along the second floor, through the foyer, and back into the library. Several undead now guard the books; dispatch them, then find the correct book in the northeast corner, on a table in the middle landing. However, it's sealed tight, so head back to the party via the westward ground level passage (optionally exploring the servants' wing for secrets to Blackthorne Manor if not already done) to deliver the book to Manfred.
Head up to the balcony to watch Manfred deliver the spectral tome to the magister overhead before. He is able to read it and confirms there is indeed a shrine in the northwest wing, so head there with Emmrich to investigate. On the way, the team will pass through a storage room filled with boxes. Take the stairs to the second level and platform back across the boxes to reach a chest if not already looted.
Explore the northwest wing
Continue down the stairs after Emmrich to the shrine door, which is locked. Lucky for the Veilguard, Manfred arrives with the mansion key looted from pockets unknown. This is the last chance to loot the rest of the manor, so explore the surrounding area before committing. When finished, take the key from Emmrich and open the door.
Inside lies a skeleton in state: a Storm Age body from the Verheims family. Emmrich is able to speak to it via his corpse-whispering, but the spirit is reluctant to share information with a stranger. However, Manfred surprises everyone present by speaking: "Hello!" Given the presence of a willing dead companion, the spirit concedes the knowledge that Johanna is building something dreadful nearby, and the entrance is behind a red tapestry.
The quest marker indicates a path to the west, but first, loot a small room to the east full of chests. Then head through the marked doorway, but before descending the wooden stairs, loot the long hallway to the left, where the chest glimpsed on the previous quest may finally be looted. Then take the long stairway down to the cellar, where a codex entry waits near a fallen skeleton.
Solve the cellar puzzle
The key to the cellar puzzle from Blackthorne Manor.
The next room contains a number of undead, a puzzle, and the next red banner in the northeast corner, near a hidden entrance in the wall; it's blocked, but Manfred is able to squeeze through a nearby crack in the wall and open it from the other side. An elevator waits to take Rook to the depths of Blackthorne Manor, but there's a puzzle to be solved first.
In the previous room, three sigils high on the west wall show which three levers to pull. Two levers are readily accessible; the third is behind a barred door. The third is in the room with the elevator; take the ramp up to the wisp, looting the lyrium chest and a codex entry along the way. Soothe the wisp and bring it back to the barred gate, at last granting access to the third lever and another leaf in the
Seneschal's Notebook
.
Once all three levers have been pulled, return to the now-open door beneath the three sigils. Inside the Decrepit Shrine is a large chest and a body laid beneath a flowering banner, along with a
note
.
When everything has been looted, take the elevator down.
Stop Hezenkoss's bone golem
A towering monstrosity looms in the distance, ready to be animated by Johanna's Gloaming Lantern as she taunts the group. Loot the small chest on left before proceeding right into the workshop. A number of ravenous dead lie in wait, and a ramp up on the right side leads to a small chest and an
Ink-Splattered Note
, which reveals the guests to tonight's party are all people on whom Hezenkoss wishes revenge.
When ready, drop off the ledge. A view straight ahead reveals a vast chasm eerily reminiscent of the
Grand Necropolis
, then head left through the double doors to another crop of strong undead. Fight across the bridge; on the other side, Emmrich will realize this is the missing Basalt Hypogeum of the Necropolis, transposed here by Hezenkoss so that its wards would hide her creation.
As the narrow path opens up, Rook faces a Deathchill Pride demon with an entourage of powerful undead, and Johanna taunts Emmrich that she can sense Manfred sneaking about. However, Manfred once again comes to aid the team by lowering a ladder that allows for progression. Climb it, then jump down the broken ledge to reach the golem.
Confront Hezenkoss
The bone golem stealing the souls of Hezenkoss's guests.
Emmrich must attempt to release the souls trapped in the lantern, but Rook remembers the strain it took on him to just release a single soul last time. Indeed, it overwhelms him, and Hezenkoss comes forth at last to mock him for it. Emmrich attempts to talk her down, nearly reaching her at one point by calling on their friendship, but when he suggests she intends to puppet her golem creation, she angrily retorts she intends to
become
it instead.
The golem comes to life, Hezenkoss atop its shoulder, and bursts through mansion floor into the ballroom. The surprised guests are lifted into the air by beams of green light, their souls drawn out by the Gloaming Lantern at the golem's heart, and the final confrontation with Hezenkoss begins.
Hezenkoss, like all undead, is vulnerable to Necrosis and resistant to Cold. Throughout the fight, several waves of undead will spawn to help Hezenkoss, She uses bursts of necrotic energy and long, powerful ice beams; Rook can hide behind the pillars as needed. Once her health is finally dropped, the last cutscene of Blackthorne Manor will begin.
With Rook and Emmrich pinned down by necrotic energy, only Manfred is able to approach the Gloaming Lantern. He bravely runs forward to tear the lantern from Johanna's abomination and flings it to Rook, only to be rewarded with a crushing blow from the construct. Emmrich, torn between despair and inspiration at his friend's sacrifice, risks his life to release the trapped souls from the lantern. The guests collapse at the return of their souls; the trapped spirits swoop down and consume Hezenkoss, who lets out one final agonized cry as the
Hand of Glory
creeps across the stage.
A lich lord of the Necropolis.
Devastated to find Manfred lifeless, Emmrich and Rook return him to the Necropolis to see what can be done. At his request, a lich lord of the Necropolis arrives to deliver an impossible ultimatum: to let Manfred go forever, or give up the possibility of lichdom to bring him back. If Emmrich is to become an immortal lich, he must be at peace with allowing his loved ones to pass through death without him.
Ultimately, Rook will encourage him to pursue one or the other, resulting in one of two paths for the necromancer.
Should Emmrich decide to revive Manfred, he will remain mortal and bring back his friend during the following quest,
Heir to the Dead
. Otherwise, he will let Manfred rest and attempt lichdom during
Will and Testament
.
Note:
If Rook is in a romantic relationship with Emmrich they will remain so regardless of this choice, even though an Emmrich bound for undeath initially tries to break it off.
Rewards
Completing the quest yields:
3750 XP
Increased bond with the companions taken on the quest
100 Mourn Watch strength
Crested Focus
(keepsake for Emmrich)
Results
Johanna Hezenkoss is holding a gathering at Blackthorne Manor. Emmrich must infiltrate the manor, then find and destroy the Gloaming Lantern, before Hezenkoss can unleash the artifact's terrifying power in a bid to conquer Nevarra.
As Hezenkoss oversees her guests in the main hall of the Blackthorne Manor, Emmrich desperately searches the grounds for the Gloaming Lantern. This spirit-imprisoning artifact grants its wielder incredible powers, and Emmrich must find Hezenkoss's workshop and destroy the lantern to stop his nemesis' sinister plans of conquest.
Hezenkoss has crafted an enormous undead monster, fueling it with the spirits trapped in the Gloaming Lantern. Emmrich must destroy the lantern and free its spirits before Hezenkoss permanently bonds with the titanic monstrosity and invades the capital of Nevarra.
If Emmrich is convinced to not bring Manfred back:
Emmrich stopped Hezenkoss and freed the spirits in the Gloaming Lanterns, thanks to Manfred's sacrifice. After bidding the brave skeleton farewell in the Necropolis, the senior necromancer became determined to protect the world from future terrors by going beyond death. He decided to attempt lichdom and is preparing himself for the final rites.
Starts
Will and Testament
.
If Emmrich is convinced to bring Manfred back:
Emmrich stopped Hezenkoss and freed the spirits in the Gloaming Lanterns, thanks to Manfred heroically sacrificing himself. Torn between reviving his skeleton companion or attempting lichdom, Emmrich chose a chance to bring Manfred back to the land of the living.
Starts
Heir to the Dead
.
Notable loot
Some of the loot that were available during the previous visit to Blackthorne Manor will still be available during this mission if not acquired during the previous visit. Note that this mission is the final visit to the manor.
The following locations and their items are newly accessible during this mission:
Forgotten Shrine
Glacial Loop
(ring)
Death's Beacon
(weapon for Emmrich)
Striking Misfortune
(light armor)
Secret Passage/Decrepit Shrine
Essence Vortex
(unique ring)
Duelist's Blade
(rogue) /
Timeworn Mageknife
(mage) /
Guard's Broadaxe
(warrior)
Hezenkoss's Workshop
Twin Palladium
(rogue and warrior) /
Glacial Talus
(mage)
Innervate
(rune)
Approval

Emmrich Approves
Consulting the ancestor
Oh, my. A body from the house of the Verheims prepared during the Storm Age!
(Diplomatic) Admire it later? —
Emmrich Approves

(Stoic) Please focus.

By the golem

Codex entries

Notes
If the mausoleum to the west of the house was visited during
House of the Dead
and then again during this quest, Emmrich will repeat his line from the first time about there being fewer corpses than there should be, but none of his other location lines here repeat.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Scrolls of Banastor
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Scrolls_of_Banastor
======================================================================

Quest
The Scrolls of Banastor
Quest Giver
The Mages' Collective
End
The Mages' Collective
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Scrolls of Banastor
is a side quest obtainable from
The Mages' Collective
Quest Board
. The Collective requests that an individual with a certain moral flexibility scour
Ferelden
for five ancient and powerful
Scrolls of Banastor
. The scrolls contain information pertaining to "dark and terrible" blood magic rituals.
Walkthrough
Depending on how soon you acquire this quest, it can go one of three ways:
Get this quest as soon as possible, because you're going to visit all of the places (as mandated by the plot) anyway. In this way, it pretty much solves itself.
Get the quest later in the game, and just revisit the places.
Simply acquire all five scrolls during the course of the game, then hand them in as soon as you receive the quest in
Redcliffe
.
Scroll Locations
The location of the five scrolls are as follows:
Senior Mage Quarters
of the
Circle Tower
: Ancient Texts in the southeast ruined room. In the same room as the first 3 blood mages, but on the other side.
Great Hall
of the Circle Tower: Ancient Texts In first room in the northwest large room.
The
Ruined Temple
visited in
The Urn of Sacred Ashes
quest, directly south of
Haven
. It is in a library in the southeast corner of the map. There are several bookshelves with books on them so you can't miss it.
Western section of the Ruined Temple, called the Cultist Chambers. From the Main Hall map marker, take the west door to the Cultist Chambers area. The scroll is in the room just northwest of the Cultist Chambers map marker, in a pile of books marked 'Ancient Texts'.
Lair of the Werewolves
.  The scroll is in a pile of books marked 'Ancient Texts' in the southeastern chamber, the area just north of your final meeting with the
Lady of the Forest
and
Zathrian
. (It is possible to miss this one on your initial visit, due to the cutscene where the
Warden
interacts with the
Gatekeeper
wolf.  If this happens, simply return to the Lair after the conclusion of
Nature of the Beast
and collect the scroll.)
Circle Tower (second floor), The Scrolls of Banastor, first location
Circle Tower (third floor), The Scrolls of Banastor, second location
Ruined temple (first floor), The Scrolls of Banastor locations
{Note: The location of the first scroll is marked incorrectly on the map. Check the room just above, on the left.}
Lair of the Werewolves, The Scrolls of Banastor location
Reward
175 XP
5
Notes
Unfortunately, there does not appear to be a set scroll for each location; if you're missing one, your only option is to revisit each location until you find which one you missed.
A common problem is to miss the scroll in the Lair of the Werewolves. Many players neglect to explore the rooms in the final area when interrupted by the werewolves' dialogue and ensuing quest line.
See also
Scrolls of Banastor

Quest
The Shaper's Life

Wall of Memories,
Orzammar Shaperate
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
The Shaper's Life
is an unmarked
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Acquisition
The quest is started by touching the Wall of
Memories
in the
Shaperate
of
Orzammar
. This also generates the first part of
Codex entry: The Shaper's Life
.
You can also start this quest by examining any of the three rune stones (although the quest does not appear in your Journal).
Walkthrough
The Warden
must then seek out three
rune stones
. In order to access all of them the Warden must have advanced the quest line
A Paragon of Her Kind
sufficiently.
At the left hand of the
Mines Commander
in the
Orzammar Commons
right before the entrance to the
Deep Roads
.
In the northeast caves of
Caridin's Cross
.
In the west central room of the
Dead Trenches
.
Once all the
dwarven
runes have been interacted with, the Warden must return to the Shaperate and interact with the Wall of Memories one more time. The codex entry will update one last time stating that the Warden has been blessed as they have "walked the path of the Shaperate".
Rewards
Depending on the Warden's
class
:
Shaperate's Blessing
, for a
warrior
Warden.
Shaperate's Blessing
, for a
rogue
Warden.
Shaperate's Blessing
, for a
mage
Warden.
Bugs
Due to a coding error, the quest provides no reward.
pc
users can use this
mod
to fix this bug.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Shield of the Knight Herself
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Shield_of_the_Knight_Herself
======================================================================

For other uses, see
Shield of the Knight Herself
.
Quest
The Shield of the Knight Herself
Act
2
Start
Ser Varnell's Refuge
End
Aveline
(
Viscount's Keep
)
Rewards
Shield of the Knight Herself
Appearances
Dragon Age II
The Shield of the Knight Herself
is an Act 2
companion quest
for
Aveline
in
Dragon Age II
.
Acquisition
The quest is acquired by looting the gift item
Shield of the Knight Herself
in
Ser Varnell's Refuge
during the
main quest
Offered and Lost
.
Walkthrough
Speak with Aveline at her office in the
Viscount's Keep
.  Note that if
Calling on the Captain
,
Doubts that Linger
,
The Captain's Condolences
, any stage of
The Long Road
, or
Questioning Beliefs (Aveline Act 2)
are active, those conversations will take place first, in that order.
Hawke
will present the shield to Aveline, who in turn gets upset and throws the shield to the ground, with an insincere "Thank you."
Hawke then can ask what the meaning of her reaction is in either the diplomatic, humorous, or aggressive choices. The following dialogue changes depending on the status of
Ser Wesley's Shield
:
If Aveline has the shield equipped, she will state, "I have a shield.  It belonged to Wesley.  It has served me well."
If you have it in the Inventory, she will complain that "I have a shield.  It belonged to Wesley.  You seem to recognize its worth as you pass it around."
With either of these, she follows up with, "Are you suggesting that I give it up entirely?  Pawn it away and be done with it?"
If it has been sold, destroyed, or stashed in the Personal Storage chest, she will complain that "I had a shield.  It belonged to Wesley.  It was difficult to let it go, but I did so.  Utility over sentiment, right?  We sold it, didn't we?"
Hawke still won't understand the reason for Aveline's anger.  The Diplomatic and Direct dialogues will reference her lingering feelings about
Wesley
and, if
The Long Road
has been completed, whether or not she should tell
Donnic
.  Aveline mentions that it's not about Wesley, that Hawke has her doing things she doesn't necessarily agree with.  "I'm not sure where I'm going as it is. Replacing what I was with this... I mean, who is Ser Aveline to me?" you can respond with one of four dialogue choices, at which point the quest concludes.
Result
Quest concludes depending on Hawke's final dialogue choice, with either friendship or rivalry gained for Aveline and a new shield:
"I don't care, just use it."
Aveline: rivalry (
+15
), Hawke retakes the shield
"I'll take it back then."
Aveline: friendship (
+15
), Hawke retakes the shield
"Don't make it such a big deal."
Aveline: rivalry (
+15
), Aveline keeps the shield
"It's just a gift for a friend."
Aveline: friendship (
+15
), Aveline keeps the shield
Reward
If Aveline keeps the shield, she automatically equips
Shield of the Knight Herself (Aveline)
. (If she does not yet have the minimum 34 Strength, it goes into inventory but is restricted to Aveline.)
If Aveline returns the shield,
Shield of the Knight Herself (unrestricted)
is added to the inventory.  Note that it can be equipped to her later, only with different stats.
In addition, 250 XP for choosing "Don't make it such a big deal" or "It's just a gift for a friend".
Notes
The
unrestricted
version of the shield has slightly different stats: instead of +88 attack and 190 armor, it has +2 strength and 174 armor.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Siege of Weisshaupt
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Siege_of_Weisshaupt
======================================================================

Quest
The Siege of Weisshaupt
Location
Weisshaupt
Start
Lighthouse
End
Weisshaupt
Previous
A Change on the Wind
Next
After the Storm
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Acquisition
Returning to the Lighthouse after both
Shades of the Blight
and
Something Wrong
have been completed, immediately triggers a cutscene where
Davrin
explains that the
First Warden
is recalling all
Wardens
back to
Weisshaupt
as a
darkspawn
horde is advancing on the fort led by an
archdemon
.
Rook ends the conversation promising to speak to
Solas
to see if he has any information that may help and asks Davrin to ready the team.
Once the conversation ends this quest begins.
Walkthrough
After receiving word that the gods are marching towards Weisshaupt, the team must seize the opportunity to destroy them.
Prepare for Weisshaupt
Speak to Solas to learn more about the gods' plans and how to kill them. Rook will have to tell Solas they are willing to do whatever it takes to get the Wardens on their side before he is fully cooperative with them however. Rook learns that in order to kill the Evanuris they must first kill their dragons to render them mortal, then strike them down with the
Wolf's Fang
.
Make any preparations for the siege by upgrading and enchanting gear, upgrading the
Caretaker's workshop
to get better loot, or look over skills and abilities. Note that for this mission Davrin is required as one of the two party members. The other companions will occasionally be visible and have short conversations with Rook as they pass each other on different levels of the buildings during the events.
Enter Weisshaupt
Proceed to the
Eluvian
or use the map to travel directly to Weisshaupt. The whole team is present for the mission but Rook will only have Davrin and one other companion with them. As soon as the party arrives they discover that the Eluvian took them into a storage room at Weisshaupt before an attack sends the mirror down into the courtyard, splitting the party.
Find Antoine and Evka
In the storage room they meet a little girl,
Mila
, later found out to be the
blacksmith's
daughter, who warns them about darkspawn outside.
Get to the War Room
Dispatching the darkspawn in the courtyard allows Mila to show the team towards the War Room where
Evka
and
Antoine
should be, so follow her to a secret passage and open it. Warden Commander Janos is noted as having gone down fighting when they pass his corpse along the path.
When Mila stops by the next set of doors, head right to a stack of boxes blocking a staircase. Destroy them and head up to grab some loot. There's also some behind the shelves between that staircase and the door.
Through the door the Veilguard is greeted with the disembodied face of
Ghilan'nain
in the clouds and her
archdemon
terrorising the Wardens. Davrin - and Rook, should Rook be a Grey Warden - will hear
the Calling
, visualized as an agonizing headache. Should Lucanis be in the active party he will question how he's supposed to kill a cloud with a dagger. Proceed forward, destroying blight bulbs along the edge of the building, as Grey Wardens are fighting off an army of
darkspawn
below.
After more darkspawn Mila leads on to another door, behind which is the War Room. Here the
First Warden
is reacting to the situation poorly and Rook has a choice to either talk him into helping or knock him out to take charge. Regardless of how it's done, the plan of action is to use an old dragon trap that Antoine hopes can capture the archdemon, so Rook heads there to prepare it.
Find the Dragon Trap
Continue through the door to witness the archdemon burn a group of Wardens, then defeat the darkspawn. The archway ahead is blocked with blight but before destroying it, grab the chest above it by breaking the lock on a ladder to the far left of the door.
Prepare for another fight and drop down the ledge. On the right side of the battlements there's a ballista that can be used to fight off the horde of darkspawn as well as destroy multiple blight bulbs. In the left corner of this area, below the tower, one of the blight bulbs hides a chest.
Climb up towards the quest marker to reach the next level of the battlements where another ballista awaits, this one under multiple blight bulbs. To free it, follow the two blight cords to and destroy the nodes at each end - one is just next the ballista and the other is a little closer to the outside wall. Use it to shoot the bulbs inside and the left side of the tower to unlock the path forward. Shoot the darkspawn on the other side before leaving the ballista or fight them as normal.
In the tower there's a hole down into the next section of the map but before jumping down, destroy the two blight bulbs on the scaffolding on the left side then jump across the hole and climb up to the small chest revealed there.
Get to the Library
With the way forward collapsed Davrin suggests going through the library instead so follow the markers in that direction. At the point where an opening in the wall is covered by blight cords there's a small chest hidden under some barrels. The map alerts about another chest that's actually in the courtyard below so ignore that for now and slide down to the next level below.
Fight a horde of smaller darkspawn, then destroy blight to get through to the next area. One of the blight bulbs sit high on a wall and when destroyed it reveals to smaller bulbs right below it. Destroy them to reach a chest and some other loot before continuing. After the next set of doors two companions not in the active party, or Antoine and Evka (depending on your choice with the First Warden), will call down from an upper floor and instruct Rook to follow their torch forward. Climb over some barriers to face some darkspawn and a mini boss - The Relentless Wall. Defeat it and continue through the wall where it came through, initially blocked by some blight.
Follow the quest marker, eventually seeing another companion, or Antoine, with a torch. When looking directly at this person - turn around to find a
trapped
chest and some other loot behind boxes.
Continue down into a little courtyard with some darkspawn and blight bulbs. Before destroying the wall of blight, grab the chest on some scaffolding to the left of it by using Assan to drop down a stack of climbable wood. Head through that hole in the wall to see two companions running ahead. Follow the markers and witness Evka or the First Warden fighting a big tentacle above. Round the corner of the platform they were on to see a blight-covered chest in their spot. To reach it, destroy the blight underneath that platform. This is the chest that was previously mentioned as unreachable in an earlier section as Rook is now below that tower.
As Rook approaches the next area a huge tentacle will block the way, but Mila shows up to extend the team a ladder and escape upwards. Follow her, and at the point she runs up an L-shaped staircase but takes cover in the middle of it, take a left in the middle of the stairs to a two-story kitchen area where there's some loot including a big chest.
Up the stairs, face off with two Greater Ogres with company. As they deal heavy damage, avoid standing in blight pools which amplifies their damage further.
After they are defeated a horde of smaller darkspawn will start appearing around many indestructible blight bubbles. As it's looking grim for the group their companions open a previously blocked door and are reunited. The Warden's blacksmith and Mila's father Holden shows up to seal the door behind them and buy them some time. Evka also joins them to reaffirm their plan on using the dragon trap buy using Solas' dagger as bait.
Find the Dragon Trap
In the library Holden can suggest taking the Eluvian to safety and asks to borrow some companions to help set that up. There's another chest signal here that can't be resolved yet, so take the elevator up to discover that Ghilan'nain has blighted even the inside of Weisshaupt. Watch for exploding blight bombs falling from the ceiling. In the middle of the room is a little reading nook with blight bulbs to destroy and a the chest that was mentioned to loot. There's also some loot on either side of the next wall of blight to fetch before moving on.
Davrin will remind the party that once he, as a Grey Warden, kills the archdemon he will die too but assures them he's ready to make that sacrifice. Should Rook also be a Warden they will bring that up as an option but Davrin insists it be him so that Rook can finish the job by leading the Veilguard.
Next is another section of library, also full of blight. Keep following the cords of blight, destroying nodes and looting on the way.
Fight through a final batch of darkspawn and a blight wall to reach the dragon trap and trigger a cutscene. Watch Rook bait Ghilan'nain's attention and her archdemon get trapped by ancient chains to the cheers of the gathered Wardens. Davrin steps up, sword in hand and asking Rook to give Assan a hug from him, ready to make the sacrifice but is suddenly interrupted by the First Warden. His attitude depends on if Rook knocked him out or reasoned with him but regardless he will get Davrin to step aside and take his place to strike the killing blow. Before he can finish the job Ghilan'nain appears to defend her pet, killing the First Warden and using his life essence to mutate her dragon enough to free itself.
Weaken Ghilan'nain
Rook and the team must defeat Ghilan'nain's mutated Archdemon
Razikale
in multiple stages.
As the archdemon nears death Davrin again confirms that he will finish it off, with Rook telling him that they will never forget him.
Eventually the mutated dragon falls limp and Davrin strikes it down permanently. He is chocked to see the blight streaming from the corpse passing him by and going into Ghilan'nain instead, sparing his life unexpectedly. As she is screaming in rage Lucanis takes his opportunity to strike at her with the lyrium dagger but at the last moment she deflects him and he only scratches her face, confirming her vulnerability but not harming her significantly.
Enraged Ghilan'nain makes the blight overwhelm what remains of the battlefield, forcing everyone to escape through the eluvian back to the lighthouse to regroup.
Rewards
After conversation in the War Room:
+100 Grey Wardens Strength
Completing this quest yields:
4,500 XP
Increased bond with all companions:
Davrin: +500; additionally: 3x25 for defiling darkspawn during the quest
the rest: +250
+250 Grey Wardens Strength
700
2 Dragonfire Catalyst

(warrior) /

(mage)
Results
Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain are marching their darkspawn army toward Weisshaupt, which gives the team an opportunity to destroy them.
The gods have attacked Weisshaupt, which gives the team a chance to destroy them. The quest is quickly in trouble when the eluvian drops the team into the middle of a siege.
Ghilan'nain and her Archdemon are laying siege to Weisshaupt Fortress. After confronting the First Warden, the team hatches a plan to slay the Archdemon with Weisshaupt's dragon trap.
After regrouping in the library, the team decides to risk using Solas's dagger as bait to draw the Archdemon into the trap.
Ghilan'nain has transformed her Archdemon into a three-headed monster that must be defeated before Lucanis can strike the killing blow on the god.
Ghilan'nain laid siege to Weisshaupt, and her Archdemon destroyed much of the Grey Warden fortress. Still, Davrin managed to kill the Archdemon, and Lucanis wounded Ghilan'nain, reminding her that she's now mortal.

Trivia
The library contains a number of callbacks to wardens in previous games. In the room where
Evka
and
Antoine
are found, there is a pedestal with the joining chalice used by
the Hero of Ferelden
in Ostagar for their joining. Further on in the library is a note from
Aura
regarding Warden
Kristoff
's effects. A letter of commendation for the Silverite Wings of Valor given to the original Warden Blackwall that
Thom Ranier
took the identity of can be found on a table. In the upper level of the library, there is a report that follows the movements of
Hawke
's father,
Malcolm Hawke
.
The conversation with Davrin that begins this quest is a quest in itself called
A Change on the Wind
, although it does not appear in the journal after completion.

======================================================================
PAGE: The Singing Blade
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/The_Singing_Blade
======================================================================

Quest
The Singing Blade
Type
Main quest
Location
Ritual Site in
Arlathan Forest
Previous
Shadows Crossing
Next
The Enemy of My Enemy
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
The Singing Blade
is the fifth
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. According to
Morrigan
, it is crucial that
the team
recovers
the dagger
that Solas used during his ritual: it holds great power and could even be the key to defeating the gods.
Rook
,
Harding
, and
Neve
therefore head back to the ritual site to look for it, while
Bellara
remains to look at their
eluvian
.
Acquisition
This quest is received after completing
Shadows Crossing
, after which the team will immediately travel to the Ritual Site.
Walkthrough
Solas has lost his lyrium dagger, an ancient magical artifact of great power. Harding is eager to return to the site of his ritual, hoping to find the dagger before it falls into the wrong hands.
Find the lyrium dagger
Speak with Harding upon arriving, who admits she holds regrets from the outcome of the ritual. Rook has several options to respond, though only one of them results in approval changes. Hop down the wooden scaffolding to uncover an area of residual magic that the team suspects was the original location of the dagger, though it is now missing.
Retrieve the lyrium dagger
Head deeper into the ruins to glimpse a ghoul holding a blue magical object before it runs away. Keep chasing the ghoul suspected of taking the dagger and you'll run into a single hurlock, followed by a simple beam generator puzzle to advance through a door. Just aim the beam at the energy node above the gate. Once through, loot the chest. Afterward, head out into a clearing and take care of a group of darkspawn before destroying the blight boils to venture further in.
Walk under a stone bridge and turn left to climb up some cliffs that will let you access a chest after crossing over the bridge. Return to the main path where you'll need to destroy two blight boils to continue further. One is located on the wall to the left of the blocked path, whereas the other is located underneath the bridge constructed from the housed power crystal. Remove the crystal to uncover the remaining blight boil below. Loot the revealed chest and place the power crystal in the next housing to raise the gate.
Take care of the group of darkspawn in the next area and align the two beam generators to open a gate that leads to a chest. One of the beam generators can be accessed by a short stairway, while the other's access is a ladder hidden behind a breakable wooden wall to the left of the locked gate. Return to the main area and clear a blight boil blocking the path toward the objective. You'll encounter a Protected Javelineer and a group of ghouls blocking your path. Take care of them and once you've entered the Old Quarry, drop down into the water and head north to find another chest. Continue back toward the quest marker to reach the final clearing and fight the ghoul.
The Lyrium Ghoul is a fast-attacking boss enemy. Its basic attacks can be dodged or blocked, but be sure to evade its conal lyrium attack that applies Necrosis with every hit.
When the fight is over, Harding stops Rook from picking up the dagger and picks it up herself. As she tries to hand it to Rook, blue lyrium-colored veins begin spreading over her body. The rock beneath her gives way, causing her to fall from the cliffside. However, her voice becomes layered and echoed as she speaks: "This is the eternal hymn, the prayer, and the proclamation. Isatunoll. I am. We are. Free again. Whole again. Here again. Here...  again..." She then rises out from the rock itself with her eyes glowing blue and thick blue lyrium veins covering her body. As the glow fades, a few ghouls try to sneak up on the party, and Harding freezes and shatters them with her newly gained magical abilities.
Harding being thrown off the quaking cliffside after touching Solas' lyrium dagger.
The second phase of the boss battle begins. Harding's stone-wielding magic summons rock walls from the ground, encasing the battle to a stone arena while darkspawn attack. The stones retract as more darkspawn appear. After fighting a few waves, the Lyrium Ghoul joins in again, this time protected by a barrier. Other darkspawn will continue to spawn throughout. It gains a new move where it jumps into a blight pool to disappear from the fight for a while, which gives you time to take care of other darkspawn in the fight.
The team agrees to head back to the Lighthouse and a cutscene plays showing
Elgar'nan
and
Ghilan'nain
observing the battle using magic. It is revealed that the Lyrium Ghoul was Ghilan'nain's "pet" tasked with retrieving the lyrium dagger from the ritual site. Due to this failure, they set their sights on crafting a new dagger - this time made with
red lyrium
instead.
Back at the Lighthouse, Neve, Harding, and Rook meet in the library to discuss Harding's newfound magical abilities. Rook has the opportunity to give their opinion on these powers, and this conversation also gives Rook their first romance dialogue opportunity with her. Harding leaves the conversation, stating she'll send word to the Grey Wardens about their encounters with the blight and darkspawn, while Rook intends to try and speak with Solas again.
This section contains spoilers for
:
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
.
Rook's reaction toward Harding's magical abilities has the power to sway her outlook and actions later in the game. Showing support at this stage will cause her to be more confident and have a positive disposition toward them in later acts, whereas being wary or against them may have an opposite effect.
Rewards
1,850 XP
My Regards
- Uncommon Shortbow (Harding)
300 gold
Everyone's bond increased
Harding's bond increased
Codex Update
Results
The team has returned to the site of Solas's ritual, hoping to find Solas's lyrium dagger before the power artifact falls into the wrong hands.
The team returned to Solas's ritual site, hoping the find the lyrium dagger that Solas lost. They pried the ancient artifact away from the darkspawn ghouls that were drawn to it, but touching the dagger has had a profound effect on Harding, seemingly rekindling a forgotten link between her and "the Stone". What this means for Harding remains to be seen.
