PAGE: Thief in the House of Learning
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Thief_in_the_House_of_Learning
======================================================================

Quest
Thief in the House of Learning
Location
Orzammar
,
Orzammar Shaperate
Start
Shaper Assistant Milldrate
End
Shaper Assistant Milldrate or Jertrin
Other NPCs
Shady Corebit, Fixer Gredin
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Thief in the House of Learning
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Acquisition
Talking to Shaper Assistant Milldrate in the
Orzammar Shaperate
triggers this quest. He will mention that a
casteless
has stolen a rare tome from the
Shaperate
, and that he believes it was a bald man from
Dust Town
. He also claims that it was the first time that someone dared to steal from the Shaperate.
Walkthrough
Head to
Dust Town
and find Shady Corebit. He will attack the party as soon as
the Warden
talks to him.
Once you have killed him, take the
Proving Receipt
off his body. It informs you that somebody at the
Proving Grounds
has the tome.
Go to
Orzammar Commons
and continue across the bridge to the Proving Grounds.
The quest choices in
Dragon Age Keep
In the North Wing of Orzammar Proving, talk to Fixer Gredin. He will immediately attack you. The North Wing will be accessible only after talking to
Dulin Forender
to receive the quest
A Lord's Trust: The First Task
or after completing the quest
A Prince's Favor: The First Task
. On his body, you will find the book,
A Volume of Shaper History
.
Finally, head back to Milldrate in the Shaperate to return the book, or alternatively, you can sell the book to the fence Jertrin standing against the wall near Gredin for 2
30
. You must sell the book to
Jertrin
immediately once you initiate a conversation with him if that is your plan. Once you exit the conversation, he will no longer speak with you even if you still have the book.
Result
2
30
if you decide to sell the book to Jertrin and no known consequence occurs.
If not, Milldrate will give you his thanks but nothing more (no money, no experience points).
Gallery
Shady Corebit
Fixer Gredin
Jertrin

======================================================================
PAGE: Through the Shadows
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Through_the_Shadows
======================================================================

Quest
Through the Shadows
Type
Companion quest
Location
Arlathan Forest
Previous
Hummingbird's Lament
Next
The Forest of Spirits
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Through the Shadows
is a
companion quest
for
Bellara
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. The plot with Cyrian and Anaris has come to a head, and Bellara must do all she can to stop them.
Acquisition
Speak with Bellara in the
Lighthouse
after completing
Blood of Arlathan
,
Hummingbird's Lament
, and the follow-up conversation to Hummingbird's Lament at the Lighthouse (in which Bellara worries about being forced to kill
Cyrian
).
Walkthrough
Bellara  must stop Anaris when his ritual endangers all of Arlathan Forest, even if it  means going against her own brother, Cyrian.
Help Bellara stop Cyrian
Meet Bellara in the Mystical Tower to begin the quest. Bellara has taken Rook to a nearby ruin in the evening, and the ritual has already begun. They only have a short time before the ritual pulls their bodies apart, but Bellara is grateful Rook has come.
Before exploring, backtrack to find a memento hidden in some crates. In addition, a codex entry can be found on the other side of a large golden wolf statue shortly north from the quest's start.
Continue until arriving at the Boardwalk Gates, where the bridge will be unavailable. Two crystals need two beam generators to be activated. First, point the beam generator to the crystal above the door to the left. This will provide access to the second beam generator, which can then be pointed to a receiver across the gap. Return to the first beam generator and point it to the second node across the gap. With the bridge available, Rook can enter the ancient capital.
Enter the ancient capital
Immediately to the left will be a Veil rift, which will explode and break down a wall. Collect the Graven Opal (ring), then continue up the stairs until you reach the Great Hall. Drop down into the main hall to access the next objective.
Before following Bellara to the far end of the great hall, take the left-side stairs up and cross the bridge that hangs over the hall. There, use Spite's Reach to summon a ramp up to a chest and a
codex entry
. On the way down, look over the right edge to see another chest to grab before returning to Bellara.
Delay the ritual
Speak to Bellara at the console, where the tearing-apart feeling is the worst. Destroying it should buy Rook time to stop the ritual, so defend against a wave of
demons
and
Fade-touched Sentinels
while destroying three amplifiers around the area. Activate the console again and repeat the process. Repeat one more time, then return to the console to finally disrupt this portion of the ritual and drop the enormous magical artifact through the floor.
Find the ritual site
Jump down the hole and open the chest immediately on the left. Continue along the linear path, fighting demons along the way, until reaching and passing through a portal at the end.
Get past the barrier
In the Lower Chamber, five nodes need to be energised to unlock the console in this room. Up the stairs on the left there's a battery next to a chest; place it into the socket of the beam generator immediately to the left of the console. However, point it back towards the entrance at a node above the main door to create a bridge above, allowing Rook to reach the east side of the hall.
Up the left stairs again, take an immediate left to aim one of the beams towards the socket to the left of the portal. Take the ladder directly up the previous stairs and aim the first beam generator there to the left-most node. Cross the newly created bridge and aim that beam to the right-most node. Take the stairs down on the right side and release the ladder down, but instead of climbing down, follow the intermediary path to aim the beam on this floor to the right of the portal.
Jump down and remove the battery from the left-side socket (the one pointing back at the entrance door) and place it to power up the final beam generator. to the right of the console. Point it above the portal to energize the fifth and final socket. Finally, activate the portal via the console.
Find the ritual site
Proceed through the now-open door, open the chest, and drop down the hole to the lower corridor. Take the door at the end of the corridor to confront
Anaris
and
Cyrian
.
Stop the ritual
The ritual to give Anaris a physical body has already begun. Cyrian is shocked to see Bellara there and exclaims he told her to stay away; Anaris gloats that he will soon become flesh and restore himself to power. However, as Cyrian finishes the ritual, something goes wrong, blasting Anaris backwards and apparently destroying him.
Cyrian approaches, removing his bond mask and revealing he sabotaged the ritual of his own volition. Bellara's arguments during
Hummingbird's Lament
were successful, persuading him to trust her heart as the Vora'shivan he has always known.
Unfortunately, as Cyrian suggests they leave, Anaris rises from the pool behind him. He has apparently still managed to attain a mortal form despite the treachery, and flings Cyrian into a nearby pillar for his betrayal.
Rook must now defeat Anaris's new physical form. Destroy the three spheres floating behind each pillar to shatter Anaris's invulnerable shield, along with any spawning demons. These must be destroyed three times before Anaris is finally defeated. As he implodes, he shrieks in fear that he must escape "its eye" before vanishing into ash.
Afterwards, Bellara rushes to the side of her dying brother. He apologizes for everything and thanks Bellara, then requests she stay with him until it's light. She agrees, and the two say their final goodbyes.
Aftermath
Speak to Bellara at the Lighthouse. She grieves Cyrian a second time, reviews his journal, and asks Rook what she should do with the Archive. At this point, Rook may advise her to keep the Archive, using its information to help the elven people despite its danger; or free the Archive, allowing the elves to move forward with a clean slate.
Either way, Bellara will achieve
Hero of the Veilguard
status, her commitment to the Veilguard now unquestioned.
Rewards
2500 XP
100 Veil Jumpers strength
400
Common appearance for a class-specific weapon
Sunspoked Spectrometer
(keepsake for Bellara)
After this quest, Bellara can be spoken to at the Lighthouse. At the end of this conversation, she will gain
Hero of the Veilguard
status, along with:
The heroic armor
Reborn Leathers
and the skill
Chain Lightning
, if the Archive is freed, or
The heroic armor
Archivist's Mail
and the skill
Lingering Strike
, if the Archive is kept.
Results
Bellara needs  help to stop Anaris and Cyrian from performing their ritual in Arlathan  Forest.
Anaris has begun a ritual that endangers all of Arlathan Forest. Bellara's brother Cyrian, who still serves the twisted Forgotten One, is key to the ritual. Bellara races to Arlathan Forest, desperate to stop the ritual without sacrificing the brother she still loves.
Anaris is defeated and banished from the world once again. Cyrian subverted the ritual in a final attempt to help Bellara, only for Anaris to strike him down with a final, spiteful blow.

(rogue ring) /

(armor for Bellara)

======================================================================
PAGE: Thy Brother's Killer
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Thy_Brother's_Killer
======================================================================

Quest
Thy Brother's Killer
Start
The Mages' Collective
,
Lake Calenhad Docks
End
The Mages' Collective
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Thy Brother's Killer
is a quest received from
The Mages' Collective
Quest Board
at
Lake Calenhad Docks
. A cabal of
maleficarum
are practicing blood magic near the home of a member of the Collective, who requests that they be brought to justice before they draw the attention of the
templars
to the area.
Walkthrough
The action takes place in the north of the
East Brecilian Forest
- the map will have an 'X' for the northern Ancient Tombstone used during
The Mage's Treasure
quest, which happens in the same area (the Ancient Tombstone to the very north of the map, not the one to the east).
When you first enter the area, two
ogres
will be near the tombstone.  Kill them both, then exit and reenter the area.  When you return to the tombstone, the four maleficarum (two
normal
and two
lieutenants
) will be present, standing around an altar. If you have already been to this area and killed the ogres before starting this quest, the four maleficarum will be there, and you will not have to exit and reenter the area.
Skills
The maleficarum are challenging enemies, able to use the following at level 14:
Weakness
Vulnerability Hex
Misdirection Hex
Lightning
Chain Lightning
Winter's Grasp
Cone of Cold
Fireball
Blood Magic
Blood Wound
Blood Sacrifice
Note: Only one maleficarum can use Blood Magic abilities. Maleficarum also like to synchronise their spells: for instance, two of them might cast
Chain Lightning
or
Misdirection Hex
together, while the other uses
Weakness
or
Cone of Cold
.
Strategy
The fight will only start after you talk to them. Characters with
Stealing
skill may wish to pickpocket them all first.
Mana Clash
makes the fight trivially easy - one hit is enough.
If you do not have this useful anti-mage spell, use a long-term
Area of Effect
spell on them, save, possibly quick-load to reset cooldown times and only
then
talk to them.
Paralysis Explosion
is particularly good in this instance, preventing three of them from doing anything to you for a considerable amount of time, as the mage you talk to will never be affected. Given their high number of spells, casting
Glyph of Neutralization
afterwards would certainly be worth it, preventing one or two of them from spellcasting. Of course, other anti-mana abilities would be as useful as ever in this situation. If your party has neither, then spread the party members out to minimize the effect of AoE spells cast at them.
An alternative strategy
Playing on PC, even on Nightmare, this fight can be very easy:
The moment the fight starts, pause the game.
Set party members with
Poison-Making
skill to throw
Shock Bombs
(which drain mana in addition to health) or other grenades into the midst of the four maleficarum.
Release the grenades and instantly re-pause the fight.
Switch to
Alistair
and have him target the four maleficarum with
Holy Smite
.
Unpause the game and let these two effects devastate the four mages.
Pause the fight again.
These two attacks can kill one of the two normal-ranked mages outright and leave the other mages heavily damaged and low on mana.
Assess the damage of the remaining enemies, and set your entire crew to dispatch the one with the lowest health.
As they are standing up (Holy Smite will have blown them off their feet), position
Morrigan
into the midst of them and cast
Mind Blast
.  This will keep the other mages from casting at you while you take them down.
Bugs
As of patch 1.04 the bug that caused the maleficarum not to appear in the PC version of the game seems to have been fixed. However, just to be safe, clear out the ogres before taking
Wynne
into the area. If they do bug, causing Aneirin to spawn will fix it.
If darkspawn are not cleared out of this area around the tombstone prior to taking the quest in the PC Steam version, the maleficarum will not appear.
Rewards
Black Hand Gauntlets
from one of the dead
maleficar
.
Return to any Mages' Collective Liaison for your reward:
175 XP
2

======================================================================
PAGE: To Catch a Thief
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age II main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/To_Catch_a_Thief
======================================================================

Quest
To Catch a Thief
Act
2
Location
Lowtown
Start
Hawke Estate
End
Lowtown
Previous
Following the Qun
Next
Demands of The Qun
Appearances
Dragon Age II
To Catch a Thief
is an optional Act 2
main quest
in
Dragon Age II
.  It is only available if
Isabela
was recruited during Act 1.
Acquisition
This becomes available at the conclusion of
Following the Qun
.
Walkthrough
Hawke Estate
Return to the
Hawke Estate
in
Hightown
to find Isabela and
Aveline
arguing, both in need of help. Hawke must choose who to help first.
Isabela reveals that she found the relic that she had lost. Her pursuer,
Castillon
, will spare her life if the relic is returned.
Note:
If Hawke does not choose to help Isabela first, she will leave the party permanently and this quest ends.
Lowtown
Head to
Lowtown
at night with Isabela in the party to find Wall-Eyed Sam.  Things begin to heat up with a fight against a Sten and a couple of
Qunari
.
Note:
Pick up any dropped loot during this battle; when the Qunari are dead you do not regain control until you have left this area.
Note:
Isabela is going to leave the party temporarily (perhaps permanently) after the conversation after this fight, so you may want to remove some/all of her gear.
A cutscene reveals that the relic Isabela had stolen was a tome written by
Ashkaari Koslun
, the founder of the
Qun
and, according to Fenris, the "most revered being in their history." Because of Isabela's theft, the Arishok has been unable to simply leave
Kirkwall
until he has reclaimed what was lost under his command.  At the end of this conversation, Hawke chooses between reclaiming the tome for Isabela, which grants:
Isabela: friendship (
+15
),
Varric: friendship (
+10
),
Merrill: friendship (
+10
),
Aveline: friendship (
+5
)
Sebastian: rivalry (
+5
),
Or reclaiming it to return to the Qunari, which grants:
Isabela: rivalry (
+15
),
Merrill: rivalry (
+10
),
Aveline: rivalry (
+5
),
Sebastian: friendship (
+5
).
Note:
If you go to the door before triggering the fight, you will skip to the cutscene, and be put in the next section without doing the fight at all.
Lost-End Foundry
Enter the
Dark Foundry
for a fight with
Tevinter
Mages and some Qunari led by a Sten.  These mages are immune to spirit damage, so use frost and nature, which in turn will deal double damage to the Qunari.  Isabela runs away at the start of the battle to chase Wall-Eyed Sam, leaving the party. You can either keep your distance until the Qunari or the Tevinter Mages have killed each other or go kill all of them yourself (500 XP). In the room to the north-west, you'll find
Lambswool Insoles
in a crate - an armor upgrade for Isabela.
Note:
The saw blade shut off switches are
Master
level, meaning they require 40 cunning to detect (or the equivalent using
bonus to disarm traps
items).  No XP is received for pulling the levers. There are two in total, one through each door on the long single room to the north east of the area. They are not important, you can avoid them easily.
Exited Lost-End Foundry
After leaving the building, Hawke will find
Isabela's Letter
on Wall-Eyed Sam's body and the quest ends.
Result
Isabela's letter contains an apology and says that she is leaving, either because you promised her the relic (if you did) or because it would require a "better soul" than hers to return the relic to the Qunari (if you demanded she return it).
If Hawke has romanced Isabela at some point in the past or she has a Friendship/Rivalry score of fifty or greater
before
starting this quest, she will return with the relic during the quest
Demands of The Qun
.
Rewards
1000 XP (quest completion)
Several generic Qunari-related weapons, accessories, or armor
Non-generic rewards:
Gauntlets of the Ashaad
Lambswool Insoles
(Isabela's armor upgrade)
Trivia
While Isabela is describing the relic she mentions one of the options as "Cousland", which is the family name of the human noble
Grey Warden
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Bugs
During the conversation with Aveline and Isabela at the Hawke Estate the start of this quest, you may still see Gamlen in the background (from when he was here during the start of
All That Remains
).
There is a chance that Hawke won't have to battle the Sten and his Qunari at all. Just move straight towards the foundry entrance and click on it.  Hawke will be brought to a cutscene without having to fight.
If Isabela has the
Thumbs Up
friendship benefit, Hawke will retain the attack-speed bonus even after Isabela leaves the party.

Quest

Dragon Age: Origins
Tortured Noble
is a quest received in the dungeon of the
Arl of Denerim's Estate
.
Walkthrough
Oswyn
can be found in the torture room in the basement of the Arl of Denerim's estate. There will be five Torturers in the room with him, one of which is a Boss rank. After defeating them, free Oswyn from the rack and speak to him.
If you ask him why he was being tortured, he will tell you his wet nurse's son was a friend of his, and had been a soldier at Ostagar.  When he returned, he told Oswyn that the men were ordered to turn their backs on Cailan before the Darkspawn overtook him.  The friend disappeared the next day, and when Oswyn went looking for him he was captured after accepting a drink from a stranger.
The Warden can then receive a reward by speaking to Bann Sighard, located in the
Gnawed Noble Tavern
. Speaking to Sighard brings this quest to an end.
Rewards
A vote for the Warden in the
Landsmeet
.
The Bann will talk to Bann Reginalda of the White River Bannorn as well.
40
(50
with a second persuasion check)
250 XP (if you accept a reward of at least 40
)
If you decline the opportunity to request a reward in gold sovereigns, you will forfeit both coin and experience points as rewards. The Bann will still support you at the Landsmeet, though. Additionally, if you respond with the "I need no thanks for simple compassion" option,
Wynne approves (
+1
), if she is in the party.

Quest

Tough Love
is a
romance
-specific
companion quest
for
Iron Bull
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Acquisition
In order for Tough Love to be activated, one must enter a romantic relationship with the Iron Bull. Additionally, the
Inquisitor
must approach the Iron Bull about the status of their relationship and prompt the dialogue option discussing how serious their relationship is. The Iron Bull will indicate that under the Qun, most never enter serious relationships but when two
Qunari
(or people) choose to commit, each wears half a dragon's tooth.
Walkthrough
Once the Inquisitors has prompted this dialogue, the quest will be active. The Inquisitor must kill a
dragon
and craft the necklace using the Requisition table either in
Skyhold
or any camp site.
Requirements:
Obsidian
(1)
Dragon's Tooth
(1)
Results
Upon delivering the necklace, there will be a comical cut scene between the Inquisitor and Iron Bull in the nude, where the two are interrupted by
Cullen
,
Josephine
and
Cassandra
. Following this, the Inquisitor will be prompted to pass over the necklace and enter a relationship with the Iron Bull.

Dragon Age II
Tranquility
is an Act 1
main quest
in
Dragon Age II
.
During this quest
Hawke
helps
Anders
, a former
Grey Warden
, free a fellow mage from the
Templars
.
Acquisition
This quest is automatically available after completing the main quest
A Business Discussion
.
Walkthrough
Lirene's Fereldan Imports
Travel to
Lirene's Fereldan Imports
in
Lowtown
and speak with
Lirene
. She tells you about the Grey Warden, Anders, and directs Hawke to
Anders's Clinic
in
Darktown
.
Promise not to harm
Anders
:
Bethany: friendship (
+5
)
Varric: friendship (
+5
)

+5
)
"He sounds too good to be true." without any investigation
Varric: friendship (
+5
)
"Tell me where he is, or else.":
Varric: rivalry (
+5
)
Lowtown
A group of angry Fereldan refugees protecting Anders is waiting outside.
Bethany
or
Carver
can be asked to step in to defuse the situation.
Bethany: friendship (
+5
)
Carver: friendship (
+10
)
A diplomatic
Hawke
can also get the refugees to back off peacefully with the "We're on the same side" option.
Bethany: friendship (

)
Carver: friendship (

)

)

) (does not occur after the 1.04 patch)

)

)
All other dialogue options lead to combat.
Note:
You receive the same amount of XP from talking your way out of this as you do fighting them, although fighting them may result in some dropped loot.
Anders's Clinic
Travel to Anders's Clinic in Darktown. Anders initially reacts to Hawke's presence with hostile uncertainty but after asking for a way into the
Deep Roads
, he proposes a favor for a favor. Anders explains that he came to
Kirkwall
to free his mage friend
Karl Thekla
, who is a prisoner in the
Gallows
. Help Anders bring Karl to safety, and he'll provide Hawke with Warden maps of the Deep Roads.
Choosing to investigate and selecting the "Help him escape the Circle?" option:
"The mages need their freedom.":
Fenris: rivalry (
+10
)

Note:

Fenris: friendship (

)
Note:
You do not get this if you Investigated and asked Anders "Help him escape the Circle?"
The spirit of Justice comes forth.
Note:
If you travel immediately to "Hightown - Night" from Darktown on the Kirkwall map, you will enter Hightown via the staircase just a few feet from the Chantry.  This can help you avoid
Guardsman Pretenders
if you haven't wiped out a sufficient amount of them and enter the Chantry without injuries.
Hightown (night)
Meet Anders near the
Kirkwall chantry
in
Hightown
at night and head inside.
The Chantry (night)
There is a
Crate
in the southwestern room. Karl can be found on the opposite side, to the right of the entrance. He had been made
Tranquil
and set up as bait. Anders is enraged by what has been done to his friend, and as the party is attacked by the templars, the spirit of
Justice
overtakes him.
The initial wave consists of:
Templar Lieutenant (Elite) – drops
Ser Alrik's Letter
– this must be looted quickly, as below
3 Templars (Normal)
1 Templar Archer (Normal)
Once most, if not all, of the normal ranks are defeated (or if enough time passes), a second wave appears:
Templar Hunter (Elite) – drops random loot
2 Templars (Normal)
1 Templar Archer (Normal)
The corpses must be looted before all enemies are dead and the cutscene begins.
Once the templars have been defeated, Karl becomes himself long enough to ask Anders to kill him rather than let him live as one of the Tranquil. No matter how you advise Anders, he will fulfill Karl's request.
Telling Anders to kill Karl:
Bethany: friendship (
+5
)
Telling Anders let Karl live:
Bethany: rivalry (
+5
)
Strategy
This fight is notoriously difficult on
Nightmare
, due to the lack of adequate stun and crowd-control abilities at such an early stage of the game. One way to win the encounter is to order your companions to hold position and move them as a group (highlight all four characters, L1/LB + R1/RB on console gamepads, select "Move to Point") either back to the Chantry entrance or across the second floor to the chamber mirroring the starting position via the walkway behind the giant statue of Andraste. If you haven't attacked them all with an AoE, you will likely pull just a couple of enemies, namely the Lieutenant, while leaving the others to fruitlessly attack Anders, who is invincible for this fight, allowing you to take out those who pursued you. You may even be able to "drop out" of combat, fully recovering and being able to save, before returning to Anders to repeat the process.  The Templar Lieutenant and the Normal-ranked melee units tend to follow you to the latter position while leaving the archers behind, whereas the former position tends to pull an archer (or both) with them, not to mention the stairs and corners tend to make it difficult to target the enemies compared to a level surface on the second floor, and the walkway presents a choke point ideal for
Fireball
,
Bursting Arrow
, and
Firestorm
if available.  Templars have no elemental immunities even on Nightmare and are especially vulnerable to Frost and Spirit, making
Cone of Cold
one of the most effective spells to use.
After three enemies are killed, the backup wave will spawn in, and the Templar Hunter and Lieutenant working together can easily decimate your party.  The Lieutenant can pitch bombs to interrupt and slow you down while casting a health-regenerating aura on his allies, so damage him as best you can (preferably with AoE attacks to damage the other Normal-ranked units, killing one of them when their health dips low enough).  Stunning him removes the aura and prevents him from using a healing potion, but odds are he will resist
Miasmic Flask
, thus you'll have to settle for briefly interrupting him with
Pinning Shot
.  The Hunter, like any Assassin, can be flushed out of cover with Cone of Cold to temporarily halt his backstabbing, and can be damaged much more easily than the Lieutenant, so he will fall easily enough if all four party members focus their attacks on him.  This is of course more difficult with the Lieutenant still alive and regenerating the Hunter's health.  If you're continuing to have trouble, reload a save from prior to this quest and come back after fighting some
Gangs
in the streets to raise your level, then come back with spells like Firestorm,
Hex of Torment
, and
Horror
.
Anders's Clinic
The party is automatically transported back to the clinic where you can learn more about Anders's unique condition.
Codex entry: History of Kirkwall: Chapter 4
can be found to the left.
Result
Anders
joins the party.
Codex entry: The Tranquil
unlocks automatically upon completion of the quest.
Unlocks the following quests:
Talk to Anders
(
companion
)
Questions and Answers
(
companion
)
Enemies Among Us
(
main
)
The First Sacrifice
(
secondary
)
Fools Rush In
(
secondary
)
Rewards
100 XP (after the confrontation with the refugees, regardless of the outcome)
200 XP (for killing the templars)
400 XP (quest completion)
Codex entries
The Tranquil
Trivia
The Templar Lieutenant (Elite) that is slain is the recipient of
Ser Alrik's Letter
. By looting the corpse, it is revealed that Ser Bardel colluded with Ser
Otto Alrik
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Traps are a Girl's Best Friend
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Traps_are_a_Girl's_Best_Friend
======================================================================

Quest
Traps are a Girl's Best Friend
Location
Lothering
Quest Giver
Allison
End
Allison
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Traps are a Girl's Best Friend
is a side quest in
Lothering
.
Allison
has heard that
Barlin
is setting traps around his land to protect himself from the
darkspawn
, and she wants to protect herself in a similar fashion. Since Barlin has already acquired all the traps in the village, she hopes someone can make her three
Spring Traps
.
Walkthrough
Note:
If you fail a
Stealing
attempt in Lothering prior to starting this quest, it will become unavailable - Allison will refuse to open a dialogue at all.
This quest is only available to a group with the
Trap-Making
skill. If Allison is approached and no one in the active party has this skill, the quest will initially not be obtainable, and the dialogue with her cannot be triggered again. It will become available again, however, if an active party member has the skill.
Completing the quest is simple: convert three
Trap Triggers
into Spring Traps and give these to Allison.  Prior to first passage through Lothering, Triggers are available from the Ostagar
Quartermaster
, Barlin in
Dane's Refuge
, and the crates near the area entrance where the party first enter the area.
Rewards
50
(for each set of three traps)
100 XP (for each set of three traps)
Exploit
This quest is endlessly repeatable (until
Lothering
is destroyed). The first attempt to speak to Allison after first completion gives another "thanks for the traps I feel a lot safer now" and no dialogue, but after this she will be ready to accept more traps every time you speak with her.
In the downloaded
ps3
version, in the menu wheel, the number of traps (over one set of 99) rarely match what you bought, sometimes appearing to reduce at the same time; it has no bearing on the actual total you have (checking your equipment inventory will show the truth of this).
Problems and mitigation
One very important note to make is that you will receive experience each time you do this as well. You will rapidly gain levels, which can lead to difficulty when you next encounter enemies with leveled gear while you will likely still be using grey iron. Use with caution, especially on the PC version.
Item tier bug
If you unequip everything from your party except for items that don't scale in level, sell them all in
Dane's Refuge
after achieving your desired level and gold,and  exit the building then go back in, everything will have scaled up to the appropriate level so you can have tier 7 equipment  at the beginning of the game.
This is the procedure recommended for exploiting the
item tier bug
. Please note that this method either doesn't work on the PC version, or requires
Warden's Keep
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Trespasser (quest)
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Trespasser_(quest)
======================================================================

Quest
Trespasser
Location
Winter Palace
Start
War table
,
Skyhold
End
Elven Ruins
Previous
Attend the Exalted Council
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
-
Trespasser
Trespasser
is the primary
main quest
from the
Trespasser
DLC
for
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Two years after the fall of
Corypheus
, Divine
Victoria
has called an
Exalted Council
to determine the fate of the
Inquisition
.
Orlais
and
Ferelden
have strong opinions, and both of their representatives would have the Inquisitor's ear.
Acquisition
Complete the
war table
operation
Attend the Exalted Council
at a cost of 8 power.
Note:
Activating the war table operation triggers a "point of no return"; you will no longer be able to travel to other areas via the World Map--including
Skyhold
--and you cannot do any quests from the main game or any other DLC, even after finishing the Trespasser content. However, all the items you've placed in your storage chest in Skyhold will be transported to a new storage chest.
Walkthrough
The Winter Palace
Upon arriving at the
Winter Palace
Josephine
informs
The Inquisitor
of the current political state, with Orlais looking to control the Inquisition and Ferelden seeking for it to be dismantled altogether. After arriving and being greeted by Mother
Giselle
, the Inquisitor can explore the grounds of the palace.
Note:
There are several unmarked side-quests that can be completed here. The quests
Expensive Bones
and
Harlequin Tag
can be completed at this time, while
Painting Correction
can be started.
The initial objectives are to speak with the Fereldan ambassador,
Teagan Guerrin
, and to speak with the Orlesian ambassador,
Cyril de Montfort
. Both can be found by following their respective quest markers on the map.
This time also provides an opportunity to reunite with the Inquisitor's
companions and advisors
.
Note:
If their relationship was good enough,
Sera
offers the Inquisitor the chance to become a
Red Jenny
. Accepting or declining will have a small impact on the
Epilogue (Trespasser)
.
Once the conversations are complete, the Inquisitor can walk through the main gates to begin the Exalted Council.
Note:
Beginning the Exalted Council will lock off spending time with your companions
Proceedings in the council do not get far as it is interrupted by a messenger stating that
Leliana
needs to see the Inquisitor immediately. She has found a dead, fully armored Qunari in the palace.
Use the tracking ability to follow his blood trail up a nearby trellis and into an adjoining room with an active
Eluvian
. On the other side of the Eluvian, the Inquisitor will discover
The Crossroads
, a gateway to many other Eluvians.
Note:
Immediately to the left, the Inquisitor can find a skeleton; picking up the statue will start the unmarked quest
Halla Treasure Hunt
.
Follow the blood trail to an inactive Eluvian, then follow the blood trail to the right, which leads to an active mirror. Entering this Eluvian leads to the
Elven Mountain Ruins
.
To the right of the
Elven Mountain Ruins
eluvian is another which leads to the
Abandoned Chateau
; here the Inquisitor can loot
The Hand That Cuts
, a unique ring, from a chest protected by several demons.
Elven Mountain Ruins
Behind the crates to the left of the dead Qunari on the staircase, there is an Eluvian which leads to a
Superb Belt of the Inferno Pact
behind a wall of purple flame. Back on the initial staircase, go behind the entrance to find a
Superb Belt of Urgency
behind more purple flames. Following the main path leads to more dead Qunari and another Eluvian. This mirror takes the party to a broken bridge with Qunari on the other side. In front of them is a broken statue on a pedestal. Go through the Eluvian to the left to find a Spirit Champion, two Spirit Archers and two Spirit Shadows. If the Inquisitor drank from the
Well of Sorrows
, use the special dialogue option to pass safely. Otherwise they will attack you after speaking some words in
elven
. Behind the entrance to the tower is a
Superb Belt of the Storm Pact
behind more purple flame.
Note:
For all purple flames encountered during
Trespasser
, using the
Anchor Discharge
's invulnerability reduces damage received to zero for eight seconds. Casting
Barrier
and/or having
Guard
will help minimize damage while passing through the flames. Casting
Barrier
combined with
Fade Step
will allow the controlled character to pass through the flames undamaged, but be careful not to overshoot the mark. Additionally, the
Necromancer
ability
Simulacrum
or the
Knight-Enchanter
ability
Fade Cloak
can be used to cross the flames. For flames that are positioned in front of dead-end loot areas, the active character only needs to pass through once to obtain the loot. After obtaining what was on the other side, switching to another character and simply walking away will allow for the previously active character to rejoin the party, bypassing the flames altogether.
On the side of the tower examine a
mosaic
to dissolve the barrier and discover that the valley the party is in was created by
Fen'Harel
as a refuge for
slaves
. Enter the Eluvian inside to go to another tower. After climbing the stairs examine another mosaic. This one relays a message that Fen'Harel is not a god, but just a mortal. Descend the stairs left by bringing down the barrier. Inside the party will find another mosaic claiming that the
Evanuris
are not gods either. Behind this mosaic there is an elven device that causes the Inquisitor's anchor to react. This grants them the
Anchor Discharge
ability. Use this new ability to light the room and find a wolf statue that will fit into the broken bridge. It will also cause several Spirit Shadows to attack.
Note:
Be sure to enter the room after the final mosaic with the Inquisitor as the controlled character. As any controlled character will gain the
Anchor Discharge
ability; however, only the Inquisitor can wield it.
After defeating them, return to the bridge to place the statue on the pedestal. After crossing the bridge the party will be assaulted by Qunari. After defeating them, and entering the Forgotten Sanctuary, they will witness a fight between elven spirits and some Qunari. After their defeat, the party will encounter a mural depicting the Dread Wolf removing a number of elves'
vallaslin
.
Note:
Before entering the Forgotten Sanctuary, head around the northern side of the building and go west. Midway up the northern coast and downhill, there will be a tree stump near the water (if the Golden Halla is spotted, the party has come too far west). Head towards it and pick up the codex entry
Note: Qunari Notes
on the Qunari corpse nearby. This is the first of four codex entries required to unlock a hidden dialogue option with the DLC's antagonist.
The party must complete a
veilfire
puzzle, part of the "
Lateral Thinker
" achievement, to continue. The clue is "The Dread Wolf keeps its gaze on the one light that illuminates the way forward." Light veilfire from the alcove outside, then light the brazier that the wolf statue is looking at. It is the second from the staircase on the left. Press the button to reveal a hidden staircase. Be sure to loot the box in front of the statue containing the bow
Arrowwood
.
Under the stairs a mosaic stating how the vallaslin is removed a sign of freedom is found. In the Hidden armory, there are yet more Qunari. After killing them, loot the body of the Shock Trooper to find the greataxe
Single Incarnation
and some
orders
. These orders reveal that the Qunari are looking to infiltrate Halamshiral. Look around to find the
note
regarding the agent of Fen'Harel.
Note:
The crates and treasury inside the Hidden Armory cannot be accessed until later in the game. Once the Inquisitor has the Anchor Blast upgrade, come back to the Forgotten Sanctuary and go all the way to the back of the structure (to the alcove with the veilfire brazier). If they are standing facing the lake behind the Sanctuary with the veilfire torch at their back, they will need to turn left (south-west coast of the island) and wind their way down the path to the water. Detonating the gaatlok bombs here will clear a path to the treasury which can be looted for over 200K in gold and valuables. After entering the Hidden Armory, go left and up the stairs to find a group of crates and a wooden wall which can be detroyed for some sigil loot.
Return to the Winter Palace to inform the Inquisition advisors. After they have been informed, return to the crossroads to continue the investigation. (Note: After the cutscene, the Inquisitor does not get teleported back to the Crossroads. The new Eluvian is in the room under the top right-hand stairs, which was previously barred.) On returning to the Crossroads, there will be a new path. Follow it to the
Deep Roads
.
Note:
Before entering the
Deep Roads
, if the Inquisitor has found all Halla statuettes for the side quest
Halla Treasure Hunt
, they can finish it by returning the Top Hat to the skeleton immediately to the left after entering the Crossroads. This provides a bonus of +6 Cunning.
Also, there are two new areas available. The
Hidden Hollow
, to the left of the
Deep Roads
Eluvian, holds
The Eye That Weeps
. The
Forgotten Tower
, located to the right reachable by examining the Dragon eggs that create extensible bridges, contains
The Bind That Guides
.
The Deep Roads
Upon arrival the party will be attacked by two Qunari Spearmen, a Ben-Hassrath Agent, and a Qunari Shock Trooper. In the room immediately after them, a dead Qunari can be looted to find 22 units of
Veil Quartz
and evidence that there has been a bad cave-in. Proceed down the staircase, using the Anchor Discharge to light the way as needed. Eventually, the party will be attacked by a group of
deepstalkers
. In this same room is a
Codex entry: Torn Notebook in the Deep Roads, Section 1
on a sleeping bag near the staircase.
Further down the path is
Jerran
a former
templar
who had converted to the
Qun
. However, he has now grown disillusioned with them too. He reveals that the Qunari attacking the Inquisition are led by
Viddasala
. She has bought her troops here to the Deep Roads to mine for
lyrium
to strengthen the
Saarebas
as part of a plan called "Dragon's Breath". As he no longer wants to follow the Qunari, he states that the Inquisitor can obtain gaatlok primers to blow up the mine and hinder the operation. The Inquisitor can either spare or kill Jerran. If he is killed,
Cole
,
Dorian
, and
Varric
disapprove and a
Superb Amulet of Second Wind
can be looted from his body. If they choose not to kill him (
Iron Bull
slightly disapproves), the amulet can still be looted from his body on the way back out as he will be killed by Qunari. Speaking to him gains the
Codex entry: Viddasala
.
Continuing the descent to the Lower Walkways, there will be an attack by more deepstalkers. After being attacked by a second group there will be another section of purple flame. Behind this is a
Sigil of the Highland Ravager
. Before crossing the bridge to Mining and Processing, there is a
Sigil of the Deepstalker
in an unlocked chest.
Note:
The second codex entry
Note: Qunari Notes
needed to unlock the secret dialogue option at the end of the DLC, is next to and just before the purple flames that are just south of the area marked with blue lines on the map. **Make sure to pick up this codex entry now because the Lower Walkways will become inaccessible once the Deep Roads are flooded.**
Continue down the path to be attacked by more Qunari. The barrels of gaatlok can be detonated with fire, electricity, or manually to make fights easier. There is the codex
Codex entry: Torn Notebook in the Deep Roads, Section 2
before crossing the Great Bridge of Stone.
Continue to Central Supply where there will be another fight with Qunari. Drawing them out of the Central Supply Room in ones and twos and ranged attacks from the bridge just outside the room work well as the Qunari in the room will not attack party members outside of it. Loot
Heart of Rage
from the Saarebas and the
Armada Captain's Knife
from a chest. There is a
note
on a desk nearby too. Then proceed to pick up the primers. Follow the quest markers to place the four primers and detonate the bombs in order to destroy the Qunari mining structures. As each structure is detonated, more Qunari will spawn and attack. It may be easier to fight them by retreating to better lit areas such as the bridge back to Central Supply than in the tight, dark, basalt caverns. Close to the most south-west structure, walking west around the ledge is the codex
Codex entry: Torn Notebook in the Deep Roads, Section 3
. Working the way out through the lower walkways, a woman's voice will taunt the Inquisitor about things they cannot see and several groups of invisible Assassins will attack.
Note:
The supposedly invisible assassins often show up as red dots on the compass scope and going into tactics mode will make them completely visible as red outlines. They can also be spotted as floating crosshairs in regular mode.
After this has been completed, the cavern begins to flood, and the party must make their way out.
Note:
The Inquisitor can return to this location later. Once they have the
Anchor Blast
upgrade, they can clear a passage near the Grand Stairway. If they take the Eluvian there, they will be transported to an unknown ruin. There is a fresco about the
Titans
and find another veilfire puzzle here.
Once again, as the Inquisitor informs the advisors of the developments, they are interrupted by news that one of the Inquisition soldiers has been involved in a scuffle with an Orlesian servant. The Inquisitor can choose to resolve the situation by detaining either the guard or the servant. Whatever they choose (neither choice changes any future dialog or outcome), the Inquisition guard will hand over a note she found and Leliana will translate it. The note states that the Viddasala can be found through a mirror near a bookcase. You determine to follow this lead and return to the Crossroads once more.
Once there follow a new path to find the mirror described. Enter it to go to the
Shattered Library
.
The
Ancient Jail
, which houses
The Skin That Stalks
, can be reached via one of the extensible bridges on the left-hand side of the  island with the
Shattered Library
's eluvian.
The Shattered Library
Take a right at the previous mirror and examine the egg shaped object. Continue upwards to the first landing. Examine the same type of object there. This will cause a path to roll out before you, leading to another mirror. This mirror leads to the
Ancient Jail
, see that page for details.
Continue upwards to the bookshelves. Collect the three codex entries from the papers on the ground and proceed through the mirror.
In the initial room, there are the codices
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: Signs of Victory
,
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: Exile of the Forbidden Ones
, and
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: Homecoming
lying on the tables. Walk behind this room to find
Codex entry: The Very Private Diary of Sandal Feddic, DO NOT READ!!!!
, a
Superb Corrupting Rune
, a
Superb Frost Rune
, and a Modify Weapons stand.
Proceed down the central path to talk with an
Archivist
. This
spirit
relays some of the history of the area confirms that the Qunari have come here to learn more about the
Veil
. She also mentions that the librarians were recently woken by an unknown force.
Venture down the path to left of the Archivist to find a "
merchant
" who sells the
unique shield
:
The Best Defense
and its
schematic
:
The Best Defense Schematic
.
On the main platform behind the Archivist, the party will notice more Qunari on an "inverted island". Activate the red device to raise a portion of the steps needed to get there. However, more portions need to be raised in order to reach them. Follow the path to find another mirror to enter the Courtyard.
In the Courtyard are
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: A Flowering Imago
,
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: Duel of a Hundred Years
,
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: Attentive Listeners
,
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: Birds of Fancy
, as well as two more Eluvians near the central courtyard and a third around at the eastern edge. This third one can be completely ignored and is only used at the end to make the return trip shorter.
Note:
It is better to start with the lower Eluvian on the left up the small set of steps. Completing the Broken Tower veilfire puzzle will allow you to carry a veilfire torch which is used to unlock a hidden veilfire rune in the Lower Archives accessed by the Eluvian up the longer flight of stairs.
Go through the lower Eluvian on the left up the small set of steps to enter the Broken Tower. Go up the staircase to speak to another Archivist. Proceed through the mirror to get to Scholar's Retreat. Go up the stairs to find an elven fresco. Behind this, and protected by purple flames, is a
Sigil of Lyrium
and
Note: The Treachery of the Wolf
(This is the third codex entry required to unlock the secret dialogue option at the end of the DLC). Go down the stairs opposite from where you ascended to find a red device. Once activated, make your way back to the courtyard. In the Broken Tower Qunari Defenders will attack you.
Note:
In the Broken Tower, the Inquisitor can complete a veilfire puzzle needed for the Lateral Thinker achievement. See
here
for details.
Once the party has returned to the Courtyard after using a second red device, the Inquisitor will gain the
Anchor Blast
upgrade.
Go through the Eluvian that is up the long flight of stairs to enter the Sundered Hall. Go left immediately after entering to find
Fade-Touched Honeycomb
on skeleton swarming with bees. Climb the remains of a staircase near the skeleton and a Qunari who looks like he's been "scared to death". There will also be another aspect of the Archivist.
Talk to it and go through the mirror behind the Archivist to get to the Lower Archives. If the party carried a veilfire torch here from the Broken Tower, go straight past the stairs leading down and turn right into the small cul-de-sac, where they will find a veilfire rune on the ground. Interacting with the rune will summon a Fear Demon and several poison spiders, who will promptly try to swarm the party. They can loot a
Superb Amulet of Barrier
after killing the Fear Demon.
Follow the stairs down to a more well preserved section of the library. It houses the codices
Codex entry: Hard in Hightown: Chapter ???
(which Varric will react to, if he is in the active party),
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: The Deepest Fade
, and
Codex entry: Vir Dirthara: Raising the Sonallium
. Across from this there is another red device. Activate it and go back to the Courtyard. On the way back, the party will be attacked by four Ben-Hassrath Assassins and two Qunari Spearmen.
At the Courtyard, four
Librarian
spirits and numerous
Fearlings
attack. Once they have been defeated, the island is finally reachable.
On arrival, the party comes face-to-face with the Viddasala. She states that the Qunari plan on assassinating the leaders of the South and converting those who remained. In addition, she mentions how an agent of Fen'Harel had disrupted their plans. She departs, but leaves behind a contingent of Qunari to attack.
Loot
Stinking Cheater
, and the
Darvaarad Keystone
from a defeated Qunari. Search the surrounding antechambers for the
Codex entry: The Risk of Saarebas
, a
Superb Amulet of Evasion
, a
Superb Amulet of Critical Chain
, and the
Elgar'nan Enaste Schematic
(behind a wall of purple flame). Then proceed to speak to the final aspect of the Archivist.
It tells the Inquisitor that the Viddasala went to
The Darvaarad
, and that entry requires the keystone, and the words "maraas nehraa". The spirit then raises a path expediting the return to the Winter Palace.
While consulting with the Inquisition advisors back at the palace, Leliana explains that barrels of gaatlok have been found throughout places of power throughout Thedas. Further, someone in the Inquisition was responsible for transporting gaatlok to the Winter Palace. While Josephine laments the growing corruption of the Inquisition, the anchor flares up and the Inquisitor resolves to go the Darvaarad.
Note:
The unmarked side quest
Fireworks
is now available in the southwest corner of the palace's courtyard.
The Darvaarad
Note:
This is point of no return, if the Inquisitor has not completed the
Coroner
achievement and the first three parts of the
Lateral Thinker
achievement, do it before entering The Darvaarad. They should also have three of the four codex entries required to unlock the secret dialogue option at the end of the DLC.
As the party proceeds down the initial path, they will be attacked by multiple Qunari. Eventually they will make their way to The Courtyard. Looting the body of one of the Qunari here will yield a
Belt of Retaliation
and a
Gatehouse Key
. Use the key to unlock the control room housing the controls for the locked door. In the control room is
Codex entry: Logs of a Darvaarad Gatekeeper
and the controls. To unlock the door to the next area, turn the right wheel until the right pole is fully retracted, then turn the left wheel until the left pole is retracted, then finally turn the center wheel until the top pole is fully retracted. Once this is done the door will open.
Before proceeding through the vault door, the party can explore the Research Tower to the left. It houses
Codex entry: Saarath
and a veilfire puzzle (see
here
for details). The fourth and final codex entry to unlock the secret dialogue option at the end of the DLC is found on the top floor of the Research Tower on a table next to the halla statue.
Note:
Since Anchor Discharge now deals damage, the Inquisitor can use it to explode gaatlok barrels. The explosion deals massive damage to any melee enemies around the Inquisitor, and the invulnerability prevents the party from getting hurt.
When the party enters the barracks, the first door to the right will be locked, so continue down using one of the other doors. After defeating the first group of Qunari, there is the codex
Codex entry: Written Instructions for New Workers
on a table, and in an unlocked chest in the corner, a
Superb Belt of the Winter Pact
as well as the key to the locked room encountered earlier. Use it to enter the room and loot the
Amulet of Physical Immunity
. Proceed down to the study which has
Codex entry: Letters and Replies
as well as more information on the Qunari plans.
On reaching the gaatlok factory, the party will discover that the plan is named Dragon's Breath as they are using an actual
dragon
. Viddasala orders her men to attack. She also commands the
Iron Bull
to attack. If
Bull's Chargers
were sacrificed, or the Inquisitor did not complete
Demands of the Qun
, he will always turn on the Inquisitor even if
romanced
. He will show up here even if he is not in the active party. If this happens, the party must kill him. Otherwise he refuses to follow the order. Once these troops are defeated, search the room for the
Codex entry: Orders Posted in the Factory
.
On the path out is the
Codex entry: Animal Handler's Logbook
. This path leads to the Venom Extraction Chamber. Here the Inquisitor has the choice of killing or freeing the dragon
Ataashi
. To free her, use the Anchor Blast ability to blow up a wagon that is on one of the rings trapping her, then use the wheels on the upper walkway to move the flames and clear a path to the gates. One possible combination of wheel turns is to turn the left wheel 10 turns, the middle wheel 5 turns and the right wheel 9 turns. After clearing a path, pull both of the levers near the gate to open them. If Ataashi is killed, there is no loot on her.
After dealing with the dragon, again chase after Viddasala. Catching up to her this time, she reveals that
Solas
is a chief agent of Fen'Harel and states her intention to kill him. She then enters another Eluvian.
Elven Ruins
After speaking with Viddasala, the party is immediately transported to some
Elven Ruins
. As the party begins to chase the Qunari, the anchor creates an uncontrolled explosion and the Inquisitor gains the
Anchor Meltdown
ability. Be aware that now, if your focus builds up fully, an uncontrolled explosion will occur and damage your party.
Continue chasing Viddasala down, battling through multiple Qunari and going through multiple Eluvians until eventually she sends the
Saarebas
Saarath
to attack the party. Before his health is fully depleted, Saarath will break his chains and flee. See
Saarath (strategy)
for tips on how to defeat him.
There are a number of Eluvians in the next courtyard. Only one leads to where the party needs to go, the rest lead back to the courtyard and turn red and inactive after being used. After leaving the courtyard, there is a single Eluvian being guarded by Saarath. Defeat him and the Inquisitor will go through the mirror alone.
Upon entering, they find themselves face-to-face with a number of Qunari turned to stone. Solas can be heard in the distance, speaking Qunlat. The Inquisitor makes their way through the Qunari statues to find Solas and Viddasala alone. Switching back to the Common Tongue, Solas asks her to tell the Qunari to trouble him no further, then turns his back on her. Unwilling to comply with his demands, Viddasala readies herself to throw a spear at him. Before she can finish, Solas' eyes flash blue and she is turned to stone, as were the other Qunari. The Inquisitor approaches Solas, but another flare from the Anchor makes them fall to their knees before him. Once Solas makes the Anchor stop acting up, they proceed to have a conversation.
If you found the
Qunari notes
while travelling, a special dialogue option becomes available: the Inquisitor has figured out on their own that Solas is not an agent of Fen'Harel, but Fen'Harel himself, and confronts him about it.
During the conversation, Solas reveals that he was Solas first, and that Fen'Harel came later. It was a title given to him as an insult, though he himself wore it as a badge of honor. After the Evanuris murdered Mythal and threatened to destroy the entire world, he resolved to banish them permanently by creating the Veil. This caused the downfall of the elven people, destroying countless marvels dependent on the Fade like the Vir'Dirthara and taking away the elves' immortality. When Solas woke up from his slumber, only a year before joining the Inquisition, he was horrified by the consequences of his actions, feeling like he was walking through a world full of Tranquil. Solas had hopes for restoration, however. His plan was to use the Anchor to enter the Fade and tear down the Veil. It went awry because he need someone to bear the damage of the first unlocking of the anchor and had not foreseen a Tevinter magister having unlocked the secret of effective immortality. He joined the Inquisition because he thought it was the best chance for the people of Thedas. He will also reveal that he has spies of his own in the Inquisition, like the guard who first led the Inquisitor to the dead Qunari in the Winter Palace, and found the Qunari spy with the gaatlok barrel. Though his plan to restore his people will cause the destruction of the current world, Solas would not see them fall under the Qun, as he would rather see them die in comfort.
Once the conversation is exhausted, the Anchor will flare up again. To save the Inquisitor's life, Solas takes the Anchor, along with their left forearm, and leaves through the eluvian (which are now back in his control.)
There is another special dialogue option available to those who never exhausted one of Solas' dialogue trees in the main game. The Inquisitor will declare themselves uninterested in what Solas has to say, leading him to very briefly summarize the situation before taking the Anchor and leaving through the eluvian.
The Inquisitor then returns to the Exalted Council to decide the fate of the Inquisition: serve under Divine Victoria, or disband entirely.
Rewards
There are no items granted for completing the quest. See
Items (Trespasser)
for a complete list of items that can be found throughout the quest.
Results
The fate of the Inquisition is decided. See
Epilogue (Trespasser)
for all the possible fates of the Inquisition and your companions.
Codex entries
For a complete list, see
Category:Trespasser codex entries
.
Note texts
For a complete list, see
Category:Trespasser texts
.

Bugs
In some cases a primer is missing on one or more bombs. In particular, players (all platforms) have reported an isue with a missing primer in the chamber where you need to detonate a bomb to escape. A few players have managed to detonate the bomb to clear the passage by using fire magic next to the bomb, but the only reliable solution is to replay the sequence by loading an earlier savegame (lowering the difficulty while replaying the quest to speed up things is recommended) and save your game in new savegame slots frequently until you get past this point.

CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests

Quest
Unbound
Location
Ruined Temple
Tapster's Tavern
Lower Ruins
Quaint Hovel
Start
Find one of the adventurers
End
Gaxkang the Unbound
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Unbound
is a
side quest
that requires
the Warden
to find some missing notes in order to uncover an ancient mystery.
Walkthrough
In order to obtain this quest you need to do three things (in no particular order):
Get the letter off the dead adventurer's corpse located down the first hallway on the left after entering the
Ruined Temple
(the ruin is accessible during or after
The Urn of Sacred Ashes
questline).
Go to
Tapster's Tavern
in the
Commons
of
Orzammar
and talk to the nervous adventurer skulking in the northwest corner. After some dialogue, he will give the Warden a set of notes.
Afterwards head to the
Lower Ruins
in the
Brecilian Forest
and acquire the
journal
found in a bone pile of presumably another adventurer. The journal is located just south of the large "fire trap" room of the Lower Ruins.
Once all three are completed, travel to the
Dirty Back Alley
in southeastern
Denerim
. Interact with the door to a Quaint Hovel which is the home of Vilhm Madon and ask about
Gaxkang
to initiate dialogue and a difficult battle. See
Gaxkang the Unbound (strategy)
for tips on how to defeat it.
Result
Upon defeat, Gaxkang will drop the
Keening Blade
and several other items. There is a slight chance he will drop a
Fade Wall
shield and up to
5
0
0
on his corpse. In addition, the Warden can find about
10
0
0
and the
Elementalist's Grasp
in the chest situated in the back room.
After defeating Gaxkang, the
Dirty Back Alley
will disappear from the Denerim Map. The area will then be inaccessible permanently. It is highly recommended to complete the quests
Back Alley Justice
,
Blood of Warning
, and
Notices of Death
prior to initiating Unbound.
Note:
The quest will not complete until the Warden loots Gaxkang. If the Warden kills Gaxkang and leaves the
Dirty Back Alley
without looting him, the area can only be reentered after completing the three aforementioned quests.
See also
Unbound

Quest
Under Her Skin
Location
Skyhold
Summer Bazaar
,
Val Royeaux
Shrine of Dumat
Start
Leliana
End
Skyhold
Rewards
1,934 XP
Influence 400
Power 3
Previous
Champions of the Just
Related
Before the Dawn
(mutually exclusive)
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Under Her Skin
is an
advisor quest
for
Leliana
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
. It is only available if
the Inquisitor
chooses to side with the templars instead of the mages.
Much like the
Inquisition
,
Corypheus
has invested great time and effort building a trusted inner circle.
Calpernia
leads the
Venatori
—and it seems she's been busy. In the search for a a particular set of elven ruins, she and her army of
Tevinter
extremists have taken a keen interest in a merchant named
Vicinius
. Perhaps finding him would shed more light on Calpernia and her motivations.
Acquisition
Available after talking to Leliana in
Skyhold
. This quest should be completed before taking on
What Pride Had Wrought
, as it will unlock additional options when confronting Calpernia.
Walkthrough
Val Royeaux
A
memory crystal
Travel to
Val Royeaux
and find
Vicinius's Home
. It can be accessed from the docks, just north of the
Summer Bazaar
.
Find
papers
in the first room, then deal with the
Venatori
upstairs. Their party consists of two Zealots, one Stalker and a Spellbinder.
Further searching of the home will get you another
note
(also in the gallery below). In his study, you finally find
Vicinius
's body. Examine it to get a lead for pieces of a
memory crystal
, three of which have to be
discovered
to get an audible memory recording of what has happened here.
Don't overlook the
charred notebook
in the corner of the room; eventually leave and head back to
Skyhold
.
Skyhold
Show the memory crystal to
Leliana
, who will send you to speak with
Dagna
in the Undercroft.
Note:
If Dagna has not yet been recruited, the war table mission
Acquire the Arcanist
must be completed.
Leliana joins the meeting, and after viewing some mysterious event and discussing it, a new
war table
operation becomes available: head to the War Room and complete
Plant Crystal in Venatori Headquarters
with Leliana. After the report, visit her in her tower, to see if the receiving crystal is working.
Reports to Calpernia
will be stored in your
codex
, and information about a location connected to
Dumat
is needed. Return to the War Room and let Leliana for the cost of 1 power
investigate the Shrine of Dumat
.
Shrine of Dumat
Use the world map to travel to the
Shrine of Dumat
.
The Inquisitor can approach the front door by either going straight forward or along the terraces on either side.  Moving along either terrace will cause a rift-like spawning of a
Rage demon
, two
Shades
and two
Wraiths
, though there is only one wave. If not triggered on the way in, it may also be triggered on the way out.
Inside, you will have to fight some
Shades
,
Wraiths
and
Greater Terror Demons
. There are six red glowing crystals scattered across the area. Activating any one of them will activate the side quest
Corypheus's Memories
.
Note:
The
Superb Dragon-Slaying Rune Schematic
may be found in a bag on a desk on the lower floor.
Once you move past the main hall, you will find the door to the next chamber guarded by a
Pride demon
, a shade and two wraiths. Beyond this door
Magister
Erasthenes
can be found locked behind some sort of a magical barrier at the far end of the Shrine. Talk to him to learn more about Corypheus and Calpernia and then decide his fate. Whether you kill him or let him live, however, there will be no major effect to the game progress.
Return to Skyhold
Back at Skyhold, inform Leliana about the Magister to finish the quest. If you let Erasthenes live earlier, Leliana says that she (i.e. her agent) has interrogated him in the shrine and states that the Inquisition may learn more about Corypheus from him. If he was subjected to Tranquility, she also notes that he is as grateful to you for ending his pain as his muted emotions allow. If you choose to kill Erasthenes, Leliana will be a little bit upset about your decision, arguing that it would be better if you had let him live so that she could gather more information about Corypheus' mission.
Note:
Allowing Magister Erasthenes to live will not grant an additional
agent
for the
Inquisition
.
Approval
I'll free you from your pain. (Kill Erasthenes)

Sera Slightly Approves

Cole Approves
You're too useful to kill. (Let Erasthenes live)

Notes
Though
Iron Bull
usually
slightly approves
when a
group of Venatori is killed
, this doesn't apply to the encounter in
Vicinius's Home
.

Solas
,
Vivienne
, or
Dorian
, are present, they will comment on the lighting cage spell binding Erasthenes.
Bugs
ps3
When attempting to enter Vicinius' house In Val Royeaux, a bug may occur during the loading screen where the game force closes and you are returned to the Xbox home screen reloading doesn't seem to fix this. This bug appears to be fixed with Patch 5, although it is not listed in the patch notes.
pc
When the Inquisitor approaches the door of Shrine of Dumat, they will encounter a Fade rift opened from the dragon-head statue next to the door which will summon demons after a couple of seconds. As with common rifts, it will stop summoning demons if the party moves away and retreats immediately. Attempting to approach the door again after retreating, the rifts won't summon the demons again and the party can enter the shrine without having a combat. However, when the task of interacting with Magister Erasthenes is finished and the shrine exited, the rift will activate and summon demons. Walking to the far end of the entry and clicking the fence to exit the location will avoid a combat at this stage.
pc
The bag containing the schematic may be missing.
Gallery

Quest
Unfinished Business
Location
Hinterlands

Cassandra

Cassandra
Rewards
1,324 XP
Influence 600

Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Unfinished Business
is a
companion quest
for
Cassandra
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
Since the
Seekers
' strange absence, some of their high profile cases have been left unchecked. Help Cassandra hunt the targets down.
Acquisition
This quest becomes available after talking to
Cassandra
in
Skyhold
.
Walkthrough
Several
Apostate
Mages
and Rebel
Templar
targets are scattered across
Ferelden
and
Orlais
.
The targets needing eliminating include:
Ser Rebenger Torn and his renegade Templars
Ser Rebenger Torn is encamped in the
Hinterlands
. The easiest way to reach the target is to fast travel to the Forest Inquisition Camp (the westernmost camp on the World map).
Leo of Lucien Bay is encamped in the
Western Approach
. The target can be found in the
Echoback Canyon
; fast traveling to the Echo Back Canyon Inquisition Camp will be the most efficient starting point. However, in order to reach the target, the northern part of the Approach must first be unlocked by capturing
Griffon Wing Keep
.
Ser Hildebrandt is encamped on the
Exalted Plains
. The target is located in the
Crow Fens
; the area can be accessed by completing the war table operation
Gain Access to Ghilan'nain's Grove
. Fast travel to the Fens Inquisition Camp and head to the
Dead Hand
. Exiting from rear of the
puzzle room
will lead straight to the target. Hildebrandt will be a much lower-level enemy compared to others encountered in the area.
Jepler the Unbound is encamped in the
Emerald Graves
. The target can be found in the northeastern part of region. Either fast travel to the Direstone Inquisition Camp or to the entrance of
Din'an Hanin
to reach the target. The
Din'an Hanin
fast travel marker can be accessed after completing the war table operation
Investigate Elven Ruins
.
Gordon the Frank is encamped in
Emprise Du Lion
. The target is located at
Elfsblood Tower
; fast travel to the Sahrnia Inquisition Camp and head north. The target can be reached without having to explore much of the Emprise.
Rewards
Killing Ser Rebenger Torn and Ser Hildebrandt each yield:
Gordon the Frank and their followers
128 XP
Influence 80
Killing Jepler the Unbound and Leo of Lucien each yield:
242 XP
Influence 80
Killing Gordon the Frank yields:
331 XP
Influence 150
Completing the quest yields:
1,324 XP
Influence 600
Power 2
Approval
Cassandra Greatly Approves
and
Approves
each time a target is killed if she is in the active party.
Cassandra Approves
each time a target is killed if she is not in the active party.
Bugs
Targets might not be at their respective locations when
the Inquisitor
first arrives in the region after receiving the quest. Leaving the area and returning can potentially allow for the target to spawn.
Using the
Spirit Mark
ability on a target (using either
Dorian
or an Inquisitor with the
Necromancer
specialization) can soft lock the quest by preventing the target from being counted as dead. Leaving the map and returning should fix the issue.
