======================================================================
PAGE: Where the Dead Must Go
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Where_the_Dead_Must_Go
======================================================================

Quest
Where the Dead Must Go
Location
Necropolis Halls
Previous
Completing the Veilguard
or
The Dragon Slayer
Next
The Dragon Slayer
or
Shades of the Blight
Walking the Graves
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Where the Dead Must Go
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
and
Emmrich
's recruitment mission.
Acquisition
This quest is acquired by speaking to Bellara during the quest
Completing the Veilguard
.
Walkthrough
The team enters the mysterious Grand Necropolis to recruit Fade expert Emmrich Volkarin, but evil mages have plans of their own.
Note:
Multiple chests and areas will be visible but inaccessible until the
Grand Necropolis
becomes available as an exploration level.
Find Emmrich in the Grand Necropolis
Travel to the
Grand Necropolis
. Upon arriving, the team ventures into the Necropolis Halls and encounters Professor Emmrich Volkarin animating a skeleton to perform routine work. Bellara introduces herself as the person who has been corresponding with him. Emmrich offers to continue their conversation after he investigates the strange disturbance coming from further down the hall. Before proceeding down the hall, backtrack to the elevator for a codex which will not otherwise become available until after completing
Hauntings: From Beyond
.
If Rook is a
Mourn Watcher
, they will comment that they have not been in this specific part of the Necropolis before and didn't think they would ever get the chance to see it for themselves.
Investigate the disturbance in the Shrouded Halls
Follow Emmrich through the Necropolis halls until reaching a
despair demon
, then deal with it. Continue following Emmrich to investigate how the demon managed to slip through the Necropolis defenses. Upon reaching the Hollow Belfry, the group encounters a large horde of undead inhabiting the room with sabotaged defenses. Take out the undead and grab the chest in the northeast section of the room against a wall.
Discover what happened to the bell
Approach Emmrich and he'll ask Rook to find a wisp to unlock the door that leads up to the bell's unwanted chains. A wisp can be pulled from the southwest corner of the room and brought back to the door. Once through the door, follow the path until reaching a room where several undead reside. Take care of them and then continue until reaching the Vault of the Beloved where several despair demons lie in wait for anyone who steps on the ice patches. Fighting the despair demons is optional, though taking them out will make walking around much easier. Within the second alcove on the left is a memento.
Investigate the locked door toward the end of the hall, and Emmrich will call upon the wisps for aid. Three wisps will spawn throughout the hall; find and guide them back to the door to unlock it. Grab the chest just inside the door. Travel to the next open room and fight another group of undead. Once they have been dealt with, open the closed door to continue on your path.
The next area, Cascades, requires a small amount of platforming, but before opening the door to continue forward, walk to the end of the wooden plank on the right and jump onto a section of rock to grab a memento. Through the door is the room where Emmrich will learn who has summoned the despair demons. Light the three braziers within the room and then approach the skeleton on the pedestal. Emmrich will use his
corpse whispering
ability to uncover the
Venatori
as the perpetrators. Take the wisp to the locked door on the far side of the room to continue.
Drive out the Venatori
In the next room, take out a group of Venatori. Once complete, grab the contents of the chest on the north side of the room. Continue on the path to the west and take out the other group of Venatori in the area. A prisoner remains after the Venatori have been cleared out: a Tevinter slave brought as a planned blood sacrifice. Speak to him, and then continue to the next area with the Belfry mechanisms. Destroy all of the crystals holding the chains while clearing out any Venatori encountered in the process. After breaking the last chain, drop down into the chamber and speak to Emmrich at the center of the room to start the ritual for the bell.
Fight off waves of Venatori and demons as Emmrich conducts the ritual over the course of three minutes. Be prepared to fight off Venatori assassins, mages, shades, rage demons, and despair demons. Once the timer has reached its end, any remaining demons will scatter. Examine the Venatori's equipment and grab the nearby chest as well before speaking with Emmrich to depart.
Rook, their companion, Emmrich,
Myrna
, and
Vorgoth
speak on their reasons for requesting Emmrich as a Fade specialist. Emmrich's undead companion,
Manfred
, joins to offer tea. Despite many of the Mourn Watchers never leaving Nevarra, Myrna finds it necessary for Emmrich to leave so he can assist.
Rewards
+100 The Mourn Watch Strength
New Missive Available
Codex Update
Emmrich's bond increased
Chosen companion's bond increased
Results
Bellara has the name of a Fade expert: Professor Emmrich Volkarin, senior necromancer. But the professor is away on an expedition. To recruit him, the team must brave the shadowy depths of the Grand Necropolis of Nevarra.
Someone's unleashed despair demons in the Grand Necropolis. Determined to stop the culprits, Professor Emmrich Volkarin has asked the team to help him banish the demons by unchaining a mystic bell in the heart of the haunted vaults.
Venatori have invaded the Grand Necropolis, unleashing despair demons in the crypts. The team must help necromancer Emmrich Volkarin banish the demons by unchaining a mystical bell and then defeat the cultists stealing powerful magic from the vaults.
Drawing on his necromantic powers, Emmrich banished the despair demons infesting the Shrouded Halls and drove out the invading Venatori. Learning that malevolent ancient gods are causing chaos in the Fade, the professor joined the team to help stop these mystical threats to creation. Unbeknownst to him, the Venatori plot is the beginning of a chain of events that will lead Emmrich to a dark figure from his past.
Emmrich Volkarin is recruited
Approval
Upon arriving at the Grand Necropolis
Charmed.

(Humorous) Neat... skull-helmet? -
Emmrich approves
(Stoic) Are you dead or not?
Or if Rook is a Mourn Watcher:
Rook! Myrna' mentioned you.
(Diplomatic) Good things, I hope.
(Humorous) Good control on the skeletons. -
Emmrich approves

At the least! The implications of what Rook's witnessed are-ah, thank you Manfred!

(Humorous) Tea! That's so nice.
(Stoic) No thanks.
(Mourn Watcher) He's precious. -
Emmrich approves
I've spent my life exploring the Fade and speaking to spirits. If Rook needs an expert, none are better qualified than I.
Glad to have you in a fight.

Just keep the necromancy safe.

Emmrich approves

Trivia
The special dialogue for a Mourn Watcher background reveals that Manfred is wearing the old Mourn Watch uniform and that he made it himself.

======================================================================
PAGE: Wicked Eyes and Wicked Hearts
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Inquisition main quests

======================================================================

Quest
Wicked Eyes and Wicked Hearts
Location
Winter Palace
Start
War table
Previous
From the Ashes
Next
Here Lies the Abyss
or
What Pride Had Wrought
Appearances
Dragon Age: Inquisition
Wicked Eyes and Wicked Hearts
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: Inquisition
.
The fate of
Empress Celene
, and in turn, all of
Orlais
, has been threatened. The
Inquisition
must intervene.
Acquisition
The quest becomes available after speaking to
Josephine
during
From the Ashes
. It can be initiated by spending 30 Power at the
war table
, recommended for levels 12 to 15.
Note:
It is advised to have as much open inventory space as possible before beginning. Those with 60 or fewer free slots may have to sacrifice several items.
Walkthrough
Notes
See the
Endings
section of this page first if you are looking for a specific outcome with respect to the throne of Orlais. Certain outcomes require very specific steps to be taken or conversation dialogue options to be chosen.
Bringing
Sera
along will open up an additional side quest,
Red Jenny's Stashes
; this quest will not be available otherwise.
If the Inquisitor's love interest is present, a dance scene will trigger after the quest has concluded and the final interaction with Morrigan has occurred.
Note:
If Iron Bull is the Inquisitor's love interest, the dance will not be shown on screen, only alluded to.
It is only possible to complete all three side quests (Red Jenny's Stashes,
Throwing Away Money
and
The Great Blackmail Hunt
) if the halla statuettes were used to open the Lower Garden Door (costs 3 statuettes) and the Empress's Private Quarters (costs 5 statuettes).
Note:
Opening the aforementioned doors will prevent the reconciliation ending between Briala and the Empress.
There are a large number of valid variations in how to complete the various side quests and, for many of them, there are several paths to accomplish them. Not all possibilities can be documented. Specific item locations for the
Caprice coins
,
scandalous secrets
, Red Jenny stashes, and
halla statues
are not listed below; however, a running total is provided periodically. Follow the side quest links to find more specific information.
Halla counters below are incremental and do not account for halla you have "spent" opening doors. In-game counts may vary depending on choices made. A total of 11 halla statues can be found, however only 10 are required to complete the quest.
It is never noted in game, but eavesdrop circles sometimes require multiples listens to yield a 'scandalous secret'. This is particularly the case for ones that do not have written dialogue and are only vague whispers that go toward the counter. The Inquisitor will give a dismissive statement such as, "Waste of time," "Boring," or "Just gossip," if the secret is not obtained. The circle will disappear momentarily but reappear shortly after. To obtain a point for the secret, the Inquisitor will need to eavesdrop again, sometimes more than once on the same spot. Every eavesdrop circle has a purpose and will disappear once that purpose is served.
The only exceptions to this rule are two circles that appear after exploring the servants' quarters, both of which can be used to spy on a group of men guarding the trophy room. The dialogue is the same every time, with one gentleman telling his friend about a 'knife-eared serving girl in the kitchen'. The other exception is the circle used to listen to the two people talking on the balcony in the palace exterior.
To obtain the Belle of the Ball
trophy/achievement
for this quest, Court Approval must reach 100/100 at some point during the quest.
It is possible to end this quest with
Court Approval 100/100
. See
here
for details,
Gallery
.
Enter the
Winter Palace
Upon the
Inquisition
's arrival at the
Winter Palace
in
Halamshiral
,
the Inquisitor
is greeted by
Grand Duke Gaspard de Chalons
at the exterior entrance. Gaspard informs the Inquisitor that he believes
Briala
intends to disrupt the negotiations.
Court Approval starts between 25-45 out of a maximum of 100. If Court Approval reaches 0, it will be game over as the Inquisitor is thrown out of the ball.
If the Inquisitor reaches a maximum of 100 Court Approval at any time then gets a bonus, the game will show
Court Approval 0
.
The Inquisitor begins with a base Court Approval value of 40.
Human
mage
Inquisitors, and
elf
and
dwarf
Inquisitors of any class, take an automatic, immediate hit of
Court Approval -10
due to social stigma.
Likewise, all
Qunari
Inquisitors of any class take a hit of
Court Approval -15
. Human rogues and warriors will automatically receive
Court Approval 5
.
Speak to the noblewoman in the corner to begin the subquest
Montbelliard's Ring
. Choosing the middle option "Interesting stratagem" results in
Court Approval 5
. Choosing the bottom option "Find it yourself" causes
Court Approval -5
. Locate her
ring
on the nearby lion carving and return it to her for
Court Approval 5
, or tell her you couldn't find the ring and keep it to sell later. The latter will void any court approval, XP and Influence gains.
Note:
If after finishing the conversation with Gaspard, the Inquisitor runs directly to the noblewoman and speaks to her, before the immediate approval/disapproval hit (the one based on the Inquisitor's origin), then you can avoid it entirely. This is an exploit.
Head up the western stairs to begin
Even Gilded Walls Have Ears
. This short subquest introduces the eavesdrop mechanic. Once completed, move past the nobles to the end of the covered walkway and search for the Storage Key.
Head back downstairs and turn right to find the storage room. Enter the room and search to find the first Caprice coin in a pot. Picking it up begins the side quest
Throwing Away Money
. Picking up the secret document from the table in this room begins the side quest
The Great Blackmail Hunt
.
Note:
If you miss the document or the coin now, there is no way to retrieve them after entering the Palace.
Note:
The boss battle at the end of this quest can be skipped if Court Approval is at least 85. Regardless of if you plan on completing the 'Throwing Away Money' or 'The Great Blackmail Hunt' side quests, it's worth picking up what you find as tossing money in the fountain will give you 1 court approval each, while secrets delivered to Leliana will give you two each. This means that there is up to an additional 75 points that can be lost throughout the quest, though achieving 0 at any one point in the quest will still result in getting removed from the ball and a game over.
Head up the eastern stairs and pick up a halla statuette to begin the side quest,
Herd of Stone Halla
. Returning downstairs, the stone halla can be used to
Unlock the Eastern Storage
below, or saved for later.
Note:
The Eastern Storage door should not be unlocked if you want to reconcile Celene and Briala
and
complete the side quests
Red Jenny's Stashes
, and
Throwing Away Money
, as unlocking all the halla doors in the Winter Palace requires more halla statuettes than are available. Choosing which doors to unlock sometimes grants XP and Influence, but more importantly can also affect the outcome of this quest. For full details, see
Herd of Stone Halla
.
Side quest totals: 1/15
Caprice coins
, 1/11
halla statuettes
and 1/30
scandalous secrets
.
Enter the Vestibule
The Inquisitor arrives at the bottom of a staircase in the Vestibule. Going up the stairs, they will hear various nobles gossipping and they can interact with Cullen, a noble and two eavesdrop circles for scandalous secrets which can be done now or after entering the ballroom below. Cassandra and Varric can be found near Cullen, if brought on the quest. After wandering around the Vestibule, interact with either Gaspard or the Ballroom door to enter the Ballroom.
Enter the Ballroom and approach Celene
The Grand Duke, the Inquisitor, advisors and party members (except Cole) will be announced at court. In order to hear all of the announcements, do not move to the top of the steps by
Gaspard
too quickly.
Introductions
The herald of the ball will introduce the various companions and advisors:
Approaching Celene
Accompanying the Inquisitor:
Vivienne
: Madame Vivienne, First Enchanter of the Circle of Magi, Enchanter of the Imperial Court, Mistress of the Duke of Ghislain.
Iron Bull
: The Iron Bull, leader of the famed mercenary company Bull’s Chargers. As the name might imply.
Solas
: The Lord/Lady Inquisitor’s elven serving man, Solas.
Blackwall
: Warden Blackwall of Val Chevin, constable of the Grey. Bearer of the Silverite Wings of Valor.
Sera
: Her Ladyship Mai Bhalsych of Korse. (Sera snickers)
Cassandra
: Seeker Cassandra Allegra Portia Calogera Filomena– (Cassandra: Get on with it!) …Pentaghast. Fourteenth cousin to the King of Nevarra, nine times removed. Hero of Orlais, Right Hand of the Divine.
Varric
: Renowned author Varric Tethras. Head of noble House Tethras, deshyr of Kirkwall to the Dwarven Merchants Guild.
Dorian
: Lord Dorian Pavus, member of the Circle of Vyrantium, son of Lord Magister Halward Pavus of Asariel.
Cullen
: Ser Cullen Stanton Rutherford of Honnleath. Commander of the forces of the Inquisition. Former Knight-Commander of Kirkwall.
Leliana
: Lady Leliana, Nightingale of the Imperial Court. Veteran of the Fifth Blight.
If romanced by warden who becomes Queen: Mistress to the Queen of Ferelden.
If romanced by a warden who becomes Prince Consort: Mistress to the Prince Consort of Ferelden.
(continues) Seneschal of the Inquisition and Left Hand of the Divine.
Josephine
: And Lady Josephine Cherette Montilyet of Antiva City, Ambassador of the Inquisition.
Cole
is never announced, even if
he is made more human
.
Approaching Celene
After you arrive next to Gaspard, the
Grand Duchess Florianne de Chalons
will be introduced and
Empress Celene
will greet the Inquisitor:
Top option -
Court Approval 10
(Only for human rogues and warriors, and for mages of all races with Vivienne in the party)
Middle option -
Court Approval 5
Bottom option -
Court Approval -15
Vivienne Greatly Disapproves
The second set of choices have no court approval changes.
Leliana
will ask the Inquisitor to see her when they have a moment.
Speak with Leliana
Note:
The following outlines one of the myriad ways this quest can be completed. The important warning is to not pick up the last of the three required items until all side quests are completed as desired. After that, there will be a forced rush to the ballroom to progress the quest and some opportunities may vanish.
Mingle with the court and the party members and advisors.
Lady Mantillon
can be found here but will not converse with the Inquisitor at this time. Volume 1 of
A Compendium of Orlesian Theater
is on a couch here.
If she is brought along for the quest,
Sera
will be near the northeast corner of the ballroom, and asking about her "friends" will begin
Red Jenny's Stashes
. Speaking with her can also bring approval changes, see
companion approval
.
Go back to the Vestibule and a scene with Leliana begins. She mentions that an occult advisor seems to have charmed the empress and the court, while the Inquisitor mentions that Gaspard is pointing fingers at
Ambassador Briala
.
Both leads warrant investigating the Guest Wing. To progress, three items must be found:
The Cylinder Seal
in the Hall of Heroes.
Letter
in the Grand Library.
Negotiations
in the room of the upper Guest Gardens.
These can be acquired in any order, but once the third one is picked up, the Inquisitor will have limited time to return to the Ballroom.
The doors leading to the Grand Library from the Vestibule are locked from the other side, so another way in will need to be found.
Explore the Grand Library
Investigate Briala's agents
Hall of Heroes, Lower Level
Return to the entrance to the guest wing and enter the Hall of Heroes. The hall of heroes is located to the west as you exit the ballroom into the Vestibule. Grab the Caprice Coin at the bottom of the staircase on the right side. Approach the Whispering Elf then eavesdrop on the elves to learn about a package delivered to a room in the upper garden.
Continue into the guest wing. There is a eavesdrop point next to the first golden lion on your left for a scandalous secret. Head into the room to the east, speak to Duke Germain and ask about Gaspard being his nephew for
Court Approval 15
. The Council of Heralds Vassal in the guest wing will be pacing the hallways. Choose "Philippe is a jerk!" for
Court Approval 10
(the game displays Court Approval 15 but it actually adds 10). The other option gives
Court Approval -10
(the game displays Court Approval -15 but it actually removes 10). Either response serves as evidence to use against Gaspard. There is also a Caprice coin on the floor between the two southern lions in Duke Germain's room.
Proceed down the hallway to find another Whispering Elf to eavesdrop on. For an elven Inquisitor, they will speak to them directly with a warning of danger in the Servant's Quarters.
Continue down the hallway into the next room and through the door onto the balcony. Searching will find the first item,
The Cylinder Seal
next to a flower pot.
Note:
Do not talk to the group of three guards in the south room more than once. Doing so will cause a drop of
Court Approval -10
. It is also worth noting that if you do speak to them more than once, you will not receive a notification of any drop in approval.
Return to the guest wing and exit west into the Guest Garden. Celene's ladies-in-waiting will deliver a message from the empress. At the entrance, turn right, there is an eavesdrop point behind one of the pillars for a scandalous secret. There are also two nobles in the southwest portion of the garden that you can eavesdrop on for
The Great Blackmail Hunt
. Note that this circle can be eavesdropped twice for a scandalous secret. After eavesdropping on them enough times, the Inquisitor can eventually talk to the nobles and choose which one to support. Both options result in a gain of Influence and Power. One of Red Jenny's stashes can also be found in the open room by this eavesdropping circle.
Note:
This agent is not listed as an
Inquisition perk
after returning to
Skyhold
but does add power and influence and counts towards the "Persuasive"
achievement
.
Climb up the lattice next to the fountain in the garden and a Court Approval timed section begins.
The Inquisitor enters an area deemed off-limits. The Court notices the Inquisitor's absence and as a result, Court Approval will steadily drop (at a rate of about -1/min) while in this area.
The time limit is meant to induce a sense of urgency. However, this does not mean you cannot take your time (within reason) to explore all rooms and look for loot and various quest objectives. Further, the approval drop does not occur when the game is paused, so frequent pausing to move the camera around can reduce the loss. There are many ways to regain approval, as detailed below.
Once up the lattice, turn right and follow the railing around two turns to the right. Halfway down the rail is a Halla statue which you will need in just a moment. Pulse your search and you will turn up a Secret Document on the corner of the railing.
Return to the lattice, continue straight ahead, and open the Upper Garden Door located to the west of the lattice. It costs one halla statuette to be opened. You must open this door to advance the main quest. Grab the loot from the chest and pick up the
Negotiations
on the floor once you enter the halla door to obtain the second item in the objective.
Celene's occult advisor
Grand Library.
Return to the lattice and turn left to enter the Grand Library doors, located directly to the north of the lattice.
Note:
You can choose to search the rest of the library now or return later, however a couple of side quest items don't immediately appear in the library at this stage.
Move past the urns on the left and search the bookshelf to find a hidden switch. This opens the back door of the library. Go in but
do not
pick up the
Letter
just yet or the rush to the ballroom will start.
Instead, turn right, grab the
veilfire
torch, and light the urns in the previous room in the following order:
Etienne I
→
Reville
→ Etienne II →
Judicael I
→ Judicael II →
Florian
.
Simply:
Face the room with the garden entrance behind you.
Light the RIGHT side of urns, closest to farthest.
Return to the garden entrance and face the room.
Light the LEFT side of urns, closest to farthest.
Down here is a
map
3  2  1
X ==========>
6  5  4
X is the room where you got the veilfire torch.
Head down into the secret study, and the first chest on the right always contains
Confusion Grenade Recipe
One of the other chests always contains a
Masterwork Magister Staff Blade Schematic
.
Go back up the stairs from the secret study, turn left and pass through either door. Go down the stairs to find rooms on the left and right. The room on the right is locked and only accessible to Rogue Inquisitors. To the right there is also a hidden book that updates
Halamshiral Investigation - Clues
. The room to the left contains a loot chest and a scandalous secret. Continue down the long hall—picking up another scandalous secret on the tables by the windows—to double doors leading back to the Vestibule. Be sure to open one of these doors to allow easier access later.
If
Cole
was brought along, he will be here for dialogue. None of the options change his approval.
Side quest totals: 3/15
Caprice coins
, 2/11
halla statuettes
, 13/30
scandalous secrets
and 1/3
Red Jenny stashes
.
Return to the room where the veilfire is and pick up the
Letter
.
Return to the ballroom
The first bell will ring, signaling that it's time to return to the ballroom. The fastest route is back through the library, rather than going back down the lattice. Once the Inquisitor steps foot into the Vestibule or the Guest Gardens, Court Approval will no longer deteriorate.
Three bells ring before the Inquisitor must enter the Ballroom. The timing of entrance can gain or lose Court Approval as follows:
Enter after the first bell, on time - No Court Approval change
Enter after the second bell, fashionably late -
Court Approval 10
Enter after the third bell, actually late -
Court Approval -20
Attempt to enter the ballroom to instead meet Celene's occult advisor,
Morrigan
. She encountered and killed a
Tevinter agent
and hands over a key she found on his body.
Note:
There are 2 instances in total where the bell rings and the Inquisitor must return to the ball room, each has its own separate first, second, and third bells. Entering the ballroom now will not count as entering the ball for the second time, but allow for some dialogue sequences. The next set of bells that counts towards the approval will ring after returning from the servants' quarters.
Before entering the servants' quarters
Now is a good time to re-enter the Ballroom which will commence the Behind Every Empress subquest.
As noted above, entering the ballroom now does not count towards the "on time/fashionably late/actually late" approval.
Turn right and go down the side of the room. Talking to Leliana will give you the opportunity to drop off the thirteen scandalous secrets you have at this point, which are required to complete
The Great Blackmail Hunt
.
Note:
Depending on how you're doing on the approval thus far, you might want to hold on to the Secrets until after having done the Servants' quarters part, since you'll likely waste points by adding 13*2 = 26 to your current total.
Converse with Lady Mantillon, who is located near Leliana, and ask her to dance as a prerequisite for
Dance with the Dowager: The Allemande
(requires the
Nobility Knowledge
perk
and
Court Approval 60+
). This also grants
Court Approval 5
.
At the first right, exit the hall to the balcony and talk to Briala. None of the dialogue options with her have an effect on the main quest. Talking to her marks the quest stage for her as done.
Re-enter the ballroom and turn right immediately to the right to find the ladies-in-waiting by the statue. Speak with Lady Fleur, the middle of the ladies-in-waiting to complete Behind Every Empress.
Note:
The conversation prompt for Lady Fleur might bug out. Try reloading a save to have it reappear.
Continuing counter-clockwise, ignore the next balcony. Celene is there but cannot be spoken with. Gaspard is on the next balcony. Talking to him presents his take on the situation and others, completing the three-way opinion circle of the three parties.
Note:
If you have trained to be a Champion, there is a special dialogue option when interacting with Gaspard that increases approval by 20)
To get to the Servants' Quarters, return to the vestibule door and exit. Re-enter the Hall of Heroes and go to the west end of the lower level. Go through the door to the servant's quarters.
Explore the servant's quarters and Grand Apartments
Court Approval timed section begins
Equip everyone's arms and armor. This area is off-limits so Court Approval is ticking away, though time is frozen in the inventory screen.
Note:
As of the final patch, court approval will drop in the Servants' Quarters, Gardens and Grand Apartments (previously, only the Grand Apartments incurred the court approval penalty), meaning you cannot avoid approval drop in this section. You can return to the Hall of Heroes from the Servants' Quarters until you drop down to the fountain to reset the timer, however, you will lose approval after that point until you return to the ballroom. Even taking your time should lose you no more than 8 to 10 court approval. A tip to minimize approval loss is to make a save right after entering the servants' quarters and equipping your gear, and then do a rehearsal run through of the area, reload, then do a proper run as quickly as possible afterwards.
Examine the corpse for a bit of companion-dependent dialogue. Turn right and go through the archway into the bedroom. Continue counter-clockwise into the kitchen. Do not miss the Halla statue on the wooden beam above you. To grab it, you may either jump onto kitchen table or onto a three-tier pile of barrels against the wall. From the barrels, you may jump onto the beam and tiptoe to the statuette. The barrels are by the east wall. On the way to the Halla statue along the beams, there is a book which gives a codex entry on the Servants' Quarters of The Winter Palace. Counter-clockwise again takes you to the entry room. Turn right and exit to the gardens.
Before dropping down before the fountain, turn to the left and move to the back corner of the balcony. There will be a Halla statue in the back left corner.
Head towards the fountain and fall down to begin a cut scene. An emissary for the Council of Heralds is found dead, and a nearby weapon bears the Chalons family crest.
Find the
Harlequin
Kill the group of
Venatori
that appear.
There's a
Red Jenny Stash
in the gardens, to the southwest (left from facing the fountain), if that quest is active. Though it may be more time efficient to pick it up when leaving the area, either via the apartment balcony or the library, since that'll take you very near that spot.
Go north and grab the loot by the fountain on the right and continue north. There will be Venatori off to the left. Kill them or ignore them.
Turn east and head up the staircase. Climb the ladder to reach a balcony with a caprice coin at the end. Enter the adjacent room and look right to find a halla statuette.
Go back down to the ground floor and kill the Venatori if you didn't before. Continue west until a vine covered wall is reached. Turn left and go through the archway.
Enter the grand apartments, turning left and enter the bedroom. There's a caprice coin on the middle bed to the left.
Exit the room and go forward into the kitchen. There is a
Supply Cache
here. Return to the previous room and turn right. Go through the small room into the dining room. Kill the Venatori in the dining room, then grab the caprice coin on the table between the two flowerpots in the south west corner of the room (it will be the rightmost table when facing them).
Exit south to the library. Exit east from the library into the room with the fireplace and the long tables. Kill the Venatori. Head up the stairs, turn left and clear out the room to the left.
The nearby halla door leads to Celene's Vault and requires three halla statuettes to open. If opened, the Inquisitor will find
Masterwork Crow Blade Schematic
and an elven locket which is essential to one of five possible
quest endings
.
Note:
Opening this door may prevent the Inquisitor from completing
Red Jenny's Stashes
and obtaining the
public truce
ending.
Turn right out of the room, past the stairs and right again. Continue to the next corridor to face off against the Harlequin and a large group of Venatori. Or alternatively, just turn left, and follow where the corridor leads.
The Harlequin always drops
Crescent Slicer
. A scene with Briala begins after all enemies are eliminated.
Return to the Ballroom
Before leaving this room, be sure to grab the Halla statue that was visible in the cutscene with Briala and any dropped loot from the enemies killed. Dropping off the right side of the balcony is the quickest way down, but there is some not-so-apparent loot to be found. Once the cutscene with Briala is over and you've picked up your loot and the Halla statue, head north to the next room to find an open window on the right wall. Jump diagonally left across to the larger balcony to find more loot near some square crates. This might take more than one attempt, so if you fail, simply switch to another party member to attempt it up to 4 times. Return to the railing you landed on from the open window, jump down to land on a lower balcony near a large pile of gold and a corpse. Either jump off this lower balcony or switch to another party member that is still up top.
If the balconies are not taken, turn around, go back through the desk room, exit south, take an immediate left and go downstairs. Take a right at the bottom of the stairs, a left into the library and straight out into the gardens. Kill the Venatori skulking around here. Be sure to pick up the
Red Jenny Stash
by going west, then turn left around the corner, if you didn't get it earlier (right from the library exit, then left past the lamp post). Go past the elven agents (no dialogue options), go up the stairs and open the gate that goes back to Main Palace.
Court Approval timed section ends
Side quest totals: 6/15
Caprice coins
, 6/11
halla statuettes
, 13/30
scandalous secrets
and 2/3
Red Jenny stashes
.
Dance with Florianne
Go back through the Hall of Heroes, turn right at the Ballroom door and wait for the second bell for extra Court Approval. It is possible to miss the first bell due to the game loading. The Inquisitor will make a comment when the second bell rings.
Gaspard's Clandestine Meeting
is active now but can be completed later since time is an issue. On entering the ballroom a scene begins with Florianne. Any options not listed below have no court approval changes.

Florianne ultimately suggests investigating the Royal Wing. Another scene with the advisors begins immediately.
Leliana
reminds the Inquisitor that
Corypheus
wants chaos in the
empire
. She also says that there are many ways to bring stability to the empire, and not all involve Celene (hinting at multiple endings for the quest). Note you will be given four dialogue options at this point, choosing the 'i need more information' option means you can carry on and do all the optional stuff.
Find material in the Royal Wing
At this point the Inquisitor can optionally confront Gaspard and Briala with any evidence gathered so far, as well as learn more about their backgrounds if they didn't do so earlier.
Note:
Many new side quest items now appear in the Ballroom, Vestibule, Hall of Heroes, Guest Wing, in the Guest Garden and on its balcony and in the Grand Library. There are also a few in the Trophy Room. Now is also a good time to access the
Lower Garden Door
off the Guest Garden which also contains several items, unless the statuettes are being saved for a different purpose. If Varric's brought along, speak to the Council of Heralds and choose "You're fans of Varric's?" for
Court Approval 5
Trophy Room
Before heading to the Royal Wing, approach the Trophy Room on the north side of the Hall of Heroes and trick Ambassador Anton and his comrades into talking to Cullen, which also awards
Court Approval 10
.
Otherwise, if the Inquisitor is an elf, they can ask for Whispering Elves next to Ambassador Anton to make a distraction. However, this won't reward Approval points.
The Trophy Room is an off-limits area where Court Approval will be reduced. If ignoring the side quests, simply open the rear door, enter the office and loot the
Secret Orders
as material to blackmail Gaspard. The door on the left can be opened if the Inquisitor is a rogue.
Exiting the Trophy Room to the Hall of Heroes halts the drop in Court Approval.
Head to the royal wing located up the east stairs at the north end of the Vestibule.
Note:
Inside the Royal Wing the Inquisitor can access the Empress's Private Quarters, two Caprice coins, three scandalous secrets and four halla statuettes. If all other side quest items were found before entering, the Inquisitor should have 13/15
Caprice coins
, 7/11
halla statuettes
and 27/30
scandalous secrets
with
Red Jenny's Stashes
complete. You can exit the Royal Wing to collect any items you might have missed at any point before going through the doors opposite the balcony doors. If pursuing the Celene/Briala reconciliation ending, the elven locket must have been found
and
discussed with Briala, the Empress's ladies-in-waiting and the Empress herself. However, discussing the locket with both Celene and Briala will prevent the Public Truce ending.
The three points where blackmail evidence may be obtained are:
Against Briala: the elven servant
Against Celene: the soldier in the bed
Against Gaspard: the mercenary captain after closing the rift
Entering the Royal Wing resumes the periodic loss of Court Approval.
A Harlequin assassin attempting to murder an elven servant.
The Inquisitor will need to re-equip weapons, armor and helmet. Companions should appear ready to go.
There is a secret on the table immediately to the left.
Go up the stairs and into the Royal Quarters. Take a right and then a right through the door. Loot the two containers, one secret and the halla.
Exit the room and go past the locked door; a scream will be heard upon approaching the next door on the right. Open the door and a cutscene plays where the Inquisitor defenestrates a Harlequin. Interviewing the elven servant provides the blackmail evidence against Briala. There is also a halla and two lootboxes inside the room.
Exit the room through the passageway, enter the darkened hallway and go past the door with the Ferelden soldier yelling and down the steps to retrieve a halla from the floor. Return to the cutscene room, but do not enter yet.
Exit the room to the hallway and the Empress's Private Quarters are on the right which requires five halla statuettes to unlock (check
Herd of Stone Halla
for the locations of the last ones if you need them). Asking the soldier tied to the bed to testify against Celene is required to complete two of the side quests and to achieve the
public truce
ending. Otherwise, asking the soldier to keep this quiet awards
Court Approval 10
.
A Caprice coin can be found on the desk to the right of the bed when the Inquisitor is standing at foot of the bed, facing the desk. There is also a chance for
companion approval
gains (Cassandra, Dorian and/or Sera only). Leaving the soldier in the bed will restart the cutscene each time upon returning to the bed. If additional approval for Dorian or Sera is desired, leaving the soldier in the bed and returning repeatedly will allow for additonal approval gains. The soldier however must be set free to allow access to the coin.
The final Caprice coin is in a room off the darkened hallway, see
Throwing Away Money
for directions.
Note:
Once all the scandalous secrets and Caprice coins have been found, the Inquisitor should exit the Royal Wing immediately to (a) talk to Leliana and deliver the blackmail in order to complete
The Great Blackmail Hunt
and (b) toss all the coins into the coin fountain to maximize Influence gains for
Throwing Away Money
). Once the trap is sprung, any opportunity to complete these quests is lost.
Evade the trap
Note:
It is possible to end this quest with
Court Approval 100/100
. See
Gallery
. In order to do so you must gain maximum approval before entering "Jardin de Rȇverie" in the "Royal Wing". Because it is an off-limits area where
Court Approval
will steadily drop you must move fast, frequently pausing to move the camera around, and (if necessary) head back few times to the far eastern room (the balcony) on the "Upper Level" (where approval doesn't drop). Having a
rogue
with
stealth
and
Fade-Touched
gear make it a lot easier. Finishing with 100/100 doesn't actually do anything. As long as you have obtained 100/100 at some point, and finish with at least 85/100, you'll earn the Belle of the Ball trophy/achievement and unlock the non-violent ending. As such, ending with 100/100 is only its own reward.
Warning: This next section will prevent opening Celene's quarters.
The assassin unmasked
Go through the doors opposite the balcony doors. This causes a cut scene to take place and the party is ambushed. There will be a dialogue with Florianne which provides the following option (all other choices have no effect):
Corypheus insisted...
It's not too late to end this. -
Sera Slightly Disapproves
I'm sure he'll get used to it. / Corypheus will be disappointed. -
Sera Slightly Approves
The
Fade rift
will spawn a random combination of
wraiths
,
shades
,
greater terrors
,
despair demons
and/or
rage demons
. Close it and there is a very brief interval during which the rift drop can be looted prior to a scene beginning with the mercenary captain in which he agrees to testify against Gaspard. If the Underworld Knowledge
perk
has been selected, an option to get his company to work for the Inquisition appears. This awards Influence, Power and
companion approval
.
Note:
The Agent Acquired graphic is displayed again, but no new agent is listed upon returning to Skyhold.
Return to Cullen with the information gathered
Head west to the locked door, turn right and loot the box. Go through the east door onto a balcony to find some gold (the balcony also serves as a safe zone where Court Approval does not drop). Then head south into Le Requiem and dispatch a small group of
Venatori
.
Jardin de Rêverie
Proceed down the stairs to encounter the final group of Venatori. Head through the northern hallway, then proceed into the next room and
Choose a Lower Royal Wing Door
if enough halla statuettes are held. Each door requires five halla statuettes and both doors can be opened (if you've gathered and saved ten of the eleven halla statuettes in the Winter Palace, even though the quest text by the doors suggests otherwise).
Head back and turn right, open the door and continue into a larger courtyard. Take a right and head through the north door into the kitchen, which contains the final supply cache. The room to the west ("Dining Room") has 2 small loot chests. There is a short dead-end hallway by the west-most chest that is a safe zone for Court Approval. Go back out through the kitchens and straight across the courtyard. Open the door, turn left, go up the stairs and loot the footlocker. Go straight back and the doors will activate a cutscene.
Confront Grand Duchess Florianne
The scene begins as Cullen asks the Inquisitor what they should do:
I'll talk with Florianne.
(Publicly expose the duchess' crimes to the court, avoiding further bloodshed.)
Requires Court Approval of 85 or greater
Necessary to gain the Belle of the Ball achievement
Boss battle with Florianne is skipped.
Detain the duchess.
(Save Celene's life, then deal with the duchess.)
Leads to boss battle with Florianne
Wait for Florianne to attack.
(Allow the empress to die before dealing with the duchess.)
Leads to boss battle with Florianne
Each decision results in different
companion approval
outcomes.
If choosing to fight Florianne, see
Florianne de Chalons (strategy)
.
Denouement
"Hours after you were evicted from the Winter Palace, news spread across the land that Empress Celene was dead and Grand Duke Gaspard standing accused of treason. Orlais stood leaderless: in the chaos, Corypheus conquered it all too easily, overwhelming Inquisition forces lacking the allies needed to stop him."
Following the confrontation with the Grand Duchess and after
deciding the fate of Orlais' throne
, a new scene plays as
Morrigan
informs the Inquisitor that she has been named the Imperial liaison to the Inquisition.
If a romance is being pursued, the Inquisitor's love interest will join them on the balcony. Accepting the offer of a dance will result in
approval
(not applicable to advisors, of course). If there is no love interest or they were not brought to the Winter Palace, Josephine will come out to talk.
If Sera is the Inquisitor's love interest, then this approval can only be obtained before the finale while Sera is still in the ballroom: a dialogue option suggests that the couple dance together, an offer which Sera declines humorously.
The scene then moves to
Skyhold
, and
Leliana
and
Cassandra
's future is discussed, after which the quest completes.
Endings
Regardless of a boss battle occurring, the final sequence of the quest involves a series of cutscenes and dialogue choices which decide the fate of Orlais' throne.
Celene rules alone
Empress Celene addresses the court.
Celene can maintain her place on the throne and rule alone.
After Florianne springs her trap, the Inquisitor must:
Keep Celene alive.
Reveal evidence of Gaspard's crimes to the Empress ("Gaspard was planning a coup.")
Celene and Briala rule together
Celene and Briala's relationship can be reconciled. Celene will maintain her place on the throne with Briala's support. Briala is given the title of Marquise.
The Inquisitor must:
Retrieve the elven locket from Celene's vault. This is found in the Servant's Quarters/Grand Apartments (upper level) by opening a halla door that requires three halla statuettes.
Note:
Opening this door prevents the Inquisitor from completing
Throwing Away Money
. If the right doors are chosen, the Inquisitor can complete
The Great Blackmail Hunt
or
Red Jenny's Stashes
but not both. See
Herd of Stone Halla
for more details.
Speak with
both
Briala and the Empress's ladies-in-waiting in the main ballroom and show them the locket. This leads to a cut scene involving the Inquisitor and the Empress.
Keep Celene alive.
Give Briala partial credit for rooting out the assassins ("Briala helped stop Florianne.")
Use the gathered evidence against Gaspard and accuse him of treason (and)
Request that Briala be rewarded ("What about Briala's reward?")
Note:
Choosing to spare Gaspard will result in Celene ruling alone.
Public truce
All three leaders can be blackmailed and forced into a public truce.
The Inquisitor must:
Enter the Empress's Private Quarters (which requires five halla statuettes) and convince the naked soldier to testify against her.
Retrieve blackmail material on Briala.
Retrieve blackmail material on Gaspard.
Have at least 85 Court Approval (any lower and Celene will dismiss your accusation as "histrionics").
Keep Celene alive.
Try to force the three leaders to a truce ("All of you are at fault.")
Emperor Gaspard addresses the court.
Gaspard rules alone
Gaspard can become emperor and rule alone.
The Inquisitor must:
Allow Celene to be assassinated
Use blackmail material to discredit Briala and support Gaspard.
Gaspard and Briala rule
Gaspard can become emperor, with Briala pulling the strings from the shadows.
The Inquisitor must:
Allow Celene to be assassinated.
Use blackmail material to force Gaspard to submit to Briala.

Trivia
Wicked Eyes and Wicked Hearts quest concept
Most of the quest names in the main plot refer to specific verses of
Chant of Light
. "Wicked eyes and wicked hearts" refers to the Canticle of Silence 3:6.
If Blackwall is brought for the quest, he will be found conversing with a noble who thinks he recognizes Blackwall from a Lord Rudalt de Lancre's company of soldiers.
If she is brought along, Vivienne mentions that there are six members of the
Council of Heralds
present, and that the seventh is "indisposed," further complicating negotiations. Her personal quest–which cannot be unlocked until Wicked Eyes and Wicked Hearts has been completed–reveals that her deathly ill lover, Duke
Bastien de Ghislain
, was the head of the Council of Heralds, and was likely indisposed due to his illness.
There are three dwarven emissaries in the Vestibule who mention
House Helmi
is trying to mount an expedition to
Amgarrak
, but
House Dace
is blocking the expedition into the
thaig
. They also express concerns about the flow of
lyrium
in
Minrathous
.
If the Hero of Ferelden is a dwarf and survived, the same three dwarves are wondering about the disappearance of Paragon
Brosca
/
Aeducan
.
There is a
krogan
head in the trophy room. This is a reference to the game series
Mass Effect
.
In the Fumeur section, one of the nobles is trying to figure out classic puzzles such as
Knights and Knaves
, the
Tower of Hanoi
,
Fox, Goose, Beans
and the
Riddle of the Sphinx
.
Everyone in the Winter Palace except the servants, the Inquisitor, and their companions and advisors, wears a mask as proper Orlesians do. This is particularly noticeable with respect to
Vivienne
, who does wear a mask when the Inquisitor
first meets her
, but not here.
Bugs
After the announcements and speaking to Celene for the first time, the Inquisitor bows. Leliana can be seen down the stairs with Cullen and Josephine (if you wait out and don't rush into cutscene, you'll see them approaching after you, in the actual cutscene you can see Leliana coming right beside the Inquisitor). When the Inquisitor turns to the left to leave, Leliana has miraculously appeared at the top of the steps.
In the
Game of the Year Edition
, she moves up the lower stairs, passes round Gaspard to go up, then reappears with Cullen and Josephine, then she's back at the top of the stairs.
ps4
pc
If you free the naked soldier in the Empress' bedroom using the hush up option, you may experience a random court approval drop message, but court approval does not change.
After completing the quest, even if the Inquisitor choses to have all 3 parties work together, Leliana may comment that Celene is "rooting out the last of Gaspard's supporters" when asked for any updates at Skyhold.
Be careful when moving between the Vestibule and the Grand Ballroom as it is possible for the door's destination to change to the room you're already in if you get too close, causing the game to never leave the loading screen or an exceptionally long loading time. The same problem exists with the door between the Vestibule and the Royal Wing.
If the Inquisitor uses necromancy in the Harlequin fight, the cut-scene required for progression may not trigger.
The work-around is to enable friendly fire (settings -> gameplay -> friendly fire) and AOE any of the raised opponents to death.
Or, use
Spirit Mark
to instantly kill all raised spirits.
While in the Ballroom after being introduced to Celene, if the Inquisitor asks Vivienne if there is anything to look out for, she will comment on Cole behaving even more strangely than usual, even if Cole is not in the party.
When the American-accented female inquisitor is in a romance with Solas, her voice may become male when asking him to dance. This
mod
corrects this mistake.
When coming and going from the vestibule and ballroom, the door may be labeled as both "Vestibule" and "Grand Ballroom." Rotate the camera and the position of the Inquisitor to get the correct label to appear. If entering the door when labeled as the same room you're in, the game will take a long time loading, and you'll reappear in the same room.
When being introduced in the ballroom in the beginning of the mission, if you stop before going up the stairs and look up to the right, you will see another Leliana standing on the other side of the railing, standing in the same position as the Leliana next to Cullen and Josephine, that you can highlight if you put your cursor over her.
In the garden area, the minstrel sings songs translated into French, but the captioning remains localized.
The approval drop in off-limit areas can be avoided by quicksaving and quickloading rapidly - as reloading the game resets the countdown of the approval drop.
Side quests involving the halla statuettes may become permanently stuck on the side of the screen where active quests are shown even after progressing beyond the Winter Palace.
Sera can be asked to dance by a female Inquisitor even if not in a romance with her.
If the player saves and reloads after the first bell has rung, the second bell may not ever trigger.
If Varric is brought along,
Codex entry: Council of Heralds
will be unobtainable.

Winter Palace Exterior Upper Balcony Map

======================================================================
PAGE: Will and Testament
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Will_and_Testament
======================================================================

Quest
Will and Testament
Type
Companion quest
Location
Lighthouse
Necropolis Halls
Previous
The Sacrifice of Souls
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Will and Testament
is a
companion quest
for
Emmrich
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. Emmrich has chosen to become a lich, and the rites are ready to be completed as soon as his chosen witness, Rook, is available.
Acquisition
Speaking to Emmrich after completing
The Sacrifice of Souls
and choosing to embrace lichdom leads to this quest.
Walkthrough
Having decided to embrace lichdom, Emmrich makes his final preparations for the rites before returning to the Necropolis.
Meet Emmrich in the Grand Necropolis to begin the ceremony.
Deep in the Necropolis, Emmrich and his witness, Rook, are greeted by the lich lords. It is time for Emmrich to be judged by the dead in a ceremony (to which Rook is not privy). If the two are a couple, the liches may refer to Rook as Volkarin's beloved, and Emmrich tells Rook that they are the most magnificent thing to have ever happened to him. Rook may choose to tell Emmrich they love him, which he will reciprocate.
He,
Myrna
, and
Vorgoth
then enter the next chamber. Time passes before the three emerge again, Vorgoth with a bloody blade and Emmrich as a lich. If in a romance, he will confirm that he still has feelings for Rook after his transformation, something that was uncertain beforehand.
The scene transitions back to the
Lighthouse
where the Veilguard is gathered for Emmrich's announcement. However, the secret is already out; in a humorous scene, everyone gossips about the event until Emmrich, in frustration, reveals his true appearance, silencing them all. Despite skepticism and hesitation from multiple members, he is ultimately accepted as he is.
Rewards
Completing the quest yields:
250 XP
Emmrich attains
Hero of the Veilguard
status along with the heroic armor
Lich's Vestige
and the skill
Death's Caress
.
Results
Emmrich has studied the secrets of lichdom for decades, but the final rites are fraught with danger. After ensuring that everything is in order, the senior necromancer will head to the Necropolis and employ all of his magic and courage toward fulfilling his dreams of undead immortality.
The rites have succeeded: Emmrich Volkarin has ascended to become a lich lord, one of the immortal guardians of the Necropolis. Becoming a lich has opened Emmrich's senses to the Fade, allowing him to scry wonders and mysteries beyond his wildest dreams. Although he can put on a mortal illusion to not frighten people outside Nevarra, the professor always appears in his true form to those he trusts.
Approval
Upon quest completion:
Emmrich's bond increases
Missives
From the Necropolis
Trivia
Depending on the status of
Harding's
personal questline, either she or Vorgoth will be the one to spill the beans about Emmrich's transformation beforehand. If it was Harding, she sheepishly asks if it was meant to be a secret, and if it was Vorgoth, Emmrich calls them a 'chatterbox' - not the first reference to the Mourn Watch being full of gossip.

======================================================================
PAGE: Winding Ways
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard main quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Winding_Ways
======================================================================

Quest
Winding Ways
Type
Main quest
Location
The Crossroads
Previous
The Smuggled Relic Case
or
Sea of Blood
Next
An Unfamiliar Sense
Echoes of the Past
The Dragon Slayer
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Winding Ways
is a
main quest
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. A new area of the
Crossroads
has unlocked, but it's swarming with
Antaam
.
Acquisition
This quest is acquired as soon as the player receives their first quest in either
Arlathan Forest
or the
Rivain Coast
. Any of the following quests, whichever is the first one that is acquired, will trigger Winding Ways:
An Unfamiliar Sense
Echoes of the Past
The Dragon Slayer
The earliest that Windings Ways can be triggered is after completion of either
The Smuggled Relic Case
or
Sea of Blood
, since completing one of them is required to start An Unfamiliar Sense.
Walkthrough
The team must fight through the gods' minions in the Crossroads as they find their way to a nexus of eluvians.
Find a way to Arlathan
Enter the
Crossroads
, head to the middle dock on Beacon Island, and catch the Caretaker's boat to a new island. Follow the quest marker and encounter a group of
Antaam
battling some guardians. Take out all enemies and burst the blight boil blocking your path into the clearing with the eluvians.
Rewards
None.
Results
The gods' forces are everywhere in the Crossroads, as is the blight. The team must forge ahead to reach more eluvians if they're to oppose Elgar'nan and Ghilan'nain back in the mortal world.
Fast travel to Arlathan Forest unlocks
Approval
None.
Codex entries
None.
Notes
This quest does not appear in the journal once completed.
Travelling to begin The Dragon Slayer will also complete Winding Ways.

Walkthrough

to give her the
gift
. This will start a conversation about Merrill's surprise at receiving a gift (
friendship
) or about Master Ilen's halla carving "obsession" (rivalry).
Result
Merrill: friendship (
+15
) if already tending toward friendship with Merrill (Approval >= 0)
Merrill: rivalry (
+15
) if already tending toward rivalry with Merrill (Approval < 0)
Achievement/Trophy "Gift Giver" (unless already unlocked)

======================================================================
PAGE: Words of Fire
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: The Veilguard companion quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Words_of_Fire
======================================================================

Quest
Words of Fire
Type
Companion quest
Location
Rivain Coast
Previous
Lair of the Dragon King
Next
Horns and Halla at the Hilt
Appearances
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
Words of Fire
is a
companion quest
for
Taash
in
Dragon Age: The Veilguard
. Now that they have decided their path, they must choose how to move forward.
Acquisition
After completing the final conversation of
Lair of the Dragon King
, leave and return to the
Lighthouse
. Speak to Taash, who has heard from someone who can translate the text on the tablet revealed by their
adaari
fire. However, the person insists on meeting them in person in
Rivain
.
Walkthrough
Taash gets a translation of the ancient Qunari tablet that holds clues to their past... and future.
Taash and Rook meet
Karash
on the
Rivain Coast
, who alerts them others have come to the beach.
If Rook encouraged Taash to become more
Qunari
, Karash reveals the new arrivals are true
Qunari
, not
Antaam
, and they have offered to take him back to
Par Vollen
if he wishes. If Rook encouraged Taash to become more
Rivaini
, the new arrivals are Rivaini
seers
, who have offered to let him join their ships and teach the weather.
The Qunari linguist and her ship.
Either way, he thinks he is ready to go, and staying here without
Shathann
would make him sad. He recognizes what Taash has done with their mother's horn (grafted to Taash's own or worn as a necklace) and bids them farewell.
Follow Taash along the path, pausing to throw rocks into the ocean. At the end of the path is the person who called them: either
Seer Rowan
(if Taash became more Rivaini) or a Qunari linguist called Ashkaari Issdar
(if Taash became more Qunari), whose boat was small enough to dodge the Antaam blockades.
Seer Rowan reveals she found a
spirit
in her dreams who knows the old language and respects the
Qun
. While the Rivaini tradition is for the spirit to fully possess the seer, this spirit respects the Qun and will therefore only speak to Rowan, who will relay the message. She also explains she asked to meet Taash here specifically, as this spirit recognizes this place is important—and peaceful—to Taash. She agrees Karash will heal alongside the Rivaini, though admits his teaching of the weather may not be as useful to a people of sailors.
The Qunari linguist reveals she was once a friend of Shathann's. She assures Rook Karash will be safe with the Qunari and that he will go to those who can help heal the mind. She explains she studies both
Qunlat
and the culture of the First Expedition in hopes of bettering the Qunari people.
Either way, the translator states the tablet reveals information about the
adaari
and the history of the Qunari, and informs them the tablet's message is sacred. It cannot be written down, only passed down verbally. Taash breathes fire on the tablet, restoring the missing runes, and the translator recites the following message:
"By dragon-flame come these words. Trust no tale not hidden by the fire. When the great foe struck, we embraced the blood of dragons. Champions blessed with fire protected us and saw the unseen. We escaped to distant shores, but found no peace. Disease will lay us low. If more of us flee across the ocean, it means our people failed. Prepare them for the Devouring Storm."
The Qunari translator states this means Taash holds a place of honor in the Qun, and says
Shathann
was right to take Taash from the Qunari and protect them. Rowan says this means Taash has a purpose, and they must prepare to fight against the ancient enemy the Qunari fled.
Either way, Taash stalks away in frustration, effectively ending the conversation. Go after them, following the path clockwise until reaching the high platform Taash stands on, roaring at the sky.
Taash struggles with the responsibilities left to them, as well as the fact that Shathann is gone. Rook helps them come to terms with how much they miss her, and they conclude that while the Qunari thought they were a mistake and the Dragon King thought they were a weapon, Shathann was the only one who believed in them.
Depending on their path, they battle either with with the idea that the Qunari are fractured or that the Qunari have lost so much of their history. If Taash has become more Qunari, they decide to help protect the Qunari; they must be united against whatever comes next, including the Devouring Storm. They will reach out to the Antaam and try to persuade them to rejoin the Qun. If Taash has become more Rivaini, they decide to investigate Qunari legend and history regarding the Devouring Storm and recruit Antaam to the ranks of the
Lords of Fortune
.
Once this conversation concludes, the quest completes.
The next time Rook returns to the Lighthouse, a missive will be waiting from the translator.
Rewards
Completing the quest yields:
500 XP
150 gold
Codex update
Results
Taash has received word that the ancient Qunari tablet has been translated. They are ready to meet a Rivaini seer for some answers.
The ancient Qunari tablet's translation proved that adaari like Taash are not a mistake, but were created specifically to fight an ancient threat known as the Devouring Storm. While Taash still mourns, they now know know their mother was right about Taash having a higher purpose, and Taash will honor that calling as best they can.
Approval
Meeting Karash
They asked how I was. They asked if I would like to come with them and teach about the weather.
Do you want to go? —
Taash Approves
I'd miss your predictions.
You're okay with seers?
(Question) The air is running away?
(Repeats)
Right. Let's go see what the damn tablet says.
Are you okay? —
Taash Approves
Why out here?
Lead the way. —
Taash Approves
Finding Taash after the talk with Rowan or the Qunari linguist
She can't just leave me with this! She can't just... leave me...
It's okay to miss her.
Tell me what she'd say. —
Taash Approves
She didn't. —
Taash Approves
How did they forget everything the tablet said? How did they lose so much?
Who cares what they think? —
Taash Approves
Politics.
They were at war.
Codex updates
Taarlok Itwari (Remembering the Fallen)
Ancient Tablet: Questions My Mother Would Have Asked
Missives

Trivia
If Rook suggests they hold hands to summon the spirit, Rowan curtly replies this is not
Nevarra
.

======================================================================

Quest
Wounded in the Forest
Location
West Brecilian Forest
Start
Deygan
End
West Brecilian Forest
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Wounded in the Forest
is a side quest found in the
West Brecilian Forest
.
Deygan
, an
elven
hunter, is found wounded in the forest.
The Warden
must decide what to do.
Walkthrough
In the West Brecilian Forest, just past the first encounter with
Swiftrunner
, past the waterfall to the east, then south, the Warden can find a wounded elven hunter, Deygan. After sputtering a few blood soaked words, he collapses and the Warden is presented with several options:
Return to the
Dalish Camp
with Deygan, thus saving his life and earning a rare slice of gratitude from the elves.
Wake him back up and talk to him.
Wake him back up and kill him.
Loot his body (then take him back to the camp, or kill him).
Choosing to return Deygan to the Dalish Camp triggers a cut scene where the party meets a patrol lead by
Mithra
. She will take Deygan from there, and the party is returned at the start of the West Brecilian Forest.
Wynne
and
Leliana
and
Sten
will gain
(
+4
) approval if you choose to return the hunter to the Dalish Camp. If
Morrigan
is also in your party, the two will engage in some religious banter but Morrigan will not lose any approval. If you bring Deygan's body to the camp
Oghren
will gain
(
+2
) approval.
If you kill Deygan:
If
Leliana
is in the party she says that Deygen is helpless; you can you go ahead and kill him
(
-5
) or let him live.
If
Shale
is in the party:
If
Leliana
is in the party
Shale disapproves (
-3
)
If
Leliana
is not in the party then Shale says that Deygan is not infected; you can go ahead and kill him (
Shale disapproves (
-3
)) or let him live.
If
Wynne
is in the party:
If
Leliana
is in the party
Wynne disapproves (
-5
)
If
Leliana
is not in the party then Wynne says that asks you to remind her of this the next time you need healing; you can go ahead and kill him (
Wynne disapproves (
-5
)) or let him live.
At this point, the quest will be marked as completed, but the Warden can go back to the Dalish Camp and speak with Zathrian, who directs the Warden to speak with Deygan (if he is alive).
Note:
If you leave the wounded Deygan where he is and complete
Nature of the Beast
, then the elf will no longer be there. If you had initiated the quest, then it will remain open and you won't be able to complete it in any way.
Rewards
If the Warden loots Deygan while he is unconscious or after you kill him:
Deygan's Dal'Thanu
Deygan's Boots
Figurine
If the Warden kills Deygan, leaves his body in the forest, and then loots his corpse:
Dar'Misu
7
Elf-Flight Arrow
Deygan's Boots
Figurine
Note:
Deygan's Boots can be traded to the
Mad Hermit
in the
East Brecilian Forest
.
Exploit
You can get two sets of Deygan's items, and a few other miscellanea, by looting him when unconscious, waking him, killing him when he next falls unconscious, and then looting his body.
Note:
This is not possible on the
pc
Another way is after you loot and kill him, to use the Reaver skill
Devour
which will take Deygan's body and leave only his bones, which can be looted.
Back at camp
If Deygan is saved, he can later be found back in the camp near
Athras
. 
If the Warden did not loot his gear, Deygan will offer a
Sapphire
as a reward.
If the Warden did loot Deygan's gear, he will instead ask if the Warden found a small bone figurine next to his body, back in the Forest. The Warden may answer:
I did, actually.
(
Persuade
) No, I didn't. Sorry.
The first option leads to the Warden being able to return the figurine; Deygan offers a
Sapphire
as a reward.
The second option leads Deygan to say "Ah, what a shame" and run away.
If Deygan offers a Sapphire:
Accept it; you get the Sapphire and 100 XP.
Tell him to keep it. You don't get any XP.
If you decide to kill Deygan after you loot his gear, you can opt to take his body back to the Dalish Camp anyway; instead of Deygan himself, Mithra will ask you if you found the figurine (as she believes Deygan would want to be buried with it). If you return the figurine, Mithra will tell you Zathrian may have a reward for you; if you speak to Zathrian, he will give you the Sapphire (you don't get the option to tell him to keep it) and you also get 100 XP.
Clan attitude
This quest gives an opportunity to affect the Clan Attitude global variable, which is a measure of the clan's approval of you.
If Deygan is alive and returned to camp, he will offer you a Sapphire as a reward. 
If you tell him to keep it, Clan Attitude will increase.
If Deygan is dead and his equipment was looted, when he is returned to camp, Mithra will ask if the player found his
Figurine
. The Clan Attitude will decrease if the player:
Admits to taking it, but refuses to return it.
Admits to taking it, but no longer has it.
Replies with
"No, nothing like that."
but fails the persuade check (Persuade 2 required).
Gallery
Exact location of Deygan on the map

Quest
Wynne's Regret
Location
Camp
Quest Giver
Wynne
End
Wynne
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Wynne's Regret
is
Wynne
's
companion quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Walkthrough
Talk to
Wynne
about her past and have her become
warm
towards the Hero.
During a
World map encounter
named
Hillside Path
, Wynne will collapse at the end of battle. Head to camp and talk to her.
Talk to Wynne (
this may require multiple conversations
) until the Hero has the option of asking her about any past regrets. She will tell the story of her old apprentice
Aneirin
. At this point the quest should be added to the journal, and
Wynne approves (
+3
).
Go to the
Dalish Camp
in the
Brecilian Outskirts
and find an elf named
Sarel
sitting on a bench. He will tell the Hero of Aneirin. If they have completed
Nature of the Beast
, they may need to speak to him multiple times.
Have Wynne in the party, travel to the
East Brecilian Forest
and go just north above the
Mad Hermit
's clearing.
Aneirin
will hand over an amulet called
Aneirin's Token
that augments Wynne's
Vessel of the Spirit
spell and protects her.
Wynne approves (
+4
).
Result
125 XP
Approval from
Wynne
Aneirin's Token
Healer available in
East Brecilian Forest
Notes
Another way to activate this quest is by asking Wynne if she has ever had any regrets during Camp dialogue
If the Hero sides with the werewolves in Nature of the Beast, then they can still encounter Aneirin by going to the East Brecilian Forest.
The Hillside Path encounter seems to only trigger after speaking to her in camp until she asks the Hero about their experiences with abominations.
Bugs
pc
If you talk to Aneirin before talking to Sarel, the next time you visit the Dalish Camp, he will have the "quest icon" over his head and will be able to talk to him, though you will have already completed the quest.
ps3
This personal quest can be difficult to trigger, however exhausting dialogue options in and out of camp will result in in a quest update.
In the conversation between Aneirin and Wynne, they will talk about First Enchanter Irving as if he is still alive even if Irving died during the events of the
Broken Circle
quest.

======================================================================
PAGE: Zerlinda's Woe
CATEGORIES: Dragon Age: Origins side quests
URL: https://dragonage.fandom.com/wiki/Zerlinda's_Woe
======================================================================

Quest
Zerlinda's Woe
Location
Dust Town
Start
Zerlinda
End
Zerlinda
Other NPCs
Ordel
Brother Burkel
Appearances
Dragon Age: Origins
Zerlinda's Woe
is a
side quest
in
Dragon Age: Origins
.
Walkthrough
This section contains spoilers for:
Dragon Age: Origins
.
In the streets of
Dust Town
,
the Warden
can find a beggar named
Zerlinda
in need of assistance. If the Warden talks to her, she will ask for money for her son. If money is handed over (5
) or the Warden asks about her situation, she will explain that she fell in love and had a son with a
casteless
man. Because, in
dwarven
society, children inherit the
caste
of their same-sex parent, her child is casteless and her family has thrown her out unless she abandons the child in the
Deep Roads
. Her child's father abandoned her as he was hoping Zerlinda would bear a daughter so that she would be
mining caste
like her mother and thus as the child's father be accepted to live in Zerlinda's home. Simultaneously, Zerlinda's father,
Ordel
, also expelled her from his
house
as he could not petition the
Assembly
for status of his casteless grandchild and it would bring a lot of embarassment to the rest of his family if he kept a casteless child inside his home.
There are various options available:
The Warden can convince her that she shouldn't leave
Orzammar
and help reconcile her with her family.
She will ask why you are helping. Agreeing to help her will net
(
+2
) from
Wynne
if she is in the party.
To persuade her family to help, travel to
Tapster's Tavern
in the
Orzammar Commons
, where her father Ordel is usually located at evenings.
Corra
, the hostess of Tapster's, can also be asked if Ordel is inside and she will mention that he just returned from the mine so he isn't very chatty.
The Warden can force him to change his mind about her using persuasion (requires Improved
Coercion
) or intimidation (requires Expert Coercion).
Once he has agreed to welcome her back, return to Zerlinda and tell her the good news.
If the
Chant in the Deeps
quest for
Brother Burkel
has been completed and there is now a chantry in Orzammar, you may:
Tell her to go to the
Orzammar chantry
. She will ask the Warden to speak with the Brother as she doesn't know much about the
Chantry
.
Go to Brother Burkel. You may ask if he has a job for her, food and shelter for the boy, or you can ask if he will take the boy as a ward of the Chantry. He will happily agree to any of these requests.
Return to Zerlinda with the good news. The next time she can be found
praying
inside Orzammar's chantry.
The Warden can encourage Zerlinda to go to the surface. This nets
(
+1
) from
Wynne
if she is in the party.
A final option is to persuade Zerlinda to consign the child to the perils of the
Deep Roads
and go home (requires Improved Coercion).
Result
The quest choices in
Dragon Age Keep
Completing the quest by finding a solution to her problem nets 200 experience.
Note:
Successfully persuading Zerlinda to consign her son does not net 200 experience unlike all other options.
If the Warden chose to reconcile Zerlinda with her family, she promises that when her son becomes of age, he will become a knight in the Warden's service.
Bugs
If the Warden gives 5
to Zerlinda, no money will actually be taken from your inventory. Furthermore, the option to give Zerlinda the money is also available even if the Warden does not have 5
at that moment.
If the Warden tells Zerlinda that you could talk to her family, say that you were hoping she'd offer you more of a reason, and tell her to fix her own problems, then the conversation ends. You get this quest (uncompleted) but you cannot finish it - she never told you about her father, and if you talk to her again she only says "I have to find food for my child."  The quest remains active in your Journal and says "You refused to help Zerlinda, the young mother. She remains alone and penniless."
